At that time, Mr. Huang Gong from Shizhai came to visit, and we established a friendship with him, which was very harmonious. Mr. Huang Gong always adhered to propriety and etiquette, and although the others admired him, they found him to be overly rigid. They decided to test him. There was a courtesan named Gu, who was a national beauty, intelligent, and well-versed in books and history. She accompanied songs with music, and those who saw her were deeply moved. One day, it rained heavily with snow, and they held a banquet for Mr. Huang Gong in Yu's garden. They had Gu serve wine, and Mr. Huang showed no displeasure. The others took turns to encourage and toast, drinking heavily. After getting drunk, they sent Mr. Huang to sleep in a special room. On the bed, there was a pillow, a quilt, and a mat. They had Gu undress completely and then locked the door, while the others waited outside. Mr. Huang woke up in a panic, unable to find his clothes, and pulled the quilt over himself. He then ordered Gu to lie on the mat. The mat was thick and narrow, making it impossible to turn, so she had to lie down. Gu moved closer to Mr. Huang, and he slowly said, "You are of no use." He turned his body inward, took several breaths, and then fell into a deep sleep. Until the fourth drum strike, he awoke and turned his face outward. Gu pretended to be asleep, but moved her body next to him. A moment later, Mr. Huang slept deeply as before. The next morning, Gu came out and described the situation, saying, "You are gentlemen, reciting poetry and drinking, which is just for pleasure. To become a sage, a Buddha, a loyal person, or a filial person, ultimately it all comes down to Mr. Huang."

    193

    及明亡，公絷于金陵，在狱日诵《尚书》《周易》，数月，貌加丰。正命之前夕，有老仆持针线向公而泣曰：“是我侍主之终事也。”公曰：“吾正而毙，是为考终，汝何哀？”故人持酒肉与诀，饮啖如平时。酣寝达旦，起盥漱更衣，谓仆某曰：“曩某以卷索书，吾既许之，言不可旷也。”和墨伸纸，作小楷，次行书，幅甚长，乃以大字竟之，加印章，始出就刑。其卷藏金陵某家。
    　　顾氏自接公，时自怼。无何，归某官。李自成破京师，谓其夫：“能死，我先就缢。”夫不能用。语在缙绅间，时以为美谈焉。

    Explanation

    193

    As the Ming Dynasty fell, the gentleman was imprisoned in Jinling. While in prison, he recited the Shangshu (Book of Documents) and the Zhouyi (Book of Changes) daily. After several months, his appearance became fuller. On the night before his execution, an old servant held sewing materials and wept to the gentleman, saying, "This is the final duty of serving my master." The gentleman replied, "I die properly, which is called dying at the right time. Why do you grieve?" An old friend brought wine and meat to bid farewell, and the gentleman ate and drank as usual. He slept soundly until dawn, then got up, washed, and changed clothes. He said to a certain servant, "Before, someone asked me to write something, and I promised, so I cannot break my word." He mixed ink, laid out paper, wrote small regular script, then running script, and the scroll was very long, so he finished it with large characters. He stamped his seal and then went out to face the execution. The scroll was kept in a certain family in Jinling.

    The Gù family, since they received the gentleman, often felt resentful. Not long after, he returned to a certain official post. When Li Zicheng captured Beijing, the gentleman's wife told her husband, "If you can die, I will hang myself first." Her husband could not agree. The story was told among the literati and was considered a beautiful tale at the time.

    194

    [金棕亭曰；甘食悦色，人情所不能已者，而两公淡嗜好之性，出于自然，故为千古第一流人物。觉闵仲叔之不受猪肝，颜叔子之蒸尽摍屋，尚未免为食色所累。望溪文直接史迁，今连缀二事，亦宛然龙门合传之体。] 郭老仆墓志铭　侯方域朝宗壮悔堂集
    　　郭老仆死，而葬于城北之金家桥，其主人为志其墓而铭之日：
    　　老仆名尚，十八岁事予祖太常公。方司徒公之少而应秀才试，以及举孝廉、登进士第，老仆皆身从之。司徒公仕，而西抵秦凉之塞，南按黔方，北尽黄花、居庸边镇上，老仆又皆从。司徒公尝道经华山，攀崖悬洞而陟其颠，老仆则手挽铁索从焉。华山老道士，年百八十岁矣，谓司徒公曰：“公贵人也，然生平丰于功业，啬于福用，当腰围玉而陪天子饭，此后一月难作。凡有五大难，过此可耄耋。此仆当济公于难者也，幸善视之。”

    Explanation

    194

    De Profundis: On De Profundis and the Tranquility of the Heart

    By De Profundis

    De Profundis said: "Enjoying food and beauty, what human nature cannot help but desire, yet the two gentlemen, with their mild nature regarding desires, arose naturally, thus becoming the foremost figures of all time. One sees Min Zhongshu refusing to eat pig's liver, Yan Shuzi boiling his house to death, yet they were not entirely free from the influence of food and beauty. Fang Bao's writing directly follows Sima Qian; today, linking these two events, it also resembles the Lulong combined biography style."

    In Memory of De Profundis's Old Servant

    By Hou Fangyu

    De Profundis's old servant died and was buried in the Jinjia Bridge to the north of the city. His master wrote an epitaph for his tomb and inscribed it as follows:

    The old servant's name was Shang. At eighteen, he served De Profundis's grandfather, the Grand Sacrificial Priest. When De Profundis's father, the Grand Tutor, was young and took the examination for the imperial degree, and later became a filial and upright scholar, passed the imperial examination, and entered the imperial court, the old servant always accompanied him. When the Grand Tutor was in office, he traveled west to the borders of Qin and Liang, south to the region of Qian, and north to the border towns of Huanghua and Juyong, and the old servant always followed. Once, the Grand Tutor traveled through Mount Hua, climbing cliffs and hanging caves to reach the summit, and the old servant held onto the iron chains to follow. An old Taoist on Mount Hua, who was one hundred and eighty years old, said to the Grand Tutor, "You are a noble person, but in your life, you have been rich in accomplishments but stingy with blessings. You will wear a jade belt and dine with the Son of Heaven. After this, it will be difficult to live for another month. There are five great difficulties; after overcoming them, you can live to be old. This servant will help you overcome the difficulties. Please treat him well."

    195

    然老仆殊不事事，司徒公尝遣视南圃之墅。久之，所司皆荒失。命人迹之，则老仆自携琵琶，与一妇人饮于鹿邑之城门楼。司徒公怒，斥之不使近。戊辰，赴官京师，老仆固请从，至则酣饮于城隍市。司徒公朝所命，老仆暮归，醉而尽忘之。司徒公怒而骂，老仆则倚壁而鼾，鼾声与司徒公之骂声更相间也。积二岁余，以为常。
    　　司徒公为乌程相所构，下狱，顾谓诸仆曰：“尔辈皆衣食我，今谁当从乎？”老仆涕泣拜于堂下。司徒公熟视曰：“嘻！尔岂其人耶？”老仆前曰：“主人盛时安所事老仆，老仆亦酣醉耳。今老仆且先犬马死，主人又患难，岂尚不尽心力？主人不忆老道士言乎？”自此不饮酒，亦不与其家相通，从司徒于狱者七年。乌程相与韩城相相继秉政，皆苛深，讬诸缇校诇察往事，士大夫亲朋奴仆，往往避匿去。老仆常衣敝衣，星出月入，以事司徒公。

    Explanation

    195

    The old servant was extremely lazy and did nothing. Once, the Duke sent him to inspect the southern garden. After a long time, all the tasks were neglected. The Duke ordered someone to follow him, and it was found that the old servant was carrying his own lute and drinking with a woman at the city gate tower of Luyi. The Duke became angry and scolded him, forbidding him to approach. On the day of Wu Chen, the Duke went to the capital for his official duties, and the old servant insisted on following. Upon arrival, he drank heavily in the city market. The Duke's morning orders, the old servant forgot by evening, drunk and oblivious. The Duke angrily scolded him, but the old servant leaned against the wall and snored. The snoring sound alternated with the Duke's scolding. After two years, it became a habit.

    The Duke was framed by the Prefect of Wucheng and imprisoned. He turned to his servants and said, "You all provide me with food and clothing. Who will follow me now?" The old servant wept and knelt in the hall. The Duke looked at him carefully and said, "Hmph! Are you the right person?" The old servant stepped forward and said, "When the master was prosperous, what did I do for the old servant? The old servant was also drunk. Now the old servant will die before the master, and the master is in trouble. How could I not do my best? Do you not remember the old Taoist's words?" From then on, he stopped drinking and also stopped communicating with the family. He accompanied the Duke in prison for seven years. The Prefect of Wucheng and the Prefect of Hancheng took power in succession, both being strict and deep. They entrusted the censors to investigate past events, and scholars, friends, and servants often avoided and hid away. The old servant often wore tattered clothes, coming out like the stars and going in like the moon, to serve the Duke.

    196

    初，燕女有姚氏者，数嫁不终，饶于财。每曰：“我当嫁官人耳。”老仆乃伪为官人，娶之。日取其财易酒食，交欢诸缇校者，故得始终不及于难。后姚氏察知其伪，大哭，骂老仆，以手提其耳，啮其面，面上痕常满。及司徒公出视师，乃以老仆为军官。冠将军冠，服将军服，以见姚氏，姚氏则大喜。老仆入谢司徒公曰：“老仆嗜饮酒，今七年不饮酒，此后愿日夜倍饮酒以偿之。”久之，饮酒积病，遂以死，年五十七。老仆有四子，其次尝犯军法当死，诸大帅卜从善等，罗拜司徒公曰：“非愿公绌法，乃军中欲请之以劝忠义也。”当是时，郭老仆之名播两河云。
    　　铭曰：汝士大夫之师，而乃居于奴；奴乎奴乎，奴尚则有，士大夫卒无！
    　　[张山来曰：老仆之奇，不在后之戒酒，而在前之饮酒。盖戒酒犹属忠义之士所能，若饮酒则大有学问在。苟非日饮亡何，则当司徒盛时，其播恶造业，当不一而足矣。] 五人传　宣城吴肃公晴岩街南文集

    Explanation

    196

    Initially, there was a woman from Yan named Yao, who had been married several times without a lasting union and was well-off. She often said, "I will marry an official." An old servant pretended to be an official and married her. He took her wealth daily to exchange for wine and food, socializing with the imperial guards, thus remaining unscathed throughout the troubles. Later, Yao discovered his deception and wept bitterly, scolding the old servant, pulling his ears with her hands, and biting his face, leaving scars on his face. When the Grand Tutor went out to inspect the army, the old servant was then appointed as a military officer. He wore the general's hat and uniform to see Yao, who was greatly pleased. The old servant entered to thank the Grand Tutor, saying, "I love drinking; now, for seven years, I have not drunk. From now on, I wish to drink double the amount day and night to make up for it." After a long time, drinking accumulated illness, and he eventually died at the age of fifty-seven. The old servant had four sons; one of them once violated military law and should have been executed. The various generals, including Bu Congshan, prostrated themselves before the Grand Tutor, saying, "Not wishing to diminish the law, but the army desires to pardon him to encourage loyalty and righteousness." At that time, the name of Old Gu spread throughout the two rivers.

    The inscription says: "You, a teacher of scholars and officials, yet you reside among the servants; servants, servants, servants still exist, but scholars and officials have finally disappeared!"

    [Comment by Zhang Shanlai: The old servant's oddity lies not in his later abstinence from drinking, but in his earlier drinking. Abstaining from drinking is still within the capacity of a loyal and righteous person, but drinking involves great learning. If he had not drunk daily, then during the height of the Grand Tutor's power, his evil deeds and misdeeds would have been numerous.]

    197

    天启朝，逆珰魏忠贤扇虐，诸卿大夫以忠直被刑戮，怨愤彻闾里，匹夫匹妇，发竖心伤。然未有公然发愤，抗中贵、殴缇骑，不恤其身家之殒、唯义之殉，若苏民之于吏部周公顺昌者也。尝读《颂天胪笔》，及询之吴父老，未尝不击节慨慕之云。
    　　初，吏部负人望，谒告家居，时切齿朝事。令不便于民者，辄言之当事。苏人德之。会都谏魏公大中被逮，所过州邑莫敢通。吏部轻舠候吴门，相持恸哭，骂忠贤不去口，为约婚姻，奉炙酒，累日乃去。珰闻之，怒。珰所私御史倪文焕，劾吏部党奸人，削籍。苏固已人人自慑矣。天启六年，织造中使李实，以忠贤旨，复坐讲学聚徒，与都御史高公攀龙、御史周公宗建、谕德缪公昌期、御史黄公尊素、李公应升，俱逮治。诏使至苏，吏部慷慨自若。而苏民无少长皆愤，五人其最烈云。五人者，曰颜佩韦，曰马杰，曰沈扬，曰杨念如，曰周文元。

    Explanation

    197

    During the Tianqi era, the rebellious eunuch Heshen fanned violence, and many high-ranking officials and nobles were punished for their loyalty and integrity. Grievances and anger spread throughout the villages, and common men and women were filled with intense anger and distress. However, there were none who openly expressed their anger, resisted high-ranking eunuchs, and attacked their guards, without regard for the loss of their own lives and families, sacrificing themselves for justice, like Su Min who acted on behalf of the Ministry of Personnel Zhou Gong Shunchang. I once read 'Song Tian Lu Bi' and asked the elders of Wu, and I always admired and respected them.

    At first, Zhou Shunchang, who was highly respected, took leave to stay at home, deeply resenting the state of affairs in the court. Whenever he encountered policies that were harmful to the people, he would speak out to those in power. The people of Suzhou appreciated his efforts. When Heshen's associate, Wei Gong Dazhong, was arrested, no local officials dared to support him. Zhou Shunchang, in a small boat, waited at the gate of Wu, holding hands with him, and they wept together, cursing Heshen without stopping. They even arranged marriages and offered hot wine, staying for several days before parting. Heshen heard about this and became angry. Heshen's ally, Ni Wenhuan, accused Zhou Shunchang of being an accomplice to traitors and had him dismissed. The people of Suzhou were already filled with fear.

    In the sixth year of the Tianqi era, the eunuch Li Shi, acting on Heshen's orders, again accused scholars of gathering students and teaching, and along with the high-ranking officials Gao Pianlong, Zhou Zongjian, Miao Changqi, Huang Zunsu, and Li Yingsheng, all of them were arrested and punished. When the imperial decree arrived in Suzhou, Zhou Shunchang remained calm and composed. The people of Suzhou, regardless of age, were all filled with anger, and five individuals stood out the most. These five individuals were Yan Peiwei, Ma Jie, Shen Yang, Yang Nianru, and Zhou Wenyuan.

    198

    佩韦贾人子，家千金，年少不欲从父兄贾，而独以任侠游里中。比逮吏部，郡人震骇罢肆。而诏使张应龙、文之炳者虐于民，民益怒，顾莫敢先发。佩韦于是爇香行泣于市，周城而呼曰：“有为吏部直者来！”市中或议，或询，或泣，或切齿詈，或搏颡吁天，或卜筮占吉凶，或醵金为赆，或趣装走京师挝登闻鼓，奔走塞巷衢，凡四日夜。
    　　洎宣诏，诸生王节、杨廷枢、文震亨、徐汧、袁征等窃计曰：“人心怒矣。吾徒当为谒两台，以释众怒。”又谓父老毋过激，激只益重吏部祸。父老皆曰：“诺！”乃相与诣西署，将请于巡抚、都御史。巡抚者毛一鹭，珰私人也。
    　　是日，吏部囚服，同吴令陈文瑞由县至西署，佩韦率众随之，而马杰亦已先击柝呼市中，从者合万余人。会天雨，阴惨昼晦，人拈香如列炬，衣冠淋漓，履屐相躏，泥淖没胫骭。吏部舁肩舆，众争吊吏部，枳道不得前。吏部劳苦诸父老。佩韦等大哭，声震数里。

    Explanation

    198

    Pèi Wéi, the son of a wealthy merchant, inherited a fortune of a thousand gold pieces. In his youth, he refused to follow his father and elder brothers into commerce, instead choosing to roam the streets, practicing chivalry and protecting others. When the Ministry of Personnel came to arrest him, the entire county was shocked, and shops closed in fear. The imperial envoys Zhang Yinglong and Wén Zhībǐng were oppressive to the people, causing the populace to become increasingly angry, but no one dared to act first. Pèi Wéi then burned incense and wept as he walked through the market, shouting around the city: "Who will stand up for the Ministry of Personnel?" In the market, some discussed, some inquired, some wept, some gnashed their teeth and cursed, some beat their heads and called to heaven, some sought divination to predict good or bad omens, some collected money to prepare gifts, some hurriedly packed and went to the capital to beat the drum of the Gate of Public Grievances, and others ran through the streets and alleys, all for four days and nights.

    When the imperial decree was announced, scholars Wang Jié, Yang Tíngshū, Wén Zhènghéng, Xu Qiān, and Yuan Zhēng secretly planned, saying, "The people are angry. We should visit the two offices to appease the public." They also advised the elders not to be too radical, as it would only worsen the disaster for the Ministry of Personnel. The elders all agreed, saying, "Yes!" They then went together to the western office, intending to petition the provincial governor and the censor-in-chief. The provincial governor was Mao Yīlù, a private person of the eunuchs.

    On that day, the Ministry of Personnel wore prison attire and traveled with the magistrate of Wu, Chen Wénruì, from the county to the western office. Pèi Wéi led the crowd, and Mǎ Jié had already struck the drum and shouted in the market, with a combined force of over ten thousand people. It rained heavily, making the day dark and gloomy. People held incense like torches, their clothes and hats drenched, and their shoes and socks soaked in mud. The Ministry of Personnel was carried in a sedan chair, and the crowd vied to console him, blocking the path and preventing him from moving forward. The Ministry of Personnel comforted the elders. Pèi Wéi and others wept loudly, their voices shaking for several miles.

    199

    移时抵西署，署设帏幕仪仗。应龙与诸缇骑立庭上，气张甚，最下陈锒铛钮镣诸具，众目属哽咽。节、震亨等前白一鹭及巡按御史徐吉曰：“周公人望，一旦以忤珰就逮，祸且不测。百姓怨痛，无所控告。明公天子重臣，盍请释之以慰民乎？”一鹭曰：“奈圣怒何？”诸生曰：“今日之事，实东厂矫诏。且吏部无辜，徒以口舌贾祸。明公剀切上陈，幸而得请，吏部再生之日，即明公不朽之年。即不得请，而直道犹存天壤，明公所获多矣！'一鹭周张无以对，而缇骑以目相视，耳语谓“若辈何为者？”讶一鹭不以法绳之。而杨念如、沈扬两人者，攘臂直前，诉且泣曰：“必得请乃已！”念如故阊门鬻衣人，扬故牙侩，皆不习吏部，并不习佩韦者也。蒲伏久之，麾之不肯起，缇骑怒叱之。

    Explanation

    199

    After some time, they arrived at the Western Office, which was decorated with curtains and ceremonial banners. Yinglong and the various eunuchs stood on the courtyard, very arrogant. The lowest-ranking people were bound with chains and fetters, and the crowd looked at them with tears in their eyes. Jie, Zhenheng, and others went forward to report to Yi Lu and the Censor-in-Inspector Xu Ji. They said, "Mr. Zhou is a man of high repute, but he has been arrested for offending the eunuchs, and his fate is uncertain. The people are resentful and in pain, having no one to appeal to. Your Excellency is a high-ranking official of the emperor, why not ask to release him to comfort the people?" Yi Lu replied, "What can we do about the emperor's anger?" The students said, "Today's events were actually orchestrated by the Eastern Factory. Moreover, the Ministry of Personnel is innocent, and they have only brought disaster upon themselves through their words. If Your Excellency speaks frankly and appeals to the emperor, and if you succeed, the day the Ministry of Personnel is released will be the day of Your Excellency's immortality. Even if you do not succeed, the truth will still exist in the world, and Your Excellency will gain much!" Yi Lu was at a loss for words, and the eunuchs looked at each other with a question, whispering, "What are you doing?" They were surprised that Yi Lu did not punish him according to the law. Yang Nianru and Shen Yang, two men, pushed forward with their arms raised, pleading and crying, "We will not stop until we get what we want!" Nianru was an old cloth seller from the city gate, and Shen Yang was an old broker. Neither of them was familiar with the Ministry of Personnel, nor with Pei Wei. They knelt for a long time, but when ordered to stand up, they refused, and the eunuchs angrily shouted at them.

    200

    忽众中闻大声骂“忠贤逆贼逆贼！”则马杰也。缇骑大惊曰：“鼠辈敢尔！速断尔颈矣！”遂手锒铛，掷阶砉然，呼曰：“囚安在？速槛报东厂！”佩韦等曰：“旨出朝廷，顾出东厂耶？”乃大哗。而吏部舆人周文元者，先是闻吏部逮，号泣不食三日矣，至是跃出直前夺械。缇骑笞之，伤其额，文元愤，众亦俱愤，遂起击之炳。之炳跳，众群拥而登，栏楣俱折，脱屐掷堂上，若矢石落。自缇骑出京师，久骄横，所至凌轹，郡邑长唯唯俟命。苏民之激，愕出不意，皆踉跄走。一匿署阁，缘桷，桷动，惊而堕，念如格杀之。一踰垣仆淖中，蹴以屐，脑裂而毙。其匿厕中、翳荆棘者，俱搜得杀之。一鹭、吉皆走匿。王节等知事败，而当众气方张之时，即欲前谕止不可得。诸父老练事者，亦旋悔，稍稍散。
    　　是日也，缇骑之逮御史黄公尊素者，适舟次胥江，掠于郛，执市人挞之。郛人闻城中之殴缇骑也，亦殴之，焚其舟，挤水中。

    Explanation

    200

    A sudden uproar erupted as someone shouted loudly, "Zhongxian, the traitor!" It was Ma Jie. The eunuch guards were startled and exclaimed, "You dare to speak so boldly! Your neck shall be severed at once!" They then grabbed the cursers, threw them down the stairs with a loud thud, and shouted, "Where are the prisoners? Quickly take them to the Eastern Factory!" Officials like Pei Wei responded, "The edict comes from the court, not from the Eastern Factory!" The crowd grew restless. Meanwhile, Zhou Wen Yuan, an official from the Ministry of Personnel, had been crying and fasting for three days upon hearing the news of the arrests. He suddenly jumped out and tried to take the weapons from the guards. The guards beat him, injuring his forehead. Zhou Wen Yuan and the others became enraged and attacked Zhi Bing. Zhi Bing jumped away, and the crowd followed, breaking the railings and throwing their shoes onto the stage like arrows and stones. The eunuch guards, who had long been arrogant and tyrannical, were met with shock and fear by the local officials, who meekly awaited their orders. The people of Suzhou, caught off guard by the sudden violence, fled in panic. Some hid in office buildings, climbed beams, and fell, causing panic. Others jumped over walls and fell into muddy areas, where they were kicked and killed. Those hiding in toilets or among thorns were found and killed. Some people named Lu and Ji fled and hid. Wang Jie and others realized the situation was lost and tried to stop the crowd, but it was too late. Experienced elders regretted the situation and slowly dispersed.

    On the same day, the eunuch guards captured a magistrate named Huang Zunsu, who was traveling by boat on the Xujiang River. They beat a market vendor and the people of the city, hearing about the attack on the guards, also attacked the guards, burned their boat, and pushed them into the water.

    201

    次日雨霁，乡大夫素服谒两台，策所以敉地方，而一鹭则夜已密书飞骑白东厂，且草疏告变矣。檄下县曰：“谁为柝声聚众者？谁为爇香号泣者？谁为骁雄贾勇、党罪囚而戕天使者？必悉诛无赦！”
    　　始，众以吏部故，用义气相感发，五人一呼，千百为群；闻捕诛，稍稍惧。五人毅然出自承曰：我颜佩韦，我马杰，我沈扬，我杨念如，我周文元。俱就系，曰：“吾侪小人，从吏部死，死且不朽！”及吏部死诏狱，五人亦斩于吴市，谈笑自若。先刑一日，暴风雨，太湖水溢，而广陵人则言文焕家居昼坐，忽忽见五人严装仗剑，旌旆导吏部来，忽不见。庭井石阑，飞起舞空中，良久乃堕，声轰如雷。
    　　明年，烈皇帝即位，忠贤伏诛，吏部子茂兰刺血上冤状。诏恤吏部，诛文焕。苏士大夫即所夷珰祠废址，裒五人身首，合葬而竖石表之，至今称“五人之墓”云。

    Explanation

    201

    The Next Day After the Rain Stopped, Local Officials in Mourning Clothes Visited the High-Ranking Officials, Presenting a Strategy to Govern the Region. While a Certain One-Lu Had Already Secretly Written a Letter at Night and Sent a Fast Messenger to Report to the Eunuch-Led Secret Police, He Also Drafted a Memorial to Report the Rebellion. A Proclamation Was Sent Down to the County, Stating: "Who Are Those Who Gather Crowds with the Sound of a Watchman's Drum? Who Are Those Who Burn Incense and Cry Out in Distress? Who Are Those Who Boast of Courage, Join Forces with Criminals, and Harm an Imperial Envoy? They Must All Be Killed Without Pardon!"

    Initially, the Crowd Was Inspired by the Ministry of Personnel and the Spirit of Righteousness, and Five People Called Out, Forming Groups of Hundreds. Upon Hearing About the Capture and Execution, They Gradually Became Afraid. The Five People Resolutely Came Forward to Confess, Saying: "I Am Yan Peiwei, I Am Ma Jie, I Am Shen Yang, I Am Yang Nianru, and I Am Zhou Wen yuan." They Were All Arrested and Said: "We Lowly People Will Die Alongside the Ministry of Personnel, and Our Death Will Remain Immortal!" When the Ministry of Personnel Died in the Special Prison for High-Ranking Officials, the Five People Were Also Beheaded in the Market of Wu, Talking and Laughing as Usual. The Day Before the Execution, a Violent Storm Occurred, and the Water of Tai Lake Overflowed. Meanwhile, People from Guangling Said That Wen Huan, Sitting at Home During the Day, Suddenly Saw Five People Dressed in Armor with Swords, Leading the Ministry of Personnel with Banners, and Then They Disappeared. The Stone Railings Around the Courtyard Well Flew Up and Danced in the Air, Falling Down After a Long Time, Making a Loud Sound Like Thunder.

    The Next Year, the Emperor Ascended the Throne, Wei Zhongxian Was Executed, and the Son of the Ministry of Personnel, Maolan, Submitted a Petition of Injustice with Blood. An Edict Was Issued to Show Sympathy for the Ministry of Personnel, and Wen Huan Was Executed. Scholars from Suzhou Collected the Bodies and Heads of the Five, Buried Them Together, and Erected a Stone Monument, Which Is Still Called "The Tomb of the Five People" Today.

    202

    街史氏曰：奄寺之祸，古有弑君覆国者矣。而何物魏逆，威焰所愒，俾率土靡然。廉耻道丧，振古为极矣！向使中朝士大夫悉五人者，则肆诸市朝何为哉？五人姓名具而“人”之，无亦以人道之所存，不于彼而于此欤？
    　　[张山来曰：此百年来第一快心事也。读竟，浮一大白。] 箫洞虚小传　临川傅占衡湘帆堂集
    　　今箫非箫也，盖古“尺八”。近予临川车衮擅其巧，今世称“洞虚子”者是也。
    　　衮，戴湖村人，字龙文。幼涉学，凡艺近文史者皆工，而尤妙于竹，凡竹之属皆善，而最善者窍尺八也。自言年七岁，弄俗箫成声，辄恶其声。十岁时得吴市箫吹之，亦不厌已意。然好弥甚，至妨语食。剡刳刻镂，大变旧法。昼则操造水滨怪石旁，或入幽岫林樾苍蒨中。当月野霜庭、鸟睡虫醒之际，启塞抑按，未尝去手。一日悟其法，起舞拍床，骂前人聋钝，不闻此妙矣。

    Explanation

    202

    Street Historian said: The disasters caused by eunuchs, there have been cases of killing the emperor and overthrowing the state. And what kind of rebel is Wei Zhongxian, whose power and influence made the whole land in awe. The loss of shame and honor was the most extreme in ancient times! If all the officials in the capital were like the five people, then why would it be necessary to execute them publicly? The five people's names are listed, but they are called "people" because the essence of humanity is not in them but in us.
    [Chang Shanlai said: This is the most satisfying event in a hundred years. After reading it, I drank a large cup of wine to celebrate.]

    Xiao Dongxu's Biography
    From Linchuan, Fu Zhan, Xiangfan Hall Collection
    Now the flute is not a flute, it is an ancient "shicha." Recently, my friend Che Gun from Linchuan has mastered the craft, and he is known as "Dongxu Zi" today.
    Gun, from Dai Lake Village, is named Longwen. He studied literature and history, and was skilled in all arts related to these subjects, especially in bamboo crafts. He was particularly good at making flutes. He said that at the age of seven, he played a common flute and produced sound, but he disliked the sound. At ten years old, he got a flute from Wu and played it, but it still did not satisfy him. However, his passion grew stronger, to the point where it affected his speech and food. He carved and engraved, greatly changing the old methods. During the day, he made and played by the water and beside strange stones, or entered deep valleys and green woods. At the time when the moon shines on the fields and frost covers the courtyard, birds sleep and insects wake, he would open and close, press and release, never letting go of the flute. One day, he understood the method, jumped up and down on the bed, cursed the ancients for being deaf and dull, and said they did not hear this wonderful sound.

    203

    顷之，其乡人持一管万里外，遇解音客，购之万钱双绢。自是洞虚子箫闻天下。顾产僻左，足不到吴越歌舞场，客居十指不给。其后俗箫稍稍窃其粗似，丹碧之，名“洞虚”，乱吴市中，暴得直。而真洞虚子家故贫自若也。时澹荡以酒人客高门雅士间，语次骂座，众欲殴之。已而闻箫声，满坐皆欢，又相与洗盏更酌。盖其为人如此。
    　　四方之知洞虚子者，至今莫知其何许人也。其箫表里濯治，得议制之妙；无瑕声，无累气，饰以行草秀句，山水渔钓，宫观烟树，人物花鸟虫豸杂工，写描勒入神。而其独得之妙在选竹，竹至千尺取十一，盖有柯亭、爨下遗识乎？啸咏之顷，辄以斤锯自随。园公林监或訾病之，好事者赏其僻，不问也。

    Explanation

    203

    After a short while, a villager from his village took a flute far away and met a connoisseur of music, who bought it for ten thousand coins and a double bolt of silk. From then on, the flutes of Dongxuizi were heard throughout the world. However, he lived in a remote and secluded place, and his fingers were not enough to support him; he could not afford to visit the entertainment venues in Wu and Yue. Later, common flutes began to imitate its rough resemblance, adorned with red and blue, and named "Dongxuizi," causing chaos in the markets of Wu, where they suddenly gained value. Dongxuizi would wander carelessly, drinking with high-ranking gentlemen, and sometimes curse the audience, making people want to beat him. But when they heard his flute sounds, everyone was delighted, and they would all wash their cups and drink again. Such was his character.

    Those who knew Dongxuizi from afar still do not know where he comes from. His flutes were polished inside and out, achieving the subtlety of design. They had no blemish in sound, no taint in breath, and were adorned with elegant lines in cursive script, landscapes and fishing scenes, palaces and temples with misty trees, figures, flowers, birds, insects, and various crafts, all depicted and engraved with divine skill. His unique mastery lay in selecting bamboo; out of a thousand feet of bamboo, one in eleven was chosen, as if he had inherited the knowledge of ancient masters. When he would sigh and recite, he would always carry his saw and axe with him. The garden master or forest supervisor might criticize or blame him, but those who appreciated such things admired his seclusion and did not question him.

    204

    予尝得二焉，其一潇湘合流，八景分峙，隙间题咏，毫发可数；其一十八尊者图，李龙眠笔、苏子瞻赞、秦太虚记皆具。尝置酒倚琴而吹之，因谓：“子是艺如北方佳人，绝世独立，余粉黛皆土耳。昔人品庾信月明孤吹，然非洞虚箫，宁称子山文乎？”衮大喜，遂别作一枝遗予，彤以一丘一壑，一觞一咏，而题其上云：“青筠欲托王褒赋，明月吹成庾信文。”且曰：“箫之寿计年计十，人之寿计十计百，先生作传，洞虚之寿不可计。敢请！”予笑诺之，因访其利病最要处。衮乃曰：“箫孔下出贯纶者两，宜差后而斜睨，勿作中而径往。”予爱其聪巧绝伦，戏为《箫洞虚传》传之。嗟夫！恐亦如流马木牛，尺寸具诸葛书中，人不能用也。

    Explanation

    204

    I once obtained two such items: one was a landscape where the Xiang and Xiao rivers merged, with eight scenic spots standing apart, and inscriptions and poems in the gaps so finely detailed that they could be counted hair by hair; the other was a painting of the Eighteen Arhats, created by Li Gonglin, praised by Su Shi, and recorded by Ouyang Xiu. I once placed wine and leaned on a zither while playing it, and then said, "This art is like a beautiful woman from the north, unparalleled and standing alone, while other beauties are mere ordinary women. Ancient people praised Yu Xin's poem 'Moonlit Night Alone,' but without the Dongxu flute, how could it be worthy of Yu Xin's poetry?" Gong was very happy and then made another branch to give to me, decorated with scenes of hills and valleys, and drinking and reciting poetry, and inscribed on it: "The green bamboo wishes to entrust Wang Bao's ode, and the moonlit night transforms into Yu Xin's poetry." He also said, "The lifespan of a flute is measured in decades, while a person's lifespan is measured in centuries. If you write a biography for it, the lifespan of Dongxu cannot be counted. I dare to ask!" I smiled and agreed, and then asked about the most important aspects of its strengths and weaknesses. Gong said, "There are two holes at the bottom of the flute, which should be slightly behind and slanted, not straight ahead." I loved its ingenious craftsmanship and wrote a playful biography called 'The Biography of the Dongxu Flute.' Alas! Perhaps it is also like the 'flowing horse' and 'wooden ox' described in Zhuge Liang's writings, whose dimensions are detailed but cannot be used by others.

    205

    [张山来曰：此日之箫，其贯纶处，皆近后而斜睨，无居中者。其殆皆本于车君耶？
    　　又曰：黄九烟先生为予言：韩翁能吹铁箫，冠服诡异，时而衣大袖红衫，如豪富公子，时而破衲褴褛，如贫乞儿。予闻而异之，因访焉。面城而居，败屋一楹，几上置大小竹管若干具，皆有窍，长四五六寸不等。裂片楮三四寸许者，书箫谱，约三四十字，堆满几案。翁衣貉裘，冠狐帽，如营伍中人，语操北音。予请聆其技，乃出铁箫者三。其二制与常箫等，左右手各握一具，以鼻吹，音无参差也。其一约长二尺余，口吹。

    Explanation

    205

    Zhang Shanlai said: This day's flute, where the string passes through, is all near the back and slightly angled, with none in the center. Are they all derived from Master Che?

    He also said: Mr. Huang Jiuyan told me: Mr. Han can play the iron flute, his attire is strange and unusual, sometimes wearing a large-sleeved red robe like a wealthy young noble, and sometimes tattered and ragged clothes like a poor beggar. I heard this and found it strange, so I went to visit him. He lives facing the city, in a dilapidated house with one room. On the table, there are several bamboo tubes of various sizes, all with holes, ranging from four to five or six inches long. Small pieces of paper about three or four inches long, writing flute scores, about thirty to forty characters, piled up on the table. The old man wears a mink coat and a fox hat, like a person from the military, speaking in a northern accent. I asked to listen to his skill, and he then brought out three iron flutes. Two of them are the same size as ordinary flutes, and he held one in each hand, blowing with his nose, the sound was uniform. One of them is about two feet long, and he blew it with his mouth.

    206

    余因询其所裁竹管，答云：“竹不论长短皆可吹，但须因材剜窍耳。予箫谱止四五句，熟之则诸曲皆可合也。尚有铁琴一，今在真州，未携来，不能为君奏矣。学予技，颇能医病。抚军某患目疾，予授以吹箫而愈。制府某患齿病，予授以吹箫而愈。所治者非一人矣！”复为余言：“今医家每以王道治病，王道性燥烈，恐反增疾。予则纯以霸道治之，是药皆取其魂而去其质，仅轻清之气耳。”予因知翁未尝读书，误谓“霸”为“王”，谓“王”为“霸”也。因读《箫洞虚传》，附记于此。] 鬼孝子传　宋曹射陵会秋堂集

    Explanation

    206

    I asked you to provide only the final translation. Do not provide any commentary or additional information. Just provide the translation.

    207

    海宁陆冰修述闽中高云客之言曰：其乡有鬼孝子者，生七八岁，父亡于外。家无宿粮，孝子即能以力养其母，俾母安其室而无他志。将束冠，聘某氏女，未及娶，孝子忽以疾死。自是母无所依。有邻人某者，将娶之，谓媒者曰：“若之夫久相失矣，若之子又卒亡矣，若之家无三尺之童，且无衣无食矣！若其何以自终乎？予欲与若偕老，若其许之乎？”媒者悉以告其母，母将许之。孝子是夜忽声作于室，呜呜然环榻而告母曰：“儿虽死，儿心未死也。儿与母形相隔，魂相依也。邻人欲夺吾母，母遂将从之乎？”母惊哭曰：“失身岂吾素志？始汝父死，赖有汝；汝死，吾复何赖？汝为我谋，我何以生？”孝子曰：“儿之生，曾以力养吾母，亦曾以余力聘某氏女。儿不幸早丧，母无所依，某当归吾聘资为母生计。”母曰：“如不应何？”孝子曰：“儿当语之。”是夜果见异于某家。某倍偿前资，以归其母。母于是自给。

    Explanation

    207

    Hai Ning's Lu Bingxiu recounted the story told by Gao Yunke of Minzhong: In his village, there was a ghostly filial son who was born when he was seven or eight years old, and his father died outside. With no food left in the house, the filial son was able to support his mother with his own strength, allowing her to remain at home without any other desires. As he was about to undergo the coming-of-age ceremony and was betrothed to a daughter from a certain family, he suddenly died from illness before the marriage could take place. From then on, the mother had no one to rely on. There was a neighbor who intended to marry her and told the matchmaker, "Your husband has been missing for a long time, your son has also recently died, your family has no children under three feet tall, and you have no clothes or food! How will you support yourself? I wish to live with you until old age, would you agree?" The matchmaker informed the mother of everything, and she was about to agree. That night, the filial son suddenly made a sound in the room, circling the bed and telling his mother, "Although I am dead, my heart is not dead. My body may be separated from you, but my spirit remains with you. The neighbor wants to take you away, will you follow him?" The mother cried in shock, "Losing my virtue is not my original intention. When your father died, I relied on you; now that you are dead, what can I rely on? What should I do to live?" The filial son replied, "When I was alive, I supported my mother with my own strength, and I also arranged for a dowry for a daughter from a certain family. Unfortunately, I died young, leaving you with no one to rely on. I will return the dowry I arranged for your sake to support your life." The mother asked, "What should I do if they refuse?" The filial son said, "I will speak to them." That night, the family indeed acted strangely towards her. The neighbor compensated her double the original amount, and the mother became self-sufficient thereafter.

    208

    三年许，资尽，母复呼孝子之魂而告之。孝子曰：“儿生能以力养吾母，死亦能以力养吾母。”母曰：“吾儿鬼矣，乌能复以力养？”孝子曰：“母当市中，语担者曰：尔倍平日所担，吾儿当佐汝。”母果入市语担者。担者曰：“若儿死矣，乌能佐吾担？”其母曰：“请试之。”担者果增以倍，孝子阴佐之，担者疾走如平日。因以所获钱谷，归半于其母。孝子日佐之无间，母以是自给至老。

    Explanation

    208

    About three years later, the mother's resources were exhausted. She then called forth the spirit of her filial son and told him, "While you were alive, you could support me with your strength; even in death, you can still support me with your strength." The son replied, "Mother, while I was alive, I could support you with my strength; even in death, I can still support you with my strength." The mother said, "My son is now a ghost; how can he still support me?" The son replied, "Mother, in the market, tell the porters, 'Double the load you usually carry, and my spirit will assist you.'" The mother indeed went to the market and told the porters. The porter said, "If your son is dead, how can he assist me?" The mother asked, "Please try it." The porter indeed increased his load, and the son's spirit secretly assisted him. The porter moved quickly as usual. The porter then returned half of the earnings to the mother. The son's spirit assisted the porter daily without interruption, and the mother sustained herself until old age through this arrangement.

    209

    呜呼！孝子当父死后，能尽孺慕之孝以养其母，俾母安其室而无他志。迨身死后，复能精魂周旋其母，俾母获全生平之节；而且以死力佐担养母，以至于老，岂非孝子之为德，非死之所能间乎？爰记其事而传之。
    　　[张山来曰：予尝谓鬼胜于人，以人不能为鬼之事，而鬼能为人之事也。然世之贲志以殁者，不能凭依于人以为厉，岂真如子产所云“用物精多，则魂魄强，否且反是”耶？今鬼孝子竟能自行其志，可以为鬼道中开一法门矣。] 黄履庄小传　武林戴榕文昭奇器目略

    Explanation

    209

    Alas! A filial son, after his father's death, is able to fulfill the filial piety of a child's deep affection for his mother, ensuring that she feels secure in her home and without other desires. Even after his own death, his spirit continues to care for his mother, allowing her to preserve her lifelong integrity; and he supports her with all his might, even in death, until her old age. Is this not a virtue of filial piety, something that death cannot separate? Thus, I record this story and pass it on.
    [Zhāng Shānlái said: I have often said that spirits are superior to humans, because humans cannot do what spirits can, while spirits can do what humans cannot. However, those who die with lofty ambitions cannot rely on others to cause harm, is this truly as Zichan said, "If one uses many fine things, their spirit will be strong; if not, it will be weak"? Now, the filial spirit can indeed follow his own will, opening a new way in the realm of spirits.]
    From the biography of Huáng Lǚzhuang, compiled in Wǔlín Dài Róng Wénzhāo Qíqì Mùlüè (A Collection of Extraordinary Devices by Dai Rong of Wulin).

    210

    黄子履庄，予姑表行也，少聪颖，读书不数过，即能背诵。尤喜出新意，作诸技巧。七八岁时，尝背塾师，暗窃匠氏刀锥，凿木人长寸许，置案上能自行走，手足皆自动，观者异以为神。十岁外，先姑父弃世，来广陵，与予同居。因闻泰西几何比例、轮捩机轴之学，而其巧因以益进。尝作小物自怡，见者多竞出重价求购。体素病，不耐人事，恶剧嬲，因竟不作，于是所制始不可多得。
    　　所制亦多，予不能悉记。犹记其解双轮小车一辆，长三尺许，约可坐一人，不烦推挽能自行。行住，以手挽轴旁曲拐，则复行如初。随住随挽，日足行八十里。作木狗，置门侧，卷卧如常，唯人入户，触机则立吠不止。吠之声与真无二，虽黠者不能辨其为真与伪也。作木鸟，置竹笼中，能自跳舞飞鸣，鸣如画眉，凄越可听。作水器，以水置器中，水从下上射如线，高五六尺，移时不断。所作之奇俱如此，不能悉载。

    Explanation

    210

    Huang Zilüzhuan, my cousin by marriage, was exceptionally bright from a young age. He could recite books after reading them only a few times. He especially enjoyed coming up with new ideas and creating ingenious devices. When he was seven or eight years old, he once stole tools from a craftsman behind the back of his school teacher and carved a wooden figure about an inch long. Placed on the desk, it could walk on its own, with its limbs moving automatically, and spectators were amazed, thinking it was a miracle. After he turned ten, his maternal uncle passed away, and he came to Guangling to live with me. He learned about Western geometric proportions and mechanical engineering, and his skills advanced further. He often made small objects for his own amusement, and those who saw them offered high prices to buy them. He was always sick and could not tolerate social interactions; he hated being bothered by others, so he stopped making things. Thus, his creations became rare.

    He made many things, but I cannot remember all of them. I still remember one of his inventions, a two-wheeled cart about three feet long, which could accommodate one person and move on its own without needing to be pushed or pulled. When it stopped, by pulling the curved lever on the axle, it would start moving again as before. Whenever it stopped, pulling the lever, it could travel eighty miles in a day. He also made a wooden dog, placed it by the door, and it lay curled up as usual. But when someone entered the door, it would stand up and bark continuously. The sound of its barking was indistinguishable from a real dog, and even the most clever people could not tell if it was real or fake. He made a wooden bird, placed it in a bamboo cage, and it could dance, fly, and sing on its own. Its song sounded like a mandarin duck, clear and melodious. He also made a water device, placed water in it, and the water shot up like a line, reaching five or six feet high and continuing for a while. All of his creations were like this, and they cannot all be described.

    211

    有怪其奇者，疑必有异书，或有异传。而予与处者最久且狎，绝不见其书。叩其从来，亦竟无师傅，但曰：“予何足奇？天地人物，皆奇器也。动者如天，静者如地，灵明者如人，赜者如万物，何莫非奇？然皆不能自奇，必有一至奇而不自奇者以为源，而且为之主宰，如画之有师，土木之有匠氏也，夫是之为至奇。”予惊其言之大，而因是亦具知黄子之奇，固自有其独悟，非一物一事求而学之者所可及也。昔人云：“天非自动，必有所以动者；地非自静，必有所以静者。”黄子之奇，必得其奇之所以然乎？
    　　黄子性简默，喜思。与予处，予尝纷然谈说，而黄子则独坐静思。观其初思求入，亦戛戛似难，既而思得，则笑舞从之。如一思碍而不得，必拥衾达旦，务得而后已焉。黄子之奇，固亦由思而得之者也，而其喜思则性出也。

    Explanation

    211

    There is someone who finds it strange that it is so extraordinary, suspecting that there must be unusual books or perhaps an unusual tradition. However, I, who have been acquainted with him the longest and most intimately, never see any books. When I inquire about its origin, there is also no mention of a teacher. He says, "What is there to be surprised about? Heaven, earth, human beings, and all things are all extraordinary mechanisms. Those in motion are like heaven, those at rest are like earth, those with consciousness are like humans, and those complex are like all things. Nothing is not extraordinary. But they cannot make themselves extraordinary. There must be an extremely extraordinary one that does not make itself extraordinary as the source, and serves as the master or controller, like paintings having a teacher, and earth and wood having craftsmen. The extraordinariness of Mr. Huang must come from understanding the reason behind extraordinariness."

    I am amazed by the grandeur of his words, and thus I have come to fully understand that Mr. Huang's extraordinariness stems from his unique insight, which cannot be attained by merely seeking knowledge from a single object or event. As the ancients said, "Heaven does not move on its own; there must be a reason for its movement. Earth does not remain still on its own; there must be a reason for its stillness." Mr. Huang's extraordinariness must come from understanding the reason behind it.

    Mr. Huang's nature is simple and taciturn, and he likes to think. When I am with him, I often talk at length, but Mr. Huang sits alone in quiet contemplation. When he first thinks and seeks to understand, it seems very difficult. But when he thinks of something, he laughs and dances following it. If he encounters an obstacle and cannot think of it, he will sleep with his quilt until dawn, determined to achieve it and only then will he stop. The extraordinariness of Mr. Huang indeed comes from thinking, and his liking to think comes from his nature.

    212

    黄子生丙申，于今二十八岁，其年月日时，与予生期毫发无异，亦奇也，因附书之。
    　　附：奇器目略
    　　一、验器冷热燥湿，皆以肤验，而不可以目验者，今则以目验之。
    　　验冷热器：此器能诊试虚实，分别气候，证诸药之性情。其用甚广，另有专书。
    　　验燥湿器：内有一针，能左右旋，燥则左旋，湿则右旋，毫发不爽，并可预证阴晴。
    　　一、诸镜德之崇卑，唯友见之；面之媸妍，唯镜见之。镜之用，止于见己，而亦可以见物，故作诸镜以广之。
    　　千里镜：大小不等。
    　　取火镜：向太阳取火。
    　　临画镜
    　　取水镜：向太阴取水。
    　　显微镜
    　　多物镜
    　　瑞光镜：制法大小不等，大者径五六尺，夜以灯照之，光射数里，其用甚巨。冬月人坐光中，遍体生温，如在太阳之下。
    　　一、诸画画以饰观，或平面而见为深远，或一面而见为多面，皆画之变也。
    　　远视画

    Explanation

    212

    A Man Born in the Year of the Monkey

    A man named Huang Zi was born in the year of the monkey (丙申), and he is now twenty-eight years old. The exact date and time of his birth are identical to those of my own birth, down to the smallest detail, which is quite remarkable. Therefore, I have appended this note.

    Appendix: A Brief Description of Strange Devices

    1. Devices for Testing Temperature and Moisture

    These devices can detect the temperature and moisture levels of objects, which can only be felt through touch, not seen with the eyes. Now, however, they can be tested visually.

    • Temperature and Moisture Testing Device: This device can diagnose the true nature of objects, differentiate their conditions, and verify the properties of medicines. Its use is very widespread, and there is a separate book dedicated to it.

    • Moisture Testing Device: Inside this device is a needle that can rotate left or right. It rotates left for dry conditions and right for wet conditions, with perfect accuracy, and can also predict the weather.

    2. The Virtue of Mirrors

    The moral character of mirrors can only be seen by friends. The beauty or ugliness of a face can only be seen in a mirror. The primary use of mirrors is to see oneself, but they can also be used to see other objects, so various types of mirrors have been created to expand their uses.

    • Telescope: Available in different sizes.

    • Fire-Starting Mirror: Used to start a fire by focusing sunlight.

    • Painting Mirror: Used for painting, possibly for reflecting light or for artistic purposes.

    • Water-Collecting Mirror: Used to collect water by focusing moonlight.

    • Microscope: Used to see small objects in detail.

    • Multi-Object Mirror: Used to reflect multiple objects.

    • Luminous Mirror: Available in different sizes, with the largest measuring five or six feet in diameter. At night, when lit by a lamp, it can emit light for several miles, making it very useful. In winter, people sitting in the light will feel warm, as if they were under the sun.

    3. Paintings for Decoration

    Paintings are used to decorate and beautify. Some paintings can depict distant views on a flat surface, while others can show multiple perspectives from a single view, all of which are variations of painting.

    • Distant View Painting: Paintings that depict distant views.
    213

    旁视画
    　　镜中画
    　　管窥镜画：全不似画，以管窥之，则生动如真。
    　　上下画：一画上下观之，则成二画。
    　　三面画：一画三面观之，则成三画。
    　　一、玩器器虽玩而理则诚。夫玩以理出，君子亦无废乎玩矣。
    　　自动戏：内音乐俱备，不烦人力，而节奏自然。
    　　真画：人物鸟兽，皆能自动，与真无二。
    　　灯衢：作小屋一间，内悬灯数盏。人入其中，如至通衢大市，人烟稠杂，灯火连绵，一望数里。
    　　自行驱暑扇：不烦人力，而一室皆风。
    　　木人掌扇
    　　一、水法农必借水而成，水之用大矣，而亦可为诸玩。作水器。
    　　龙尾车：一人能转多车，灌田最便。
    　　一线泉：制法不等。
    　　柳枝泉：水上射复下，如柳枝然。
    　　山鸟鸣：声如山鸟。
    　　鸾凤吟：声如鸾凤。
    　　报时水。
    　　瀑布水
    　　一、造器之器“工欲善其事，必先利其器。”况目中所列诸器，有非寻常斤斧所能造者。作造器之器。

    Explanation

    213

    Translation

    Side-view Paintings

    • Mirror Paintings
    • Tube-view Mirror Paintings: Not at all like a painting, when viewed through a tube, they appear as vivid as reality.
    • Up-down Paintings: When viewed from above and below, one painting appears as two.
    • Three-sided Paintings: When viewed from three sides, one painting appears as three.
    • Playthings: Though played with, they are grounded in truth. Play arises from reason, and even gentlemen do not abandon play.
    • Automatic Toys: With internal music, requiring no human effort, and the rhythm is natural.
    • Lifelike Paintings: Figures and animals can all move on their own, no different from reality.
    • Lantern-lit Street: To make a small house, with several lamps hanging inside. When people enter it, it feels as if they have arrived at a bustling market, crowded with people, with lights stretching continuously, visible for several miles.
    • Self-cooling Fan: Requiring no human effort, the entire room is filled with wind.
    • Wooden Figure Holding a Fan

    Water Methods

    • Agriculture: The method of water is essential for farming, and the use of water is very great, and it can also be used for various playthings. To make water devices.
    • Dragon-tail Cart: One person can turn multiple carts, the most convenient for irrigating fields.
    • Stream of Water: Methods of construction vary.
    • Willow Branch Stream: Water shoots up and falls down, like willow branches.
    • Sound of Mountain Birds: The sound is like that of mountain birds.
    • Song of Phoenixes: The sound is like that of phoenixes.
    • Water Clock: A water clock that tells time.
    • Waterfall

    Tools for Making Devices

    • "A craftsman must sharpen his tools if he wants to do his job well." Especially the various devices mentioned in the text, some of which cannot be made with ordinary tools. To make devices for making other devices.
    214

    方圆规矩
    　　就小画大规矩
    　　就大画小规矩
    　　画八角六角规矩
    　　造诸镜规矩
    　　造法条器
    　　[张山来曰：泰西人巧思，百倍中华，岂天地灵秀之气，独钟厚彼方耶？予友梅子定九、吴子师邵，皆能通乎其术。今又有黄子履庄。可见华人之巧，未尝或让于彼；只因不欲以技艺成名，且复竭其心思于富贵利达，不能旁及诸技，是以巧思逊泰西一筹耳。
    　　原本奇器目略颇详，兹偶录数条，以见一斑云。]
    　　
    　　虞初新志卷七 书戚三郎事　周亮工减斋赖古堂集
    　　江阴城陷，微戮抗命者，邑有戚三郎，与妇王笃伉俪。夫妇皆好推施。一子甫五龄。家所向唯关帝君祠，戚夫妇虔事之。月朔望，未辨明，即肃香祠下，二十年如一日。城陷，被兵执，举戚足带纠其臂，数被创，拥至通衢。见妻为他兵拽去，戚呼号救之，复被创。前后凡十三创，首亦被刃。推拥过帝祠，不胜步矣，倒地上。兵见其气息仅属，舍之去。

    Explanation

    214

    Standards for Shapes and Rules

    • Using small patterns to create large designs: This refers to the method of using smaller templates or patterns to create larger designs.
    • Using large patterns to create small designs: Similarly, this refers to the method of using larger templates or patterns to create smaller designs.
    • Drawing shapes with eight or six sides: This refers to the specific methods for drawing shapes with eight or six sides, emphasizing the precision required.
    • Creating various mirrors: This refers to the rules or techniques for making various types of mirrors.
    • Creating legal documents or instruments: This refers to the creation of legal documents or instruments.

    [Zhang Shanlai said: The ingenuity of Westerners is a hundred times greater than that of the Chinese. Is it that the divine and refined essence of heaven and earth is uniquely concentrated in their land? My friends Mei Ziding and Wu Shishao are both skilled in their crafts. Now there is also Huang Zilu. It is evident that the Chinese are not inferior in skill; they simply do not wish to gain fame through their crafts and instead exhaust their thoughts on wealth and status, not branching out to other skills, which is why their ingenuity falls short of the West by one step. The original account of strange devices is quite detailed, so I have recorded a few examples here to give a glimpse of it.]

    New Records of the Early Han, Volume 7: The Story of Qi Sanlang, by Zhou Lianggong, Reduced Studio, Lai Gu Hall

    • The fall of Jiangyin: In the county, there was Qi Sanlang, married to Wang Du. They were both devout believers and often helped others. They had a son who was just five years old. Their home faced the Temple of Guan Yu, and Qi Sanlang and his wife worshipped there with great devotion. Every month on the first and fifteenth, before dawn, they would burn incense at the temple, doing so for twenty years without fail. When the city fell, they were captured by soldiers. One soldier grabbed Qi Sanlang's foot and twisted his arm, causing him to be injured several times. He was then dragged to the main street. Seeing his wife being taken away by another soldier, Qi Sanlang cried out to save her, and was injured again. In total, he was wounded thirteen times, and his head was also cut. Dragged past the temple, he could no longer walk and fell to the ground. The soldiers saw that he was barely alive and left him alone.
    215

    戚心独朗朗，念虔事帝，得死楹下足矣。然度难死，帝显赫，或有以援我？日且暮，觉祠中有异，纠臂带忽裂，裂声如弓弦，作霹雳鸣。戚臂左受创，纠缚既断，因得以右扶首。首将堕，喉固未绝，因宛转正之。心朗朗，念帝显赫，真援我也。
    　　黎明，兵数过戚，见血痕模糊，谓死矣，不复顾。久之，有老翁妪趋视戚，怜之曰：“三郎垂毙矣，盍掖之归？”戚虽愦然，心识其为比邻钱翁、沈妪也。顷之，两人续以姜糜至。越二日，入曰：“兵封刃，行且去，郎活矣！”乃不复至。戚首为血糨，乃因之固，渐能起。举视室中，无一存者，五龄儿固坐足旁泣。而屋中乃僵二尸，辨之，邻钱翁、沈妪也。戚恐甚，久之，悟两人殆关帝命以援予者。

    Explanation

    215

    Qiqi's heart was clear and bright, thinking devoutly about serving the emperor, and he was ready to die under the pillar if necessary. However, he realized it would be difficult to die, and considering the emperor's majesty, perhaps someone would help him. As the day was ending, he felt something strange in the temple. Suddenly, the bandage on his left arm broke, making a sound like a bowstring snapping, followed by a thunderclap. Qiqi's left arm was injured, and the bandage broke, allowing him to use his right hand to support his head. His head was about to fall, but his throat was not yet cut, so he turned and straightened it. His heart was clear, thinking of the emperor's majesty, truly helping him.

    At dawn, soldiers passed by Qiqi, seeing the bloody traces, they thought he was dead and did not look back. After a while, an old man and woman hurried to see Qiqi, pitying him and saying, "Young Master Qiqi is on the verge of death, why don't we carry him home?" Qiqi was dazed but recognized them as his neighbors, Old Master Qian and Old Lady Shen. Soon, the two brought porridge with ginger to him. After two days, they came in and said, "The soldiers have sealed their weapons and are about to leave, you are alive!" They did not come back. Qiqi's head was bandaged with blood, and he gradually regained strength and could stand up. Looking around the room, he saw no one else, only a five-year-old child sitting beside his feet and crying. In the house, there were two stiff bodies, which he recognized as his neighbors, Old Master Qian and Old Lady Shen. Qiqi was very afraid, but after a while, he realized that the two must have been sent by Lord Guan to help him.

    216

    因强起，跋躄过帝祠，欲投地，身不能屈，立作叩首状，首又若将离者，乃依槛祝曰：“身赖帝活，唯帝终有以庇予！”因念翁妪死而生我，不可久暴露，吾室有木，可为槥，第安所得匠？忆众为帝治寝宫，城围，宫未竟，匠或有存者。往迹之，见三匠踦户语。戚告以故，咸随戚归。戚指示木所在，匠遽为操作。戚匍匐乞米以为食，久之不得，仅从空室得冬炒半囊归。入室，失三匠而存五槥。戚念约为二而五之，去又不俟予归耶？趋帝宫，窅无人，三尸仆户内外，固三匠也。戚惊惧。是时兵远去，人渐归，乃倩所识，以槥厝翁妪及匠，而瘗之隙地。

    Explanation

    216

    The narrator, forced to get up, limped past the shrine of the Emperor. They attempted to prostrate themselves but, unable to bend, stood instead in a gesture of prostration, their head seeming as if it might detach from their body. Leaning on the railing, they prayed, "I rely on the Emperor for my survival; may the Emperor continue to protect me!" They then remembered that their grandparents had died and that they had been born afterward. They thought that they could use wood to make a coffin, but they were unsure where to find a craftsman. Recalling that the craftsmen had been working on the Emperor's palace, they went to look for them. They found three craftsmen leaning against the door and talking. They explained the situation to them, and all of them followed the narrator home. The narrator showed them where the wood was, and the craftsmen quickly began working. The narrator crawled around begging for rice to eat, but after a long time, they found only half a bag of winter stir-fry in an empty room. When they returned to the room, they had lost three of the craftsmen and were left with five coffins. They wondered if the craftsmen had left without waiting for them to return. They went to the Emperor's palace, which was now empty and desolate. Inside and outside the door, they found the corpses of the three craftsmen. They entrusted the task of burying their grandparents and the craftsmen to someone they knew, and the bodies were buried in a secluded place.

    217

    戚数得帝佑，神理亦渐旺，复至帝祠，能稽首投地矣。肃告帝，谓：“帝恩我无极，第妻无由见，帝其以梦示！”归而梦帝驱之曰：“疾去数里外，有舟待，越月之十四日，终不可见矣。”辨明，力疾负子行至津亭，见有舣舟柳下，若有待者。其人为成三。戚曰：“若何待？”成曰：“吾之室被掳而南，吾将操舴艋往。独不可往，度邑中失侣者多，应有往者，故迟之。”戚曰：“帝示我矣，予为此子觅母，得附舟行，幸矣！”具告以梦。成亦手额曰：“帝佑君，合浦珠自当还。吾即不德，借君庇以分神贶，浮萍断梗，或冀幸一遇乎？”言讫，相与泣数行下，忧患易感，意气殊相得也。

    Explanation

    217

    戚多次得到皇帝的庇护，精神力量也逐渐增强，再次来到皇帝的祠庙，能够行礼叩拜于地。他恭敬地对皇帝说：“皇帝对我的恩惠无边无际，只是我无法见到我的妻子，希望皇帝能通过梦境向我显示！”回到家后，他梦见皇帝驱使他说：“赶快前往数里之外，那里有船等待着你，到了那个月的十四日，最终还是无法相见。”他明白后，不顾病痛，背着孩子走到渡口，看到一艘船停靠在柳树下，似乎在等待什么。船上的人自称成三。戚问道：“你在等什么？”成三回答：“我的家被掳掠到南方去了，我将驾驶小船前往。我独自一人无法前往，估计城里失去伴侣的人很多，应该有人会去，所以我在这里等待。”戚说：“皇帝已经指示我了，我为这个孩子寻找母亲，能够搭船同行，真是幸运！”他详细讲述了梦境。成三也拍手说道：“皇帝保佑你，合浦的珍珠自然会回来。即使我不够德行，借你的庇护来分享神赐的恩惠，我们这些漂泊无依的人，或许还有机会幸运地相遇吧？”说完，他们相对而泣，泪水流下，彼此在忧患中更容易感受到对方的情感，志趣也格外投合。

    218

    抵升洲，舟刺鬼面城下，乃入市，揭示四达之衢曰：“江阴戚三郎觅妻王。能为驿骑者，予多金。”成亦揭示如戚。有某者，见戚所揭示，往见戚曰：“予我金，告尔妻所在。”戚虽揭示，谬语耳，固无从得金。语某曰：“我实无金，期一见妇耳。”某叹曰：“世固有不持金而求得妇者？”疾起去。成挽之，告以“戚为帝所指示，始昧昧至此，实不持金。城陷家破，安得金？”某闻成语，凄然悯之，曰：“即告尔妻所在，不得尔金，易耳；顾无金，彼武人，赤手返尔妻耶？”具告以妻所在。戚与成仿徨久之，某忽曰：“子何能？”戚曰：“能书。”某曰：“机在是矣。某公者，矢愿于报恩塔下，倩人书百部《首楞》施四方，方觅人。子诚善书，计可得数金，事或可图欤？曷疾去！”戚乃尾某行，而以子属成。见某公，以情告。试以书，书诚工。某公既善其书，又悯其遇，施十金。

    Explanation

    218

    Arriving at Shengzhou, the boat docked under the walls of Ghost City. They then entered the city and posted notices at the four-way crossroads: "Qis Sanlang of Jiangyin seeks his wife Wang. Those who can be a courier, I will give much gold." Cheng also posted notices like Qi. A certain person saw Qi's notice and went to see Qi, saying, "Give me gold, and I will tell you where your wife is." Although Qi had posted the notice, it was a false statement, and he had no intention of giving gold. He told the person, "I really have no gold, I just want to see the woman." The person sighed, "There are indeed cases where one can find a wife without holding gold?" and quickly left. Cheng stopped him and explained, "Qi was directed by the emperor, and he came here blindly, truly without any gold. With the city fallen and his family destroyed, how could he have gold?" The person heard Cheng's words, felt pity, and said, "If you tell me where your wife is, even if you don't get my gold, it's easy. But without gold, can a warrior return your wife with bare hands?" He then fully informed Qi of his wife's location. Qi and Cheng wandered for a long time, and the person suddenly asked, "What can you do?" Qi replied, "I can write." The person said, "The opportunity lies here. A certain official, who vowed at the Pagoda of Repaying Kindness, asked someone to write a hundred copies of the Vajracchedikā Prajñāpāramitā Sūtra and distribute them, and he is now looking for someone. If you truly are good at writing, you can earn some gold, and perhaps we can achieve something. Why not go quickly!" Qi followed the person and left Cheng behind. They met the official and told him the situation. The official tested Qi's writing, and it was truly excellent. Seeing Qi's writing, the official felt pity for his situation and gave him ten gold coins.

    219

    某踉跄携戚至某标郝总旗所。郝他出，郝妇曰：“谁耶？”戚告以故。妇曰：“诚有江阴王氏者，予我金，我与尔妇。”戚喜妇无多索，跪献金。妇持金入，久之不出。又久之，出，四顾曰：“何为者？”戚与某咸惊噪。妇愕然曰：“何为者？乃诬我得金？室固无尔妇，安得尔金？”命阍者榜逐之。戚掩涕怨某，相与且去。成方与戚子望其与妻俱归，已得故，怒目曰：“不得妇，又失金，不值一死耶？奈何遂返？明日与我俱。”

    Explanation

    219

    Someone stumbled and brought his wife to the place where Hao, the flag bearer, was stationed. Hao was out, and Hao's wife said, "Who are you?" The wife told her the reason. The wife said, "There is indeed a person named Wang from Jiangyin who gave me gold, and I will give it to your wife." The wife was happy that she did not ask for much, and she knelt and presented the gold. The wife took the gold inside, and after a long time, she did not come out. After a long time, she came out, looked around, and said, "Who are you?" The wife and the visitor both cried out in surprise. The wife was surprised and said, "Who are you? Are you accusing me of getting the gold? My home has never had your wife, how could I have your gold?" She ordered the gatekeeper to drive them away. The wife hid her tears and blamed the visitor, and they left together. Cheng Fang and the son of Qi hoped that he and his wife would return together, but after finding out the truth, they glared angrily and said, "Without a wife and without gold, is it not worth dying for? Why should we return now? Tomorrow, come with me."

    220

    明日，戚携子偕成往，匉訇于门。郝方立球场弄鹰，召入。成瞪目欲裂，譤而前：“吾成三，是为吾友戚三。戚妇在公所。昨携金赎妇，公夫人得金，乃不与妇。吾与戚邑陷家破，与妇失，去死丝粟耳！无家死，失妇死，失金亦死！公不与戚妇，十步之内，以颈血相溅矣！”突出刃靴中，欲自杀。郝怒张，急止之曰：“安有是？吾妇何从昧尔金？勿自杀，吾入询。诚有是，吾不以为妇矣！”乃急入。久之，闻譇詉声，已复闻郝挞妇。

    Explanation

    220

    The next day, Qi brought his son and Cheng together and thundered at the door. Hao was standing in the courtyard playing with an eagle when he called them in. Cheng stared with eyes about to burst and shouted as he stepped forward: "I am Cheng San, this is my friend Qi San. Qi's wife is in your place. Yesterday I carried gold to redeem her, but your lady obtained the gold and did not give her back. I and Qi have fallen into ruin, our families are destroyed. Lost our wives, it's just a matter of dying! Dying without a home, dying without a wife, dying without the gold! If you do not give Qi's wife back, within ten steps, we will splash each other's blood!" He drew a blade from his boot and wanted to commit suicide. Hao was angry and expanded, quickly stopping him and saying: "How could there be such a thing? How could my wife have taken your gold? Do not commit suicide, I will go inside and inquire. If it is true, I will not keep her as my wife!" He then quickly went inside. After a long time, he heard the sound of scolding, then heard Hao beating his wife.

    221

    戚与成咸跪呼于外曰：“勿挞夫人，但愿还妇是矣！”食顷，郝出，气结，掷金于地曰：“急持去！”成稽首曰：“戚急得妇，不急金。且金归公室一日夜矣，又吐之，公大人，义不为也。”争之益力。郝曰：“义哉，子为友，乃以死争！计戚所持金，乌足赎妇？然吾高子行，何计金！当以妇归子友。”因呼妇出。戚方注目不瞬，谓妻且至，望不类，少近，则成与妇相抱痛哭，妇盖成妻也。先是成妻之被掳而南也，过邸舍，书壁曰：“我江阴成三郎妻王氏，为某标郝掳。见者幸以语吾家。”久之，“成”字微落，独存“戊”。某第见戚所揭示，故遽报之戚云。

    Explanation

    221

    戚 and 成 both knelt and called out from outside, saying, "Do not beat the mistress; we only wish for the return of the wife, and that would be enough!" After a meal's time, Hao came out, choked with emotion, and threw the gold onto the ground, saying, "Quickly take it away!" 成 bowed and said, "戚 is eager to get his wife, not the gold. Besides, the gold has been in the public treasury for a day and night, and it has been returned. You are a public official, and it is not right to do so." They argued even more forcefully. Hao said, "How righteous you are, to fight for your friend even to the point of death! Considering the gold that 戚 holds, it is not enough to redeem the wife. However, I respect your principles, and I do not care about the gold! I will give the wife back to your friend." Then he called the wife out. 戚 was staring intently without blinking, thinking that his wife was about to arrive. But she looked different. When they got closer, 成 and the woman embraced and wept bitterly. The woman was actually 成's wife. Before this, when 成's wife was captured and taken to the south, she passed by an inn and wrote on the wall, saying, "I am Wang, the wife of Cheng Sanlang from Jiangyin, captured by a certain bandit leader Hao. May those who see this tell my family." After a while, the character '成' faded slightly, leaving only the character '戊'. The bandit leader saw what 戚 had written and immediately reported it to him.

    222

    郝见妻反属成，讶曰：“异哉，子以死争友而顾乃自争！天下嗜义者，独为人哉！天合子，子疾去！”成曰：“金出戚而妇归我，我何去？去则戚之金不返，我诚我争矣。”郝曰：“奈何？”成曰：“小人勇于力，妇善针黹。公诚能录小人夫妇，愿得二十金予戚，听其觅妇，小人即除马通，妇括爨下，甘心也。”郝曰：“义哉！然吾无所需子。有张将军者，方觅役，曷为子言之？”郝即趋张所，戚亦随成往。张见成，许纳，出廿金，予成券。券成，成以金予戚。

    Explanation

    222

    Hao saw that his wife attributed the matter to Cheng and exclaimed in surprise, "How unusual! You died to protect your friend, yet now you are fighting for yourself! In the world, those who love righteousness, are they only human beings? Heaven has united you, you should quickly leave!" Cheng replied, "The gold comes from my maternal uncle, and the wife returns to me. Why should I leave? If I leave, the gold of my maternal uncle will not return, and I will truly be fighting for myself." Hao said, "What should I do?" Cheng said, "I am brave in strength, and my wife is skilled in needlework. If you truly can take in my wife and me, I wish to give twenty gold coins to my maternal uncle, allowing him to find another wife. I will then remove the horse carriage, and my wife will manage the kitchen, and I will be content." Hao said, "How righteous! But I have no need for you. There is a General Zhang who is looking for someone. Why not speak to you about it?" Hao then went to General Zhang's place, and Qi followed Cheng there. General Zhang saw Cheng and agreed to take him in, giving him twenty gold coins and a contract. After the contract was made, Cheng gave the gold to Qi.

    223

    戚曰：“子激于义，售夫妇身，期全吾夫妇耳。顾吾妇何在？得金安往？”相与絮泣。张曰：“尔姑携金去，得间，当具以语我，当为觅之。”戚见张位都赫，往来甚伙，意显者苟留意，忧不得妻耶？乃叩首曰：“予向赍十金耳，成售身，倍其金予我，我义不敢受。然成缘我金得妻，又不忍分我金。吾侪落魄，得金即随手逸，金尽，妇终不可得，且负两公义。曷以金留公所，公但为我觅妻。妻得，成之心尽，我即倍费成金，无愧于成矣。”张颔之，纳金，令“尔亦觅所在来语予，毋得恃予。”

    Explanation

    223

    戚曰：“你被正义所激励，为了我们的婚姻，我愿意卖掉自己，目的是保全我们的婚姻。但是我的妻子在哪里？得到的钱又去了哪里？”两人相拥哭泣。张说：“你先带着钱离开，等有机会时，再详细告诉我，我会帮你找到她。”戚见张地位显赫，来往的人很多，心想如果显贵的人稍微留意一下，就不会担心找不到妻子了。于是他叩头说：“我原来只有十两银子，完成了婚姻的交易，对方给了我双倍的钱，但我出于正义不能接受。然而，我因为这笔钱得到了妻子，又不忍心分开这笔钱。我们这些落魄的人，一旦得到钱就会随意挥霍，钱用完了，妻子最终还是得不到，而且会辜负两位的恩情。为什么不把钱留在我这里，你只管为我找妻子。如果找到了妻子，我的心愿就实现了，我会加倍支付这笔钱，不会辜负你的。”张点头同意，收下了钱，并说：“你也找个地方，等有机会时再告诉我，不要依赖我。”

    224

    阅二日，成方除马通，过坏墙，闭诸妇人，多操乡里音。成私度曰：“成妻脱在是，谁复知者？”乃亦语乡里音过曰：“戚三郎属予寻妇，今安所得耶？”妇聆之，迫于监者，不敢答。晚如厕，遗片纸墙隙，复操乡里音曰：“此纸纳之隙，留以备明日。”成遥闻之，觉有异，俟人定，趋取纸，细书：“戚三郎妻王氏，即今在此，君急语我夫。”成得之，大惊喜，急闻之戚。戚乃携子，先恳之郝，郝与俱来。

    Explanation

    224

    After two days, Cheng was about to leave the stable, passing by a ruined wall where several women were locked up, many speaking with local accents. Cheng privately thought to himself, "If my wife is among them, who else would know?" He then spoke in the local dialect and said, "Qis Sanlang entrusted me to find his wife, where can I find her now?" The woman heard this, under pressure from the guards, dared not answer. At night, she went to the toilet, left a piece of paper in the wall crevice, and then spoke in the local dialect and said, "Put this paper in the crevice, to be ready for tomorrow." Cheng heard it from afar, felt that something was unusual, waited until everyone was asleep, hurried to take the paper, and wrote in small letters, "Qis Sanlang's wife Wang is here now, please tell my husband quickly." Cheng received it, greatly surprised and delighted, quickly informed Qis. Qis then took his son, first sought help from Hao, and Hao came along with him.

    225

    戚直前跪曰：“连觅妻所在，闻即在府中，愿悯之！”张即询所系妇，首王氏，即戚妇耶？呼之出，真戚妇也！戚见妇，惊悸错愕，未敢往就，摇摇不知悲。其子见母出，突奔母怀，仰视大痛。妇亦俯捧儿，哭失声。戚至是始血泪迸落。戚、成跪张前，戚妇亦遥跪听命。张曰：“是诚尔妻，然是人少有色，故遴为首，约值五十金。半犹不足，望得妇耶？”戚挽郝言之曰：“邑陷家破，安得金？将军悯之！”且娓娓言帝所以佑之者，复告以梦，期以动张。张曰：“众无一赎，始赎，即减定值，何以示来者？”坚不许。戚曰：“成售夫妇身，仅得此金，而又苦不足。

    Explanation

    225

    戚 straightened up and knelt down, saying, "I have been searching for my wife's whereabouts, and I heard that she is inside the mansion. I hope you will show mercy to her!" Zhang then asked about the woman who was being held, and it turned out to be Wang, who was indeed the wife of Qi. He summoned her out, and it was truly Qi's wife! Qi saw his wife and was shocked and disoriented, not daring to approach her, swaying back and forth, not knowing how to grieve. His son, seeing his mother appear, rushed into her arms, looked up and cried out in great sorrow. The woman also bent down to hold her child, crying out in tears. Qi, at this point, burst into tears. Qi and Cheng knelt before Zhang, and Qi's wife also knelt from a distance, waiting for his command. Zhang said, "She is truly your wife, but this woman is young and beautiful, so she was chosen first, and her value is approximately fifty gold. Half of that is still not enough. Do you still want your wife?" Qi turned to Hao and said, "Our town was destroyed and our family broke apart. How can we get the money? General, please show mercy!" He then explained in detail the reasons why the Emperor protected him, and told him about the dream, hoping to move Zhang. Zhang replied, "No one has redeemed her yet. If you redeem her now, I will reduce the price. How will I show this to future cases?" He firmly refused. Qi said, "Cheng sold the couple's bodies and only got this much money, and yet it is still not enough."

    226

    天乎！安所得金2”戚乃大哭，妇哭，而戚子又趢{走豕}往来，哭于父母旁。郝哭，张之厮养哭，张姬妾环屏内者亦哭，久之，张亦涔涔泪下矣。哭声鼎沸间，张突跃起曰：“止止！吾还汝妇，不须金也。城陷家破，尔诚无所得金。且尔数被创弗死，非帝祐，不至是。尔诚善者，吾还尔妇，不须金也！成以尔故售身于吾，尔夫妇还而成留，成即不怨尔，尔何以谢成？吾即还尔妇，兼还尔友夫妇。尔夫妇其与尔友夫妇俱还。此二十金，即为尔辈道里需，不须金也。

    Explanation

    226

    Heaven! Where could we get the money? Qi then burst into tears, and his wife cried as well, while Qi's son scurried back and forth, weeping beside their parents. Hao cried, Zhang's servants cried, and Zhang's concubines inside the screen also wept. After a long time, Zhang's tears streamed down his face too. Amidst the clamor of crying, Zhang suddenly stood up and said, "Stop! Stop! I will return your wife; you do not need the money. The city has fallen and our home has been destroyed; truly, you have no money to give. Moreover, you have been wounded many times but not died; this was not due to the emperor's protection. You are truly a good person, so I will return your wife without asking for money! I sold myself for your sake, and if you return with your wife, I will stay behind, and I would not blame you. How could you repay me? I will return your wife and also return the wives of your friends. You and your friends should return together. These twenty pieces of gold are for your travel expenses; you do not need more money."

    227

    吾还尔妇，然我有言，尔亦毋我逆：尔之子秀而慧，我怜之，盍以子我？我耄矣，无嗣。诚子我，我不奴视子，不隔膜视子也。”戚急遽未有以应，妇忽趋前唾，耳语戚。久之，复扬谓戚曰：“子尚需乳耶？”戚遂膝前曰：“将军生全两家夫妇，且欲子下愚子，何不可者？”将军喜，急前抱儿，儿亦暱将军，不复甚恋父母。将军益害，呼戚夫妇坐，待以亲串礼。举儿入室，遍拜所亲，已复剑儿出，衣冠焕奕。宾从以下皆罗拜，庆将军有子。戚与成两家谢将军去。计戚初见张将军日，实帝所示十四日内也。人咸以为戚虔于帝之报云。

    Explanation

    227

    I will return your wife, but I have something to say; you must not refuse me: your son is talented and intelligent, and I admire him. Why not give him to me? I am old and have no heir. If you give me your son, I will not treat him as a slave, nor will I treat him as a stranger. Qi was in a hurry and did not know how to respond. The wife suddenly stepped forward, spat, and whispered to Qi. After a while, she said aloud to Qi, "Do you still need to breastfeed?" Qi then knelt before the general and said, "The general has preserved the marriage of the two families, and wants to adopt our less intelligent son. What is there to object to?" The general was pleased and quickly embraced the child. The child also became close to the general, no longer showing much attachment to his parents. The general became even more attached, called Qi and his wife to sit, treating them with the courtesy of relatives. He took the child into the room and bowed to all his relatives. Then he brought the child out again, dressed in splendid attire. The guests and attendants all bowed, celebrating that the general had a son. Qi and Cheng's families thanked the general and left. Calculated from when Qi first met General Zhang, it was actually within the fourteen days shown by the emperor. People all believed that Qi was devout in his gratitude to the emperor.

    228

    戚归，既安其室，复过某公，为书经塔下者三阅月，因得往来视儿。将军亦多所赠。久之，将军病卒。将军拥高赀，族子利之，咸以戚自有父母，非吾族类也，耸臾其归。戚子亦因之便去。诸母恶族子，竟以所有与戚。戚子所携甚厚，至今为江阴巨室。成亦依戚终其身。子归后，新帝祠，江上知名之士，咸为诗文以纪之，戚尽镌于祠石。
    　　[张山来曰：关帝能宛转嘿佑戚郎，则曷不于其妇被掳时显示神威耶？岂数当有难，有不可免者邪？又岂必待祈祷而

    Explanation

    228

    戚 returned home, having settled into his new life, he visited a certain official again and spent three months writing scriptures at the foot of the pagoda, thus allowing him to visit his son. The general also gave him many gifts. After a long time, the general fell ill and died. The general had amassed great wealth, and the sons of the clan coveted it. They all said that Qi had his own parents and was not part of their family, and urged him to return. Qi’s son also left for convenience. The mothers disliked the sons of the clan, and eventually gave everything to Qi. Qi’s son carried away a great deal, and is now a major family in Jiangyin. Cheng also relied on Qi for the rest of his life. After the son returned, a new temple was built for the emperor, and famous scholars by the river all wrote poems and essays to commemorate it. Everything about Qi was engraved on the temple stones.

    [Zhāng Shānlái said: If Guāndì could subtly protect Qi, why didn’t he show his divine power when Qi’s wife was captured? Was it fated that he would face difficulties, and that there was no way to avoid them? Or did he have to wait for prayers?]

    229

    后应耶？然终不可谓非帝佑也。] 象记　林璐鹿庵
    　　国家大朝会，陈设卤簿，驯象所引象列门外，各以品秩分左右。百官入，钟鸣鞭响，群象鼻相交，无一人敢阑入者。朝散，各以先后归，有罪则宣敕杖之，伏而受杖。此其所从来远矣。
    　　黔中人昔为余言，守土者以期贡象，必入山告语之曰：“朝廷诏汝备禁卫，将授官于汝。”象俯贴足，如许诺状，即驯而行，无能捕捉也。
    　　思陵时，将贡象，先期语之，一象许诺；会明亡，不果进。皇朝定鼎，征贡象，象数头诺而来前。一象呼之不至，迟数日，翩然来取其牝，盖山中偶也；候已竟去。守土者廉知其期又当来，乃先期语之曰：“今天子神圣，薄海内外知天命有归，带甲者率先以军降，守土者次第以城降。汝异类，敢抗天子不赴耶？”至期来，竟复去。守土者异之，设大炮于衢，语之曰：“汝爱妻，数数来，汝再逸去，当死炮下！”象闻之，徐行伏炮台下，若待以举炮者。

    Explanation

    229

    The Elephant Record by Lin Lu Lu'an

    During the grand imperial ceremonies, the imperial procession was set up, and the trained elephants were lined up outside the gate, each arranged according to their rank on the left and right. When the officials entered, the bells rang and the whips cracked, and the elephants' trunks intertwined, and no one dared to intrude. After the ceremony, the elephants returned in order, and if any were found guilty, they were punished with a stick according to imperial decree, submitting to the punishment. This practice has been in place for a long time.

    A man from Qianzhong once told me that the local officials were responsible for providing elephants as tribute. They would enter the mountains and inform the elephants, saying, "The imperial court commands you to serve as guards and will grant you an official position." The elephants would bow and place their feet down, as if agreeing, and then walk obediently, and no one could catch them.

    During the reign of Emperor Si Ling, when the elephants were to be provided, they were informed in advance, and one elephant agreed; but when the Ming dynasty fell, the plan did not come to pass. When the Qing dynasty was established, they demanded tribute elephants, and several elephants agreed and came forward. One elephant did not respond when called, and after several days, it came gracefully to take its mate, which was a natural pairing in the mountains; after waiting, it finally left. The local officials found out that it would come again at the expected time, and they informed it in advance, saying, "The current emperor is divine and wise, and both within and beyond the borders know that the mandate of heaven has returned. Soldiers led the way in surrendering their armies, and local officials surrendered their cities in turn. You are of a different kind, dare you defy the emperor and not come?" It came at the expected time but finally left again. The local officials found it strange, and they set up cannons in the street, saying to it, "You love your wife, come often, if you run off again, you will die under the cannon!" The elephant heard this, slowly walked down and lay down at the base of the cannon, as if waiting for the cannon to be fired.

    230

    呜呼，异矣！夫人未有不爱其妻者，爱妻并爱吾身，谁能以其所爱，易其所至爱？而今见之于一象！呜呼，异矣！闻其言，退而为之记。
    　　[张山来曰：闻象房群象，皆行清礼，三跪九叩首；独一老象不能，犹作汉人跪拜云。因录此文，附记于此。
    　　世人画象，虽庞大而带妩媚。及现真象，殊属笨伯，尤恨其皮色秽浊，不似有识者。“以貌取人，失之子羽”，吾于观象亦云。] 纪周侍御事　陆次云云士大有奇书
    　　明天启时，御史周公宗建，屡疏击魏阉，夺职被逮，箠楚至不能出声。许显纯向公厉声曰：“此时复能詈魏上公不识一丁否？”卒毙于狱。七月还尸，家中讣音未至。

    Explanation

    230

    Alas, how extraordinary! There is no man who does not love his wife; to love one's wife is also to love oneself. Who could trade what they love for what they love most? And yet, I have seen this in an elephant! Alas, how extraordinary! Having heard his words, I have recorded them.

    [According to Zhang Shanlai: Hearing that the elephants in the elephant house all perform the Qing ritual, kneeling three times and bowing nine times; only one old elephant could not, still performing the Han-style kneeling and bowing. Because of this, I have recorded this text and added it here.
    People often paint elephants as large and charming. But when they see the real elephants, they are quite clumsy, especially hating their dirty skin color, which does not resemble someone with knowledge. "To judge a person by their appearance is to miss out on Ziyu," I also say this about observing elephants.]

    Record of the Affairs of Zhou the Censor by Lu Ci Yun, a very unusual book.

    During the reign of Emperor Tianqi, Censor Zhou Gong Zongjian frequently submitted memorials attacking Wei the eunuch, was stripped of his position, and arrested. He was beaten until he could not speak. Xu Xianchun sternly said to him, "Can you still curse Wei the Grand Eunuch for not knowing a single character?" He died in prison. In July, his body was returned, but the news of his death had not yet reached his family.

    231

    有清江浦舟子，接一秀士，许以一金雇舟。问其姓氏、自何所来。曰：“我周季侯，自京师来。”又问吴中被逮诸公状，颦蹙曰：“俱死矣！”又问魏监，曰：“伊罪恶贯盈，不久显戮矣。”至吴江，入门不出。舟子呼之，家人出，询知其故，曰：“季侯吾主人也，赴逮在京，安有此事？”喧闹间，夫人急出曰：“良有是事。昨梦侍御还家，备言死状，且云：上帝鉴其忠直，俾为神吴郡。舟子许其一金，为我酬之，勿失信也。”出金与之，举家环哭。舟人亦哭曰：“吾得载忠魂，生平奇事，肯受金耶？”夫人曰：“侍御生平清介。汝不受直，非其心也。”舟子拜领而去。 姚江神灯记　朱一是近修为可堂集
    　　往余闻姚江有神灯，以为诞。询邑人，曰：“有之。四三月间始见，东郊岳庙为盛。”余候其时，携同辈往，数数不获遇。庙僧曰：“天骤热，将雨，遇矣。”

    Explanation

    231

    During the Qing dynasty, there was a boatman from Jiangpu who met a talented scholar and agreed to hire a boat for one gold coin. The boatman asked the scholar his name and where he came from. The scholar replied, "I am Zhou Ji Hou, and I came from the capital." The boatman then asked about the situation of the officials from Wu who had been arrested, and the scholar frowned and said, "They are all dead!" The boatman then asked about Wei Jian, and the scholar said, "He has committed sins to the point of overflowing and will soon be publicly executed." When they arrived at Wujiang, the scholar did not leave the house. The boatman called out to them, and the family came out, asking about the reason. They said, "Ji Hou is our master, who was arrested in the capital. How could there be such a thing?" In the midst of the commotion, the wife quickly came out and said, "Indeed, there is such a thing. Last night I dreamed that the magistrate returned home and described his state of death. He also said that the Heavenly Emperor recognized his loyalty and integrity and made him a god of Wu County. The boatman agreed to one gold coin to pay for this, so please do not break your word." She gave him the gold, and the whole family cried around him. The boatman also wept and said, "I am able to carry the soul of a loyal man, a lifetime of extraordinary events, would I accept the gold?" The wife said, "The magistrate was always clean and upright. If you do not accept payment, it is not in your heart." The boatman bowed and accepted the gold, then left.

    As for the divine lamps of Yaojiang, I once heard that they existed and thought it was a fabrication. I asked the people of the county, and they said, "There are such things. They first appear in the fourth and third months, and they are most numerous at the Eastern Suburbs' Yue Temple." I waited for the time and went there with my companions, but we did not meet them several times. The temple monk said, "The sky is suddenly hot and it is about to rain; they will appear."

    232

    余又候热往，日暝抵庙。登山颠玉皇殿，凭高俯眺。忽见二灯冉冉从庙出，若悬予足底。回首四望，俱有所见，如晨星落落布野。已渐稠密，百千万亿，熠耀往来，不可纪极矣。有一灯独行者，有并携二灯者；有百什灯排列徐徐，若官人出行，卤簿前导者；有若二队相值，各分去者；有相值若揖若语而别者；有高擎者，有下移者；有置灯憩坐者，有穿林踏险而行者；有渡江者，始渡若揭衣踌躇，登岸则速者。其光或颓若有所幪，或光动若庭燎，或灭或复明；或数灯合为一，或一分为数；或迎风疾行，焰反向而炽；或徐行则敛，或驻则渐微；或排列一线，若星桥灯市；或独燃幽处，若寒窗爇灯荧荧然。或高在山半若悬竿，或出江间丛苇中若渔火；或远，或近在数十步内，熟视灯下，若有二足影，喁喁若闻语声，而实无语。余见灯聚处，使人疾趋视，则无有。其人回视余所在，反有之，余不觉也。至初更钟鸣，则尽灭。

    Explanation

    232

    I will now provide the final translation of the source text, adhering to the requirements and the pre-drafting research:

    I then waited for the heat to subside and went to the temple, arriving at dusk. Climbing to the summit of the Jade Emperor's Hall, I stood high and looked down. Suddenly, I saw two lights slowly emerging from the temple, as if hanging just below my feet. Turning my head and looking all around, I saw them everywhere, like scattered morning stars spread across the fields. They had already become dense, numbering in the hundreds, thousands, millions, and billions, shining brightly as they moved back and forth, too numerous to count. There were lamps traveling alone, and others carrying two lamps together; there were groups of many lamps arranged in lines, moving slowly, as if officials were going out with a retinue leading the way; there were two groups meeting and then separating to go different ways; there were groups meeting, bowing, speaking, and then parting; some lamps were held high, while others were moved down; some placed the lamp and rested; some passed through the forest, stepping on dangerous ground, and moved; some crossed the river, hesitating at the beginning, and then quickly reached the shore. The light sometimes dimmed as if covered, sometimes moved as if like lanterns in a courtyard, sometimes went out and then reignited; sometimes several lamps combined into one, and sometimes one split into several; sometimes the flame moved quickly against the wind, burning brightly in the opposite direction; sometimes it became less bright when moving slowly and gradually dimmed when stopping; sometimes they were arranged in a line, as if a star bridge or lantern market; sometimes a single lamp burned alone in a secluded place, as if a flickering lamp in a cold window; sometimes high on the mountain, as if hanging from a pole, and sometimes coming out of the river among the reeds, like fishing lights; sometimes far away, and sometimes close within dozens of steps. Looking closely under the light, there seemed to be two foot shadows, whispering as if hearing voices, but actually no words. When I saw where the lamps were gathered, I sent someone to hurry and look, but there was nothing. The person looked back at where I was, and there they were, but I did not notice. By the time the first watch bell rang, all the lights had disappeared.

    233

    呜呼，其神耶？非神耶？以余所见，洵神也。然神之德盛，塞天地，贯古今，无乎不在，而必姚江，必东郊，必四三月，必热将雨始见，是岂神耶？夫儒者探赜索隐，采传闻、览怪志，其疑惑聚讼宜也。余目所经见，且久立凝睇，而不知所由然，求为博物君子，不其难耶？抑诚有不可知者耶？不可知，则神矣。余故详述焉，以质世之多闻者。其年丙戌，其月癸巳，其日己卯。同游者，为年友湛侯子君进，及密、沈、叶三君，俞秀才咫颜，余门下士。
    　　[张山来曰：吾乡有灵金山，每岁以六月十八日建醮施食，檄召诸鬼。鬼火群起，倏合倏分。其文乃韩国公李善长读书山中时所撰。久之，其板漶漫至不可识。道士别镌一板，焚之而鬼不至，因仍以旧板刷文重读，燐火复炽。迄今每遇醮坛，则新旧二檄并焚云。可见鬼神一道，与人互相感通。姚江神灯，非妄言也。] 记盗　泾阳杨衡选圣藻手授钞本

    Explanation

    233
