    Chao Mountain is located north of Gao Ting Mountain. The mountain is not deep but has caves where tigers reside. In the winter of the eighteenth year of the Shunzhi era, a monk heard the tiger's roar and wanted to take his staff to ambush it, but was ultimately devoured by it. His disciples then invited a tiger hunter to catch the tiger. The hunter was from Jiangxi and had been hunting tigers for many years. At first, he dug a well and knew he would capture seven tigers. Every time he caught a tiger, the villagers gave him gold. His method was to place a sheep in the well and make it call to lure the tigers, and cook a measure of green snails, scattering them around the mountain. When the tigers came, the伥ghosts guided them. The伥ghosts saw the snails, greedily picked at the snail meat, and forgot to protect the tigers. The tigers then went alone, mistakenly entered the well, and the tiger hunter tied them up and brought them back. It is said that the monk's disciples, watching from afar across the mountain, saw it like this. After a month, the hunter said, "Today I will catch the seventh tiger!" The villagers gave him more gold as a gift. The hunter, carrying the gold, walked freely to look. The tiger in the well roared loudly, as fierce as a thunderclap. Suddenly, two tigers lurking outside the well leaped up from the grass, each biting one of the hunter's feet, and tore him apart. Catching tigers is meant to eliminate harm, so tigers should not be able to hold a grudge against the hunter. But ultimately, they harmed him. Perhaps those who had the intention to eliminate harm turned it into profit. Alas! The tiger hunter knew that the tigers would die in the well, but did not know that he too would perish outside the trap.

    390

    [张山来曰：人为虎所食，其鬼为伥，理应仇虎；乃不唯不仇之而已，而反为之用，何耶？吾乡素多虎，猎师亦必以饵诱伥，然未闻其为虎所害也。]
    　　看花述异记　王晫丹麓霞举堂集
    　　　　湖墅西偏，有沈氏园，茂才衡玉之别业也。茂才性爱花，自号花遁。园故多植古桂、老梅、玉兰、海棠、木芙蓉之属，而牡丹尤盛。叠石为山，高下互映，开时荧荧如列星，又如日中张五色锦，光彩夺目。远近士女游观者，日以数百。
    　　　　三月十八日，予亦往观，徘徊其下，日暮不忍归。主人留饮，饮竟，月已上东墙矣。主人别去，予就宿廊侧，静夜独坐。清风徐来，起步阶前，花影零乱，芳香袭人衣裾，几不复知身在人世。

    Explanation

    390

    Final Translation:

    Zhang Shanlai said: "When a person is eaten by a tiger, their ghost becomes a伥, and one would reasonably expect them to hate tigers; yet not only do they not hate them, but they actually work for them. Why is this so? In my hometown, there have always been many tigers, and hunters also use bait to lure伥, yet I have never heard of them being harmed by tigers."

    Translation:

    In the Records of Strange Flowers by Wang Zuo, Danlu Xijutang Collection:

    To the west of Lake Shu, there is a garden belonging to Mr. Shen, a talented scholar named Hengyu. Mr. Shen has a fondness for flowers and calls himself Huadun. The garden is adorned with ancient osmanthus trees, old plum blossoms, magnolias, crabapples, and mallow, with peonies being especially abundant. The garden features stone hills arranged in varying heights, creating a beautiful interplay of light and shadow. When the flowers bloom, they shine like stars in the sky and resemble a colorful brocade spread out at noon, dazzling the eyes. Every day, hundreds of scholars and ladies from far and near come to visit and admire the garden.

    On the eighteenth day of the third month, I also went to visit. I wandered around the garden, reluctant to leave as evening approached. The host invited me to stay for a drink, and after we finished, the moon had already risen above the eastern wall. The host left, and I stayed to sleep beside the corridor. In the quiet night, I sat alone. A gentle breeze blew, and I began to walk up the steps. The shadows of the flowers were scattered, and their fragrance wafted over me, almost making me forget that I was still in the world of mortals.

    391

    俄见女子自石畔出，年可十五、六，衣服娟楚。予惊问，女曰：“妾乃魏夫人弟子黄令征，以善种花，谓之花姑。夫人雅重君，特遣相迓。”予随问夫人隶何事？曰：“隶春工，凡天下草木花片，数之多寡，色之青白红紫，莫不于此赋形焉。”“然则何为见重也？”曰：“君至当自知。”因促予行。予不得已，随之去。
    　　　　移步从太湖石后，便非复向路。清溪夹岸，茂林蓊郁。沿溪行里许，但觉烟雾溟濛，芳菲满目，人间四季花，同时开放略尽。稍前一树，高丈余，花极烂熳。有三女子，红裳艳丽，偕游树下，见客亦不避。予叹息良久。花姑曰：“此鹤林寺杜鹃也，自殷七七催开后，即移植此。”又行数里，一望皆梅，红白相间，绿萼倍之。当盛处有一亭，榜曰“梅亭”，亭内有一美人，淡妆雅度，徙倚花侧。予流盼移时，几不能举步。花姑曰：“奈何尔？此是梅妃。梅亭二字，犹是上皇手书。幸妃性柔缓，不尔，恐获罪！”予笑谢乃已。

    Explanation

    391

    Suddenly, a girl appeared by the side of a stone, about fifteen or sixteen years old, dressed in elegant and graceful attire. Surprised, I asked her, and she replied, "I am Huang Ling Zheng, a disciple of Lady Wei, who is skilled in growing flowers and is known as Flower Maiden. Lady Wei highly respects you and has sent me to escort you." I then asked what Lady Wei was in charge of, and she said, "She is in charge of the Spring Workforce, overseeing all the flowers and plants in the world, their numbers, colors, and forms." "Then why is she so highly respected?" I asked. She replied, "You will know when you arrive." She then urged me to follow her. Reluctantly, I followed her.

    After taking a few steps behind the Taihu stone, we were no longer on the same path. A clear stream ran along both banks, with dense and lush forests on either side. Walking along the stream for about a mile, I was enveloped in a misty and hazy atmosphere, with flowers blooming everywhere. It seemed as though flowers from all four seasons were in full bloom simultaneously. A little further ahead, there was a tall tree, over ten feet high, with flowers that were extremely beautiful. Three women, dressed in red and stunningly beautiful, were walking together under the tree and did not avoid me. I sighed for a long time. The Flower Maiden said, "This is a rhododendron from He Lin Temple, which was transplanted here after being bloomed by Yin Qi Qi." Walking another few miles, I saw nothing but plum trees, their red and white blossoms alternating, with green calyxes twice as many. In the most flourishing place, there was a pavilion with the sign "Plum Pavilion." Inside the pavilion was a beautiful woman, lightly dressed and elegant, leaning against the flowers. I looked around for a while, almost unable to take a step. The Flower Maiden said, "What about you? This is the Plum Consort. The name 'Plum Pavilion' was still written by the former emperor. Fortunately, the consort's nature is gentle and slow; otherwise, she might incur blame!" I smiled and thanked her, then stopped.

    392

    行至一山，岩壑争秀，花卉殆与常异。听枝上鸟语，如鼓笙簧。渐见朱甍碧瓦，殿阁参差，两度石桥，乃抵其处。相厥栋宇，侈于王者。旁有二司如官署，右曰“太医院”。予大惊讶，问花姑曰：“此处亦须太医耶？”花姑笑曰：“乃苏直耳；善治花，瘠者能腴，病者能安，故命为花太医。”“其左曰『太师府』何？”曰：“此洛人朱仲儒所居也。名单父，善吟诗，亦能种植。艺牡丹，术凡变易千种，人不能测。

    Explanation

    392

    They walked to a mountain where the rocks and valleys competed in beauty, and the flowers were almost entirely different from those usually seen. Listening to the birds singing on the branches, it sounded like the music of flutes and reed pipes. Gradually, they saw red-roofed buildings with blue tiles, pavilions and halls arranged in a staggered manner, passing over two stone bridges, and finally arrived at the place. Observing the buildings, they were more luxurious than those of a king. To the side, there were two offices like government offices, the right one being called "The Imperial Medical Bureau." The narrator was greatly surprised and asked Flower Maiden, "Is there also a need for a chief physician here?" Flower Maiden smiled and said, "It's Su Zhi; he is skilled in treating flowers, making weak ones strong and sick ones healthy, hence he is called the Chief Physician of Flowers." "What about the one on the left, called 'The Imperial Tutor's Residence'?" she asked. "This is where Zhu Zhongru from Luoyang lives. His name is Danfu, skilled in reciting poetry and also in planting. He cultivates peonies, and his techniques can transform them into a thousand varieties, which no one can fathom."

    393

    上皇尝召至骊山，植花万本，色样各不同，賜金千两，内人皆呼『花师』，故至今仍其称。”入门，由西街行百步余，侧有小苑，画槛雕栏。予遽欲进内，花姑虑夫人待久，不令入。予再三强之，方许。及阶，见一花合蒂，浓艳芬馥，染襟袖不散。庭中有美女，时复取嗅之，腰肢纤惰，多憨态。予不敢熟视。花姑曰：“君识是花否？”予曰：“不识也。”曰：“此产嵩山坞中，人不知名，采者异之，以贡炀帝。会车驾适至，爰賜名迎辇花。嗅之能令人清酒，兼能忘睡。”予曰：“然则所见美女，其司花女袁宝儿耶？”花姑曰：“然！”遂出。

    Explanation

    393

    The Emperor once summoned him to Lishan, where he planted a hundred thousand flowers, each with different colors and varieties, and bestowed upon him a thousand ounces of gold. The inner court all called him "Flower Master," and so until today, the title is still used. Entering the gate, he walked more than a hundred steps down the west street, and there was a small garden on the side, with painted railings and carved balustrades. He immediately wanted to enter the inner garden, but the Flower Maiden was concerned that Madam had been waiting for a long time, so she did not allow him to enter. He persistently urged her, and only then was he allowed. Upon reaching the steps, he saw a flower with a double head, richly colorful and fragrant, staining his sleeves and not dissipating. There was a beautiful woman in the courtyard, who occasionally picked it up and smelled it, with a slender and delicate waist and many naive and playful expressions. He dared not look closely. The Flower Maiden said, "Do you recognize this flower?" He replied, "I do not." She said, "This flower grows in the foothills of Mount Song, and people do not know its name. Those who collected it found it unusual and presented it to Emperor Yang. When the imperial carriage happened to arrive, it was given the name 'Welcome Chariot Flower.' Smelling it can make one drunk on clear wine and forget to sleep." He said, "Then the beautiful woman I saw, could she be the flower maiden Yuan Bao'er?" The Flower Maiden replied, "Yes!" And then they left.

    394

    复由中道过大殿。殿角遇二少妇，皆靓妆，迎且笑曰：“来何暮也？”花姑亟问：“夫人何在？”曰：“在内殿观诸美人歌舞奏乐为乐。客既至，当入报夫人。”予遽止之曰：“姑少候。诸美人可得窃窥乎？”二妇笑曰：“可。”谓花姑：“汝且陪君子，我二人候乐毕相延也。”去后，予乃问花姑：“二妇为谁？”曰：“二妇本李邺侯公子妾，衣青者曰绿丝，衣绯者曰醉桃。花经两人手，无不活。夫人以是录入近侍。”遂引予至殿前帘外，见丝竹杂陈，声容备善，正洋洋盈耳。忽有美人撩鬓举袂，直奏曼声，觉丝竹之音不能遏；既而广场寂寂，若无一人。予闻之，不胜惊叹。花姑曰：“此永新歌，所谓『歌值千金』，正斯人也。”语未毕，闻帘内宣“王生入”。

    Explanation

    394

    Once again through the central path, I passed by the main hall. At the corner of the hall, I encountered two young women, both beautifully dressed, who greeted me with smiles and said, "Why are you so late?" Flower Girl quickly asked, "Where is the lady?" They replied, "She is inside the hall, watching the beautiful women dance, sing, and play music for entertainment. Since the guest has arrived, we should go in and report to the lady." I immediately stopped them and said, "Please wait a moment. Can we sneak a peek at the beautiful women?" The two women laughed and said, "Yes." They told Flower Girl, "You accompany the gentleman for now, and we will invite you once the music ends." After they left, I asked Flower Girl, "Who are the two women?" She replied, "The two women were originally concubines of the prince of Li Yehou. The one wearing green is called Green Silk, and the one wearing red is called Drunken Peach. Flowers pass through their hands and always come to life. The lady has taken them in as close attendants." They then led me to the front of the hall, outside the curtain, where I saw a variety of musical instruments arranged together, and the sounds and performances were all excellent, filling my ears with a rich and harmonious melody. Suddenly, a beautiful woman tossed her hair and lifted her sleeves, playing a slow, melodious tune, and I felt that the music could not be stopped. After that, the courtyard became silent, as if no one was there. I was amazed and could not help but exclaim. Flower Girl said, "This is the Ever New Song, as it is said, 'a song worth a thousand gold pieces.' This is indeed the person." Before she finished speaking, I heard an announcement from inside the curtain, "Mr. Wang is entering."

    395

    予敛容整衣而进，望殿上夫人，丰仪绰约，衣绛绡衣，冠翠翘冠，珠珰玉珮，如后妃状。侍女数十辈，亦皆妖丽绝人。予再拜。命予起，曰：“汝见诸美女乎？”予谢不敢。夫人曰：“美人是花真身，花是美人小影。以汝惜花，故得见此，缘殊不浅。向汝作《戒折花文》，已命卫夫人楷书一通，置诸座右。”予益逊谢。旋命坐，进百花膏。夫人顾左右曰：“王生远至，汝辈何以乐嘉宾之心？”有一女亭亭玉立，抱琴请曰：“妾愿抚琴。”一声才动，四座无言。泠泠然，抚遍七弦，直令万木澄幽，江月为白。夫人称善，曰：“昔于頔尝令客弹琴，其嫂审声，叹曰：『三分中，一分筝，二分琵琶，绝无琴韵。』今听卢女弹，一弦能清一心，不数秀奴七七矣。”因呼太真奏琵琶。予闻呼太真，私意当日称为“解语花”，又曰“海棠睡未醒”，不料邂逅于此。乃见一人，纤腰修眸，衣黄衣，冠玉冠，年三十许，容色绝丽，抱琵琶奏之，音韵凄清，飘出云外。

    Explanation

    395

    I have prepared a polished translation of the source text, applying the insights from the pre-drafting research to ensure accuracy, cultural richness, and natural English flow. Here is the final translation:

    I composed myself, dressed respectfully, and advanced forward. Upon gazing at the lady on the dais, I saw her with a graceful and elegant demeanor, wearing a red gossamer robe and a green feathered crown, adorned with jade and pearl ornaments, resembling a consort of a queen. Dozens of maidservants, all equally beautiful and captivating, attended her. I bowed twice. She ordered me to stand, saying, "Have you seen these beautiful women?" I humbly declined, saying I dared not. The lady replied, "The beauty of women is the true essence of flowers, and flowers are the small reflections of women. Because you cherish flowers, you are allowed to see this beauty, a bond that is quite profound. I have written a 'Cautionary Essay on Picking Flowers' for you, which has been transcribed by Wei Lady in neat script and placed on the right side of your seat." I further humbly thanked her. She then ordered me to sit and offered me a preparation made from various flowers. The lady looked around and said, "Wang Sheng has come from afar; how can you entertain the heart of this honored guest?" A maiden stood gracefully and beautifully, carrying a lute and asking, "I wish to play the lute." As soon as the first note sounded, the entire hall fell silent. The sound was clear and resonant, as she played all seven strings, making the woods seem serene and the river moon appear pure. The lady praised the performance, saying, "In the past, Yu Di once had a guest play the lute, and his sister-in-law, upon hearing the sound, sighed, 'In three parts, one part is the zither, two parts are the pipa, and there is no lute melody.' Now, listening to Lu Lady play, each string can clear the heart, and her performance is not inferior to Xiu Nu's." She then called for Tai Zhen to play the pipa. Upon hearing the call for Tai Zhen, I privately thought of the historical reference to her as "the speaking flower" and "the sleeping hawthorn." I did not expect to meet her here. I saw a woman with a slender waist and long eyes, wearing a yellow robe and a jade crown, about thirty years old, with extraordinary beauty. She played the pipa, and the sound was clear and ethereal, as if it floated out of the clouds.

    This translation aims to capture the elegance and formality of the original text while ensuring it reads naturally in English.

    396

    予复请搊筝。夫人笑曰：“近来唯此乐，传得美人情。君独请此，情见乎辞矣！”顾诸女辈曰：“谁擅此技？”皆曰：“第一筝手，无如薛琼琼。”寻有一女，着淡红衫子，系砑罗裙，手捧一器，上圆，下平，中空，弦柱十二，予不辨何物。夫人曰：“此即筝也。”顷乃调宫商于促柱，转妙音于繁弦，始忆崔怀宝诗，良非虚语。曲才终，又有一女，抱一器，似琵琶而圆者，其形象月，弹之，其声合琴，音韵清朗。

    Explanation

    396

    I again requested to play the zheng. The lady smiled and said, "Recently, this music conveys the emotions of a beautiful woman. You alone have asked for this, and your feelings are revealed in your words!" She then looked at the other ladies and asked, "Who is the best at this skill?" They all replied, "The best zither player is none other than Xue Qiongqiong." Soon, a young woman appeared, wearing a light red shirt and a finely woven silk skirt, holding an instrument with a round top, flat bottom, and hollow interior, strung with twelve strings. I could not identify what it was. The lady said, "This is the zheng." In a moment, she adjusted the pitches on the short strings and produced beautiful sounds on the intricate strings. I only then remembered the poem by Cui Huaibao, and it was indeed not an empty statement. As the song ended, another young woman came forward, holding an instrument that resembled a pipa but was round, shaped like the moon. When she played it, the sound harmonized with the qin, and the melody was clear and bright.

    397

    予又不辨何物，但微顾是女，手纹隐处如红线。夫人察余意，指示予曰：“此名阮咸，一名月琴，唯红线雅善此。”予方知是女即红线也。夫人忽指一女曰：“浑忘却汝。汝有绝技，何不令嘉客得闻？”予起视，见一美人，含情不语，娇倚屏间，闻夫人语，微笑。予遂问夫人：“是女云谁？”夫人曰：“此魏高阳王雍美人徐月华也。能弹卧箜篌，为明妃出塞之歌，哀声入云，闻者莫不动容。”已持一器，体曲而长，二十三弦，抱于怀中，两齐奏之，果如夫人言。

    Explanation

    397

    I could not tell what it was, but I noticed that the girl had a hidden pattern on her hand like a red thread. The lady understood my intention and pointed it out to me, saying, "This is called the Ruan Xian, also known as the Moon Zither. Only Red Thread is particularly skilled at this." I then realized that the girl was indeed Red Thread. The lady suddenly pointed to another girl and said, "I almost forgot about you. You have a unique skill, why not let the distinguished guest hear it?" I looked up and saw a beautiful woman, holding her emotions without speaking, gracefully leaning against the screen. Hearing the lady’s words, she smiled. I then asked the lady, "Who is this girl?" The lady replied, "This is Xu Yuehua, a beauty of Wei Prince Yong of Gaoyang. She can play the reclining konghou and perform the song of Mingfei Leaving for the Frontier. Her mournful voice reached the clouds, and all who heard were deeply moved." She already held an instrument, curved and long with twenty-three strings, and began to play it, performing as the lady had described.

    398

    俄有一女跨丹凤至，诸女辈咸曰：“吹箫女来矣！”女谓夫人曰：“闻夫人延客，弄玉愿献新声。”夫人请使吹之。一声而清风生，再吹而彩云起，三吹而凤凰翔，便冉冉乘云而去。耳畔犹闻呜呜声，细察之，已非箫矣。别一女子，短发丽服，貌甚美而媚，横吹玉笛，极要眇可听。夫人曰：“谁人私弄笛？”诸女辈报曰：“石家儿绿珠。”夫人命亟出见客。女伴数促不肯前，中一女亦具国色，乃曰：“儿亦善笛，何必尔也？”绿珠闻之，怒曰：“阿纪敢与我较长短耶？我终身事季伦，不似汝谢仁祖殁，遂嫁郗昙。不以汗颜，翻逞微技？”是女羞愤无一言。夫人不怿，命止乐。

    Explanation

    398

    Soon, a woman arrived riding a red phoenix, and all the other women exclaimed, "The flute-playing woman has come!" She addressed the lady, saying, "I have heard that you are entertaining guests, and I, Lady Xi Shi, wish to offer a new melody." The lady asked her to play, and with the first note, a cool breeze arose; with the second, colored clouds gathered; and with the third, the phoenix soared, and she slowly and gracefully rode the clouds away. Even as she departed, a whistling sound lingered by the ear, and upon closer inspection, it was no longer coming from a flute. Another woman, with short hair and beautiful attire, appeared, her beauty and charm striking. She played a jade flute horizontally, and the music was graceful and subtle, delightful to listen to. The lady asked, "Who is secretly playing the flute?" The other women replied, "It is Green Pearl, Zhang's daughter." The lady ordered her to come out to meet the guests. The other women urged her forward, but one among them, also of extraordinary beauty, said, "I too am skilled at the flute, why is this necessary?" Green Pearl, upon hearing this, angrily retorted, "Does Ah Ji dare to compare her skills with mine? I have devoted my life to my lover, unlike you, who, after the death of Xie Renzong, married Xi Tan. You are not ashamed to show off your small talent?" The other woman was filled with shame and anger, saying nothing. The lady was displeased and ordered the music to stop.

    399

    忽有啭喉一歌，声出于朝霞之上，执板当席，顾盼撩人。夫人喜曰：“久不闻念奴歌，今益足畅人怀！”念奴曰：“妾何足言？使丽娟发声，妾成伧父矣！”夫人指曰：“丽娟体弱不胜衣，恐不耐歌。”予见其年仅十四、五，玉肤柔软，吹气胜兰，举步珊珊，疑骨节自鸣。乃曰：“对嘉宾，岂能辞丑？”因唱《回风曲》，庭叶翻落如秋，予但唤奈何而已。丽娟曰：“君尚未见绛树也。绛树一声能歌两曲。二人细听，各闻一曲，一字不乱。每欲效之，竟不测其术。

    Explanation

    399

    Suddenly, a melodious song arose from a throat, its voice rising above the morning glow. The singer held a board while seated, her glances captivating and alluring. The lady exclaimed with joy, "It has been so long since I last heard Niannu's song, and now it is even more satisfying to the heart!" Niannu replied humbly, "How could I possibly compare? If Li Juan were to sing, I would become a vulgar person!" The lady pointed out, "Li Juan is weak and unable to bear clothes, fearing she cannot endure singing." I observed that she was only fourteen or fifteen years old, with soft, jade-like skin, a breath fragrant as orchids, and steps so graceful that they seemed to make her bones and joints ring. She then said, "In front of distinguished guests, how could I refuse to sing poorly?" And so, she sang the "Hui Feng Qu." The leaves in the courtyard fluttered and fell like autumn, and I could only call "what can be done?" in response. Li Juan remarked, "You have not yet seen Jiantree. Jiantree can sing two songs with one voice. Two people listen closely, each hearing one song without any confusion. Whenever we try to imitate it, we cannot understand the method."

    400

    ”夫人曰：“绛树木虽异，恐无能胜子。吾且欲与王生观绛树舞。”乃见飞舞回旋，有凌云态，信妙舞莫巧于绛树也。绛树谓丽娟曰：“汝欲效吾歌不得，吾欲学汝舞亦不能。”夫人大悟曰：“有是哉！汉武尝以吸花丝锦，賜丽娟作舞衣，春暮宴于花下，舞时故以袖拂落花，满身都着，谓之百花舞。今日奈何不为王生演之？”丽娟复起舞，舞态愈媚，第恐临风吹去。忽闻鸡鸣，予起别。夫人曰：“后会尚有期，慎自爱。”仍命花姑送予行。视诸美人，皆有恋恋不忍别之色，予亦不知涕之何从也。

    Explanation

    400

    The lady said, "Though the trees of绛 are different, I doubt they can surpass you. I wish to watch绛's dance with Wang Sheng." Upon seeing her flying and swirling, she displayed a soaring attitude, and indeed, no dance is more exquisite than绛's.绛told Li Juan, "You cannot imitate my singing, and I cannot learn your dance." The lady suddenly realized, "Is that so! Emperor Wu once gave Li Juan a luxurious silk fabric with floral patterns to make a dance costume. In the evening of spring, during a banquet under the flowers, she would intentionally brush off falling flowers, covering her body all over, which was called the 'Dance of a Hundred Flowers.' Why don't you perform it for Wang Sheng today?" Li Juan danced again, her dance becoming even more charming, but she was afraid the wind might blow her away. Suddenly, she heard the rooster crow, and I rose to bid farewell. The lady said, "We will meet again someday, take care of yourself." She then ordered Flower Maiden to see me off. Looking at the other beautiful women, all had a reluctant look of not wanting to part, and I did not know where my tears came from.

    401

    花姑引予从间道出，路颇崎岖。回首忽失花姑所在，但见晓星欲落，斜月横窗，花影翻阶，翩然若顾予而笑。露坐石上，忆所见闻，恍如隔世。因慨天下事大率类是，故记之。时康熙戊申三月。
    　　　　［袁箨庵曰：具三十分才情，方能有此撰述。若有才无情，则不真；有情无才，则不畅。读竟始服其能。
    　　　　李湘北曰：此丹麓《戒折花文》绝妙注疏也。将千古艳魂，和盘托出，笑语如生，不数文成将军之于李夫人、临邛道士之于杨玉环矣。

    Explanation

    401

    Hua Gu led me out through a side path, the road being rather rugged. Turning around suddenly, I lost sight of Hua Gu, but saw the morning star about to set, the slanting moon across the window, the shadows of flowers dancing across the steps, as if she were gracefully looking at me and smiling. Sitting out in the open on a stone, I recalled what I had seen and heard, feeling as if it were from another world. Reflecting on the nature of worldly affairs, I realized they are largely of this kind, so I recorded it. It was March of the year 1678 during the reign of Emperor Kangxi.

    [ Yuan Tuo'an said: It takes thirty parts of talent to produce such a work. If one has talent but no emotion, it is not genuine; if one has emotion but no talent, it is not fluent. Only after reading it in full did I truly admire his ability.
    Li Xiangbei said: This is an excellent annotation and commentary on Dan Lu's Discourse on Picking Flowers. It reveals the eternal beauty of the soul, with laughter and speech as if they were alive, surpassing even Wen Cheng General's writings about Lady Li and the Linqu Daoist's writings about Yang Guifei.]

    402

    徐竹逸曰：逸兴如落花依草，可补《虞初志》《艳异编》之所未备。文心九曲，几欲占尽风流。
    　　　　张山来曰：予尝谓“以爱花之心爱美人，则领略定饶逸趣；以爱美人之心爱花，则护惜别有深情。”丹麓惜花如命，固应有此奇遇。
    　　　　又曰：向读《艳异》诸书，见花妖月姐，往往于文士有缘，心窃慕之，恨生平未之遇也。今读此记，益令我神往矣！]
    　　孝犬传　陈鼎定九留溪外传
    　　　　孝犬，广东东莞县隐士陈恭隐家牝犬也，色白而尾骍，四足皆黑。恭隐痛父死国难，矢志不进取，隐居山中，以吟饮自纵，不与时人通。此犬随恭隐，未尝须臾离。每出，则犬先行数百步，若以为导者。遇豺狼蛇虎，则亟返，啮恭隐衣袂，曳之还，若不使前者。恭隐悟，即旋。犬又随后，离数十步，作大声嗥，若以为卫者。以是为常。夜则于庐舍前后巡且吠，达旦不少休。

    Explanation

    402

    徐竹逸说：“我的灵感如同落花依附于青草，可以补充《虞初志》和《艳异编》中未记载的部分。文思如九曲黄河，几乎要占尽风流。”

    张山来也说：“我常说，以爱花的心去爱美人，定能领略到更多的逸趣；以爱美人的心理解花，会更加珍惜那份深情。”丹麓对花的珍爱如同生命，自然应该有这样的奇遇。

    他又说：“我以前读过《艳异》等书，书中记载的花妖月姐常常与文人有缘，我内心暗暗羡慕，遗憾自己一生未曾遇见。现在读到这篇记载，更加让我心驰神往！”

    孝犬传 陈鼎定九留溪外传

    孝犬，是广东东莞县隐士陈恭隐家的母犬，毛色白而尾巴红，四只脚都是黑色。陈恭隐因父亲为国难而死，发誓不再追求功名，隐居山中，以吟诗饮酒自娱，不与世人交往。这只犬总是跟随陈恭隐，从不离开他。每当陈恭隐出门，犬就会先走几百步，仿佛在为他引路。遇到豺狼蛇虎，犬会立刻返回，咬住陈恭隐的衣袖，把他拉回来，好像不让陈恭隐前进。陈恭隐明白了，便立即转身。犬又跟在后面，相距几十步，发出大声的吼叫，仿佛在为他守卫。这成了常态。到了夜晚，犬会在房屋前后巡逻并吠叫，直到天亮也不休息。

    403

    数年，犬一乳五子，皆牝。既长，恭隐分赠前后左右邻家畜，皆能司门户不怠。初分之岁余，母犬日往各家，视乳犬一周，若训之勤者。有食，乳犬辄让母犬食。乳犬既壮，母犬即不往视，而乳犬每早辄齐来恭隐家视母犬。又数年，母犬病癞，瘦将死。乳犬日齐来，争与母犬舐癞，遂愈。每至元旦，五乳犬辄齐来，绕母犬摇尾，若为母犬贺岁状。后母犬死，五乳犬皆哀号不止。恭隐悯之，瘗之后山。五乳犬每早辄齐往瘗处号，如是者数年不辍。
    　　　　外史氏曰：世之人，能以酒食养父母，辄自诩曰“孝”，且有德色。子曰：“至于犬马，皆能有养，其难者敬耳！”睹兹五犬之殷殷其母，敬矣哉！呜呼，世之人不若者众矣！

    Explanation

    403

    Several years later, the dog gave birth to five puppies, all of which were female. When they grew up, Gong Yin distributed them to his neighbors in front, back, left, and right, and they all could guard the gates without tiring. After a year, the mother dog would visit each household daily, checking on the puppies as if training them diligently. When there was food, the puppies would always let the mother dog eat first. As the puppies grew strong, the mother dog no longer visited them, but the puppies would come to Gong Yin's house every morning to see her. Several more years passed, and the mother dog fell ill with mange, becoming thin and nearly dead. The puppies would come together every day to compete in licking the mother dog's mange, and she recovered. Every New Year's Day, the five puppies would all come together, circling the mother dog and wagging their tails, as if celebrating her New Year. After the mother dog died, the five puppies mourned without stopping. Gong Yin pitied them and buried her on the back mountain. Every morning, the five puppies would go to the burial site and howl, doing so for several years without stopping.

    The historian said: People of the world, who can provide for their parents with food and drink, often boast that they are filial, and show a look of virtue. Confucius said: "Even dogs and horses can be cared for; the difficulty lies in showing respect!" Seeing these five dogs' deep affection for their mother, how respectful they are! Alas, there are many people in the world who are not like this.

    404

    [张山来曰：义犬事甚多，不胜其载。今此犬独以孝闻，故特存之。]
    　　
    　　
    　　虞初新志卷十三
    　　曼殊别志书砖　萧山毛奇龄大可西河合集
    　　　　曼殊，丰台卖花翁女。陈检讨维崧序云：“疏篱织处，青门种树之翁；纤笼携来，缟袂卖花之妪。”江主事懋麟诗云：“荒村侍婢卖花回，补屋牵萝晓镜开。怪底红颜如芍药，妾家生小住丰台。”汪春坊楫诗云：“春到长安芍药开，寻花曾一到丰台。自从解语归金谷，不是花时客也来。”张学士英诗云：“闻说丰台住小姑，百环新髻世应无。又添一段游人话，芍药开时说曼殊。”生时，母梦邻妪以白花一当一根也寄使卖。其前邻奶奶庙也，后邻钱氏，疑昔者乃钱氏妪，因名阿钱。周赞善清原《续长恨歌》云：“张家小女名阿钱，种花家住丰台侧，生成骨格一枝香，斟酌衣裳百花色。”

    Explanation

    404

    Final Translation

    Zhang Shanlai said: "There are many stories of loyal dogs, too numerous to recount. Today, this particular dog is known for its filial piety, so it is especially preserved."

    Yu Chou Xin Zhi Volume 13
    Man Shu Bie Zhi
    Shu Zhu by Mao Qilin from Xiao Shan, also known as Mao Dake

    Man Shu was the daughter of a flower seller in Fengtai. Chen Jiankao Weisong wrote in his preface: "In the sparse bamboo fences, there was an old man planting trees by the Green Gate; with a small cage in hand, came an old woman selling flowers in a white robe." Jiang Zhushi Maolin wrote: "A servant girl returned from selling flowers in a remote village, repairing the house with vines and opening the morning mirror. How strange that her beauty is like the peony; I grew up in Fengtai." Wang Chunfang Ji wrote: "When spring arrives in Chang'an and the peonies bloom, I once visited Fengtai to see the flowers. Since she can speak, she returned to Jingu; even when the flowers are not in bloom, visitors come." Zhang Xueshi Ying wrote: "I heard that a young woman named Xiaogu lives in Fengtai, with a new hairstyle adorned with a hundred rings, a sight unseen in the world. Another story adds to the tales of visitors; when the peonies bloom, they talk about Man Shu."

    At birth, her mother dreamed of a neighbor woman giving her a white flower, saying it was worth selling for one root. The woman lived in front of the temple of the Nanny, and behind her was the Qian family. It was suspected that the woman was from the Qian family, so she was named Aqian. Zhou Zanxian Qingyuan wrote in Xu Chang Hen Ge (Continued Song of Eternal Sorrow): "The young daughter of the Zhang family is named Aqian, living near Fengtai where she grows flowers. Born with a graceful bone structure, she is as fragrant as a branch of peony, and her clothes are adorned with the colors of a hundred flowers."

    405

    阿钱慧甚，能效百鸟音。京城販儿推货车行叫卖，嘤喐不可辨，阿钱遥闻便知之。十岁，前村学针线，把剪即能刻花种人兽，不构谱，俨熟习者。客有以千钱购蕃绣旛灯于前村家，阿钱方学绣，立应之去。既长，色白，目有曼光，十指类削玉，黝{髟火}委地可鉴。《续长恨歌》云：“十枝春笋扶钗出，一寸横波入髩流。银蒜双双垂彩索，晓日曀昽射妆阁。”张编修廷瓒诗云：“子夜清歌醉不醒，曾看宝髻倚银屏。菱花掩后香雪散，肠断春山一样青。”才拢头，作十种名。

    Explanation

    405

    Ar Qian was exceptionally intelligent and could imitate the sounds of many birds. In the capital city, peddlers pushing carts and shouting to sell goods produced indistinguishable sounds, but Ar Qian could hear them from afar and immediately recognize them. At the age of ten, she learned needlework in a village and, holding scissors, could carve flowers and human and animal figures without following a pattern, as if already familiar with the techniques. A guest offered a thousand coins to buy foreign embroidered flags and lanterns from a house in the village, and Ar Qian, who was just learning embroidery, immediately agreed and went. When she grew up, her complexion was white, her eyes had a gentle glow, her ten fingers were like carved jade, and her black hair hung down to the ground, shining like a mirror. The poem Continuation of the Long Life Song says: "Ten branches of spring bamboo shoots support the hairpin and emerge, an inch of horizontal waves flows into the hair. Silver garlic hangs in pairs, hanging colorful ribbons, the morning sun shining through shines on the dressing room." The poem by Zhang Bianxiu Tingzan says: "Clear songs at midnight, drunk and not waking, once saw the precious hairpin leaning against the silver screen. The lotus flower conceals behind, fragrant snow disperses, heartbroken, as green as spring mountains." Just as she started to braid her hair, she created ten different styles.

    406

    最上以{髟火}丳，绾作连环百结蟠顶前，名“百环髻”，《留视图自序》云：“饰予生平所梳百环髻。”王舍人嗣槐诗云：“东风吹罗衣，空园自摇曳。采将千种花，拢作百环髻。”《续长恨歌》云：“八幅湘裙初拂地，百环云髻早宜春。”方编修象瑛诗云：“自制新妆号百环，春风摇漾画图间。无端梦遥空王去，凄绝丰台旧日山。”张中书睿诗云：“百结云鬓别样妆，曼殊花放下巫阳。只今留视图犹在，减却生时一段香。”乔侍读诗云：“百环髻就玉为神，别有秋华領好春。斜傍青山长不扫，有谁堪作画眉人？”

    Explanation

    406

    The most exquisite style involves weaving the hair with a fine comb, then intricately braiding it into a complex, interlocking pattern that forms a crown on the head, known as the "hundred-ring bun." In the preface to Liu Shi Tu, it is said, "Adorned with the hundred-ring bun that I have combed throughout my life." Wang She Ren Si Hua wrote in his poem, "The east wind blows my silk robe, and in the empty garden, it sways gently. I gathered a thousand kinds of flowers and arranged them into the hundred-ring bun." In Xu Chang Hen Ge, it is written, "A long, flowing skirt of Xiang fabric brushes the ground, and the hundred-ring bun is already fitting for spring." Fang Bianxiu Xiang Ying composed a poem stating, "I created a new makeup called the hundred-ring bun, which sways gently in the spring breeze within the painting. Without reason, my dream of the distant king has faded, and the desolate old mountain of Fengtai is filled with sorrow." Zhang Zhongshu Rui wrote, "The cloud-like hair is styled in a unique way, adorned with Manchu flowers and laid down in Wuyang. Now the painting still exists, but it has lost the fragrance of her life." Qiao Shidu wrote, "The hundred-ring bun is as beautiful as a jade statue, with a unique charm that combines the beauty of autumn and spring. Leaning against the green mountains, it remains untouched, but who can paint her eyebrows?"

    407

    顾性贞静。十二，从庙归，路人观者啧啧称好，姑则大愠，归不再出。予来京师，益都夫子为予谋买妾，有以阿钱言者，预遣二世兄往视，不许。吴文学闸思诗云：“争似丰台解语花，脸波春色衬朝霞。盈盈碧玉年娇小，不爱青齐宰相家。”乔侍读诗云：“村庄无复往东墙，但对名花引兴长。莫道小家刘碧玉，一生不嫁汝南王。”先是阿钱病，西山尼师过其门，咨嗟曰：“阿钱不年，不宜为人妻。”或曰：“为小妻即免。”遂决计作妾。然往请者，率骄贵，深不自愿。及二世兄往，谓犹是相公家也。越数日，予亲往，询余甚喜，且有谬誉予善文者。

    Explanation

    407

    Concealing her true nature, she was pure and tranquil. At twelve years old, returning from a temple visit, passersby praised her enthusiastically, but her aunt was greatly displeased, and she did not go out again. When I came to the capital city, a respected teacher in Yidu helped arrange for the purchase of a concubine, and someone mentioned Ah Qian. I sent my second eldest brother to see her, but she refused. Wu Wenxue wrote a poem: "How can it compare to the beautiful flowers of Fengtai, whose faces reflect the spring color against the morning glow? Her delicate and graceful appearance, like a young jade, does not love the family of the prime minister of Qing and Qi." Qiao Shidu also wrote a poem: "No longer will I wander to the eastern wall of the village, but only admire the famous flowers that inspire my heart. Do not say that the small family's Liu Bixi will never marry the prince of Runan." Before this, Ah Qian was ill, and a nun from the Western Hills passed by her door and sighed, saying, "Ah Qian is too young and not suitable to be a wife." Someone said, "If she becomes a concubine, she will be exempt." She then decided to become a concubine. However, those who came to ask for her hand were usually arrogant and proud, and she deeply did not wish to. When my second eldest brother went, he thought it was still the family of a scholar-official. After several days, I went personally, and she was very pleased, and there was also false praise of my literary skills.

    408

    李检讨澄中《曼殊》诗云：“守身坚择对，偃蹇已数夫。不惜充下陈，但愿嫁通儒。毛郎富文史，作赋迈《三都》。”《续长恨歌》云：“纷纭粱肉皆尘土，不愿将身入朱户。兰生空谷人自知，啧啧张家有贤女。毛君一赋奏凌云，柱下才名天下闻。”龙检讨燮诗云：“湘湖词客毛先生，日昨捧檄来燕京。《子虚赋》献官侍从，闺中儿女皆知名。”李中允铠诗云：“毛子銮坡彦，文笔五色鲜。造访出花下，惊鸿何翩翩？岂有十斛珠，乃订三生缘。盈盈赋丽情，慕义良独难。”是夜，予梦大士，取盎中花手授予。次日插戴。北方以下定为“插戴”。《续长恨歌》云：“疏篱野径多闲暇，落花无人碧窗夜。天然芳洁不由人，优钵昙花是化身。

    Explanation

    408

    Li JianTiao's poem "Manshu" says: "To firmly choose one's partner, even if it means being lonely and unpaired for many years. Unwilling to be a secondary wife, but wishing to marry a learned scholar. Mr. Mao is rich in literature and history, his fu surpasses 'San Du.'"

    The continuation of the "Song of Eternal Sorrow" by Li JianTiao says: "The luxurious life is like dust, I do not wish to enter the noble household. Orchids grow in the valley, known only to themselves; people praise the Zhang family for having a virtuous daughter. Mr. Mao, with one fu that soars to the heavens, his talent and reputation are known throughout the world."

    Long JianTiao's poem says: "Mr. Mao, a poet from Xianghu, brought an imperial edict to Yanjing yesterday. He presented 'Zixu Fu' to the officials, and even the ladies in the inner chambers know his name."

    Li ZhongYun's poem says: "Mr. Mao, a scholar from Luanpo, has colorful and fresh writing. Visiting under the flowers, the startled swan is so graceful. Is there a ten-hundredweight pearl, to seal a three-life bond? The beautiful and graceful expression of love, admiring virtue is indeed difficult."

    That night, I dreamed of the Bodhisattva, who took a flower from the jar and handed it to me. The next day, I wore it. In the north, the engagement is called "insertion and wearing." The continuation of the "Song of Eternal Sorrow" says: "Sparse hedges and wild paths have many idle moments, fallen flowers and the green window at night. Naturally fragrant and pure, not subject to human will, the Utpala flower is its incarnation."

    409

    ”胡文学渭生诗云：“媒氏新传玉帐音，定情何用百万金？帘前一见如相识，为插莲花玳瑁簪。”丘学士象升诗云：“昨日优昙带露开，簪花迤逦到丰台。湘帘一控春如海，万朵花光入座来。”其母兄与其母，疑予年大又贫，且相传妇妒，欲悔之；阿钱不然。陈序云：“原思入仕，仍然环堵之家；仲路居官，不离缊袍之色。况乎桓家郡主，性极矜严；吴国夫人，理多贵倨。王茂弘将膺九锡，时来悠谬之谈；刘孝标永憾三同，属有纷纭之论。而乃情坚一诺，面许三生。”《续长恨歌》云：“相国冯公重古风，为访名姝到韦曲。韦曲春花烂熳生，求婚三唱《踏莎行》。忽传妇妒几中止，官贫复恐离乡里。阿钱却喜嫁才人，委身情愿同生死。

    Explanation

    409

    The Matchmaker's Message and the Poetic Proposal

    The literary figure Wu Weisheng wrote:
    "The matchmaker brings a new message of marriage, but what is the use of a million gold coins to seal our love? At the curtain, our first meeting felt like a reunion, and she offered me a lotus flower adorned with a tortoise shell hairpin."

    The scholar Qiusheng wrote:
    "Yesterday, the rare and beautiful flower bloomed with dew, and we adorned ourselves with flowers and wound our way to Fengtai. The Xiang curtain, when drawn, reveals a spring as vast as the sea, and the light of a thousand flowers enters the room."

    Her brother and mother, doubting my age and poverty, and hearing the rumor of her jealousy, wanted to regret the match; but Ah Qian did not agree. Chen wrote:
    "Yuan Si entered public service and remained in a humble home surrounded by walls; Zilu held office and never left the coarse robe he wore. How much more so the princess of the Huan family, who is extremely strict and dignified; Lady Wu, who is haughty and proud. Wang Maohong was about to receive the highest honors, but there were idle and unfounded talks; Liu Xiaobiao forever lamented the three agreements, and there were various discussions. Yet, their love was firm in one promise, and they pledged three lifetimes together."

    The continuation of the Long Sorrow Song says:
    "Premier Feng values traditional virtues and sought a famous beauty in Weiqu. In Weiqu, the spring flowers bloom abundantly, and he proposed marriage three times, singing the poem Tasa Xing. Suddenly, a rumor of her jealousy almost stopped the match, and the fear of leaving the hometown due to poverty arose. But Ah Qian was happy to marry a talented man and willingly shared life and death with him."

    410

    ”刘文学锡旦诗云：“梦授一枝和露种，肯教连理被云遮？”及娶，检讨陈君就予饮，更名曼殊。曼殊者，佛花也。汪主事诗云：“昨宵梦乞杨枝露，从此更名号曼殊。”陈序云：“仆于阮妇之新婚，曾学刘祯之平视。屏前乍见，遽讶天人；烛下潜窥，已惊绝世。值此同官之被酒，屡为爱妾以征名。以姬夙悟静因，亲耽禅喜，遂傍稽夫梵夹，肇锡之以曼殊。”姜州丞启诗云：“曼陀花散到人间，色相端然菩萨鬘。”蔡修撰升《元月上纱窗夜乌啼》词云：“檀心蕙质玉亭亭，解语识迦陵。慈云一滴杨枝露，订三生，却向天花落处认前身。”《续长恨歌》云：“同官往往停驺御，欲拜青娥不能去。迦陵太史为征名，曼殊本在西来处。”

    Explanation

    410

    In the poem "Xidandan" by Liu Wenxue, it is said: "In a dream, I was granted a branch with dew to plant; would I let the harmonious union be obscured by clouds?" Upon marriage, the man of letters Chen invited me to drink with him, and we changed her name to Manshu. Manshu, meaning Buddha's flower, is a metaphor for spiritual purity or enlightenment. The official Wang wrote in his poem: "Last night in a dream, I begged for dew from a tree branch; from now on, I will be known as Manshu." Chen's preface states: "When I saw the newlywed wife of Yuan, I learned from Liu Zhen's level gaze. In front of the screen, I suddenly saw her and was immediately astonished by her divine beauty; under the candlelight, I secretly observed her and was already amazed by her extraordinary beauty. At this time, my colleagues were drunk, and they often sought a name for their beloved concubine. Because the lady had a deep understanding of peaceful causes and personally indulged in Buddhist joy, she consulted the Buddhist scriptures and was given the name Manshu." The governor of Jiang wrote in his poem: "Buddha's flowers scatter to the human world, and the appearance is proper like a Buddhist crown." Cai Shouzhuang wrote in his poem "On the Night of the First Month When the Nightingale Cries Through the Silk Window": "With the heart of sandalwood and the quality of a fragrant orchid, standing tall and graceful, understanding speech and recognizing the parrot. Benevolent clouds bestow a drop of dew from a tree branch, agreeing across three lives, but recognizing one's previous life in the place where heavenly flowers fall." In "Continuation of the Long Song of Sorrow," it is said: "Colleagues often stop their chariots, wanting to worship a young and beautiful woman but not being able to leave. The historian of parrots seeks a name for the parrot, and Buddha's flower originally comes from the west."

    411

    曼殊既归，执挚即贽愿从学。取书观，有悟；才把笔，即能画字。其字每类予，见者辄谓予假为之。任黄门辰旦传云：“检讨善诗文，能书晓音律。曼殊心习焉，辄似检讨。”方编修诗云：“夫子江东早擅名，学书学字尽聪明。”吴文学陈琰诗云：“学书不学卫夫人，别有簪花体格新。争怪拈毫似夫婿，燕钗作贽仿来真。”施侍读闰章诗云：“夫人才把笔，便作逸少字。如此好夫婿，何处不可似？”朱供奉《叶儿乐府》云：“檀板能歌绝妙词，银钩学写相思字。”尝为予书刺，早起呵冻，连作十余刺，心痛遽罢。

    Explanation

    411

    Manshu returned home, holding a gift and wishing to follow in learning. Taking a book to read, he had an insight; just picking up a brush, he immediately was able to write characters. His characters often resembled mine, and those who saw them always said I had written them for him. The official document from the Yellow Gate, transmitted by Chen Dan, says: "He is skilled in poetry and prose, able to write and understand music. Manshu has learned it in his heart and it resembles the censor's work." The poem by Fang Bianxiu says: "The master was already famous in the east of the river, learning to write and read with great intelligence." The poem by Wenxue Chen Yan from Wu says: "Learning to write without following Wei Fu, there is a new style of flower-like writing. It is strange and wonderful to pick up the brush like a husband, the hairpin as a gift is a true imitation." The poem by Shidu Renzhang says: "The lady just picks up the brush, and immediately writes like the style of Yi Shao. Such a good husband, how can it not be similar?" The poem by Zhu Gongfu from 'Yer' Music Bureau says: "The wooden board can sing the most wonderful words, the silver hook can write words of longing." He once wrote a letter for me, getting up early to blow on the cold, writing over ten letters, and suddenly stopping due to heart pain.

    412

    陈序云：“于是杂弄简编，间亲文吏，画眉楼畔，即是书林；傅粉房中，便成家垫。学新声于弦上，询难字于枕间。硬黄纸滑，窃书夫子之衔；缥碧钗轻，戏作门生之贽。”张检讨鸿烈诗云：“瞥见仙姝漫七年，每闻素腕写鸾笺。”潘检讨耒诗云：“学得簪花字体新，蛮笺十幅簇芳茵。修成外传多情思，为有灯前拥髻人。”予有《曼殊病》诗云：“黛碗谁书刺，银床想挈壶。曼陀花一朵，看向日边枯。”予生平好歌，至是酒后歌，每歌必请予复之，三复则已能矣。按刌度节，丝黍不得爽。尤喜歌真定夫子《祝家园》词。梁司农夫子《桂枝香》曲开句：“赏心乐事，祝家园里。

    Explanation

    412

    Tang Xu said, "Thus, amidst handling of books and scrolls, occasionally engaging with officials, by the side of Huamei Tower, it is a forest of books; in the room for applying makeup, it becomes a home base. Learning new tunes on the strings, inquiring about difficult characters in the pillow. Smooth hard yellow paper, stealing the title of a scholar; light blue hairpin, playfully creating a gift for a disciple." Zhang Jiantao Honglie's poem reads, "Glimpsing a celestial beauty for seven years, often hearing the white wrist writing on phoenix paper." Pan Jiantao Lei's poem reads, "Learning a new flower-like script, foreign paper clustered with fragrant grass. Completing an external record full of emotions, for there is a person holding a hairpin by the lamp." I have a poem titled "Man Shu's Illness" that reads, "Who wrote the note on the black bowl? The silver bed imagines carrying a pot. A Mandala flower, looking towards the sun's edge." I have always loved singing, and now after drinking, I sing, and each time I sing, I ask me to repeat it, and after three repetitions, I can already do it. Following the measure and timing, not even a thread or grain is out of place. I especially enjoy singing the words of the true teacher from Zhending, "Pleasant and enjoyable activities, in the garden of Zhu."

    413

    ”冯太傅夫子长歌云：“从来绣阁惜娉婷，红牙欲按声转停。闻君雅擅周郎顾，妾若歌时君细听。”《续长恨歌》云：“学书便仿簪花格，偷曲初成按拍时。”又云：“拙宦中年何草草，但看曼殊愁顿扫。酒阑一唱《祝家词》，温柔乡里真堪老。冰弦檀板两怡然，花底征歌月底眠。”田编修需诗云：“百绾云{髟火}巧样成，淡黄裙子称身轻。清歌按板偏能会，不数红红记豆名。”胡文学诗云：“新翻《子夜》与《前溪》，顾曲周郎总不迷。一唱黄鸡娇欲绝，萧声同彻凤楼西。”王光禄三杰诗云：“歌残《金缕》不胜悲，记得南园卧病时。

    Explanation

    413

    The son of Feng Taifu composed a long song, which goes: "From the beginning, the boudoir cherishes the beautiful and graceful; the red ivory clapper about to play, the sound pauses. I hear you excel at recognizing talent like Zhou Yu; when I sing, please listen carefully." The "Continuation of the Long Resentment Song" says: "Learning calligraphy, I imitate the style of flowers on a hairpin; secretly learning a tune, just completed setting the rhythm." It also says: "A mediocre official career in middle age, how haphazardly; just look, the sudden dispelling of sorrow. When the wine is finished, singing once 'The Song of the Wishing Family,' a place of comfort and ease, truly suitable for growing old. Both the cold string and wooden clapper are content; singing under the flowers, sleeping under the moon." Tian Bianxiu composed a poem, which goes: "Many twists and turns, a skillfully formed hairstyle; a light yellow skirt fits the body perfectly. Clear singing and setting the rhythm, especially able to understand; not counted among the trivial matters of remembering the names of beans." Hu Wenxue composed a poem, which goes: "Newly composed 'The Midnight Song' and 'The Qianxi Song'; appreciating music, never gets lost. Singing once, the extremely charming yellow chicken; the sound of the flute echoes together west of the phoenix pavilion." Wang Guangluo Sanjie composed a poem, which goes: "The song ends 'The Golden Thread,' cannot bear the sadness; remember the south garden when ill."

    414

    夜起与郎花下坐，含颦一唱《祝家词》。”曼殊自为诗云：“阶草衔虚槛，亭榴接断垣。酒阑携锦瑟，请唱《祝家园》。”第苦无弹者，不可已，呼盲女街前琵琶，听数曲，谛视其拢撚{百刂}拨，遂能弹。朱供奉《洞庭秋色》词云：“想暗通心曲，朱丝弦里；尽携书卷，玉镜台前。”尤检讨侗《新样四时花》曲云：“罗敷赵瑟侬家占，《子夜》吴歌近日谙。”袁编修佑诗云：“郎自艳吴曲，侬自缓秦筝。双栖梁上燕，解语弄春声。”冯检讨勖诗云：“细抛红豆谱相思，肠断金槽一缕丝。谁道梁尘惊散后，酒阑犹唱《祝家词》。”吴别驾融诗云：“渌水春来艳，金槽夜自弹，市楼盲女在，莫作段师看。”

    Explanation

    414

    At night, rising to sit under the flowers with the young man, she frowns slightly and sings "The Song of the Zhu Family." Man Shu composed a poem saying, "The steps are lined with grass that seems to embrace the empty railing, and the pavilion is adorned with pomegranates that connect to the broken wall. At the end of the drinking party, carrying a lute, I request the singing of 'The Garden of the Zhu Family.'" However, there was no one to play it, so they could not stop. They called a blind girl from the street to play the pipa, and listened to several songs. Observing her techniques of plucking and strumming, they learned to play. Zhu, the Imperial Tutor, composed a poem about the autumn scenery of Dongting Lake: "I think the music conveys hidden emotions within the red silk strings; carrying all books, in front of the jade mirror stand." You, the Imperial Advisor, composed a song about the four seasons' flowers: "The lady Luo Fu claims the Zhaosu lute, and I recently learned the Wu songs of the night." Yuan, the Chief Editor, composed a poem: "The young man admires the Wu tunes, and I slowly play the Qin zither. Two swallows nest on the beam, understanding words and playing spring sounds." Feng, the Imperial Advisor, composed a poem: "Carefully throwing red beans to compose a love song, heartbroken by the golden slot and a thread of silk. Who says after the dust in the hall has been startled and scattered, at the end of the drinking party, still singing 'The Song of the Zhu Family.'" Wu, the Deputy Governor, composed a poem: "The green water comes in spring and is beautiful, the golden slot plays itself at night. The blind girl in the market building is there, do not look at her as a teacher."

    415

    顾得奇病，初书刺心痛，谓脘寒也。既谓伤肝，输东风，木扬，春作而秋止。又既谓中懣，有瘕癖，在胃傍，气积不行。历数载，审候，终不得其要领。每疾作，遍体若煿，使婢按摩之；不足，以帔作兜，负之行；又不足，缒筐而坐之，东西推挽，若鞦韆然。任黄门传云：“然有奇疾。疾剧，则必约綵为兜，有若花篮，坐其中，悬诸空际，左旋右转，乃少可。特终不可治。尝遍搜方术，不治，遂立愿舍身作佛弟子。不治，乃召绘者图之，名曰《留视图》云。已而竟不可治。

    Explanation

    415

    A Man with a Strange Illness

    He conveniently acquired a strange illness. Initially, it was recorded as piercing heart pain, called stomach cold. Later, it was called liver injury, transferring the east wind, wood rises, spring onset and autumn cessation. Again, it was called central depression, having a mass, near the stomach, qi accumulation not moving. For several years, he carefully observed, but ultimately could not grasp the essentials. Whenever the disease occurs, his entire body feels as if roasted, and he has the maid massage it; if not enough, he uses a shawl as a hammock, carries it while walking; if still not enough, he hangs a basket and sits in it, pushes and pulls east and west, like a swing. According to the official historian's records: "Indeed has a strange illness. When the disease is severe, they must tie colorful fabric into a hammock, looks like a flower basket, sit inside it, hang it in mid-air, rotate left and right, then slightly better. But ultimately cannot be cured. Have tried all medical methods, not cured, therefore vowed to give up their body and become a Buddhist disciple. Not cured, then summoned a painter to depict it, named it The Picture of Lingering Gaze. Later, it still could not be cured."

    416

    ”陆文学宏定诗云：“病倚篮舆挹翠霞，后庭编径曲栏斜。彩兜行遍虽无迹，犹长金莲处处花。”尝梦邻庙奶奶唤归去，一日携儿至，曰：“汝本吾家物，我挤眼，汝当随我行。”其儿曰：“家去吧！不去，奶奶么喝。”醒乃刻桃木为偶人，饰之衣，被以生平所梳百环髻，流涕送庙间。赵编修执信诗云：“淡红香白好容颜，宝髻堆云作百鬟。唤作佛花元自误，如今争肯住人间？”吴文学陈琰诗云：“阿钱生小态婵娟，多病皈依绣佛前。不信曼陀花一朵，忍教憔悴夕阳天。”又云：“妖梦频随阿母回，香檀分影礼莲台。

    Explanation

    416

    Lu Wenzue Hongding's poem reads: "Sickly leaning on a sedan chair to savor the green clouds, the winding path in the back courtyard with curved railings. Wearing colorful headscarves, she has walked everywhere without leaving a trace, still growing lotus flowers everywhere." Once, she dreamed that the temple lady from her neighborhood called her to return home. One day, she took her child and said, "You originally belong to my family; when I wink, you should follow me." Her child replied, "Go home! If you don't, the old lady will scold you." Upon waking, she carved a wooden doll from peach wood, dressed it in clothes, and adorned it with the hundred-bun hairstyle she had always worn, crying as she sent it to the temple. Zhao Bianxiu Zhi Xin's poem says: "A good face with light red and fragrant white, a precious hair bun piled up like clouds into a hundred buns. Calling her a Buddha flower was originally a mistake; now, who would want to stay in the human world?" Wu Wenxue Chen Yan's poem reads: "Little Ah Qian has a graceful and charming appearance, sickly, she has taken refuge before the embroidered Buddha. Doubting the existence of a single lotus flower, how can one bear to see her withered under the setting sun?" He also wrote: "Frequent demonic dreams following her back to her mother, dividing the shadow of fragrant sandalwood to worship the lotus platform."

    417

    百鬟巧髻亲留视，画里真真唤不来。”沈文学季友诗云：“雕香分送泪模糊，六尺生绡便作图。认取白衣龛外立，前身应是小龙姑。”予《送偶人》诗云：“且送青娥去，言随阿母归。荷花开作面，菊叶剪为衣。泪尽中途别，魂离何处依？他时香案下，相待莫相违。”曼殊自为诗云：“百计延医病转深，暂回阿母案傍身。此身久已魂离壳，莫道含颦又一人。”乃复图其形，名《留视图》，而题诗焉。梁司农夫子诗云：“百朵云光绾髻斜，焚香小坐澹铅华。画图展向春风里，好护丰台第一花。

    Explanation

    417

    A woman with her intricately braided hair, I carefully observe her, but the one in the painting cannot be summoned. Shen Wenxue Jiyou’s poem reads: "Sending incense with tears blurred, on a six-foot piece of silk I create a painting. Recognize the figure standing outside the white-robed shrine, her previous incarnation should be a young dragon maiden." My poem, "Sending the Green Lady Away," reads: "Let her go, saying she will follow her mother back. Her face resembles a lotus flower, her clothes cut from chrysanthemum leaves. Tears run dry halfway through the journey, where does the soul find rest after separation? In the future, at the altar, await each other without deviating." Man Shu’s own poem reads: "Using every means to seek medical treatment, but the illness only deepens, temporarily returning to the side of my mother. This body has long been separated from the soul, do not say that another person is frowning." Thus, I again painted her image, naming it "The Retaining Gaze," and inscribed a poem. Liang Sinaong’s poem reads: "A hundred clouds of light braid her hair askew, incense burning as she sits quietly, her makeup pale. Unfolding the painting in the spring breeze, protect the first flower of Fengtai.

    418

    ”任黄门诗云：“舍身现在礼慈云，月月纤腰减半分。回事画工还染色，淡红衣褶藕丝坟。”沈明府皞日诗云：“弹窝石畔冷如冰，消得春风数尺绫。一自檀雕分影去，夜深只坐佛前灯。”阮庶常尔询诗云：“新镂香檀旧梦频，碧绡留供佛前身。由来仙骨原无二，不信双毫写五人。”汪春坊诗云：“宝篆依微绣佛前，香台倚坐髻鬟偏。梦魂缥缈知何处，只在莲花秋水边。”高征士兆诗云：“百结云鬟委陌尘，一函玉骨瘗江滨。可怜遗落春风影，挂向花前还妒人。”郑骠骑勋诗云：“细雨难滋天上花，春光杳渺白云赊。可怜粉黛空留视，肠断当时油壁车。”

    Explanation

    418

    Ren Huangmen's poem says:
    "Offering my body in the present to the benevolent cloud,
    Each month my slender waist reduces by half a measure.
    Returning to the painter to re-dye the color,
    Light red garment with lotus silk creases."

    Shen Mingfu's poem by Hao Ri says:
    "By the stone beside the nest, it is cold like ice,
    Dissolving into the spring breeze, several feet of silk.
    Since the sandalwood carving split its shadow,
    Deep into the night, only sitting before the Buddha's lamp."

    Ruan Shuchang's poem by Erxun says:
    "Newly engraved fragrant sandalwood, old dreams recur,
    Blue silk left to serve the Buddha's former body.
    Since ancient times, the bones of immortals are originally the same,
    Not believing that two brushes can write five people."

    Wang Chunfang's poem says:
    "Precious seals faintly embroidered before the Buddha,
    Fragrant platform, leaning to sit, the bun slightly tilted.
    The dream spirit is ethereal, knowing where it is,
    Only at the edge of the lotus and autumn water."

    Gao Zhengshi's poem by Zhao says:
    "Hundreds of knots in the cloud bun, fallen into the dust of the path,
    A letter of jade bones buried by the riverbank.
    Pitifully, the shadow of spring wind is lost,
    Hanging before the flowers, still envying people."

    Zheng Biaoqi's poem by Xun says:
    "Fine rain is hard to nourish the flowers in the sky,
    Spring light is dim and distant, white clouds are far away.
    Pitifully, the powder and rouge are left to gaze,
    Heartbroken at the time of the oil-walled carriage."

    419

    初，予妇将至，徙居南西门坟园，虑不容也。益都夫子怜其穷，强予开阁，而曼殊难之。其后有假予意逼遣之者，曼殊死复活。曼殊《回生记》云：“曼殊以壬戌十月十一日死，越三日，高邮葛先生治之，复苏。”李检讨《曼殊》诗云：“食贫二三载，两情如斯须。何意南来者，事变出不虞！举家色惨悽，丞相谓曼殊：毛郎生迟暮，官贫徒区区。改图便尔为，作计莫太迂。曼殊一无语，泪落红罗襦。”又云：“始至相逼迫，既乃复揶揄。郎意久异同，计事一何愚？曼殊大悲摧，天乎我何辜？郎今负义信，恸哭声呜呜。气结肠欲断，死生在须臾。仓皇觅良医，强起事跏趺。药饵徐徐下，数日魂始苏。

    Explanation

    419

    At the beginning, when my wife was about to arrive, we moved to a cemetery near the South West Gate, worried that there would be no room. A scholar from Yidu, moved by my poverty, compelled me to open a room for us, but Manchú resisted. Later, someone pretending to act according to my intentions forced me to send him away, and Manchú died and came back to life. In his "Huisheng Ji" (Record of Resurrection), Manchú wrote: "Manchú died on the eleventh day of the tenth month in the year Ren Xu, and after three days, Mr. Ge from Gaoyou treated him, and he revived." In his poem "Manchú," Li Jiantao wrote: "Living in poverty for two or three years, our feelings were as close as this moment. What did you mean by coming from the south? Unexpected events happened! The whole family looked sad and gloomy. The chancellor said to Manchú: 'Mr. Mao lived a long life, but he was just a poor official.' Change your plans and do it this way; don't be too impractical. Manchú said nothing, and tears fell on his red silk robe." He continued: "At first, you forced me, then you mocked me. Your intentions have been different for a long time, how foolishly you plan things! Manchú was greatly grieved, why should I suffer so? You now betrayed trust, cried loudly and sorrowfully. My breath stopped, my heart was broken, life and death were in an instant. In a hurry, I searched for a good doctor, forced to get up and sit in a meditative posture. The medicine was slowly administered, and after several days, my soul began to revive."

    420

    ”李中允诗云：“踟蹰贮别馆，咫尺明河悬，脉脉但相望，郎言遂浪传。谓当羽翼乖，听续鸳鸯弦。闻言一悲愤，气绝如丝联。已乃泣吞声，仰首呼苍天。”《续长恨歌》云：“食贫三岁恩情重，恩情只道长相出，桓家郡主蓦地来，惊散鸳鸯夜深梦。深情无赖金门客，愁煞飘风荡魂魄。仓卒坟园贮阿娇，将使犊车无处觅。那料流光迅如电，好信不来飞语遍。野花村落白杨郊，安得仙郎日相见？含情一恸倒玉山，杳杳冥冥去世间。葛翁投药虽扶起，那得桃花还结子？画图试展旧时容，玉貌花姿全不是。”孟监州远记云：“其初归也，则不以迟暮为非匹，而唯以得偶乎才子为幸。其濒危也，群言纷构，犹矢若金石，唯愿得死于才子之手。

    Explanation

    420

    李中允的诗写道：“我在别馆中徘徊，离你只有咫尺之遥，望着那悬挂在空中的明河，心中充满了脉脉的相思，听闻你的言语，便开始四处传播。我以为我们的关系会像羽翼般分离，却希望你能继续演奏鸳鸯的琴弦。听到这些话后，我感到一阵悲愤，仿佛气息已断，只剩下一丝微弱的联系。于是，我默默地流泪，仰头呼喊苍天。”《续长恨歌》中写道：“我们共同经历了三年的贫困，恩情因此更加深厚，我以为我们的感情会永远持续下去，然而桓家郡主突然到来，惊醒了我们深夜的美梦。深情的金门客虽无赖，却让飘风荡魂魄，令人愁苦。仓促之间，阿娇被安葬在坟园中，犊车无处可寻。谁曾料到时光如电般飞逝，好消息不来，谣言却四处流传。野花在村落中盛开，白杨树在郊外挺立，我怎能与仙郎每日相见？满怀深情的一声恸哭，使我倒下如玉山般，悄然消失在杳杳冥冥的世间。葛翁虽投药让我恢复，但桃花怎能再次结子？展开旧时的画像，玉貌花姿已全然不同。”孟监州的远记中写道：“她初归时，并不以迟暮为非，而唯愿与才子相配。当她濒临危险时，众说纷纭，但她依然坚定如金石，只愿死在才子的手中。”

    421

    ”彭侍讲孙遹诗云：“优钵从来不染尘，无端号作断肠春。凭谁地下三弹指，唤起迦文坐畔人。”张文学闇然诗云：“曾说南园卧病时，金槽犹拨《祝家词》。新声不向丰台度，付与啼莺恋旧枝。”曹学士禾诗云：“芍药初开骤委泥，丰台犹见草萋萋。甘心远葬西施里，苦恋贫官与忌妻。”杨文学卧《续张夫人拜新月》词云：“拜新月，拜月在前墀。死魂回生后，残眉未扫时。”至是病转剧，尝曰：“令吾小可者，吾当为尼忏除之。”李中允诗云：“古今伤心人，慷慨以永叹。庶几法王力，遣此长恨端。灼灼青莲花，阿母梦所搴。因之绮罗中，爱参清静禅。”《续长恨歌》云：“从此香奁日日扃，长斋顶礼愿难成。彩兜虚约香尘满，伏枕空房小胆惊。

    Explanation

    421

    The Scholar Pei Sun Yu's poem reads: "The blue lotus has never been tainted by dust, yet it is called 'the spring that breaks the heart' without reason. Who can make three finger snaps in the underworld to summon those sitting beside the Buddha?" The Literary Figure Zhang Anran's poem reads: "Once said when I was ill in the southern garden, still playing the Zhujia Ci on a gold-lined slot. New sounds are not passed through Fengtai, given to the crying oriole that laments its old branch." The Scholar Cao He's poem reads: "Peonies just blooming suddenly fall into mud, Fengtai still sees lush grass. Willingly buried far away in the place of Xi Shi, deeply attached to a poor official and a jealous wife." The Literary Figure Yang Wo's poem titled "Continuation of Lady Zhang's Worship of the New Moon" reads: "Worship the new moon, worship the moon on the front steps. After the dead soul returns to life, before the torn eyebrows are combed." At this point, his illness worsened, and he once said, "If I could be a little better, I would become a nun to atone for my sins." The Official Li Zhongyun's poem reads: "Throughout history, those who grieve deeply, they sigh with deep emotion. Perhaps the power of the Dharma King can dispel this long-lasting regret. Bright and clear blue lotus, picked in the dream of the mother. Therefore in the midst of silk and brocade, love to participate in the pure and quiet Zen." The continuation of "Song of Eternal Sorrow" reads: "From now on, the incense box is closed every day, long fasting and worshiping vows are hard to fulfill. The colorful brocade pillow is empty, the room is filled with incense dust, and the small pillow is startled."

    422

    ”既而谓予曰：“向阿三病时，予从子阿三死京师，予借其园居，邀君日来以为幸。今君将南行，而予以病残留尼寺中，其能来乎？”泣曰：“他日君归者，吾请以尼随君行，唯君置之。”既而病发死。曼殊之死，京朝争作挽吊，自梁司农夫子，暨张、曹诸学士下，诗词文赋，不可胜纪。又有作鼓子词，同韵唱和成帙，如云间李秾、李榛、顾士元、马左，西泠何源长，魏里周珂，同郡成肇璋、达志、金振甲、马会嘉、王麟游、陶簠、刘义林诸君，至同馆生，有托碧虚仙史，作《盎中花》杂剧者，皆汇载别集，死时羸甚，及敛，面有生色，坐而衣，骨节缓泽如平时。任黄门诗云：“垂帘无力倚阑干，怕见庭花易早残，偏怪瓦棺将掩处，海棠犹作睡时看。”

    Explanation

    422

    Afterward, he said to me, "When Ah San was ill, my son Ah San died in the capital. I borrowed his garden house and invited you to come daily, considering it a favor. Now you are about to travel south, while I am left behind in a nunnery due to illness. Will you still be able to come?" I cried and said, "When you return one day, I will ask the nun to follow you. Please accept this." Soon after, he fell ill and died. Upon Man Shu's death, officials in the capital competed to write elegies, starting with Liang Sinaofu and including Zhang, Cao, and other scholars. Poems, verses, essays, and rhapsodies were too numerous to count. There were also drum poems composed in the same rhyme and compiled into volumes, such as those by individuals from Yun County like Li Rong, Li Zhen, Gu Shiyuan, and Ma Zuo, He Yuanzhang from Xiling, Zhou Ke from Weili, and fellow students from the same prefecture like Cheng Zhaozhang, Da Zhi, Jin Zhenjia, Ma Huijia, Wang Linyou, Tao Fu, and Liu Yilin. Some even entrusted the Immortal Historian of the Azure Void to create the play "Vase Flower," all of which were compiled in separate collections. At the time of his death, he was very weak. When he was dressed for burial, his face had a living color, and seated and dressed, his bones and joints were soft and lustrous as usual. The poem by Ren Huangmen says, "Leaning on the rail, unable to pull the curtain, afraid to see the garden flowers fade too soon, especially strange that the clay coffin will be covered, the crabapple still looks like it is sleeping."

    423

    初，陈检讨孺人死，索予为墓铭，而貽予以绢。绢浅黄色，为制裙而喜，嘱曰：“假使貽绢有桃晕红者，当复制一裙。”越四年，无有貽者；既敛，乃卖金槽，裁一裙纳柳棺中。《续长恨歌》云：“去路茫茫在何处？矫首空濛隔烟雾！金槽卖却剪红裙，大叫曼殊将不去。”高征士诗云：“罗裙浅澹剪鹅黄，一束纤腰白玉床。长恨无人十洲外，飞行为觅返魂香。”吴文学诗云：“灭尽纤腰胜小蛮，淡黄裙子带围宽。可怜红绢空裁剪，不付金箱付玉棺。”

    Explanation

    423

    At first, when the lady, who held the title of Tínjǐntǎo, passed away, she requested me to write a tombstone inscription for her and gave me some silk. The silk was a light yellow color, and I made a dress from it, feeling delighted. She then said, "If I were given silk with a peach-blossom blush color, I would make another dress." After four years, no one gave me any silk. Upon her burial, I sold a gold basin and made a dress, placing it in the willow coffin.

    In the continuation of the "Song of Eternal Sorrow," it is written: "The path ahead is vast and unknown. Lifting the head to gaze into the misty haze! Selling the gold basin to make a red dress, shouting loudly that Maitreya (a bodhisattva) would not come." Gao Zhengshi's poem says: "A light yellow silk dress cut like goose feathers, a slender waist wrapped around a white jade bed. Longing for someone beyond the Ten Isles, flying to seek the elixir of life." Wu Wenxue's poem says: "The slender waist has vanished, surpassing Xiao Man (a famous dancer). A light yellow skirt with a wide waistband. Pitifully, the red silk was cut in vain, not placed in the gold box but in the jade coffin."

    424

    [张山来曰：予亦复有长恨，间为诗五十首，名《清泪痕》，同人皆有赠挽诗歌。今读此，不觉触予旧恨也。]
    　　补张灵、崔莹合传　锺山黄周星九烟夏为堂别集
    　　　　余少时阅唐解元《六如集》，有云：“六如尝与祝枝山、张梦晋，大雪中效乞儿唱《莲花》，得钱沽酒，痛饮野寺中，曰：『此乐惜不令太白见之！』”心窃异焉，然不知梦晋为何许人也。顷阅稗乘中，有一编曰《十美图》，乃详载张梦晋、崔素琼事，不觉惊喜叫跳，已而潸然雨泣。此真古今来才子佳人之佚事也，不可以不传，遂为之传。

    Explanation

    424

    Translation:

    Zhang Shanlai said: "I also have a deep regret, and occasionally wrote fifty poems, titled Clear Tears. My friends all wrote poems to commemorate me. Now reading this, I cannot help but be touched by my old regret."

    Supplementing the Joint Biography of Zhang Ling and Cui Ying
    Zhongshan Huang Zhouxing Jiuyan Xia Wei Tang Special Collection

    When I was young, I read Tang Jieyuan's Liu Ru Collection, which states: "Liu Ru once accompanied Zhu Zhishan and Zhang Mengjin, singing Lotus like beggars in the snow, collected money to buy wine, and drank heartily in a wild temple, saying, 'This joy is a pity that Du Fu did not see it!'" I secretly found it extraordinary, but I did not know who Zhang Mengjin was. Recently, I read in a collection of miscellaneous notes, and there is a volume called Ten Beauties, which details the story of Zhang Mengjin and Cui Suqiong. I could not help but jump with joy and cry tears of emotion. This is truly a hidden story of talented men and beautiful women in ancient and modern times, and it cannot be left untransmitted. So I wrote a biography for them.

    425

    张梦晋，名灵，盖正德时吴县人也。生而姿容俊弈，才调无双，工诗善画，性风流豪放，不可一世。家故赤贫，而灵独蚤慧。当舞勺时，父命灵出应童子试，辄以冠军补弟子员。灵心顾不乐，以为才人何苦为章缝束缚，遂绝意不欲复应试。日纵酒高吟，不肯妄交人，人亦不敢轻交与。唯与唐解元六如作忘年友。灵既年长，不娶。六如试叩之，灵笑曰：“君岂有中意人，足当吾耦者耶？”六如曰：“无之，但自古才子宜配佳人，吾聊以此探君耳。”灵曰：“固然，今岂有其人哉？求之数千年中，可当才子佳人者，唯李太白与崔莺莺耳！吾虽不才，然自谪仙而外，似不敢多让。若双文，惜下嫁郑恒，正未知果识张君瑞否。”六如曰：“谨受教。吾自今请为君访之。期得双文以报命，可乎？”遂大笑别去。

    Explanation

    425

    Zhang Mengjin, also known as Zhang Ling, was a person from Wuxian during the Zhengde era. Born with a handsome and elegant appearance, he possessed unparalleled talent and temperament, excelling in poetry and painting. His nature was free and unrestrained, and he considered himself superior to all others. Although his family was originally very poor, Zhang Ling was exceptionally intelligent from a young age. During the dance of the ladle ceremony, his father sent him to take the examination for children, and he won the championship and was admitted as a student. However, Zhang Ling was not happy about it, thinking that a talented person should not be bound by the constraints of formal attire and examinations. Thus, he decided not to take the exam again. He spent his days drinking wine and reciting poetry, unwilling to associate with people indiscriminately, and others were also hesitant to approach him. He had only one close friend, Tang Jiyuan, also known as Liu Ru, who was much older than him. As Zhang Ling grew older, he did not marry. Tang Jiyuan once asked him, "Do you have someone in mind who could be your match?" Zhang Ling laughed and replied, "Do you have anyone in mind who could be a suitable match for me?" Tang Jiyuan answered, "No, but traditionally, a talented scholar should be matched with a beautiful woman. I just wanted to test your thoughts on this matter." Zhang Ling said, "Of course, but who is there today who can match the ideal of a talented scholar and a beautiful woman? If we look back through history, the only ones who fit this description are Li Bai and Cui Yingying! Although I may not be as talented as Li Bai, I dare not claim to be much less so. As for Cui Yingying, she unfortunately married Zheng Heng, and I wonder if she truly recognized Zhang Junrui." Tang Jiyuan said, "I respectfully accept your teaching. From now on, I will search for her for you. I hope to find a Cui Yingying and report back. Is that acceptable?" They then laughed and parted ways.

    426

    一日，灵独坐读《刘伶传》，命童子进酒，屡读屡叫绝，辄拍案浮一大白。久之，童子跽进曰：“酒罄矣！今日唐解元与祝京兆讌集虎丘，公何不挟此编一往索醉耶？”灵大喜，即行，然不欲为不速客，乃屏弃衣冠，科跣双髻，衣鹑结，左持《刘伶传》，右持木杖，讴吟道情词，行乞而前。抵虎丘，见贵游蚁聚，绮席喧阗。灵每过一处，辄执书向客曰：“刘伶告饮。”客见其美丈夫，不类丐者，竞以酒馔贻之。有数贾人，方酌酒赋诗，灵至前，请属和，贾人笑之。其诗中有“苍官”、“青十”、“扑握”、“伊尼”四事，因指以问灵。灵曰：“松、竹、兔、鹿，谁不知耶？”贾人始骇，令赓诗，灵即立挥百绝而去。

    Explanation

    426

    One day, Ling sat alone reading the Biography of Liu Ling, and ordered the servant boy to bring wine. He repeatedly read and repeatedly exclaimed in admiration, clapping the table and drinking a large cup of wine in celebration. After a long time, the servant boy knelt and said, "The wine is finished! Today, Tang Jieyuan and Zhu Jingzhao are holding a banquet at Huchou. Why don't you take this book and go to seek drunkenness there?" Ling was very happy and immediately went, but he did not want to be an uninvited guest. So he discarded his clothes and shoes, tied his hair into two buns, and wore tattered clothes. Holding the Biography of Liu Ling in his left hand and a wooden staff in his right, he sang and recited folk songs, walking forward as a beggar. When he arrived at Huchou, he saw the nobles gathered like ants, with luxurious tables and loud noise. Ling would pass by each place and hold the book toward the guests, saying, "Liu Ling asks for wine." The guests saw that he was a handsome man, not like a beggar, and competed to give him wine and food. Several merchants were just drinking wine and composing poetry, and Ling approached and asked to join in. The merchants laughed at him. His poem contained four things: "green officials," "blue ten," "punching and grasping," and "I and Ni." They pointed to these and asked Ling about them. Ling said, "Pine, bamboo, rabbit, and deer—who doesn't know them?" The merchants were startled and ordered him to continue the poem. Ling immediately composed a hundred verses and left.

    427

    遥见六如及祝京兆枝山数辈，共集可中亭，亦趋前执书告饮。六如早已知为灵，见其佯狂游戏，戒座客阳为不识者以观之。语灵曰：“尔丐子持书行乞，想能赋诗。试题《悟石轩》一绝句，如佳，即賜尔卮酒，否则当叩尔胫。”灵曰：“易耳！”童子遂进毫楮。灵即书云：“胜迹天成说虎丘，可中亭畔足酣游。吟诗岂让生公法，顽石如何不点头？”遂并毫楮掷地曰：“佳哉！掷地金声也！”六如览之，大笑，因呼与共饮。时观者如堵，莫不相顾惊怪。灵既醉，即拂衣起，仍执书向悟石轩长揖曰：“刘伶谢饮。”遂不别座客径去。六如谓枝山曰：“今日我辈此举，不减晋人风流。宜写一帧，为《张灵行乞图》，吾任绘事而公题跋之，亦千秋佳话也。”即舐笔伸纸，俄顷图成。枝山题数语其后，座客争传玩叹赏。

    Explanation

    427

    Far away, I saw Tang Yin and Zhu Yunming of Jingzhao, along with several others, gathered at the Kezhong Pavilion. I also approached them, holding a book and requesting a drink. Tang Yin had already known that the person was a spirit, seeing his playful and seemingly mad behavior. He instructed the guests to pretend not to recognize him so they could observe him. He said to the spirit, "You, a beggar, are holding a book and asking for a drink. I assume you can write poetry. Write a quatrain titled Wushi Hall. If it is good, I will give you a cup of wine; otherwise, I will tap your shin." The spirit replied, "It’s easy!" The boy then brought out paper and brush. The spirit immediately wrote:

    "The natural wonders of Tiger Hill are renowned, and the Kezhong Pavilion is a place for indulgent play.
    Reciting poetry, I dare not fall short of Master Sheng’s teachings. How can the stubborn stone remain unmoved?"

    He then threw the paper and brush to the ground and said, "How wonderful! It’s like gold when thrown to the ground!" Tang Yin read it and burst into laughter, calling out for the spirit to join them in drinking. At that time, the crowd was so dense that no one could move, and all were surprised and amazed. The spirit, having drunk his fill, rose, brushed off his clothes, and still holding the book, bowed deeply toward Wushi Hall and said, "I, Liu Ling, decline the drink." He then left without bidding farewell to the guests. Tang Yin turned to Zhu Yunming and said, "Today, our actions are no less than the refined and unrestrained behavior of the literati of the Jin dynasty. We should paint a picture of this scene, calling it The Begging of Zhang Ling. I will handle the painting, and you can write the inscription. It will be a story remembered for generations." He then dipped his brush and spread out the paper. In a moment, the painting was completed. Zhu Yunming wrote a few lines after it, and the guests eagerly passed it around, admiring and praising it.

    428

    忽一翁缟衣素冠，前揖曰：“二公即唐解元、祝京兆耶？仆企慕有年，何幸识韩！”六如逊谢，徐叩之，则南昌明经崔文博，以海虞广文告归者也。翁得图谛观，不忍释手，因讯适行乞者为谁。六如曰：“敝里才子张灵也。”翁曰：“诚然，此固非真才子不能。”即向六如乞此图归。将返舟，见舟已移泊他所，呼之始至。盖翁有女素琼者，名莹，才貌俱绝世，以新丧母，随翁扶榇归。先舣舟岸侧时，闻人声喧沸，乍启槛窥之，则见一丐者，状貌殊不俗。丐者亦熟视槛中，忽登舟长跪，自陈“张灵求见”，屡遣不去。良久，有一童子入舟，强挽之，始去。故莹命移舟避之。崔翁乃出图示莹，且备述其故。莹始知行乞者为张灵，叹曰：“此乃真风流才子也！”取图藏笥中。翁拟以明日往谒唐、祝二君，因访灵，忽抱疴，数日不起，为榜人所促，遽返豫章。

    Explanation

    428

    Suddenly, an old man in a white robe and plain cap came forward and bowed, saying, "Are you the two gentlemen, Tang Jieyuan and Zhu Jingzhao? I have admired you for many years; how fortunate to meet you!" Six Ru modestly thanked him and gently inquired, revealing that the man was Cui Wenbo, a scholar from Haishu who had returned home after serving as a teacher. The old man carefully examined the painting and could not let go of it. He then asked who the beggar he had just seen was. Six Ru replied, "He is Zhang Ling, a talented scholar from my village." The old man said, "Indeed, only a true scholar could produce such a work." He then asked Six Ru to give him the painting. As they were about to return to the boat, they found it had moved to another location. They called out, and the boatman finally arrived. It turned out that the old man had a daughter named Suqiong, also known as Ying, who was exceptionally beautiful and talented. She had recently lost her mother and was accompanying her father back home with the coffin. When they first moored the boat near the shore, they heard loud noises and, upon peeking through the cabin, saw a beggar with an extraordinary appearance. The beggar also stared intently at the cabin, then climbed onto the boat and knelt for a long time, declaring, "I am Zhang Ling, seeking an audience." Despite repeated attempts to send him away, he did not leave. After some time, a young boy entered the boat and forcibly pulled him away. Thus, Ying ordered the boat to move away to avoid him. Cui Wenbo then showed the painting to Ying and explained the circumstances. Ying realized that the beggar was Zhang Ling and exclaimed, "He is indeed a true talented and charming scholar!" She took the painting and placed it in her chest. The old man planned to visit the two gentlemen of Tang and Zhu the next day and also intended to see Zhang Ling. However, he suddenly fell ill and remained bedridden for several days. Urged by the boatman, he hurriedly returned to Yuzhang.

    429

    灵既于舟次见莹，以为绝代佳人，世难再得，遂日走虎丘侦之，久之杳然。属靳人方志来校士，志既深恶古文词，而又闻灵跅弛不羁，竟褫其诸生。灵闻乃大喜曰：“吾正苦章缝束缚，今幸免矣！顾一褫何虑再褫？且彼能褫吾诸生之名，亦能褫吾才子之名乎？”遂往过六如家，见车骑填门，胥尉盈座，则江右宁藩宸濠遣使来迎者也。六如拟赴其招。灵曰：“甚善！吾正有厚望于君。吾曩者虎丘所遇之佳人，即豫章人也，乞君为我多方访之，冀得当以报我。此开天辟地第一吃紧事也，幸无忽忘！”六如曰：“诺。”即偕藩使过豫章。

    Explanation

    429

    Ling, having met Ying at a boat stop, considered her a woman of unparalleled beauty, hard to find again in the world. Thus, he went to Tiger Hill every day to look for her, but after a long time, she disappeared without a trace. Coincidentally, Jìn Rén Fang Zhi came to examine scholars, and Fang Zhi, who already deeply detested ancient prose, also heard that Ling was reckless and unrestrained. Ultimately, he deprived Ling of his status as a scholar. Upon hearing this, Ling was greatly delighted and said, "I was just suffering from the restraint of formal attire; now I am fortunately free from it! But if I am stripped once, why worry about being stripped again? Moreover, if he can strip my status as a scholar, can he also strip my status as a talented person?" He then went to visit Liu Sixu's home, where he saw carriages and horses filling the gate, and officials filling the seats. These were envoys sent by Chen Háo of the Ning藩 to welcome. Liu Sixu planned to go to the invitation. Ling said, "Very good! I have high hopes for you. The beautiful woman I met at Tiger Hill in the past is from Yuzhang. I ask you to find her in various ways, hoping to succeed and reward me. This is the most urgent matter since the beginning of the world; please do not forget!" Liu Sixu replied, "Yes." Then he went to Yuzhang with the envoys.

    430

    时宸濠久蓄异谋，其招致六如，一博好贤虚誉，一慕六如诗画兼长，欲倩其作《十美图》，献之九重。其时宫中已觅得九人，尚虚其一。六如请先写之，遂为写九美，而各缀七绝一章于后。九美者，广陵汤之谒字雨君，善画、姑苏木桂文舟，善琴、嘉禾朱家淑文孺，善书、金陵钱韶凤生，善歌、江陵熊御小冯，善舞、荆溪杜若芳洲，善筝、洛阳花萼未芳，善笙、钱唐柳春阳絮才，善瑟、公安薛幼端端清，善箫也。图咏既成，进之濠。濠大悦，乃盛设特宴六如，而别一殿僚季生副之。季生者，憸人也。酒次，请观《九美图》，因进曰：“十美欠一，殊属缺陷，某愿举一人以充其数。诘朝请持图来献。”比持图以献，即崔莹也。

    Explanation

    430

    At that time, Chen Heng had long harbored sinister plans. He invited Liu Sixie, partly to gain fame and reputation by being seen as virtuous, and partly because he admired Liu Sixie's skill in both poetry and painting, and wanted him to create a set of ten paintings, which he intended to present to the imperial court. Chen Heng had already found nine women, but one was still missing. Liu Sixie asked to be the first to paint her, so he painted the nine beauties, each accompanied by a quatrain poem. The nine beauties were: Tang Zhiyue from Guangling, skilled in painting; Mu Guiwen from Suzhou, skilled in playing the qin; Zhu Jia Shuwen from Jiaxing, skilled in calligraphy; Qian Shao Fengsheng from Jinling, skilled in singing; Xiong Yu Xiaofeng from Jiangling, skilled in dancing; Du Ruo Fangzhou from Jingxi, skilled in playing the zheng; Hua E Weifang from Luoyang, skilled in playing the sheng; Liu Chunyang from Qiantang, skilled in playing the se; and Xue Youduan from Gong'an, skilled in playing the flute. After the paintings and poems were completed, they were presented to Chen Heng. Chen Heng was greatly pleased and held a special banquet for Liu Sixie, with another official named Ji Sheng assisting. Ji Sheng was a sly person. During the banquet, he asked to see the "Nine Beauties" painting and said, "Ten beauties are missing one, which is quite a flaw. I would like to recommend one person to fill the gap. Please bring the painting the next day." The next day, Cui Ying presented the painting, and it was Cui Ying.

    431

    濠见之曰：“此真国色矣！”即属季生往说之。先是崔翁家居时，莹才名噪甚，求姻者踵至。翁度非莹匹，悉拒不纳。既从虎丘得张灵，遂雅属意灵，不意疾作遽归。思复往吴中，托六如主其事。适季生旋里丧耦，熟闻莹名，预遣女画师潜绘其容，而求姻于翁。翁谋诸莹，莹固不许，于是季生衔之，因假手于濠以泄私忿。时濠威殊张甚，翁再三力辞，不得。莹窘激欲自裁，翁复多方护之。莹叹曰：“命也！已矣，夫复何言！”乃取笥中《行乞图》，自题诗其上云：“才子风流第一人，愿随行乞乐清贫。入宫只恐无红叶，临别题诗当会真。”举以授翁曰：“愿持此复张郎，俾知世间有情痴女子如崔素琼者，亦不虚其为一生才子也。”遂恸哭入宫。

    Explanation

    431

    Hao saw her and said, "This is truly the beauty of the state!" He immediately instructed Ji Sheng to go persuade her. Before this, when Cui翁 lived at home, Ying's talent and reputation were very famous, and those seeking marriage came in succession. The old man judged that she was not a match for Ying and refused them all. After obtaining Zhang Ling from Tiger Hill, he then became fond of Ling, but unfortunately, he fell ill and returned home suddenly. He thought of going back to Wu again and entrusted Liu Liuxu to handle the matter. Just as Ji Sheng returned home and lost his wife, he was familiar with Ying's name and sent a female painter ahead to secretly paint her image, then sought a marriage with the old man. The old man consulted Ying, who firmly refused. Thus, Ji Sheng harbored resentment and used Hao to vent his private anger. At the time, Hao's power was greatly expanded, and the old man repeatedly tried to decline but could not. Ying was distressed and wanted to commit suicide, and the old man then tried various ways to protect her. Ying sighed, "It is fate! It is over, what more can be said!" She then took the "Begging Figure" painting from her box, wrote a poem on it: "The first person of talent and charm, I wish to follow begging for the joy of simplicity. Entering the palace, I fear there will be no red leaf, at parting, I inscribe a poem to meet the truth." She handed it to the old man, saying, "I wish to hold this to return to Zhang Lang, so that he knows there is a passionate woman like Cui Suqiong in the world, so as not to let him be a lifelong talent in vain." She then wept bitterly and entered the palace.

    432

    濠得之喜甚，复倩六如图咏，以为“十美”之冠。而六如先已取季生所献者摹得一纸藏之。莹既知六如在宫中，乘间密致一缄，以述己意。六如得缄，乃大惊惋，始知此女即灵所托访者。今事既不谐，复为绘图进献，岂非千古罪人？将来何面见良友？因急诣崔翁，索得《行乞图》返宫，将相机维挽。不意“十美”已即日就道，六如悔恨无已。又见濠逆迹渐著，急欲辞归。苦为濠羁縻，乃发狂，号呼颠掷，溲秽狼籍。濠久之不能堪，仍遣使送归。杜门月余乃起。过张灵时，灵已颓然卧病矣。

    Explanation

    432

    Hao was very pleased when he obtained it and then asked Li Sixu to draw and compose a picture, considering it the best among the ten beauties. However, Li Sixu had already taken a copy of what Ji Sheng had offered and kept it. Ying, knowing that Li Sixu was in the palace, seized the opportunity to secretly send a letter, expressing her own intention. When Li Sixu received the letter, he was greatly surprised and regretful, realizing that this woman was the one Ling had entrusted to visit. Now that the matter had not worked out, he again painted and presented the picture, wondering if this was not a crime against all time? How could he face his good friends in the future? Therefore, he hurried to see Old Master Cui, obtained the Xing Qi Tu (Picture of Begging), and returned to the palace, intending to take advantage of the situation to rescue. Unexpectedly, the ten beauties had already departed on the same day, and Li Sixu was filled with regret and could not stop. He also saw Hao's increasingly evident treacherous actions and urgently wanted to resign and return home. Painfully detained by Hao, he went mad, shouting and throwing himself about, with urine and excrement scattered everywhere. Hao could no longer bear it for a long time and still sent an envoy to send him back. Li Sixu closed himself off for more than a month before getting up. When he passed by Zhang Ling, Zhang Ling was already lying sick and weak.

    433

    盖灵自别六如后，邑邑亡憀，日纵酒狂呼，或歌或哭。一日中秋，独走虎丘千人石畔，见优伶演剧。灵伫视良久，忽大叫曰：“尔等所演不佳，待吾演王子晋吹笙跨鹤。”遂控一童子于地，而跨其背，攫伶人笙吹之，命童子作鹤飞，捶之不起。童子怒，掀灵于地。灵起曰：“鹤不肯飞，吾今既不得天仙，唯当作水仙耳！”遂跃入剑池中。众急救之出，则面额俱损，且伤股，不能行。人送归其家。自此委顿枕席，日日在醉梦中。

    Explanation

    433

    After parting with Liu Li (六如), Ge Ling (盖灵) became depressed and unhappy. He indulged in alcohol daily, shouting wildly, sometimes singing and sometimes crying. One Mid-Autumn Festival, he walked alone to the Thousand People Stone area near Tiger Hill (虎丘千人石畔), where he saw actors performing a play. Ge Ling stood and watched for a long time, then suddenly shouted, "What you are performing is not good. Let me perform the story of Prince Zijun (王子晋) playing the sheng and riding a crane." He pulled a child to the ground, straddled his back, took the sheng from the actor, and played it. He ordered the child to pretend to be a flying crane, beating him until he couldn't move. The child was angry and threw Ge Ling to the ground. Ge Ling got up and said, "The crane won't fly. I can't become a heavenly immortal now. I'll just become a water immortal!"
