Zha was greatly pleased and ordered the servant to rekindle the fire and open a new jar of wine. He made an agreement with the beggar, saying, "You will drink from a bowl, and I will drink from a cup. We will stop only when this wine is finished." The beggar drank more than thirty bowls without showing any signs of intoxication, while Zha Xiaolian lay down on his trundle bed, exhausted. The servant helped him back inside, and the beggar hesitated and left, still sleeping under the eaves. When the snow stopped and the sun rose the next morning, Zha Xiaolian woke up from his drunken stupor and told his family, "Yesterday, I drank with the Iron Beggar and had a wonderful time. Seeing how tattered his clothes were, how could he withstand this severe cold? Quickly give him my woolen robe!" The beggar put on the robe and left, not seeking to meet or thank Zha Xiaolian.

    557

    明年，孝廉寄寓杭之长明寺。暮春之初，偕侣携觞，薄游湖上。忽遇前丐于放鹤亭侧，露肘跣足，昂首独行。复挈之归寺，询以旧袍何在，曰：“时当春杪，安用此为？已质钱付酒家矣！”孝廉奇其言，因问：“曾读书识字否？”丐曰：“不读书识字，不至为丐也。”孝廉悚然心动，薰沐而衣履之。徐谂其姓氏里居。丐曰：“仆系出延陵，心仪曲逆，家居粵海，名曰六奇。只以早失父兄，性好博奕，遂致落拓江湖，流转至此。因念叩门乞食，昔贤不免；仆何人斯，敢以为污？不谓获遘明公，赏于风尘之外，加以推解之恩。仆虽非淮阴少年，然一饭之惠，其敢忘乎？”孝廉亟起捉其臂曰：“吴生固海內奇杰也！我以酒友目吴生，失吴生矣！”仍命寺僧沽梨花春一石，相与日夕痛饮。盘桓累月，赠以屝屦之资，遣归粤东。

    Explanation

    557

    In the following year, the scholar stayed at the Changming Temple in Hangzhou. At the beginning of late spring, he went on a brief trip around the lake with his friends, carrying wine to enjoy the scenery. Suddenly, he encountered the beggar from the previous year at the side of the Fanghe Pavilion. The beggar, with exposed elbows and bare feet, was walking alone with a head held high. The scholar brought him back to the temple and asked about his old clothes. The beggar replied, "It is spring now, so I don't need them. I have already pledged them for money at a tavern!" The scholar was surprised by his words and asked if he had ever studied or read. The beggar said, "If I had not studied or read, I would not have become a beggar." The scholar was moved and helped him get dressed and shod. He then asked about his name and hometown. The beggar said, "I originate from Yancheng and admire the cunning of Zhang Liang. I live in Guangdong, and my name is Liuchiqi. Only because I lost my father and brothers at a young age, and I love games, did I end up wandering the world. I thought that even the wise ones could not avoid begging, so what am I, a mere beggar? I did not expect to meet a noble gentleman like you, who appreciates me beyond the common crowd and helps me out of a difficult situation. I will never forget the kindness of a single meal." The scholar immediately stood up and grabbed his arm, saying, "Mr. Wu is indeed a remarkable person in the world! I thought of Mr. Wu as a drinking companion, but I was wrong!" He then ordered the temple monk to buy a stone of Lihuachun wine, and they drank heartily day and night. They stayed together for several months, and the scholar gave him money for shoes and sandals, sending him back to Guangdong.

    558

    六奇世居潮州，为吴观察道夫之后，略涉诗书，耽游卢雉，失业荡产，寄身邮卒。故于关河孔道，险阻形胜，无不谙熟。维时天下初定，王师由浙入广，舳舻相衔，旗旌钲鼓，喧耀数百里不绝。凡所过都邑，人民避匿村谷间，路无行者。六奇独贸贸然来，逻兵执送麾下，因请见主帅，备陈“粵中形势，传檄可定。奇有义结兄弟三十人，素号雄武，只以四海无主，拥众据土，弄兵潢池。方今九五当阳，天旅南下，正蒸庶徯苏之会，豪杰效用之秋。苟假奇以游札三十道，先往驰谕，散给群豪，近者迎降，远者响应，不逾月而破竹之形成矣。”如其言行之，粵地悉平。由是六奇运箸之谋，所投必合；扛鼎之勇，无坚不破。征闽讨蜀，屡立奇功。数年之间，位至通省水陆提督。

    558

    Liu Liangzhong, originally from Chaozhou, was a descendant of Wu Guanzhong, a high-ranking official during the Ming Dynasty. He had some knowledge of classical literature but was obsessed with hunting and gambling, leading to the loss of his job and property. He became a postal worker, but he was very familiar with the geographical features of the region. When the Qing army entered Guangdong Province from Zhejiang Province, the people fled to the countryside when the Qing army passed through the cities. Liu Liangzhong came alone to the Qing army's camp, was captured by the Qing army, and brought to the general's tent. He requested to meet with the Qing general and described the situation in Guangdong Province, suggesting that the Qing army could easily conquer the region. He had thirty brothers who were very brave, but they were not loyal to any government and were fighting each other. The Qing army was the legitimate government and was marching south. It was a time for the people to be liberated and for the heroes to serve the government. Liu Liangzhong suggested that the Qing army give him thirty letters of introduction to persuade the local leaders to surrender. The Qing general followed his advice, and the Qing army conquered Guangdong Province within a month. Liu Liangzhong was very good at planning, and his plans always worked. He was very brave and could overcome any obstacle. He helped the Qing army conquer Fujian and Sichuan Provinces and became a high-ranking official in the Qing government.

    559

    当六奇流落不偶时，自分以污贱终。一遇查孝廉，解袍衡门，赠金萧寺，且有海内奇杰之誉，遂心喜自负，获以奋迹行伍，进秩元戎，尝言“天下有一人知己，无若查孝廉者”。康熙初，开府循州，即遣牙将持三千金存其家，另奉书币，邀致孝廉来粵，供帐舟舆，俱极腆备。将度梅岭，吴公子已迎候道左，执礼甚恭。楼船萧鼓，由胥江顺流而南，凡辖下文武僚属，无不愿见查先生，争先馈赠，箧绮囊珠，不可胜纪。去州城二十里，吴躬自出迎，八驺前驰，千兵后拥，导从仪卫，上拟侯王。既迎孝廉至府，则蒲伏泥首，自称：“昔年贱丐，非遇先生，何有今日？幸先生辱临，糜丐之身，未足酬德！”居一载，军事旁午，凡得查先生一言，无不立应。义取之资，几至巨万。其归也，复以三干金赠行，曰：“非敢云报，聊以志淮阴少年之感耳。”

    Explanation

    559

    When the talented man, 六奇, was in misfortune and without a match, he considered himself destined to end in disgrace or lowly status. Upon meeting the scholar-official 查孝廉, 查脱下他的外衣在简陋的门前，赠送金子在萧寺，且有海内奇杰之誉，遂心喜自负，获以奋迹行伍，进秩元戎，尝言“天下有一人知己，无若查孝廉者”。康熙初，开府循州，即遣牙将持三千金存其家，另奉书币，邀致孝廉来粵，供帐舟舆，俱极腆备。将度梅岭，吴公子已迎候道左，执礼甚恭。楼船萧鼓，由胥江顺流而南，凡辖下文武僚属，无不愿见查先生，争先馈赠，箧绮囊珠，不可胜纪。去州城二十里，吴躬自出迎，八驺前驰，千兵后拥，导从仪卫，上拟侯王。既迎孝廉至府，则蒲伏泥首，自称：“昔年贱丐，非遇先生，何有今日？幸先生辱临，糜丐之身，未足酬德！”居一载，军事旁午，凡得查先生一言，无不立应。义取之资，几至巨万。其归也，复以三千金赠行，曰：“非敢云报，聊以志淮阴少年之感耳。”

    Translation:

    When the talented man, 六奇, was in misfortune and without a match, he considered himself destined to end in disgrace or lowly status. Upon meeting the scholar-official 查孝廉, 查脱下他的外衣在简陋的门前，赠送金子在萧寺，且有海内奇杰之誉，遂心喜自负，获以奋迹行伍，进秩元戎，尝言“天下有一人知己，无若查孝廉者”。康熙初，开府循州，即遣牙将持三千金存其家，另奉书币，邀致孝廉来粵，供帐舟舆，俱极腆备。将度梅岭，吴公子已迎候道左，执礼甚恭。楼船萧鼓，由胥江顺流而南，凡辖下文武僚属，无不愿见查先生，争先馈赠，箧绮囊珠，不可胜纪。去州城二十里，吴躬自出迎，八驺前驰，千兵后拥，导从仪卫，上拟侯王。既迎孝廉至府，则蒲伏泥首，自称：“昔年贱丐，非遇先生，何有今日？幸先生辱临，糜丐之身，未足酬德！”居一载，军事旁午，凡得查先生一言，无不立应。义取之资，几至巨万。其归也，复以三千金赠行，曰：“非敢云报，聊以志淮阴少年之感耳。”

    560

    先是苕中有富人庄廷钺者，购得朱相国《史概》，博求三吴名士，增益修饰，刊行于世。前列参阅姓氏十余人，以孝廉夙负重名，亦借列焉。未几，私史祸发，凡有事于是书者，论置极典。吴力为孝廉奏辩得免。孝廉嗣后益放情诗酒，尽出其囊中装，买美鬟十二，教之歌舞。每于良宵开宴，垂帘张灯，珠声花貌，艳彻帘外，观者醉心。孝廉夫人亦妙解音律，亲为家伎拍板，正其曲误。以此查氏女乐，遂为浙中名部。
    　　　　昔孝廉之在幕府也，园林极胜，中有“英石峰”一座，高可二丈许，嵌空玲珑，若出鬼制。孝廉极所心赏，题曰“绉云”。阅旬往视，忽失此石，则已命载巨舰，送至孝廉家矣。涉江逾岭，费亦千缗。今孝廉既没，青蛾老去，林荒石涸，而“英石峰”巍然尚存。
    　　　　[张山来曰：闻吴将军乞食时，好以荻苇于地上判某日及草封字，英雄失意而志不馁如此。至其不忘查君之德，足可谓跫然足音矣！]

    Explanation

    560

    Before this, in the region of Taoshan, there was a wealthy man named Zhuang Tingyue who purchased the historical work Shigai by the former chancellor Zhu. He extensively sought out scholars from the three Wu regions, enhanced and embellished the work, and published it. The list of those who had reviewed and consulted included more than ten names, and because the scholar known as Xianlian had long been renowned, he was also included among them. Shortly after, the scandal of private history emerged, and anyone involved with this book was severely punished according to the law. Wu, the general, intervened on behalf of Xianlian and managed to save him. After the controversy, Xianlian further indulged in poetry and wine, spending all his wealth to buy twelve beautiful young women and teach them dance and song. Every fine night, he would host banquets, hanging curtains and lighting lamps, with the sound of pearls and the beauty of flowers, so striking that it penetrated beyond the curtains, intoxicating the spectators. Xianlian's wife also had a deep understanding of music and rhythm, personally playing the clappers for the household performers and correcting their mistakes. Thus, the household music of the Cha family became a famous troupe in Zhejiang.

    In the past, when Xianlian was in the military camp, his gardens were extremely beautiful, with a famous rock formation called "Jing Stone Peak" standing in the middle, about two zhang high, intricate and delicate, as if created by a ghost. Xianlian greatly admired this rock and named it "Zhou Yun." After a few weeks, he went to visit it and suddenly found it missing. It had already been ordered to be transported on a large ship to Xianlian's home. The journey across rivers and mountains cost a thousand strings of cash. Now that Xianlian has passed away, the young women have aged, the gardens have fallen into disrepair, and the "Jing Stone Peak" still stands majestically.

    [Commentary by Zhang Shanlai: I heard that General Wu, when begging for food, used reeds on the ground to mark dates and seal letters. Even in times of hardship, he did not lose his spirit. His gratitude for the virtue of Mr. Cha can truly be called the sound of footsteps, a significant event.]

    561

    虞初新志卷十七
    　　记袁枢遇仙始末　毛际可会侯
    　　　　康熙庚辰正月廿六，钱塘庠生袁枢，字惠中，梦一长髯颁白者，自称崆峒道人，邀以入山，修炼三载，可证仙籍，且戒其弗泄。既寤，即与同人言及之。次夕复入梦云：“再泄吾言，当今汝哑。”晨起，若有人促之行，至一亩田，果见所梦道人，拉之同往，倏忽已至关外。枢以亲老固辞。道人投药一丸，恍然入腹，遂不能言。遇友引归，举家惶怖。
    　　　　中丞张公廉得之，知为观风所拔士，询其始末，枢具以笔对。怜其贫，捐俸十金与之。遂下有司捕获，大索十日不得。其父具呈，乞移咨江西天师府。七月十七日，方得天师移覆，外给治哑符二道，并仰浙江杭州府城隍司公文。中丞公亟传枢，亲赍公文诣庙焚之。归即先吞一符，觉遍体烦惫，骨节有声。夜梦一人，手持城隍谕单，上书“廿六日子堂传袁生员面谕”。至期，复梦其引入神署。

    Explanation

    561

    The New Tales of Yu Chou: Volume Seventeen
    A Record of Yuan Chu's Encounter with a Fairy by Mao Jike Huihou

    On the twenty-sixth day of the first month of the year Gengchen in the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Yuan Chu, a scholar from Qiantang, whose courtesy name was Huizhong, had a dream in which a long-bearded, white-haired man introduced himself as a recluse from Mount Kongtong. The man invited Yuan Chu to join him in the mountains for three years of cultivation, promising that he could then be recorded in the books of immortals. He also warned Yuan Chu not to reveal this secret. Upon waking, Yuan Chu immediately shared this dream with his peers. The next night, the same man appeared in his dream again and said, "If you reveal my words again, you will be rendered mute." In the morning, it seemed as if someone was urging him to leave, and he found himself at a field of one mu, where he indeed saw the man he had dreamed of. The man took Yuan Chu by the hand and they quickly arrived outside the city gate. Yuan Chu, due to his elderly parents, firmly declined. The man then threw a pill into Yuan Chu's mouth, which suddenly entered his stomach, and he became mute. A friend led him back home, and the entire family was terrified.

    Governor Zhang Gonglian learned of the incident and recognized Yuan Chu as a scholar selected during the imperial examination. He asked Yuan Chu about the details, and Yuan Chu wrote down his account. Pitying his poverty, the governor donated ten gold pieces to him. The authorities were then ordered to capture Yuan Chu, but after a ten-day search, they could not find him. Yuan Chu's father submitted a petition, requesting a consultation with the Taoist temple in Jiangxi. On the seventeenth day of July, the Taoist master finally responded, providing two talismans to cure muteness and ordering the city god of Hangzhou to provide a document. Governor Zhang quickly summoned Yuan Chu, personally delivering the document to the temple to burn it. Upon returning, Yuan Chu immediately swallowed one talisman and felt tired all over, with his bones making sounds. That night, he dreamed of a man holding a document from the city god, which stated, "On the 26th day of the month, at the lecture hall, summon the scholar Yuan for an audience." On the appointed day, he dreamed again of being led to the divine office.

    562

    烛光中，见神冠服危坐，曰：“已遣金甲神往请真人矣。”少顷，见道人偕金甲神至，城隍延之宾坐。道人向枢曰：“因你有厄，故罚哑一年。”城隍曰：“天师文内令其能言，若仍哑，何以复命？”道人曰：“既天师传命，不满一年，亦宜半载为期。然此后仍当慎言耳。”遂命之归。至廿八日，又吞一符，以天师符内嘱间七日再服故也。八月初一子时，梦人令其发声，即语言如常；屈指果及半载。赴戟门谢中丞，公曰：“天师来札云，为汝建坛作法，炼一金甲神来，三日有验，今信然矣。”

    Explanation

    562

    In the candlelight, I saw a deity wearing a ceremonial robe and sitting upright, saying, "I have already sent a golden-armored deity to invite the True Person." After a short while, a Taoist priest arrived accompanied by the golden-armored deity, and the City God invited them to sit as guests. The Taoist priest turned to the subject and said, "Because you have encountered misfortune, you have been punished with muteness for a year." The City God replied, "The Celestial Master's decree states that you should be able to speak. If you remain mute, how can you report back?" The Taoist priest responded, "Since the Celestial Master has given the command, and it is not yet a full year, it should be for half a year. However, you should still be cautious with your words afterward." He then sent the subject away. On the 28th day, the subject took another talisman, as instructed by the Celestial Master's talisman to take it again after seven days. On the first day of August at midnight, the subject dreamed of someone telling them to speak, and upon waking, found that they could speak normally; counting the days, it had indeed been half a year. The subject went to the gate of the halberd to thank the vice governor, who said, "The Celestial Master's letter states that a ritual was performed to summon a golden-armored deity, and it took three days to take effect. Indeed, it has proven to be so."

    563

    其事颇涉怪，为儒者所不道。然昔人谓城隍之神，与山川、社稷坛等，岁时致祀，以示国家怀柔百神之意，不必实有其人也。乃袍服酬对，与人世达官无异；又世外仙人，惝恍难信，而枢亲见之于城市中。城隍目为真人，必非妖魅可托。至天师爵秩相承，数千年来，白洙泗外，鲜与比盛。今以其移覆中丞公书观之，则封号亦不为倖致也。然非中丞公重士恤灾，委曲救拔，亦安能使天师建醮遣神若是哉？
    　　　　枢语余云：“方哑时，友人母病，意中若有所叩，忽信笔书云：丁丑丁丑，二人相守，玉兔东升，大家撒手。其母至丁丑日丑时而殁，至今不知其所以然也。”尤足诧异云。
    　　　　[张山来曰：天师有如此法力，其世袭也固宜。]

    Explanation

    563

    The story is quite unusual and is not discussed by scholars. However, ancient people believed that the city god, along with the deities of mountains and rivers and the altars of the state, are offered sacrifices seasonally to show the state's intention to be gentle and accommodating to all deities. It is not necessary that there actually be such a person. Yet, the city god responds in formal attire, no different from high-ranking officials in the world; and immortals from outside the world, though difficult to believe, I personally saw them in the city. The city god regards him as a true immortal, it must not be a demon or ghost that can be relied upon. The title of Celestial Master has been passed down through generations for thousands of years, and it is rarely comparable in grandeur to the sages of the Zhou and Qi regions. Now, looking at his letter to the Governor, then the title was not obtained by chance. But it was not until the Governor valued scholars and cared for disasters, and rescued with great effort, that he could make the Celestial Master perform rituals and send deities like this?

    The narrator told me: "When I was mute, my friend's mother was ill. As if something was being asked of me, suddenly wrote with confidence: 'Ding-Chou, Ding-Chou, two people holding on to each other, the Jade Rabbit rises in the east, everyone lets go.' Her mother passed away on the day Ding-Chou at the hour of the ox, and to this day, I do not know why." This is especially surprising, it is said.

    [According to Zhang Shanlai: The Celestial Master has such power, it is indeed fitting that it is hereditary.]

    564

    闵孝子传　江宁吴晋介兹
    　　　　闵孝子者，湖州之南镇人，年四十余，种田为业。少未尝读书，性粗憨，不惬于族里。屋数间，阡陌相望，晨夕率妻子奉若父唯谨。父为老诸生，年七十又二，寻病，医药不效，日益笃。孝子忧之。族里咸劝孝子急治具，不听。妻亦劝，不听。一日，父病霍然，又三日，受杖履矣！慰问者欲得其故，孝子作谩语笑谢之。人以孝子粗憨，莫之毕究。其妻亦谓得秘药活之耳。
    　　　　旬日，孝子如罹重疾，卧床第，呻吟不止，状甚苦。妻曰：“若何为者？翁前病，诚当忧，今病且起，忧何为者？”孝子唯唯，呻吟不止如故。妻以为真得疾，秘不以示，亦以乃翁病新愈，惧贻乃翁忧。一日晨起，猝见其扪心难堪状，妻益疑，因伺其寐，发所扪处视之，见创，大惊，促之曰：“若何为者？”孝子不能隐，徐曰：“予人子，不忍父病之不可救也。常闻人言，亲不可药救者，得子心片许，杂饘粥噉之，可救。

    Explanation

    564

    The Biography of Min Xianshizi by Wu Jinjie of Jiangning

    Min Xianshizi was a man from Nan Zhen in Huzhou, who was over forty years old and made his living by farming. As a youth, he had never studied, and his nature was simple and honest, which did not please the community. He lived in a few rooms, with fields stretching out before his eyes, and he led his wife and children in serving his father with solemn respect every morning and evening. His father was an elderly scholar, seventy-two years old, who soon fell ill. Despite medical treatment, his condition grew worse day by day. Min Xianshizi was deeply concerned. The community urged him to prepare for the worst, but he refused. His wife also advised him, but he still did not listen. One day, his father's illness suddenly improved, and after three more days, he was able to walk with a cane! Those who came to console them wanted to know the reason, but Min Xianshizi spoke vaguely and laughed off their inquiries. People thought Min Xianshizi was simple and honest, and did not delve deeper into the matter. His wife also believed that his father had been saved by secret medicine.

    Ten days later, Min Xianshizi seemed to suffer from a serious illness, lying in bed, moaning continuously, in a very painful state. His wife asked, "What are you doing? Your father was indeed ill, and it was right to be worried, but now that he is recovering, why are you still so concerned?" Min Xianshizi responded politely, but continued to moan as before. His wife thought he had truly fallen ill and kept it a secret, fearing that it might worry his father. One morning, she suddenly saw him touching his heart with a look of discomfort. She became more suspicious and waited until he fell asleep, then examined the spot he had touched. She found a wound there and was greatly shocked. She pressed him, saying, "What are you doing?" Min Xianshizi could no longer hide it and slowly explained, "I am a son, and I could not bear the thought of my father's incurable illness. I often heard that for those who cannot be cured by medicine, obtaining a small piece of the child's heart, mixed into porridge and fed to the father, can save him."

    565

    某日因祷土神前，愿剖心活吾父。夜半，吾父呼饮时，予引刀刺胸，出心，割若许，纳饮中以进，不意吾父果霍然也。当刺胸时，不甚楚，割毕，创即敛好，如未刺时。今始不复忍。宜秘，若勿语。”其妻哀，且闻伤心，恐死，亟白之医。医错愕曰：“吁！是顾安所得药？”妻长跽泣请，医不可却，妄出药涂之去，言必死。妻亦以为必死，泣相向。诘朝药忽迸落，创痕俱失所在矣。妻喜出望外，促孝子诣医报谢。医复错愕曰：“吁！是顾安所得活？殆有异！”

    Explanation

    565

    On a certain day, while praying before the earth god, I wished to cut out my heart to save my father. At midnight, when my father called for a drink, I drew a knife and stabbed my chest, removed my heart, cut off a piece, and placed it in the drink to offer. Unexpectedly, my father was completely cured. When I stabbed my chest, it did not hurt much; after cutting, the wound healed immediately, as if it had not been cut. Now, I can no longer bear it. It should be kept secret; do not tell anyone." Her wife was grief-stricken, heard the news, and was afraid he would die, so she quickly told the doctor. The doctor was startled and said, "Alas! Where did you get such a medicine?" The wife knelt down and wept, pleading with the doctor, who could not refuse, so he gave her some medicine to apply, saying he would surely die. The wife also thought he would surely die, and they wept together. The next morning, the medicine suddenly fell apart, and all the wounds disappeared. The wife was overjoyed and urged the filial son to go to the doctor to thank him. The doctor was startled again and said, "Alas! How could he have been saved? There must be something strange!"

    566

    医即里中人，为遍闻之里中。里中人美其里有孝子也，具闻之郡邑大夫。郡邑大夫上其事大中丞，且为孝子旌门焉。旌门日，唯其父拱立闾左。郡邑大夫让孝子出，云先二日已逸去。或曰：“孝子终粗憨人也，顾安从知接见郡邑大夫礼？”甲辰春，予游姑苏，同舟人有从南镇来者，为予言若此。惜未详其名。
    　　　　外史氏曰：割股疗亲，古不深许，矧割心者哉？然孝子故粗憨，能笃所亲，至不计其生，又旌门日，先期逸去，不欲以孝名，尚得谓粗憨哉？今世之不粗憨者，大率全躯保妻子，精于自为者也，拔一毛以利君亲，有所不为。若孝子者，可以风矣！
    　　　　[张山来曰：割肝割股，世多有之，今割心，尤奇孝也！子夏有言：“虽曰未学，吾必谓之学矣。”其闵孝子之谓耶？]

    Explanation

    566

    A Doctor from the Village

    The doctor was someone from the village, and the news of his deeds spread throughout the village. The villagers praised their village for having a filial son and reported it to the officials of the county and town. The officials of the county and town reported the matter to the Grand Censor, who planned to honor the filial son by inscribing his gate. On the day of the honor, only his father stood respectfully at the left side of the gate. The officials of the county and town asked the filial son to come out, but he had already fled two days earlier. Some said, "The filial son is still a simple-minded person, how could he know the etiquette of meeting officials of the county and town?"

    In the spring of Jia Chen year, I traveled to Suzhou, and a fellow traveler who came from Nan Zhen told me about this. Unfortunately, I do not know his name.

    The historian says:

    Cutting flesh to treat one's parents, ancient people did not highly approve of it, how much less so cutting one's heart! However, the filial son was indeed simple-minded, he was devoted to his parents, to the point of not considering his own life. On the day of the honor, he fled ahead of time, not wanting to be known for his filial piety, how can he still be called simple-minded? Those who are not simple-minded today, mostly protect themselves and their families, are skilled at taking care of themselves. To pluck a single hair to benefit one's ruler or parents, they would not do it. Such as this filial son, can be a model for others!

    [ Zhang Shanlai says: Cutting liver and thigh, such acts are common in the world, but cutting one's heart is especially remarkable filial piety! Zixia once said, "Even if he says he has not learned, I will certainly say he has learned." Is he referring to the filial son? ]

    567

    人觚　平江钮琇玉樵
    　　　　熊公廷弼，当督学江南时，试卷皆亲自批阅。阅则连长几于中堂，鳞摊诸卷于上，左右置酒一坛，剑一口，手操不律，一目数行。每得佳篇，辄浮大白，用志赏心之快；遇荒缪者，则舞剑一回，以抒其郁。凡有俊才宿学，甄拔无遗。吾吴冯梦龙，亦其门下士也。梦龙文多游戏，《挂枝儿》小曲，与《叶子新斗谱》，皆其所撰。浮薄子弟，靡然倾动，至有覆家破产者。其父兄群起讦之，事不可解。适熊公在告，梦龙泛舟西江，求解于熊。相见之顷，熊忽问曰：“海内盛传冯生《挂枝儿》曲，曾携一二册以惠老夫乎？”冯跼蹐不敢置对，唯唯引咎，因致千里求援之意。熊曰：“此易事，毋足虑也。我且饭子，徐为子筹之。”须臾，供枯鱼焦腐二簋，粟饭一盂。冯下箸有难色。熊曰：“晨选嘉肴，夕谋精粲，吴下书生，大抵皆然。似此草具，当非所以待子。

    Explanation

    567

    A Man of Integrity: Pingjiang Niuxiu Yujiao

    General Xiong Tingbi, when he was the supervisor of education in Jiangnan, personally reviewed all examination papers. When he reviewed them, he would arrange the long tables in the hall and spread out the papers like fish scales on the table. On either side, he would place a large jar of wine and a sword. Holding a ruler in hand, he would read several lines at a glance. Every time he found a good essay, he would take a large drink to celebrate the joy of his appreciation; when encountering absurd or poorly written essays, he would dance with the sword once to express his frustration. He selected and promoted talented individuals without leaving anyone behind. My friend Feng Menglong from Wu is also one of his disciples. Feng Menglong's writings were often playful, and he composed the folk songs and ballads in Guazhi'er and the book on card games, Yezixin Doushu. The superficial and frivolous young people were greatly influenced by his works, and some even lost their homes and fortunes. His parents and brothers rose up to accuse him, and the matter could not be resolved. Just as General Xiong was on leave, Feng Menglong sailed to the West River to seek a resolution from General Xiong. When they met, General Xiong suddenly asked, "It is widely known that Feng Menglong's Guazhi'er is popular. Have you brought a copy or two to give to me?" Feng was so nervous that he dared not respond, humbly admitted his fault, and expressed his intention to seek help over a long distance. General Xiong said, "This is an easy matter, there is no need to worry. I will first treat you to a meal, and then I will think about it." In a short while, they served two bowls of dried fish and burnt food, and one bowl of millet rice. Feng hesitated to eat the food. General Xiong said, "In the morning, they select fine dishes, and in the evening, they plan elaborate meals. The scholars from Wu, in general, are used to such luxuries. Such a simple meal is not the way to treat you."

    568

    然丈夫处世，不应于饮食求工，能饱餐粗粝者，真英雄耳！”熊遂大恣咀啖。冯啜饭匕余而已。熊起入内，良久始出，曰：“我有书一缄，便道可致我故人，毋忘也。”求援之事，并无所答，而手挟一冬瓜为赠。瓜重数十斤，冯伛偻祗受。然意甚怏怏，且力不能胜，未及舟，即委瓜于地，鼓棹而去。行数日，泊一巨镇，熊故人之居在焉。书投未几，主人即躬谒冯，延至其家，华宴奇哉，妙妓清歌，咄嗟而办。

    568

    As for a man living in the world, one should not seek perfection in food; one who can eat coarse food heartily is truly a hero!" Bear then indulged in eating heartily. Feng drank only a few spoonfuls of rice. Bear got up and went inside, and after a long while, he came out and said, "I have a letter here; if you pass by, please deliver it to my old friend, don't forget." Feng did not receive any response regarding his request for help, but Bear handed him a winter melon as a gift. The melon weighed dozens of pounds, and Feng bowed down to accept it. However, he was quite displeased, and moreover, he could not carry it, so before reaching the boat, he left the melon on the ground and rowed away. After traveling for several days, he docked at a large town where Bear's old friend lived. Shortly after delivering the letter, the host personally visited Feng, invited him to his home, and hosted a splendid and extraordinary banquet, with beautiful dancers and clear singing, all prepared instantly.

    569

    席罢，主人揖冯曰：“先生文章霞焕，才辩珠流，天下之士，莫不延颈企踵，愿言觏止。今幸亲降玉趾，是天假鄙人以纳履之缘也。但念吴头楚尾，云树为遥，荆柴陋宇，岂足羁长者车辙哉？敬备不腆，以犒从者，先生其毋辞！”冯不解其故，婉谢以别，则白金三百，蚤舁致舟中矣。抵家后，熊飞书当路，而被讦之事已释。盖熊公固心爱犹龙子，惜其露才炫名，故示菲薄。而行李之穷，则假途以厚济之。怨谤之集，则移书以潜消之。英豪举动，其不令人易测如此。

    Explanation

    569

    After the banquet, the host bowed to Mr. Feng and said, "Your writings are as brilliant as a rainbow, and your eloquence flows like pearls. No scholar in the world does not eagerly look forward to meeting you. It is a great honor that you have personally visited me, as if heaven has granted me this opportunity to tread on your shoes. However, I think that the distance between Wu and Chu is vast, and my humble abode, with its simple thatched roof, is not worthy of the wheels of a distinguished guest like you. I have modestly prepared a small offering to entertain your attendants; please do not refuse!" Mr. Feng did not understand the reason for this, so he politely declined and took his leave. Then, three hundred taels of silver were early carried to his boat. Upon arriving home, Xiong Fei wrote to the authorities, and the matter of being accused was resolved. Xiong Fei, who deeply loved Mr. Feng, regretted that he showed off his talents and fame, so he acted in a modest way. To help him generously, Xiong Fei provided a way to support him. To secretly resolve the matter of the accusations, Xiong Fei wrote to address the situation. The actions of a hero are so difficult to predict.

    570

    [张山来曰：使我为龙子犹，则竟作《求解挂枝儿》矣。]
    　　　　泉州府同安之厦门，前朝中左所地也。顺治初，为海寇郑锦所据，壬辰，我师进剿，郑寇大俘子女而还。有骑士挟一妇人于马上，过同安东关。妇见道旁有井，绐骑士下马小遗，即跃入井。骑士窥井大怒，连发三矢，中妇肩而去。越十日，有村民薛姓者，由村入城，行至半途，天甫向晓，忽于烟雾中见一妇人，韶年丽容，身衣碧色短襦，腰系淡黄裙，双趾纤细，文履高屐，迎前泣告曰：“妾乃厦门难妇王氏也。夫死于兵，而妾被掠，矢志不辱，投身东关道旁之井。闻君夙有高义，幸出我于井，拔箭敛尸，埋棺井侧。妾当随事默佑，以报君德。”薛应曰：“诺！”妇忽不见。是日，薛适有事于县，如意而出，因于东关往求井，妇宛然在焉。

    Explanation

    570

    Zhang Shanlai said: "If I were to become like Long Ziyu, I would end up composing 'Qiu Jie Guazhi'er.'"

    Xiamen, Tong'an District of Quanzhou Prefecture, was formerly the site of Zhongzuo Garrison in the previous dynasty. At the beginning of the Shunzhi era, it was occupied by the sea bandit Zheng Jing. In the year of the water dragon (1662), our army advanced to attack, and Zheng's bandits captured many women and children and returned. There was a knight who carried a woman on his horse, passing through the eastern pass of Tong'an. The woman saw a well by the roadside and tricked the knight to dismount and relieve himself, then immediately jumped into the well. The knight looked into the well and became very angry, firing three arrows, hitting the woman's shoulder and leaving. Ten days later, a villager named Xue, from the village to the city, on the way halfway, just as dawn was breaking, suddenly saw a woman in the mist, youthful and beautiful appearance, wearing a short green robe, light yellow skirt, slender toes, embroidered shoes, and high boots. She approached and wept, saying, "I am Wang, a woman from Xiamen who suffered. My husband died in battle, and I was captured. Determined not to be disgraced, I threw myself into the well by the eastern pass road. Heard that you have always had high virtue. Please rescue me from the well, remove the arrows, collect the body, and bury the coffin beside the well. I will silently assist you in return for your virtue." Xue replied, "Yes!" The woman suddenly disappeared. That day, Xue happened to have business in the county, left as if nothing had happened, then went to the eastern pass to find the well, and the woman was still there.

    571

    偶遇博场，薛欲验妇语，遂入场下采，复获大胜。囊钱还家，与子弟话其事，即以钱买棺，约子弟同至井所。出妇尸，颜貌如生，为之拔箭，整衣履，殡而埋之。其地去井丈余，前临大道。又月余，薛梦妇拜谢而言曰：“妾荷君之义，幸获安葬；妾身虽朽，而妾心之感君者不朽也！阴府悯妾之节，命妾香火于此。君若为妾立尺五之庙，则妾之报君，当不止曩昔矣。唯君终始之。”薛觉而惊异，次日，舁运砖土，筑成小庙，并以辦香酬赛。自后举家安顺，事事获济。远近竟相传说。不数年，绅士商民，各致钱镪，大起神宇，丹碧轮焕，而肖像于中。题其额曰：“王义娘庙。”入庙庄诚，有祷辄应；遇衣冠不洁，或出秽亵语者，立致谴责。以是土人及往来之客益加敬畏，焚叩骈集，至今不衰。

    Explanation

    571

    During a chance encounter with a gambling house, Xue wanted to test the woman's words and entered to place a bet, winning a great victory. Returning home with money in his bag, he talked about the matter with his disciples and immediately used the money to buy a coffin, inviting his disciples to the well. They brought out the woman's body, which looked as if she were still alive. He removed the arrow, tidied her clothes and shoes, and buried her. The burial site was a few meters away from the well, in front of a major road. A few months later, Xue dreamed of the woman bowing and thanking him, saying, "I bear the kindness of the lord and am fortunate to receive a proper burial. Although my body has decayed, my gratitude for the lord is eternal! The underworld pities my virtue and commands me to offer incense and fire here. If the lord builds a small temple for me, then my gratitude to the lord will not be limited to the past. Only the lord will see it through." Xue woke up in astonishment and, the next day, carried bricks and soil to build a small temple, offering incense as a reward. Afterward, the whole family became peaceful and prosperous, and all matters were successful. The story spread far and wide. Within a few years, officials, scholars, and common people donated money, and a large temple was built, brightly colored and shining, with an image placed inside. The name of the temple was inscribed as "Wang Yinniang Temple." People entered the temple with solemnity and sincerity, and their prayers were answered immediately. Those who were improperly dressed or spoke indecent words were immediately rebuked. As a result, the locals and travelers came to burn incense and bow in large numbers, and the temple's reputation has not declined to this day.

    572

    [张山来曰：节烈止为一家之事耳，阴府犹重之若此，矧为臣而殉国者乎？]
    　　事觚　钮琇玉樵
    　　　　会稽东南有山曰平水。康熙初，樵人经其下，见一大蛇如蟒，蜿蜒涧泥内，久之，涂附其身。樵人释担而观，涧旁有洞，蛇曳泥而入。樵以泥封洞口而归，遂不能言，与人酬对，唯张手作状而已。如是者三年，复过前遇蛇处，阴云乍合，雷雨骤至，霹雳一声，有龙从洞中出，腾空而去。樵人不禁大呼曰：“向我卷舌不能出声者，正此物为之也！”于是能言如初。

    Explanation

    572

    Zhang Shanlai said: Loyalty and chastity are matters of a single family, yet the underworld places such great importance on them; how much more so for those who die for their country?

    Compiled by Shiji by Niu Xiu Yujiao

    In the southeastern part of Kuaiji, there is a mountain called Pingshui. In the early years of the Kangxi era, a woodcutter passed by the base of the mountain and saw a large snake like a python moving in the muddy stream. After some time, the mud adhered to the snake's body. The woodcutter put down his burden and watched. There was a cave by the stream, and the snake dragged itself into the cave with the mud. The woodcutter sealed the cave entrance with mud and returned. He then could not speak, could only respond with gestures. This continued for three years. He passed by the place where he had met the snake again. Dark clouds suddenly gathered, and thunder and rain came quickly. A loud clap of thunder, and a dragon emerged from the cave, soaring into the sky and flying away. The woodcutter could not help but shout, "It was this creature that had previously silenced my tongue so I could not speak!" And so he could speak again as before.

    573

    [张山来曰：白龙鱼服，自当致困。今此龙乃咎樵而哑之，殊非理也。]
    　　　　荆州马洋潭有黄姓者，朴老而鳏独，为乡塾师。一女名嗣姑，生有慧质，幼在塾，随父读书。年十四，自绣白衣大士，悬之室中，礼供甚虔。一夕，忽梦大士呼而语之曰：“汝父固乡里善人，数宜有子，其奈年老何？我欲以汝子之。”因遍抚其体，啖以红丸。甫下咽，觉有热气如火，从胸臆下达两股间。迷眩者七日，歘然而起，则已化为男子矣。先是翁以嗣姑许字同里谭姓，因往告以此异，谭怒诧其妄，鸣于官。质验果真，乃解婚。四方观者云集。康熙丙辰初夏，渭川孙静庵适过其地，亦造门请见。嗣姑冠履出迎，黛粉之痕未消，瑱犹在耳也。孙有句云：“梦中变化真奇创，红颜忽作男儿相。卸却罗衫蝴蝶裙，博带宽衣相揖让。见人低首尚含羞，珠环小髻乌蛮样。”

    Explanation

    573

    张山来曰：白龙鱼服，自当致困。今此龙乃咎樵而哑之，殊非理也。
    Zhang Shanlai said: "A white dragon disguised as a fish will inevitably lead to hardship. Now this dragon blames the woodcutter and silences him, which is far from reasonable."

    荆州马洋潭有黄姓者，朴老而鳏独，为乡塾师。一女名嗣姑，生有慧质，幼在塾，随父读书。年十四，自绣白衣大士，悬之室中，礼供甚虔。
    In the village of Ma Yangtan in Jingzhou, there was a man surnamed Huang, who was simple, old, widowed, and alone, serving as a village schoolmaster. He had a daughter named Sigu, who was born with exceptional intelligence. When she was young, she studied with her father in the school. At the age of fourteen, she embroidered the Bodhisattva Guanyin in white robes and hung it in her room, worshipping it with great devotion.

    一夕，忽梦大士呼而语之曰：“汝父固乡里善人，数宜有子，其奈年老何？我欲以汝子之。”因遍抚其体，啖以红丸。甫下咽，觉有热气如火，从胸臆下达两股间。迷眩者七日，歘然而起，则已化为男子矣。
    One night, she suddenly dreamed that the Bodhisattva called to her and said, "Your father is indeed a virtuous man in the village, and he should have a son, but what can be done with his old age? I wish to transform you into a son." The Bodhisattva then touched her body all over and fed her red pills. As soon as she swallowed them, she felt a hot sensation like fire, which spread from her chest down to her thighs. She remained in a dazed state for seven days, and then suddenly awoke, having already transformed into a man.

    先是翁以嗣姑许字同里谭姓，因往告以此异，谭怒诧其妄，鸣于官。质验果真，乃解婚。四方观者云集。
    Earlier, the father had betrothed Sigu to a man surnamed Tan from the same village. Upon hearing of this unusual transformation, Tan was furious and accused it of being absurd, reporting it to the authorities. After verification, it was confirmed to be true, and the marriage was dissolved. People from all directions gathered to witness the event.

    康熙丙辰初夏，渭川孙静庵适过其地，亦造门请见。嗣姑冠履出迎，黛粉之痕未消，瑱犹在耳也。孙有句云：“梦中变化真奇创，红颜忽作男儿相。卸却罗衫蝴蝶裙，博带宽衣相揖让。见人低首尚含羞，珠环小髻乌蛮样。”
    In the early summer of the year 1686 during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Sun Jing'an of Weichuan happened to pass through the area and also visited the door to request an audience. Sigu, dressed in a hat and shoes, greeted him. The traces of black powder and rouge were still visible on her face, and the earrings were still in her ears. Sun wrote, "In a dream, the transformation is truly extraordinary; the beautiful face suddenly becomes that of a man. Unfastening the silk robe and butterfly skirt, he wears a broad sash and loose clothes, bowing and yielding to others. Seeing people, he lowers his head and still blushes, with pearl earrings and a small topknot in the style of the Wumeng region."

    574

    [张山来曰：男女幻化，史家谓之人妖。今观此，则正所以奖善也。]
    　　　　蒲州于孝廉，有爱姬曰红桃，美容止，善谈谑，尤擅名琵琶。北地闺闱多娴此技，而红桃纤指娇喉，拢弦叶曲，其调与众绝异，故才一发声，闻者即知为于家琵琶也。崇祯末，闯寇所至蹂躏，河汾间罹祸尤酷。孝廉被执，闯帅将杀之，牛金星见其年韶质秀，且已登科，丐为子师而免。红桃亦于此散失，不知所往。孝廉从金星于军，数月后，馆之晋王府中。晋府初经兵燹，虽重楼叠阁，而栋折垣颓，金粉凋落，沼荒林败，竹柏倾欹。孝廉于最后之宫，置一榻焉，妖狐昼啸于庭，奇鬼宵窥于牖，诡形怪响，百态千声。孝廉斯时虽偷息人间，实同冥域，而心念红桃，如醉如痴，一切可憎可怖之境，翻置度外矣。

    Explanation

    574

    Zhang Shanlai said: "The transformation of men and women, which historians call 'human-evil,' can also serve to reward virtue." In Pu Prefecture, there was a scholar-official named Yu Xianlin who had a beloved concubine called Red Peach. She was beautiful and skilled in conversation, particularly renowned for her talent on the pipa. Women in the northern regions were often adept at this art, but Red Peach's delicate fingers and charming voice, as she plucked the strings and played melodies, produced a tune that was uniquely different from others. Thus, when she first played, listeners immediately recognized it as the pipa of the Yu family. At the end of the Chongzhen era, the rebel forces led by Li Zicheng ravaged the land, and the region between the Yellow River and Fen River suffered especially severely. Yu Xianlin was captured, and Li Zicheng's commander intended to execute him. Niuxin, seeing Yu Xianlin's youthful beauty and scholarly achievements, begged to be allowed to teach his son, thereby sparing him. Red Peach was also lost during this chaos, and her whereabouts remain unknown. Yu Xianlin served under Niuxin in the army and, after several months, was placed in the Prince of Jin's mansion. The mansion had initially been ravaged by war, and though it boasted tall buildings and intricate structures, the beams were broken, the walls collapsed, the golden paint faded, the ponds became overgrown, and the bamboo and cypress trees leaned and fell. In the last remaining room, Yu Xianlin set up a bed. During the day, a fox would howl in the courtyard, and at night, strange ghosts would peek through the windows, producing bizarre forms and eerie sounds, a hundred different and thousand varied. Despite being alive in the world, Yu Xianlin felt as if he were in the underworld. Yet, his thoughts were consumed by Red Peach, and he was entranced and obsessed. All the things that were repulsive and terrifying were cast aside in his mind.

    575

    又逾一载，闯兵进逼京师，列营保定城北。序届残冬，云同霰集，孝廉与牛子共一行帐。薄暮雪下愈密，二鼓初报，孝廉启帐小遗，四望皎然，隐隐闻琵琶声。触其夙好，遂跣足踏雪，潜行求之。越数十行帐，独一帐有灯，声从帐出，俯而谛听，是耳所素熟者，大恸一声，身仆深雪不能起。帐中人疑其奸细，捆缚入帐，识为金星西席，乃释而询其故。孝廉曰：“家有小姬，素善琵琶。兵间散去，已逾二载，愿见之私，虽寐不忘。今宵万籁俱寂，清调远闻，恍出吾姬之手，不胜悲痛。干触麾下，疏狂之咎，尚期宥之。”帐中人亦豪者，慨焉出姬相见，果红桃也。乃复行酒列炙，俾孝廉与姬欢饮达旦。明日，言于金星，以红桃归孝廉，仍遣二骑送回蒲州。孝廉入本朝，以扬州通判终。

    Explanation

    575

    After more than a year, the rebel army, led by Li Zicheng, advanced toward the capital, Beijing, setting up camps north of Baoding. As the winter season approached, clouds and snowflakes gathered together. The scholar-official and his companion, Niu Zi, shared a tent. As evening approached, the snow fell more densely, and around midnight, the scholar-official opened his tent to relieve himself. Looking around, everything was clear and bright, and faintly, he heard the sound of a pipa (a traditional Chinese lute). Touching on his long-standing interest, he walked barefoot through the snow, secretly searching for the source of the music. Passing through dozens of tents, he found only one tent with a light. The sound was coming from that tent. Bending down to listen carefully, he recognized the melody as one he was familiar with. Overcome with emotion, he let out a loud cry of grief and fell into the deep snow, unable to get up. The person in the tent suspected him of being a spy, tied him up, and brought him into the tent. Recognizing him as the tutor of Jin Xing, they released him and asked the reason. The scholar-official said, "I have a young concubine who is skilled at playing the pipa. We were separated during the chaos of war, and it has been over two years. My private wish to see her has not left my mind, even in sleep. Tonight, all sounds are silent, and the clear melody can be heard from afar. It seems to come from my concubine's hands, and I am overwhelmed with sorrow. I have disturbed your presence, and I hope you will forgive my reckless behavior." The person in the tent was also a bold individual and graciously brought out the concubine to meet him. Indeed, it was the red peach (a nickname for the concubine). They then served wine and roasted meat, allowing the scholar-official to drink and enjoy himself with the concubine until dawn. The next day, he spoke to Jin Xing, who gave the red peach back to the scholar-official and sent two horsemen to escort him back to Puzhou. The scholar-official entered the current dynasty and ended his career as a deputy governor of Yangzhou.

    576

    [张山来曰：孝廉之念旧，帐中人之还姬，均足千古。]
    　　　　徐州李蟠，以文望雄于乡，跌宕自喜。其家去州城一二里。有赵翁者，所居之村与李村相望，晨夕往来无间也。赵翁颇饶于资，小筑数十楹，外周以垣，中分两院，而空其半，栏槛曲折，花木幽深。忽一日，有美髯老人，从空屋中曳杖而出，自号豹仙，颜如童孺，衣冠甚古。长揖赵翁，偕入其室，则屏帏之丽，几案之精，皆非素有。翁顾视骇愕。豹仙曰：“老夫生无氏族，居无井里，所至之地，安即为乡。昨从天目、天台渡江而北，遍访幽栖，曾无愜意。适见君有闲馆，绝远嚣尘，暂顿妾婢于此。当图留珠之报，用酬割宅之恩，幸无讶也。”言未既，美姬渐次出见，焚香于炉，瀹茗于盌，更侍递进，光艳照座。豹仙笑指诸姬曰：“此皆老夫养生之具矣。”赵翁告退，念其礼意既殷，谈论复雅，顿忘怪异，转与亲昵。暇则辄相过从。豹仙自言得道汉时，市朝屡变，转瞬间不觉千有余岁。

    Explanation

    576

    Zhang Shanlai said: The sentiment of the xiao lian (孝廉) remembering old friends and the act of the person in the tent returning his concubine are both worthy of being remembered for a thousand years.

    In Xu Zhou, Li Pan was renowned for his literary and scholarly achievements, and he was proud of his free-spirited nature. His home was about one or two miles from the city. There was an old man named Zhao, who lived in a village that could be seen from Li's village, and they visited each other daily without interruption. Zhao was quite wealthy, and he had built a small mansion with dozens of rooms, surrounded by walls, divided into two courtyards, with half of it left empty, featuring winding railings and deep, secluded flowers and trees. One day, a bearded old man emerged from the empty house, carrying a walking stick, and introduced himself as the "Pao Xian" (豹仙). He had the appearance of a young child, with very ancient-looking clothing. He bowed deeply to Zhao and entered the room with him. Inside, the screens and curtains were splendid, and the tables and desks were exquisitely crafted, all of which Zhao had never seen before. Zhao looked around in astonishment. The Pao Xian said, "I was born without a clan or surname, and I have no fixed place to call home. Wherever I go, that place becomes my hometown. I came from Tianmu and Tiantai mountains, crossing the river northward, and searched for secluded places, but found none to my satisfaction. I just saw that you have a quiet residence far from the noise and dust, so I have temporarily placed my maids here. I will plan to repay you with a pearl, to thank you for sharing your house with me, so please do not be surprised." Before he finished speaking, beautiful women gradually appeared, burning incense in the censer and brewing tea in the bowl, serving each other in turn, their beauty shining brightly. The Pao Xian smiled and pointed to the women, saying, "These are all the tools I use for my longevity." Zhao decided to leave, thinking that the Pao Xian's courtesy and refined conversation had made him forget any strangeness, and they became close friends. When they had free time, they would often visit each other. The Pao Xian said he had attained enlightenment during the Han dynasty, and despite the many changes in the world, he had lived for over a thousand years.

    577

    赖有狐氏八仙，从侍巾栉，红粉四班，命曰“阴猎”。逾月则遣一班于三百里外，媚人取精，挹彼注兹，合同而化，运之以气，葆之以神，延生之术，实由于此。赵翁度其心能前知，因叩以吉凶祸福，无不奇中。惊传乡曲，咸以真仙奉之。蟠独不信，一夕，痛饮极醉直造豹所，大呼“妖兽”，数其惑众之罪。豹则蚤已避去，其室閴如，而蟠仍毒詈不止也。赵翁隔院闻其声，亟往谆劝，令仆夫乘月扶归。明日，豹仙复见。

    Explanation

    577

    Relying on the eight immortals of the Fox clan, they attend to him with personal grooming and four groups of beautiful women, naming this practice "hidden hunting." Every month, one group is sent three hundred miles away to charm people and extract their essence, transferring it to him, merging and transforming it with his own, circulating it through qi, preserving it with spirit, and practicing the techniques for prolonging life, which indeed stem from this. Zhao翁 gauged that he had the ability to foresee the future, so he asked him about auspiciousness, misfortune, and calamity, and all his predictions were remarkably accurate. News spread throughout the village, and everyone revered him as a true immortal. Only Pán did not believe, and one night, after drinking heavily, he went straight to the leopard immortal's dwelling, shouting "monster" and accusing him of deceiving the people. The leopard immortal had already fled early, leaving his room empty, but Pán continued to curse him incessantly. Zhao翁 heard the commotion from across the courtyard, hurriedly went to advise earnestly, and ordered the servant to carry Pán home under the moonlight. The next day, the leopard immortal was seen again.

    578

    赵翁曰：“吾友无状，深获罪于老仙。醉人当恕，幸无较焉！”豹仙曰：“此君天禄甚高，老夫辈法当退逊。计其年满三十，当魁天下；四十六岁，位至三公。但其生平有二隐事，实伤隐德，致干天罚。且性近鬼躁，功名虽显，不免淹阻，或至迁谪。是老夫则迹本萍浮，呼当马应，既被谴驱，无庸留滞矣。”辞别出门，有顷，过觇其居，鸟语在檐，落红满地，依然一空院也。他日，赵以二隐事询李，李嘿而不悦，似有悔咎之色。康熙丁丑，蟠果状元及第，寻以事去官。

    Explanation

    578

    Zhao said, "My friend has acted improperly and deeply offended the old immortal. A drunk person should be forgiven, please do not take it too seriously!" The leopard immortal replied, "This gentleman has a very high destiny, and we old folks should step back and yield. By the time he turns thirty, he will be the top scholar in the empire; by the age of forty-six, he will reach the rank of the Three Excellencies. However, he has two hidden matters in his life that actually harm his hidden virtue, thus bringing about divine punishment. Moreover, his nature is close to that of a demon, being restless, so even if his achievements are prominent, they will not be without obstacles, or he may be exiled. As for me, my life has been like a floating duckweed, I called for a horse and it responded, since I have been punished and driven away, there is no need to stay." After bidding farewell and leaving the door, a while later, when he passed by to look at his residence, birds were singing on the eaves, and fallen petals covered the ground, it remained an empty courtyard. One day, Zhao asked Li about the two hidden matters, Li remained silent and unhappy, showing a look of regret. In the year Ding-Chou of the Kangxi era, Pan Guo passed the imperial examination as the top scholar, but soon resigned from his official position due to some matter.

    579

    [张山来曰：八狐媚人取精，则豹仙非豹，直老龟耳。李公有如许胆识，其大魁也固宜。]
    　　　　天津徐纬真，素嗜方技，纵酒落魄。康熙初，偶有江淮之行，道经山东古庙，忽闻庙中大呼“徐纬真救我！”乃解鞍小憇，又闻呼之如前，入庙遍视，并无一人，唯有一大铁钟覆地，语出钟内。徐问曰：“汝是何怪，而作人语，且呼我望救耶？”钟内语曰：“上古猿公，黄石老曾从学剑，我即其裔也。以剑术之疏，误伤良善，蒙上帝谴责，囚此钟已百有余年。今限满当出，幸君开之。

    Explanation

    579

    Final Translation

    First Paragraph:
    Zhang Shanlai said: The eight fox spirits seduce and drain the essence of others; therefore, the leopard spirit is not truly a leopard, but merely an old turtle. Li Gong, with such courage and wisdom, is indeed a great leader.

    Second Paragraph:
    Xu Weizhen from Tianjin, who has always been fond of alchemy and other esoteric arts, often indulged in wine and lived a carefree life. During the early years of Emperor Kangxi's reign, he happened to be traveling through the Jianghuai region and passed by an ancient temple in Shandong. Suddenly, he heard someone inside the temple cry out, "Save me, Xu Weizhen!" He dismounted his horse and rested for a moment, then heard the same cry again. Entering the temple, he looked around but saw no one. There was only a large iron bell lying on the ground, and the voice seemed to come from inside the bell. Xu asked, "Who are you? Why are you speaking in human language and calling for my help?" The voice from the bell replied, "I am a descendant of the ancient Yuan Gong, who once studied swordsmanship under Huang Shi. Due to my poor swordsmanship, I accidentally harmed the innocent and was condemned by the Supreme Deity, imprisoned in this bell for over a hundred years. Now my time is up, and I am about to be released. Please open the bell for me."

    This is the final, polished translation of the source text. If you have any further requests or need additional revisions, please let me know.

    580

    ”徐曰：“我无千钧之力，岂能独发此钟？”钟内语曰：“不劳君手发也，君但去钟上十二字，我即出矣。”钟体泥封，篆文苔绣，取石敲磨，有顷立尽。钟内语曰：“可矣。然须速走，稍迟半刻，不无与君有害！”徐遂跨驴疾行二三里，回望来处，云霾风暴，响若山崩，遥见大白猿，从空飞坠，叩首驴前，倏忽不见。徐生南游半载，仍还都下。天街夜静，明月满户，闻剥啄声甚急。起户纳之，则年少书生，仪容妍雅，再拜称谢而曰：“余济南之钟囚也，赖君拯拔之恩，得超沉沦之厄。上帝赦其夙愆，仍还仙秩。

    Explanation

    580

    Xu said, "I do not have the strength of a thousand catties, how could I possibly be able to ring this bell alone?" The bell spoke inside, "It does not require your hands to ring it; you just need to remove the twelve characters on the bell, and I will come out." The bell was sealed with clay, and the characters were covered in moss. Xu took a stone to scrape them off, and within a short time, they were completely removed. The bell spoke again, "It is done. However, you must run quickly; if you delay even a little, it will not be without harm to you." Xu then mounted his donkey and hurriedly rode for two or three miles. Looking back at the place he came from, there was a cloud of mist and a storm, the sound like a mountain collapsing. He saw a large white monkey flying down from the sky, bowing before the donkey, then suddenly disappearing.

    Xu traveled south for half a year and then returned to the capital. On the night of the capital's street, it was quiet, and the moonlight filled the house. He heard a very urgent knocking sound. He opened the door to admit him, and it was a young scholar with a beautiful and elegant appearance, who bowed twice and thanked him, saying, "I am the prisoner of the bell in Jinan; thanks to your grace of rescue, I have escaped the calamity of being sunk. The Heavenly Deity has pardoned my past sins and restored my rank as a celestial being."

    581

    感君厚德，没齿弗谖。念君志切鼎炉，学求图纬，今于天府琼笈，窃得道书三卷授君，以申环珠之报。必于一夕篝灯毕抄，慎毋缓也！”出书置几，匆匆辞别。徐生展阅第一卷，其文如《论语》《孝经》，曰：“平平无奇耳。”展阅第二卷，其文如《阴符》《鸿烈》，曰：“此亦不足习也。”展阅第三卷，其文皆言吐火吞刀之秘，征风召雨之奇，乃大喜曰：“我所求者正在于是！”遂亟录之。

    Explanation

    581

    Grateful for your great virtue, I shall never forget it even to the end of my life. Thinking of your urgent desire to achieve the highest goals and your pursuit of the principles of the universe, I have secretly obtained three scrolls of Daoist texts from the heavenly repository of precious books and now present them to you as a precious and valuable return. You must copy them all in one night with a lantern, be careful not to delay! The books were placed on the table, and the messenger hurriedly took his leave. Xu Sheng opened the first scroll, and the text was similar to the Analects and the Classic of Filial Piety, saying, "This is quite ordinary." He then opened the second scroll, and the text was similar to the Yin Fu and the Hong Lie, saying, "This is also insufficient for practice." When he opened the third scroll, the text spoke of the secrets of fire breathing and sword swallowing, and the wonders of summoning wind and rain. He was greatly delighted and said, "This is precisely what I have been seeking!" And so he quickly copied them.

    582

    天甫向晓，而少年已至，窥徐意在末帙，色若不怿者，叹曰：“我所以报公者，岂谓是乎？第一卷具帝王之略，第二卷成将相之才，第三卷术数之书耳。用之而善，仅以修业；用而不善，适以戕生。然缘止于此，当可奈何？”言未既，人与书俱失矣。徐原籍山阴，自获书后，尝以其术试于故乡，或捉月于怀，悬之暗室，或捏雷于掌，放之晴霄。以法为戏，取薄酬而资旅食。一日饮酒大醉，时值炎署，袒而坐于门，适凉飙骤起，向空书符，招之入袖，良久不放。怒触风伯，于袖中大吼，破袖而出，雷火继之，肤发焦枯，随以致毙。

    Explanation

    582

    Just as dawn was breaking, a young man arrived, noticing that Xu's interest was in the last volume. Seeing his face filled with displeasure, the youth sighed and said, "What I intended to offer to you, how could it be this? The first volume contains strategies for ruling a kingdom, the second volume cultivates the talents of generals and prime ministers, and the third volume is a book on divination and esoteric knowledge. If used well, it only helps in advancing one's career; if used poorly, it only harms oneself. Yet the reason is limited to this, what can be done about it?" Before he finished speaking, both the youth and the book disappeared.

    Xu, originally from Shan Yin, had obtained the book and often tested its techniques in his hometown. Sometimes he would hold the moon in his arms and suspend it in a dark room, or grasp a thunderbolt in his hand and release it into the clear sky. He used these methods as games, earning a small fee to support his travels and meals. One day, after drinking heavily during a hot summer, he sat naked at the door. Just as a cool breeze suddenly arose, he wrote a talisman into the air, called it into his sleeve, and held it there for a long time without letting it go. Angered by this, the Wind God roared fiercely inside his sleeve, burst out of his sleeve, and was followed by thunder and fire. His skin and hair burned and dried up, and thus he perished.

    583

    又康熙庚申，高州大旱。有琼山诸生黄宾臣者，自言得奇门真传。有司往请之，宾臣结坛观山寺，披发杖剑，以目视日，竟晷不下一睫，天果微雨。诘朝，烈日如故，有司诮其左道无验，宾臣于是由观山迁坛于发祥寺，登浮图第四层，上下左右，悉封以符。谓观者曰：“明午必雨。但从东南来则吉，否则当有性命之忧。”因作书与家诀。明日未时，烈日中狂风大作，宾臣谓其仆曰：“雨从西北来，不祥，尔当速去！”其仆甫下塔，霹雳一声，雨如注。有老人见一麻鹰，口含火丸，从塔顶飞入。霹厉再震，宾臣颠仆塔外，右臂一孔如针，血涔涔流不已而死。此皆素无修道之真，妄习亵天之术，宜其干神怒、遭冥诛也。

    Explanation

    583

    During the reign of Emperor Kangxi, in the year 1670, the region of Gaoyang suffered a severe drought. There was a scholar named Huang Binchen from Qiongshan who claimed to have received the authentic transmission of the "Qi Men" technique. The local officials invited him, and Huang Binchen set up a ritual altar at Guanshan Temple, wore loose hair and carried a sword, and gazed at the sun with his eyes, not blinking for the entire day. It indeed rained slightly. The next day, the sun was as intense as before, and the officials mocked his heretical method as ineffective. Huang Binchen then moved the ritual altar to Fasang Temple, climbed to the fourth floor of a pagoda, and sealed everything with talismans. He told the onlookers, "It will rain by noon tomorrow. If the rain comes from the southeast, it will be auspicious; otherwise, there will be danger to life." He then wrote a letter to his family with instructions. The next day at around 1 PM, the intense sun was accompanied by a sudden strong wind. Huang Binchen told his servant, "The rain is coming from the northwest, which is inauspicious. You should leave quickly!" Just as his servant descended from the tower, a loud thunderclap sounded, and the rain fell heavily. An old man saw a buzzard with a fireball in its mouth flying into the top of the tower. Another thunderclap followed, and Huang Binchen fell outside the tower, with a needle-sized hole in his right arm, and the blood flowed continuously until he died. This was all because he had not genuinely practiced Daoist cultivation and recklessly practiced disrespectful techniques against heaven, which is why he angered the gods and was punished by the underworld.

    584

    [张山来曰：猿公既言“用而不善，适以戕生”，何徐生之不谨耶？]
    　　　　顺治十年三月，龙溪老农黄中，与其子小三，操一小船，往漳州东门买粪。泊船浦头，浦旁厕粪，黄所买也。父子饭毕，入厕担粪，见遗有腰袱一具，携以回船。解袱而观，内有白金六封。黄谓其子曰：“此必上厕人所失者。富贵之人，必不亲自腰缠；若贫困之人，则此银即性命所系，安可妄取？我当待其人而还之！”小三大以为迂，争之不听，悻悻径回龙溪。黄以袱藏船尾，约篙坐待。良久，遥见一人狂奔而来，入厕周视，彷徨号恸，情状惨迫。黄呼问故，其人曰：“我父为山贼妄指，现系州狱，昨造谒贵绅，达情州守，许以百二十金为酬。今鬻田宅，丐亲友，止得其半。待州守许父保释，然后拮据全餽，事乃得解。故以银袱缠腰入州。因欲如厕，解袱置板。心焦意乱，结衣而出，竟失此银。我死不足惜，何以救我父之死乎？”言讫，泪如雨下。

    Explanation

    584

    Zhang Shanlai said: "Since Yuan Gong warned that 'if used improperly, it will only harm life,' why was Xu Sheng so careless?"
    In the third month of the tenth year of the Shunzhi era, Huang Zhong, an elderly farmer from Longxi, went with his son Xiao San to Zhangzhou's eastern gate to buy manure. They moored their small boat at the riverbank, where the manure Huang had purchased was located. After finishing their meal, father and son entered the outhouse to carry the manure and found a waist bundle left behind. They took it back to the boat. Upon opening the bundle, they discovered six packages of silver. Huang told his son, "This must belong to someone who lost it while using the toilet. A wealthy person would not carry such a large sum of money personally; if the owner is poor, this silver is tied to their very life. How could we take it unjustly? I will wait for the rightful owner and return it to them!" Xiao San thought his father was being overly cautious and argued with him, but Huang refused to listen. Disgruntled, Xiao San returned to Longxi on his own. Huang hid the bundle at the stern of the boat and sat waiting with the pole. After a long time, he saw a man running wildly towards the outhouse. The man searched around frantically, weeping and wailing in despair. Huang called out to ask what was wrong, and the man replied, "My father was falsely accused by bandits and is now imprisoned in the state jail. Yesterday, I paid a visit to a respected official and explained the situation to the state governor, who agreed to pay 120 gold pieces in compensation. I have sold our land and property and begged my relatives and friends, but I have only managed to gather half of the amount. I must wait for the governor to agree to my father's release, then struggle to raise the rest of the money. Only then can I resolve the matter. That is why I tied the silver bundle around my waist when I went to the state. I wanted to use the toilet, so I took off the bundle and placed it on the bench. In my anxiety and confusion, I put on my clothes and left, and I accidentally lost the silver. My death is not a concern, but how can I save my father's life?" He finished speaking and burst into tears like rain.

    585

    黄细询银数与袱色俱符，慰之曰：“银固在也，我待子久矣！”挈而授之，封完如故。其人惊喜过望，留一封谢黄。黄曰：“使我有贪心，宁肯辞六受一？”挥手使去。是时船粪将满，而子久不至，遂独自刺船归。行至中途，风雨骤作，舣棹荒村之侧。村岸为雨所冲洗，轰然而崩，露见一瓮，银灌其口。黄亦不知中有何物，但念取此可为储米器，然重不能胜，力举乃得至船。须臾，雨霁风和，月悬柳外，数声欵乃，夜半抵家。小三以前事告母，两相怨詈。黄归扣户，皆不肯应。黄因诳云：“我有宝瓮在船，汝可出共举之。”子母惊起趋船，月光射瓮头如雪，手舁而上，凿锡倾瓮，果皆白镪，约有千金。黄愕然，悟蕉鹿之非梦矣。黄之邻，止隔苇墙，卧听黄夫妇切切私语甚悉。明日，以擅发私藏首于官。龙溪宰执黄庭讯，黄一无所讳，直陈还银获银之由。宰曰：“为善者食其报，此天赐也，岂他人所得而问乎？”笞邻释黄。由是迁家人入城，遂终享焉。

    Explanation

    585

    During the inquiry, Huang Xi confirmed that the number of silver and the color of the cloth both matched. He comforted the person, saying, "The silver is definitely here, I have been waiting for you for a long time!" He then took the silver and gave it to him, with the seal still intact. The person was surprised and delighted beyond expectation, leaving a letter to thank Huang. Huang said, "If I had a greedy heart, I would rather refuse six and accept one." He waved his hand and sent the person away. At that time, the ship's waste was about to be full, but Zijiu had not arrived, so Huang returned alone. Traveling halfway, sudden rain and wind started, and he docked the boat by the side of a desolate village. The village bank was washed by the rain and suddenly collapsed, revealing a jar. The jar contained silver. Huang did not know what was inside, but thought it could be used as a grain storage container. However, it was too heavy to lift, so he had to use all his strength to carry it to the boat. Soon, the rain stopped and the wind became gentle. The moon hung outside the willows, and a few sounds of oars were heard. He arrived home at midnight. Xiao San told his mother about the previous events, and they blamed each other. When Huang returned and knocked on the door, no one answered. Huang lied and said, "I have a valuable jar on the boat, you can come out and carry it together." His wife and mother, startled, rushed to the boat. The moonlight shone on the top of the jar like snow. They carried it up, pried open the lid, and poured out the contents. It turned out to be white silver, about a thousand taels. Huang was startled, realizing that the dream of the banana and deer was not a dream. Huang's neighbor, only separated by a reed wall, lay listening to Huang and his wife talking intimately in detail. The next day, the neighbor reported to the authorities for opening private storage without permission. The magistrate of Longxi, Huang Ting, questioned him. Huang did not hide anything, truthfully stating the reason for returning and obtaining the silver. The magistrate said, "Those who do good deeds receive their reward; this is a gift from heaven. How could others question it?" He flogged the neighbor and released Huang. Thereafter, Huang moved his family into the city and enjoyed a peaceful life.

    586

    [张山来曰：先王父亦有还金事，事载《江南通志》中。先君亦阴行善事，愧我辈不能继述，日趋贫困，唯有义命自安而已。]
    　　物觚　钮琇玉樵
    　　　　岁当夏秋之交，上常巡幸口外。康熙四十年七月，驾至索尔哈济，有喇里达番头人，进彩鹞一架，青翅蝴蝶一双。上问：“此二物产于何地？”头人回奏：“生穹谷山中。鹞能擒虎，蝶能捕鸟。”天颜大喜，賜以金而遣之。又驻跸郭哈密图七立，有索和诺蛇哈密，献麟草一方，奏云：“此草产于鹿鸣山雷风岭。自利用元年至今，止结数枚，必俟千月乃成。非遇圣朝，不易呈瑞。”

    Explanation

    586

    张山来说：我的曾祖父也有过归还金子的故事，这件事记载在《江南通志》中。我的父亲也暗中做了好事，我辈感到惭愧的是无法继承和发扬这些美德，逐渐变得贫困，只能顺应命运，自我安慰罢了。

    物觚 钮琇玉樵

    正值夏秋交替之际，皇上常常巡幸边疆。康熙四十年七月，车驾到达索尔哈济，有一位喇里达番的首领，进献了一架彩色的鹞子和一对青翅蝴蝶。皇上问：“这两样东西产自哪里？”首领回答说：“它们生长在穹谷山中。鹞子能捕捉老虎，蝴蝶能捕捉小鸟。”皇上非常高兴，赏赐了他们金子后让他们回去。后来，皇上又驻扎在郭哈密图七立，有一位索和诺蛇哈密的官员，献上了一方麟草，并奏报说：“这种草产自鹿鸣山的雷风岭。从利用元年开始到现在，只结了几个果实，必须等待一千个月才能成熟。如果不是遇到圣明的朝代，这种祥瑞之草不会轻易显现。”

    587

    姑苏金老，貌甚朴，而有刻棘镂尘之功。其最异者，用桃核一枚，雕为东坡游舫。舫之形，上穹下坦，前舒后奋，中则方仓，四围左右各有花纹。短窗二，可能开合。启窗而观，一几，三椅。中袍而多髯者为东坡，坐而倚窗外望。禅衣冠，坐对东坡而俯于几者为佛印师；几上纵横列三十二牌，若欲搜抹者然。少年偶坐，横洞箫而吹者，则相从之客也。舫首童子一，旁置茶铛。童子平头短襦，右手执扇，伛而颺火。舫尾老翁，椎髻芒鞋，邪立摇橹。外而柁篙篷缆之属，无不具也。舷槛檐幕之形，无不周也。细测其体，大不过两指甲耳。康熙三十七年春，江南巡抚宋公家藏一器，左侧窗败，无有能修治者。闻金老名，赠银十饼，使完之。金老曰：“此亦我手制也。世间同我目力，同我心思，然思巧而气不静，气静而神不完，与无巧同。我有四子，唯行三者稍传我法，而未得其精，况他人乎！”

    Explanation

    587

    The Elder Craftsman of Suzhou, though appearing plain, possessed extraordinary skill in carving and engraving. His most remarkable work was a miniature pleasure boat carved from a single peach pit, depicting Su Dongpo's boat. The boat's shape was curved at the top, flat at the bottom, relaxed at the front, and dynamic at the back. In the center was a square cabin, and all around were intricate patterns. There were two small windows that could be opened and closed. When opened, one could see a table and three chairs. A figure wearing a robe and with a full beard was Su Dongpo, sitting and gazing out the window. Opposite him, wearing Buddhist robes and leaning on the table, was Venerable Foyin. On the table were 32 cards arranged in a way that suggested they were being shuffled. A young guest sat casually, playing a flute. At the front of the boat was a child holding a teapot, wearing a short-sleeved robe, holding a fan in his right hand, and fanning a fire. At the back of the boat was an elderly man, wearing a simple hairstyle and sandals, leaning and rowing the boat. The exterior features, including the rudder, oars, sails, and ropes, were all present. The sides, railings, eaves, and curtains were all complete. Measuring its size, it was no larger than two fingernails. In the spring of 1698, Governor Song of Jiangnan owned a piece of art with a damaged window on the left side, and no one could repair it. Hearing of the craftsman's reputation, he offered him ten silver ingots to complete the repair. The craftsman said, "This was also made by my own hands. Though others may have similar vision and thought, they lack the necessary calmness and focus. Even if they are clever, their spirit is not calm; even if they are calm, their spirit is not complete, which is the same as having no skill. I have four sons, but only the third son has somewhat inherited my methods, though not fully, and certainly not as well as others!"

    588

    [张山来曰：气静而神完，非深于《庄子》者不能道。]
    　　　　山东文登县僻在海隅，其濒海之地，于康熙二十二年秋，有怪物出入其间，居民互相惊告，以为鬼至。每日向夕，辄闭门墐户。如是两月，不得已而闻于县。县宰之仆高忠，勇敢有大力，告其主曰：“海怪扰民，家不贴席，此吾主之事，而亦即忠之事也。愿赐良马一匹，铦枪一枝，忠能除之。”宰如所请，忠即跨马挟枪，独至海滨。新月初升，平沙如雪，比至二鼓，见一蓝面鬼，身长一丈有余，耸角枝牙，毛肱鳞背，坐于沙上，列置熟鸡五只，浊酒十瓶，举觥独酌，运掌若扇。忠驰马直前，以枪拟其肉角。鬼惊窜入海，忠遂据其坐，裂鸡酾酒，神气益壮。少顷，海水涌立，前鬼骑一怪兽，随波而出，舞刀迎斗。相持久之，忠乘间枪刺其腹，鬼遗刀而遁。忠拾刀还县，其上有“雁翎刀”三字。宰命收贮县库。于是濒海之怪遂绝。

    Explanation

    588

    Zhang Shanlai said: "Calm in spirit and complete in essence, only those with profound understanding of the Zhuangzi can articulate this state."

    In the remote coastal area of Wendeng County, Shandong Province, during the autumn of the 22nd year of Emperor Kangxi's reign, there were reports of a monster appearing in the vicinity. The locals, fearing it was a ghost, began to warn each other. Every evening, they would lock their doors and windows. After two months of this, they could no longer bear it and reported the matter to the county magistrate. The magistrate's servant, Gao Zhong, was brave and strong. He told his master, "The sea monster is disturbing the people, and we cannot sleep at home. This is your duty, and it is also mine. I wish to be granted a fine horse and a sharp spear, and I can eliminate the monster." The magistrate agreed, and Gao Zhong mounted the horse and took the spear, riding alone to the seashore. As the new moon rose, the flat sand looked like snow. By the second drumbeat of the night, he saw a blue-faced ghost, over ten feet tall, with horns and fangs, and a body covered in fur and scales. The ghost sat on the sand, surrounded by five roasted chickens and ten bottles of cloudy wine, drinking alone and waving his hands like a fan. Gao Zhong rode his horse straight forward and aimed his spear at the ghost's horn. The ghost was startled and fled into the sea. Gao Zhong then took the ghost's seat, tore open the chicken, poured the wine, and his spirit became even more robust. Soon, the sea surged, and the ghost, riding a strange beast, emerged from the waves, wielding a sword to fight. After a long battle, Gao Zhong took the opportunity to stab the ghost in the abdomen with his spear. The ghost dropped his sword and fled. Gao Zhong picked up the sword and returned to the county. The sword had the words "Yanling Dao" engraved on it. The magistrate ordered it to be stored in the county treasury. From that day on, the sea monsters near the coast ceased to appear.

    589

    东粤省城甜水巷旗人丁姓者，入市买一溺器，命童携归，置于卧床之侧。夜起小遗，而壶口闭壅，且举之颇重，就月观之，口内皆黄蜡封固。丁以石碎之，忽见三寸小黑人跳跃而出，顷刻间长八、九尺，身衣墨色布袍，手持利刃，入室登床，将杀丁妇。丁随于床头拔剑格斗，至鸡鸣时，黑人倏然而隐。次夕更余，复见灯下，丁仍挥剑逐去。越十余日，其邻余秀士之妻告丁妇曰：“我闻五仙庙法师善治妖，盍往求焉？”是夜，黑人竟奔秀士家，大声詈曰：“我与丁妇有三世夙仇，诉之冥界。其父母兄弟死亡无遗，唯此女在耳，将尽杀以雪我冤！何与汝事，而令遣妖道驱我为？”悉碎其日用器物，愤愤出门，遂不复见。丁妇自是无恙。

    Explanation

    589

    A Man from the Manchu Clan in Sweet Water Lane, Provincial Capital of Guangdong

    A man from the Manchu clan in Sweet Water Lane, the provincial capital of Guangdong, went to the market to buy a chamber pot. He ordered a boy to carry it back and place it beside his bed. One night, when he got up to urinate, he found that the mouth of the pot was blocked and it felt quite heavy. By the moonlight, he observed that the inside of the mouth was sealed tightly with yellow wax. Ding broke it with a stone, and suddenly saw a three-inch-tall black person jump out. In a moment, it grew to eight or nine feet tall, wearing a black cloth robe and holding a sharp blade. It entered the room and climbed onto the bed, intending to kill Ding's wife. Ding followed and drew his sword to fight, until the rooster crowed. The black person suddenly disappeared. The next night, after midnight, it appeared again under the lamp, and Ding still waved his sword to chase it away. After more than ten days, Ding's wife's neighbor, the wife of Mr. Yu the scholar, told Ding's wife, "I heard that the master at the Five Immortals Temple is good at dealing with demons. Why not go and seek him?" That night, the black person ran to Mr. Yu's house and loudly scolded, saying, "I and Ding's wife have had a three-generation-old grudge. I complained to the underworld. His parents and siblings all died, leaving only this woman. I intend to kill her all to clear my grudge! What has to do with your affair, and why did you send a sorcerer to drive me away?" It broke all of his daily-use items, angrily went out, and was no longer seen. Ding's wife was safe and sound since then.

    590

    [张山来曰：报仇而隐于溺器中，亦可谓破釜沉舟。而卒不能报，徒迁怒于其邻，何也？]
    　　　　康熙壬申、癸酉两岁，西安洊饥，斗米千钱，道殣相望。渭南县民赵午鬻其子女已尽，家有一母一妻，无所得食，担其釜甑，就粟湖广。赵以其母老而善饭，常生厌弃之意。其妇王氏事姑至孝，随侍益谨。癸酉四月，行至商州山中，午谓妇曰：“老母步履艰难，汝负担先行，俟我挟之徐走。”妇是其言，遂于前途息肩以待。午狂奔追及，妇问姑何在，午曰：“少顷即至矣。

    Explanation

    590

    Zhang Shanlai said: Seeking revenge by hiding in a chamber pot can also be called a determined and all-or-nothing approach. However, he ultimately failed to seek revenge and only transferred his anger to his neighbor. Why is this so?

    In the years of Ren Shen and Gui You during the reign of Emperor Kangxi, Xi'an suffered from prolonged famine, with rice costing a thousand coins per measure, and bodies were seen everywhere along the roads. Zhao Wu, a commoner from Weinan County, had sold all his children and grandchildren. He had only one mother and one wife at home, with no food to be found. He carried his pots and pans and went to Huguang to buy grain. Zhao considered his mother old and good at eating, often harboring feelings of disgust and abandonment. His wife Wang was filial to her mother-in-law, serving her with even greater care. In April of Gui You, they were traveling through Shangzhou in the mountains. Zhao told his wife, "My mother has difficulty walking. You carry the load and go ahead, wait for me to support her and walk slowly." Wang agreed, so she rested her burden ahead to wait. Zhao ran madly to catch up, and Wang asked where her mother-in-law was. Zhao said, "She will be here soon."

    591

    ”妇怒曰：“龙钟老人，何以令其独走！”以担授午，仍回旧路觅姑，午掌掴其妇数十，携担竟去。妇回至一僻所，见其姑面缚于树，以土塞口，气将绝矣。妇亟解姑缚，揠口中土，捧泉水灌之，乃苏。伛偻负姑行二里许，其夫已为虎噬，投担委衣，残胔狼籍。妇视而啼曰：“天乎！赵午大逆，遭此虎暴。非死于虎，死于神也！”道傍闻者，无不叹息，称妇之贤而快午之毙。是时商州守戴良佐散赈龙驹寨，妇负姑行久，色状馁疲，适经寨下。戴守召询，得其详，厚赐以金，令妇还渭南养姑。感泣而归。

    Explanation

    591

    The woman was furious and said, "This bent and aged man, why did you make him go alone?" She handed the burden to Sū and returned to the old path to look for her mother-in-law. Sū slapped his wife dozens of times and took the burden away. The woman returned to a secluded place and saw her mother-in-law bound face down to a tree with dirt stuffed in her mouth, her breath nearly gone. The woman quickly untied her mother-in-law, pulled out the dirt from her mouth, held spring water and poured it over her, and she revived. The woman bent over and carried her mother-in-law for about two miles. Her husband had already been devoured by a tiger, and the remains were scattered and bloody. The woman looked at the scene and wept, saying, "Alas! Sū committed such a great wrong, and now he has met this tiger's fury. It was not the tiger that killed him, but the divine will!" Those who heard her by the roadside all sighed and praised the woman's virtue while rejoicing in Sū's death. At that time, the governor of Shangzhou, Dai Liangzuo, was distributing relief in Longjuzhai. The woman, carrying her mother-in-law for a long time, looked hungry and tired, just passing by the camp. The governor summoned and inquired, learned the details, generously gave her gold, and ordered her to return to Weinan to take care of her mother-in-law. She was moved to tears and returned home.

    592

    英德县含洸司，有猎人负弓弩射于山。适雷雨骤至，隐身蓊翳。遥见数武外老树上盘绕巨蛇，长十余丈，首大于瓮。迅雷轰轰，将迫蛇，蛇仰首吐火上冲，红光如彗，雷渐引去。少顷，雷声甚怒，复迫蛇，蛇复吐火敌雷。猎人恶其猛毒，彀弓发弩，中其尾，蛇首顿缩。霹雳大震，蛇遂击死，而猎人亦惊仆矣。闻空中有语之者曰：“无恐，当即苏也。”良久，清醒还家。家人见其背有朱书“代天除暴，延寿二纪”八字，浣之不去。此康熙辛酉四月间事，今距射蛇时已二十余载，英德人言其雄健犹昔，盖天赐之龄，固未艾也。

    Explanation

    592

    In the county of Yingde, within the district of Hanguang, there was a hunter who carried a bow and crossbow and was hunting in the mountains. Suddenly, a fierce thunderstorm arrived, and he hid himself in the dense foliage. From a distance, he saw a huge snake coiled around an old tree, about ten to twelve zhang long, with a head larger than a jar. The thunder roared loudly, as if it were about to strike the snake. The snake raised its head and spewed fire upward, the red light resembling a comet, and the thunder gradually moved away. A short while later, the thunder grew even more intense, and it once again approached the snake. The snake, in turn, spewed fire to counter the thunder. The hunter, fearing its ferocity and venom, drew his bow and shot the snake, hitting it in the tail. The snake's head suddenly retracted. A loud clap of thunder struck, and the snake was killed, while the hunter was also startled and collapsed. He heard someone speaking from the air, saying, "Do not be afraid, you will soon regain consciousness." After a long time, he regained his senses and returned home. His family noticed that there was red writing on his back: "Acting on behalf of heaven to eliminate violence, extending life by two decades." The writing could not be washed off. This event occurred in the fourth month of the year 1721 during the reign of Emperor Kangxi. Now, more than twenty years have passed since the snake was shot, and the people of Yingde say that the hunter is still as strong and healthy as before, apparently due to a lifespan granted by heaven, indeed not yet finished.

    593

    余同学友王仔衡言：其亲某，以红纸作筒，封银三钱，致贺婚家。婚家返银，拆筒展视，忽变小虾蟆一头，眼若点朱，通体白如水精，莹洁空明，骨脏俱见，趯然从纸窝跃出。捕而藏之箧，晨夕玩弄，阅三日失去。广州陈弘泰，贷钱于人而征其息，其人将鬻虾蟆万头以偿，弘泰睹而心恻，命悉放之江中，遂与焚券。数月后，骑行夜归，路间有物，光焰闪铄，惊马不前。视之，乃尺许金虾蟆也。取以还家，自此益致饶裕。夫金银本无定质，变易不常，故其聚散，每因人心以为去留。天下之溺于富贵者，取之既非以义，守之又无其道，而欲据为子孙百世之业，不亦颠乎？

    Explanation

    593

    My fellow student and friend Wang Zaiheng told me that his relative once made a red paper tube, sealed three qian of silver inside, and sent it to congratulate a newly married family. The newly married family returned the silver, and when they opened the tube to inspect it, it suddenly transformed into a small toad. The toad had eyes like dots of cinnabar, a body as white as crystal, and was lustrous, clean, and transparent, with its bones and internal organs all visible. It leaped out of the paper tube with a sudden leap. They caught it and put it in a chest, playing with it morning and evening. After three days, it was lost.

    In Guangzhou, there was a man named Chen Hongtai who borrowed money from others and charged interest. One of his debtors intended to sell ten thousand toads to repay the debt. Chen Hongtai saw this and felt pity, so he ordered them all to be released into the river and burned the contract.

    Several months later, Chen Hongtai rode at night and returned home. On the road, he saw an object shining with flashes of light, which startled his horse and made it stop. He looked at it and saw that it was a golden toad about a foot long. He took it back home, and from then on, he became increasingly prosperous.

    Silver and gold originally have no fixed nature and change frequently. Thus, their accumulation and dispersion often depend on human intention. Those in the world who are obsessed with wealth and status obtain it not by righteousness and do not have the means to maintain it, yet they want to pass it down as a legacy for generations. Is this not absurd?

    594

    [张山来曰：若虾蟆不复化去，则尤胜阿堵物也。]
    　　名捕传　姚口口伯祥
    　　　　金坛王伯弢孝廉，自言丙午偕计至德州，见道旁有捕贼勾当，与州解相噪。问之，云：放马贼昼劫上供银若干，追之则死贼手，不追则死坐累。各相向呼天，泣数行下。然贼马尘起处，犹目力可望也。忽有夫妇两骑从他道来。诸捕咸相庆曰：“保定名捕至矣！当无忧也。”诸捕控名捕马，问从何来。言夫妇泰山进香耳。然名捕病甚，俯首鞍上。其妻亦短小好妇人，以皂罗覆面，手抱一婴儿。诸捕告之故，哀乞相助。名捕曰：“贼几人？”曰：“五人。”曰：“余病甚，吾妇往足矣。”妇摇手：“我不耐烦！”名捕嗔骂曰：“懒媳妇！今日不出手，只会火炕上搏老公乎？”妇面发红，便下马抱儿与夫，更束马肚，结缚裙靴，攘臂，袖一刀，长三尺许，光若镜也。夫言：“将我箭去。”妻曰：“吾弹固自胜。”言未讫，身已在马上，绝尘而去。诸捕皆奔马随之。

    Explanation

    594

    Zhang Shanlai said: "If the frog does not transform again, then it is even more superior than that object."

    The Story of the Famous Hunter　Yao Boxiang

    Wang Botao, a scholar-official from Jintan, said that in the year Bingwu (1896), he traveled to Dezhou along with others and saw a group of hunters on the side of the road, arguing with the state officials.

    He asked them, and they replied: "The horse thieves robbed the tribute money during the day. If we pursue them, we will die by their hands. If we do not pursue them, we will die by implication."

    They all looked at each other, called out to heaven, and tears streamed down their faces.

    However, the dust from the thieves' horses could still be seen with the naked eye.

    Suddenly, a couple arrived on horseback from another road.

    The hunters all rejoiced and said, "The famous hunter from Baoding has arrived! There is no need to worry."

    The hunters controlled the famous hunter's horse and asked where he came from.

    He replied that the couple were going to Mount Tai to offer incense.

    However, the famous hunter was very ill and leaned over the saddle.

    His wife was also a small, good-looking woman, covering her face with a black silk cloth and holding an infant.

    The hunters told him the reason and begged for his help.

    The famous hunter asked, "How many thieves are there?" They replied, "Five."

    He said, "I am very ill. My wife alone is enough."

    His wife shook her head and said, "I am not in the mood!"

    The famous hunter scolded and said, "Lazy wife! If you don't act today, will you just fight with your husband on the kang?"

    His wife's face turned red, and she dismounted, handed the child to her husband, tied up her waist, tied up her skirt and boots, rolled up her sleeves, and pulled out a knife, about three feet long, shiny like a mirror.

    Her husband said, "Take my arrows." She replied, "My bullets are strong enough."

    Before she finished speaking, she was already on her horse, disappearing like dust.

    The hunters all rode their horses to follow.

    595

    须臾，追及贼骑。妇人发声清亮，顺风呼贼曰：“我保定名捕某妻，为此官钱，故来相索。宜急置，毋尝我丸也！”贼言：“丈夫平平，牝猪敢尔！”贼发五弓射妇。妇从马上以弹弓拨箭，箭悉落地。急发一弹，杀一人。四人拔刀拟妇，妇接战，挥斥如意，复斫杀一人。三人惧，少却。妇更言曰：“急置银，舁两尸去。俱死无益也！”三人下马乞命，置银，以二尸缚马上而逸。

    Explanation

    595

    In a moment, she caught up to the bandits on horseback. The woman's voice was clear and bright as she called out to them, taking advantage of the wind: "I am the wife of a famous official from Baoding, and I have come to reclaim this official money. You should quickly surrender, or you will taste my pellet!" The bandits retorted, "You are just an average man, and a female pig dares to do this!" They fired five bows at the woman. The woman, sitting on her horse, used a slingshot to deflect the arrows, and all the arrows fell to the ground. She quickly fired one pellet and killed one person. Four men drew their swords at her. The woman engaged in combat, striking with ease, and again chopped and killed one person. Three of the bandits were afraid and retreated slightly. The woman spoke again, "Quickly surrender the silver, carry the two bodies away. Both dying is of no benefit!" The three men dismounted and begged for mercy, placing the silver down, and tied the two bodies to their horses before fleeing.

    596

    未几，诸捕至，舁银而还。此妇犹旖旎寻常，善刀藏之，下马遍拜诸捕曰：“妮子着力不健，纵此三寇，要是裙襦伎俩耳。”州守为治酒，宴劳五日而去。
    　　　　姚伯祥曰：此皆伯弢口授于予，予为之记，所谓舌端有写生手也。
    　　　　[张山来曰：名捕捕贼，尚不足奇。妙在名捕之妇有此手段，真可敬也！
    　　　　想见此妇火炕上搏老公时，必有异乎人者。一笑。]
    　　南游记　孙嘉淦锡公
    　　　　游亦多术矣。昔禹乘四载，刊山通道以治水。孔子、孟子周游列国，以行其道。太史公览四海名山大川，以奇其文。他如好大之君，东封西狩以荡心；山人羽客，穷幽极远以行怪；士人京宦之贫而无事者，投刺四方以射财。此游之大较也，余皆无当焉。盖余之少也，淡于名利，而中无所得，不能自适，每寄情于山水。既登第，授馆职，匏系都门，非所好也。

    Explanation

    596

    Not long after, the captors arrived and carried the silver back. This woman was still graceful but ordinary, she put away the knife and dismounted to bow to all the captors, saying, "This girl was not very strong, she let these three bandits go, it was just the tricks of a woman." The governor prepared a feast and entertained them for five days before they left.

    Yao Boxiang said: "All of this was dictated to me by Bo Tao, and I have recorded it, which is what is meant by having a skilled hand at the tip of the tongue."

    [ Zhang Shanlai said: "It is not surprising for a famous detective to catch thieves. What is wonderful is that the wife of the famous detective has such means, she is truly admirable!
    I imagine that when this woman wrestles with her husband on the heated brick bed, she must be different from others. A smile.]

    Southern Tour Record by Sun Jiagan Xigong

    There are many ways to travel. In ancient times, Yu rode on four vehicles, carving mountains and clearing paths to control the waters. Confucius and Mencius traveled through the states to spread their teachings. Sima Qian toured the famous mountains and great rivers of the four seas to enrich his writings. Other than that, there are those who love grandeur, who go east and west to expand their hearts; there are mountain men and feathered guests who go to the remotest places to perform strange deeds; there are poor officials who have nothing to do and send their letters to all directions to seek wealth. These are the general ways of traveling, the rest are not relevant.
