One day, he rode to his hometown and encountered a sudden flood. The boatman first ferried the horse, then returned to ferry Wang Xingsi. When they reached the middle of the river, the wind picked up and the boat capsized. The horse ran from the shore into the terrifying waves, reaching its master, and in the chaos, it suddenly saved him from drowning.

    Bi Zaiyu was a general's family from Yanzhou. During the Kai Xi period, he repeatedly achieved military success. The Jin people recognized his flag and avoided him. Later, he lived in Zha. He had a warhorse named 'Heida' (Black Tiger), which was exceptionally swift and strong, and only its master could ride it. After Bi Zaiyu's death, his family tied the horse with a strong rope in the stable. Just then, there was a ceremony to welcome the gods at the Yue Temple, and the horse heard the sound of drums and gongs, thinking it was going to battle. The horse neighed, rushed out, breaking through the walls. His family was worried that it might hurt someone, so they ordered ten strong soldiers to pull it back. They then gently warned it, saying, "The general is dead, don't cause trouble for our family!" The horse raised its ears to listen, shed tears, and let out several mournful neighs before dying.

    678

    龙泉县有白马墓，即开国勋臣胡公深所乘之桃花马也。公以征陈友定，遇害，其马驰归门外，悲嘶殒绝。夫人义之，因葬焉，号为“白马墓”。
    　　　　天顺中吴之嘉定姚生，素心险异，尝构怨于母弟陆某。陆充粮长，乘马自本都夜归。姚候至中途无人，操刃伏于桥下。马亦觉之，至桥，踯躅不进。陆加鞭楚，马始进，而已杀桥下矣。是夜，月暗更幽，寂无知者。马逸归，对陆妻惊嘶不已，若有诉状。妻知夫必死非命，持灯尾马后，至一旷野，夫果死焉。妻又谓马曰：“吾夫尸虽得，然正犯不得，何以雪冤？”马即前行，首撞姚门。见姚，啮之蹴之。其妻执以闻官，乃弃姚市。

    Explanation

    678

    Longquan County has a tomb dedicated to a white horse, which was the Tao Hua horse ridden by the founding minister Hu Gong Shen. Hu Gong Shen was killed during a campaign against Chen Youding, and his horse galloped back to the gate, neighing mournfully until it died. The lady found the horse's actions commendable and buried it, naming the tomb "White Horse Tomb."

    In the Tian Shun period, there was a man named Yao Sheng from Jiading in Wu. He had a hidden, dangerous nature and had created a grudge with his maternal younger brother Lu. Lu was a grain collector and rode a horse back from his home at night. Yao Sheng waited until they were alone on the road and lay in wait under a bridge with a weapon. The horse sensed Yao Sheng and hesitated to move forward when they reached the bridge. Lu lashed the horse, and it finally moved forward, but Yao Sheng had already killed him under the bridge. That night, the moon was dark and the night was deep, and no one knew what had happened. The horse escaped and returned home, neighing in alarm to Lu's wife, as if to file a complaint. The wife knew her husband must have died an unnatural death and followed the horse with a lamp, leading to an open field where her husband was indeed found dead. She then said to the horse, "Although I have found my husband's body, I cannot report the crime directly. How can I clear our family's name?" The horse immediately moved forward, butting against Yao Sheng's door. When it saw Yao Sheng, it bit and kicked him. Lu's wife took Yao Sheng to the authorities, and he was executed in public.

    679

    孙办事家有马生驹，甚奇。令牡交其母以传种，子母俱不肯，乃涂其身以泥而交焉。及洗出本色，母子皆跳躅以死，人号为“烈马”云。
    　　　　流寇破河内，县尹丁运泰骂贼被磔。所乘马，贼骑以入县，至堂下，大嘶人立，狂逸不可制，竟触墙死。
    　　　　和硕亲王有良马曰“克勒”，犹汉言枣骝马也。高七尺，自首至尾长可丈有咫，耳际肉角寸许，腹下旋毛若鳞甲然，翘骏倍常，识者谓是龙种。王甚爱之。王薨，马蹢躅哀鸣，未几随毙。

    Explanation

    679

    Stranger Sun's household had a horse that gave birth to a foal, a very unusual occurrence. When ordered to mate with its mother to pass on its lineage, both the stallion and the mare refused. They were then coated with mud and mated. After washing off the mud, they both leaped and trampled to death. People called it a "fiery horse."

    During the time of the bandit invasion of Henan, County Magistrate Ding Yuntai cursed the bandits and was crucified. His horse, which the bandits tried to ride into the county, reared up and let out a loud neigh, becoming uncontrollable and eventually crashing into a wall and dying.

    The Prince of the First Rank had a fine horse named "Keler," which is still called a chestnut bay horse in Chinese. It stood seven feet tall and measured about ten feet and a bit more from head to tail. It had small fleshy horns about an inch long near its ears and curling hair on its belly that looked like scales or armor. It was extraordinarily swift and agile, and those who recognized it said it was a dragon's offspring. The prince loved it very much. After the prince's death, the horse wept and neighed mournfully, and soon after, it too died.

    680

    骡
    　　　　明末张贼破蜀城，蜀藩率其子女宫人投井死。王所乘白骡踯躅其旁，亦跳入殉焉。后樵苏者当阴雨暝晦时，于蜀宫故址，往往见白骡出没蔓草间。
    　　　　张行人鹤洲，讼系西曹，以常所乘骡抵逋于人。骡悲鸣不食。一日，堕其新主，自逸归。王西樵吏部与张同患难，目击其事，感之，作《义骡行》。
    　　　　羊
    　　　　邠州屠者安姓家，有牝羊并羔。一日欲刲其母，缚上架之次，其羔忽向安前双跪前膝，两目涕零。安惊异良久，遂致刀于地，去呼童椎，共事刲宰。及回，遽失刀，乃为羔衔之，致墙根下，而卧其上。屠遍索方觉，遂并释之，放生焉。
    　　　　猫
    　　　　唐时北平王家猫，有生子同日者，其一死焉，有二子饮于死母，母且死，其鸣咿咿。其一方乳己子，若闻之，起而听，走而救，衔其一置于其栖，又往如之，反而乳之，若己子然。

    Explanation

    680

    The Donkey

    In the late Ming Dynasty, when the rebel Zhang captured the city of Shu, the prince of Shu and his family and servants committed suicide by jumping into a well. The white donkey that the prince rode hesitated nearby and then jumped in to sacrifice itself. Later, woodcutters often saw the white donkey appearing and disappearing among the overgrown grass at the ruins of the Shu palace during heavy rains and darkness.

    Zhang Hézhou, a traveler, was involved in a lawsuit and detained in the western office. He used his usual donkey to settle a debt with someone. The donkey mournfully neighed and refused to eat. One day, it fell off its new owner and escaped back home. Wang Xiqiao, the Minister of Personnel, who shared hardships with Zhang Hézhou, witnessed the event and was moved by it, composing a poem titled "The Virtuous Donkey."

    The Sheep

    In the house of a butcher named An in Binzhou, there was a female sheep and her lamb. One day, he intended to slaughter the mother sheep and tied her up on the rack. The lamb suddenly knelt with both front knees before An, with tears streaming from its eyes. An was surprised and amazed for a long time and then placed the knife on the ground, going to call a child with a mallet to help with the slaughter. When he returned, he suddenly lost the knife, which turned out to be taken by the lamb and placed at the base of the wall, with the lamb lying on top of it. The butcher searched everywhere and only then realized what had happened. He then released both the lamb and the mother sheep and let them live.

    The Cat

    During the Tang Dynasty, in the household of the Prince of Beiping, a cat gave birth to kittens on the same day. One of them died, and two kittens nursed from the dead mother. The mother was dying and made a soft crying sound. The other cat was nursing her own kittens. As if she heard the dying mother's cry, she got up and listened, then ran to help. She carried one of the kittens to her nest and went back to do the same, returning to nurse it as if it were her own.

    681

    姑苏齐门外，陆墓一小民负官租出避。家独一猫，催租者持去，卖之阊门铺商。忽小民过其地，跃入怀，为铺中所夺，辄悲鸣，顾视不已。至夜，衔一绫帨，内有金五两余，投之而去。
    　　　　仁鱼
    　　　　海中有“仁鱼”，尝负一小儿登岸，偶以鬐触伤儿，儿死。鱼不胜悲痛，亦触石死。
    　　　　鳖
    　　　　宋傅庆中家得一大鳖，其婢不忍杀，放之沟中。年余，后婢有病，将卒。夜有大鳖，被泥登婢胸冰之，遂愈。
    　　　　黄德瓌家人烹鳖，将箬笠覆其釜，揭见鳖仰把其笠，背皆蒸烂，然头足犹能伸缩。家人愍之，潜放河泾间。后因患热，将殛，德瓌徙于河边屋中将养。夜有一物，徐徐上身，觉甚冷。及曙，能视，胸臆悉涂淤泥。其鳖在土间，三曳三顾而去。即日病瘥。

    Explanation

    681

    Outside the Qi Gate of Suzhou, a poor man from Lu Village fled due to unpaid taxes. He had only one cat at home, which was taken away by the tax collector and sold to a shopkeeper in the Changle Gate. Suddenly, the man passed by the shop, the cat jumped into his arms, was taken by the shop, and continuously cried out and looked back. At night, the cat carried a silk handkerchief containing more than five taels of gold and threw it away and left.

    In the sea, there is a "benevolent fish." Once, it carried a small child ashore, accidentally injuring the child with its fin. The child died, and the fish could not bear the grief and also died by touching a stone.

    In the family of Cao Fuqing, a large turtle was obtained. The maid could not bear to kill it and released it into the ditch. After a year, the maid fell ill and was about to die. At night, a large turtle, covered in mud, climbed onto the maid's chest and cooled her, and she recovered.

    The family of Huang De gui cooked the turtle, covering the pot with a bamboo hat. When the pot was lifted, the turtle was seen holding the hat upside down, its back being all steamed and burned, yet its head and feet could still move. The family pitied the turtle and secretly released it into the river. Later, due to a fever, they were about to be struck down and moved to a house by the river to recuperate. At night, there was an object that slowly climbed onto the body, felt very cold. By dawn, they could see, the chest and abdomen were completely covered in mud. The turtle was in the soil, pulled and looked back three times before leaving. The illness immediately improved.

    682

    蟹
    　　　　松江干山人沈宗正，每深秋，设簖于塘，取蟹入馔。一日，见二三蟹相附而起，近视之，一蟹八腕皆脱，不能行，二蟹舁以过簖。沈叹其义，遂命折簖，终身不复食蟹。
    　　　　蝌蚪
    　　　　绍兴郡丞张公佐治，擢金华守。去郡，至一处，见蝌蚪无数，夹道鸣噪，皆昂首若有诉。异之，下舆步视，而蝌蚪皆跳踯为前导。至田间，三尸叠焉。公有力，手挈二尸起，其下一尸微动，汤灌之，逡巡间复活，曰：“我商也，道见二人肩两筐适市，皆蝌蚪也。意伤之，购以放生。二人复曰：『此皆浅水，虽放，人必复获；前有清渊，乃放生池也。』我从之至此，不虞挥斧，遂被害。二仆随后尚远，有腰缠，必诱至此，并杀而夺金也。”丞命急捕之，人金皆得。以属其守石公昆玉，一讯皆吐实，抵死，腰缠归商。

    Explanation

    682

    Crabs
    　　　　Shen Zongzheng of Gan Shan in Songjiang, every deep autumn, sets up a bamboo fence in the pond to catch crabs for food. One day, he saw two or three crabs climb on top of each other to rise. Upon closer inspection, one crab had all eight claws off and could not move. Two other crabs carried it over the fence. Shen was moved by their loyalty and decided to break the fence, vowing never to eat crabs again.
    　　　　Tadpoles
    　　　　Zhang Gongzhao, the magistrate of Shaoxing, was promoted to the position of governor of Jinhua. On his way out of the city, he came across a place where countless tadpoles were chirping and making noise in a line, all raising their heads as if to complain. He found this strange and got out of his carriage to walk and look. The tadpoles all jumped and hopped as a guide. He reached a field where three bodies were stacked on top of each other. Zhang, being strong, lifted two of the bodies, and the one underneath moved slightly. He poured water on him, and in a moment, the person revived and said, "I am a merchant. On the road, I saw two people carrying two baskets to the market, both filled with tadpoles. Feeling sorry for them, I bought them to release. The two people then said, 'These are shallow waters; even if we release them, people will surely catch them again. There is a clear pond ahead, which is a place to release living creatures.' I followed them there, not expecting to be hit by an axe, and was thus harmed. My two servants were still far behind. They must have lured us here and killed us to steal the money tied around our waists." Zhang immediately ordered them to be captured, and the people and the money were recovered. He handed the case over to his subordinate, Shi Gong Qunyu, who, after one interrogation, got all the facts, and they were sentenced to death. The money was returned to the merchant.

    683

    蜂
    　　　　正德间，镇江北固山下，有群蜂拥王出游，遇鸷鸟攫杀之。群蜂环守不去，数日俱死。杨邃庵相公一清，令家伻瘗焉，表其上曰“义蜂”，亲作文祭之。
    　　　　太仓张用良，素恶胡蜂螫人，见即扑杀之。尝见一飞虫，投一蛛网，蛛束缚之甚急。忽一蜂来螫蛛，蛛避。蜂数含水湿虫，久之得脱去。因感蜂义，自是不复杀蜂。

    Explanation

    683

    Final Translation

    1. 蜂

    正德间，镇江北固山下，有群蜂拥王出游，遇鸷鸟攫杀之。群蜂环守不去，数日俱死。杨邃庵相公一清，令家伻瘗焉，表其上曰“义蜂”，亲作文祭之。

    Translation:
    During the reign of Emperor Zhengde, at the foot of Beigushan Mountain in Zhenjiang, a swarm of bees led by a queen went out for an outing and encountered a fierce bird that seized and killed the queen. The bees surrounded and guarded the site without leaving, and after several days, they all died. Yang Shuo'an, the official known as "One Clear," ordered a household servant to bury them, erected a tablet on the site and called it "Righteous Bees," and personally wrote an article to commemorate them.

    2. 太仓张用良，素恶胡蜂螫人，见即扑杀之。尝见一飞虫，投一蛛网，蛛束缚之甚急。忽一蜂来螫蛛，蛛避。蜂数含水湿虫，久之得脱去。因感蜂义，自是不复杀蜂。

    Translation:
    In Taicang, there was a man named Zhang Yongliang who had always hated wasps for stinging people and would kill them immediately upon seeing them. Once, he saw a flying insect fall into a spider's web, and the spider bound it very tightly. Suddenly, a bee came to sting the spider, causing the spider to retreat. The bee carried water to moisten the insect, and after some time, the insect was able to escape. Moved by the bees' righteousness, from then on, he no longer killed bees.

    This final translation aims to be both faithful to the original Classical Chinese and natural in English, incorporating the insights from the pre-drafting research to handle idiomatic expressions, cultural allusions, syntactic ambiguities, and proper nouns appropriately.

    684

    [张山来曰：佛氏谓蠢动含灵，皆有佛性。今读此录，不其然欤？]
    　　海天行记　钮琇玉樵
    　　　　海忠介公之孙述祖，倜傥负奇气，适逢中原多故，遂不屑事举子业，慨焉有乘桴之想。斥其千金家产，治一大舶，其舶首尾长二十八丈，以象宿；房分六十四口，以象卦；篷张二十四叶，以象气；桅高二十五丈，曰擎天柱，上为二斗，以象日月。治之三年乃成，自谓独出奇制，以此乘长风破万里浪，无难也。濒海贾客三十八人，赁其舟，载货互市海外诸国，以述祖主之。
    　　　　崇祯壬午二月，扬帆出洋。行至薄暮，飓风陡作，雪浪粘天，蛟螭之属，腾绕左右，舵师失色。随风飘至一处，昏霾莫辨何地。须臾，云开风定，遥见六七官人，高冠大带，拱立水次。侍从百辈，状貌丑怪，皆鱼鳞银甲，拥巨螯之剑，荷长须之戟，秉炬张灯，若有所伺。不觉舟忽抵岸，官人各喜，跃上舟环视曰：“是可用已。”即问船主为谁。述祖不解其意，匆遽声诺。

    Explanation

    684

    Translation:

    Zhang Shanlai said: According to Buddhism, all living beings with consciousness possess Buddha nature. Reading this account, is it not so?
    Hai Tianshang
    Hai Tianshang
    The grandson of Hai Zhongjie, Shuzhu, was a bold and unconventional man who, during a time of turmoil in Central China, lost interest in pursuing the imperial examinations and developed a desire to live a simple life. He spent his thousand gold worth of property to build a large ship. The ship was 28 zhang long from bow to stern, symbolizing the twenty-eight lunar mansions; it had 64 compartments, symbolizing the sixty-four hexagrams of the I Ching; its sails were divided into 24 sections, symbolizing the twenty-four solar terms; its mast was 25 zhang tall, called the "upholding the sky," with two dippers at the top, symbolizing the Big Dipper and Little Dipper. It took three years to build, and he believed it was a unique creation that could ride the long wind and break through the waves for ten thousand miles without difficulty. Thirty-eight merchants from the coastal areas rented the ship, loaded goods, and traveled to various countries overseas, with Shuzhu as the master.
    In the second month of the Renwu year of the Chongzhen era, they set sail. By late afternoon, a sudden typhoon arose, with snow-like waves reaching the sky, and dragons and other sea creatures swirling around the ship, causing the helmsman to lose his composure. Driven by the wind, they were carried to a place where the sky was dark and the location was indistinguishable. Soon, the clouds parted and the wind calmed, and they saw six or seven officials standing in a line by the water, wearing tall hats and wide sashes. There were hundreds of attendants with grotesque appearances, all clad in silver armor like fish scales, carrying swords with large claws and spears with long beards, holding torches and lanterns, as if waiting for something. Unaware, the ship suddenly reached the shore. The officials were delighted and jumped onto the ship, circling it and saying, "This is usable." They immediately asked who the ship's owner was. Shuzhu did not understand their meaning and hastily acknowledged.

    685

    诘朝，呼述祖同入见王。约行三里许，夹道皎如玉山，无纤毫尘土，至一阙门，门有二黄龙守之，周遭垣墙，悉以水晶叠成，光明映彻，可鉴毛发。述祖私念曰：“此殆龙宫也！”又逾门三重，方及大殿。其制与人间帝王之居相似，而辉煌嶻嶪，广设千人之馔，高容十丈之旗，不足言矣。王甫升殿，首以红巾围两肉角，衣黄绣袍，髯长垂腹。众官进奏曰：“前文下所司取二舟，久不见至，今有自来一舟，敢以闻。”王曰：“旧例二舟陈设贡物，今少一，奈何？”众曰：“贡期已迫，臣等细阅此舟，制度暗合浑仪，以达天衢，允宜利涉。且复宽大新洁，若将贡物摒挡，俟到王宫，以次陈设，似无不可。”王允奏，曰：“徙其凡货凡人，涤以符水，速行勿迟。”众唯唯下殿，仍回至舟，将人货尽押上岸，置之宫西琅玕池内。

    Explanation

    685

    The next morning, Shuzi was called to enter and see the king together. After traveling about three miles, the path was bright and clear like a jade mountain, without the slightest trace of dust. They arrived at a grand gate, which was guarded by two yellow dragons. The surrounding walls were all made of stacked crystal, bright and clear, capable of reflecting even the finest hairs. Shuzi thought to himself, "This must be a dragon's palace!" They passed through three gates before reaching the grand hall. Its structure was similar to that of a human king's residence, but it was magnificent and towering, with food prepared for a thousand people and flags ten zhang long, which were high enough to hold. The king had just ascended the hall, wearing a red scarf around his two fleshy horns, a yellow embroidered robe, and long whiskers hanging down to his belly. The officials presented their report, saying, "The officials below were supposed to fetch two boats, but they haven't arrived yet, now there is one boat that has come on its own, we dare to report this." The king said, "By old custom, two boats were used to display tribute goods, now there is one less, what shall we do?" The officials replied, "The tribute deadline is approaching, we carefully examined this boat, and found that its design subtly matches the celestial instrument, suitable for crossing the heavens. It is also spacious, new, and clean, if we arrange the tribute goods, wait until we reach the king's palace, and then set them up in order, it seems acceptable." The king agreed to the report and said, "Move all common goods and people, cleanse them with talismanic water, and proceed quickly without delay." The officials nodded and left the hall, returned to the boat, and moved all people and goods onto the shore, placing them in the Lánggān Pool to the west of the palace.

    686

    唯述祖不肯前，私问曰：“贡将焉往？”众曰：“贡上天耳。”述祖曰：“述祖虽炎陬贱民，而志切云霄，常恨羽翼未生，九阍难叩；幸遘奇缘，亦愿随往。”众曰：“汝浊世凡人也，去则恐犯天令，不可。”中有一官曰：“汝可具所生年月日时来。”述祖亟书以进。官与众言：“此人命有天禄，且系忠直之裔。姑许之。”俄顷，舁贡物者数百人，络绎而至。赍贡官先以符水遍洒舟中，然后奉金叶表文，供之中楼。次有押贡官二员，将诸宝物安顿。述祖私窥贡单，内开：赤珊瑚林一座，大小共五十株；黄珊瑚林一座，大小共七十株，高者俱一丈四五尺。夜光珠一百颗，火齐珠二百颗，圆大一寸五分；鲛绡五百匹，灵梭锦五百匹；碧瑟瑟二十斛。红靺鞨二十斛；玻璃镜一百具，圆广三尺，各重四十斤；玉屑一千斗，金浆一百器，五色石一万方。其他殊名异品，不能悉记。

    Explanation

    686

    述祖不愿前行，私下问道：“贡品将前往何处？”众人回答：“贡品将献给天庭。”述祖说：“我虽是偏远之地的卑微之人，但心向高远，常恨自己没有翅膀，难以叩开九重天门；幸好遇到此等奇缘，也愿随同前往。”众人说：“你不过是一个世俗凡人，若去恐怕会触犯天规，不行。”其中一位官员说：“你可写下你的出生年月日时。”述祖急忙写下并呈上。官员对众人说：“此人命运中有天赐的福禄，且是忠诚正直之人的后代。暂且允许他随行。”不久，数百人抬着贡品络绎不绝地到来。贡官首先用符水洒遍船中，然后呈上金叶表文，供奉在中楼。接着有两位押贡官，将各种宝物安置妥当。述祖偷偷查看贡单，上面列有：红珊瑚林一座，大小共五十株；黄珊瑚林一座，大小共七十株，高者约四尺五寸。夜光珠一百颗，火珠二百颗，直径约一寸五分；鲛绡五百匹，灵梭锦五百匹；碧瑟瑟二十斛。红靺鞨二十斛；玻璃镜一百面，直径三尺，每面重四十斤；玉屑一千斗，金浆一百器，五色石一万方。其他各种奇异物品，无法一一记下。

    687

    安顿已毕，大伐鼍鼓三通，乃始启行。逆风而上，两巨鱼夹舟若飞。白波摇漾，练静镜平，路无坦险，时无昼夜。中途石壁千仞，截流而立，其上金书“天人河海分界”六大字。众指示述祖曰：“昔张骞乘槎，未能过此。今汝得远泛银潢，岂非盛事！”述祖俯首称谢。
    　　　　食顷之间，咸云：“南天关在望矣。”既而及关，赍贡官、押贡官各整朝服，舁宝诸役，俱易赭色长衣，亦令述祖衣之，登岸陈设。足之所履，皆软金地，间以瑶石嵌成异彩。仰视琼阙璿堂，绛楼碧阁，俱在飘渺之中，若近若远，不可测量。门下天卿四员，冕笏传旨，令赍贡官入昊天门，于神霄殿前进表行礼。述祖及众役叩首门外，唯闻乐音缭绕，香气氤氲，飘忽不断而已。随有星冠岳帔者二人，为接贡官，察收贡物，引押贡官亦入，行礼毕，玉音宣问南方民事，北方兵象，语甚繁，不尽述。各赐宴于恬波馆，谢恩而出。于是集众登舟。

    Explanation

    687

    After the preparations were complete, they beat the crocodile drum three times to signal the start of their journey. Against the wind, two giant fish swam alongside the boat as if flying. The white waves gently swayed, like a calm mirror, the road had no smooth or dangerous parts, and time had no day or night. In the middle of the journey, a thousand-foot-high cliff stood in the middle of the stream, with six golden characters inscribed on it, reading "the boundary between heaven and earth, river and sea." The crowd pointed to Shu Zu and said, "In the past, Zhang Qian could not pass this point. Now you can far sail the Milky Way, isn't this a great event!" Shu Zu bowed and thanked them.

    Within a meal's time, they all said, "The Southern Heaven Gate is in sight." Soon they reached the gate, and the officials carrying the tribute and escorting it each dressed in their official robes. The bearers of the treasures and other servants all changed into long brownish-red clothes, and Shu Zu was also ordered to wear it. They landed and set up for the ceremony. The ground they walked on was soft golden, with jade stones embedded in patterns, creating a magical scenery. Looking up, they saw jade pavilions, crystal halls, vermilion towers, and green pavilions, all seeming close and far away, beyond measurement. Four heavenly officials under the gate, with crowns and tablets, conveyed the imperial command, ordering the officials carrying the tribute to enter the Gate of the Supreme Heaven and present their memorial at the Palace of the Divine Sky, performing the ritual. Shu Zu and the other servants bowed at the door, hearing only the continuous sounds of music and the fragrance of incense, both ethereal and enchanting. Two officials with star crowns and mountain cloaks received the tribute and inspected the offerings, leading the escorting officials to enter and complete the ritual. The imperial voice asked about the civil affairs of the south and the military situation of the north, the speech was very long and detailed, not fully described. Each was given a banquet at the Tranquil Wave Pavilion and thanked the emperor before leaving. Then they gathered the people and boarded the boat.

    688

    述祖假寐片时，恍忽不知几千万里，已还故处。因启领所押货物与同行诸人。王下令曰：“述祖之舟，曾入天界，不可复归人寰。众伴在池，宜令一见。”则三十八人，俱化为鱼，唯首未变。述祖大恸，前取舟官引至一室，慰谕之曰：“汝同行人，命应皆葬鱼腹，其得身为鱼，幸也。汝以假舟之故，贷汝一死。尚何悲哉？候有闽船过此，当俾汝归。”日给饮食如常。

    Explanation

    688

    Shuzi took a short nap, and in a dazed state, he traveled an indeterminate distance and returned to his original place. He then opened and inspected the goods under his charge and shared them with his fellow travelers. The king issued an order, saying, "Shuzi's boat had once entered the heavenly realm and cannot return to the human world. The companions are in the pond; it is appropriate to let them be seen once." Then, thirty-eight people all transformed into fish, with only their heads remaining unchanged. Shuzi greatly mourned, and the boat official led him to a room, comforting and instructing him, saying, "Your companions were destined to be buried in the bellies of fish; it is fortunate that they became fish. Because of the boat's temporary nature, your life is spared. Why should you still grieve? Wait until a ship from Fujian passes by, and you will be allowed to return." Daily provisions are provided as usual.

    689

    居久之，忽有报者曰：“闽船已到。”王召见，赐白黑珠一囊，曰：“以此偿造舟之价。”命小艇送附闽船，抵琼山还家。壬午之十二月也。家人蚤闻覆溺之信，设主发丧，乍见述祖，惊喜逾望。述祖亦不言所以，但云狂风败舟，幸凭“擎天柱”遇救得免。次年入广州，出囊中珠，鬻于番贾，获赀无算，买田终老。康熙丙子，粤僧方趾麟亲访述祖，具得其详。时述祖年已九十六，貌如五十岁人。
    　　　　[张山来曰：若非有年月姓名，便如读《太平广记》矣。
    　　　　先君尝疑李賀《白玉楼记》，谓九州万国语言文字，各不相同。今观此，则上天果与中华同矣。余谓长吉事属荒唐，今读此文，则是实有其事。但不识所谓“天人河海分界”六大字，以及贡单所列，为篆乎，为楷乎？为中国文字乎，为各国文字乎？真不可晓。]

    Explanation

    689

    After a long time, suddenly there was a messenger who reported, "The Fujian ship has arrived." The king summoned him, bestowed a bag of white and black pearls, and said, "Use this to pay for the cost of building the boat." He ordered a small boat to accompany the Fujian ship to Qiongshan and return home. It was the twelfth month of the year Renwu. The family had heard early about the news of the shipwreck, set up a spirit tablet and held a funeral. Suddenly, they saw Shuzi, who was more surprised and delighted than expected. Shuzi also did not explain the cause but said that a strong wind capsized the boat, and they were rescued with the help of the "Sky Support Pillar." The following year, he entered Guangzhou, took out the pearls from the bag, sold them to foreign merchants, gained an incalculable amount of wealth, and bought land to live out his days. In the year Bingzi of the Kangxi era, a Buddhist monk named Fang Zilin from Guangdong personally visited Shuzi, getting all the details. At that time, Shuzi was 96 years old but looked like a fifty-year-old person.

    [Commentary by Zhang Shanlai: If it were not for the date, year, and name, it would be like reading the Taiping Guangji.
    My father once doubted Li He's White Jade Pavilion Record, saying that the languages and scripts of the nine provinces and myriad countries are all different. Now, seeing this, it is true that the heavens indeed align with China. I believe that Li He's story is too fantastic, but reading this text, it seems to be a real event. However, I do not understand the meaning of the six characters "Divine Human River Sea Boundary," as well as the items listed on the tribute list—are they in seal script, regular script, Chinese characters, or the scripts of other countries? Truly, it is not understandable.]

    690

    虞初新志卷十九
    　　七奇图说　西洋南怀仁
    　　　　上古制造弘工，纪载有七，所谓“天下七奇”者是也：
    　　　　巴必鸾城。
    　　　　铜人巨像。
    　　　　尖形高台。
    　　　　茅索禄王茔墓。
    　　　　供月祠庙。
    　　　　木星人形。
    　　　　法罗海岛高台。
    　　　　公乐场附，海舶附。
    　　　　一、亚细亚洲巴必鸾城：瑟弥辣米德王后，创造京都城池。形势矩方，每方长五十里，周围计三百里，城门共一百处，门皆以净铜为之。城高十九丈，阔厚四丈八尺，以美石砌成。城楼上有园囿树木诸景，引接山水，涌流如小河然。造工者每日三十万人。
    　　　　二、铜人巨像：乐德海岛铜铸一人，高三十丈，安置海口。其手指一人不能围抱，两足踏两石台，跨下高广，能容大舶经过。左手持灯，夜则点照，引海舶认识港口，以便丛泊。铜人内空，从足至手，有螺旋梯升上点灯。造工者每日千余人，凡十二年乃成。

    Explanation

    690

    New Records of Yu Ch’iu, Volume Nineteen
    Illustrated Account of the Seven Wonders by Ferdinand Verbiest (Western)

    In ancient times, there were seven great constructions recorded, known as the "Seven Wonders of the World":

    1. The City of Babilun.

    2. The Giant Bronze Statue.

    3. The Pointed High Platform.

    4. The Tomb of King Maosuluo.

    5. The Temple Dedicated to the Moon.

    6. The Figure of Jupiter.

    7. The High Platform on the Island of Faro.

    8. Public Entertainment Venues and Sea Vessels.

    9. The City of Babilun in Asia: Queen Sermeredemid created the capital city. The city's layout is square and regular, with each side measuring fifty li, making the total circumference three hundred li. There are one hundred gates, all made of pure copper. The city walls are nineteen zhang high and four zhang eight chi thick, constructed with fine stones. The city walls feature gardens, trees, and scenic views, integrating water and mountains, with flowing water resembling a small river. Thirty thousand workers were employed daily to construct the city.

    10. The Giant Bronze Statue on the Island of Ledehai: A large bronze statue was cast on the island of Ledehai, standing thirty zhang tall and placed at the harbor entrance. The statue's fingers are so large that one person cannot encircle them, and its feet rest on two stone platforms, with a high and wide base that allows large ships to pass beneath it. The left hand holds a lamp, which is lit at night to guide ships to the harbor, ensuring safe anchorage. The statue is hollow inside, with a spiral staircase leading up to the top for lighting. Over a thousand workers were employed daily, and it took twelve years to complete the statue.

    691

    三、利未亚洲厄日多国孟斐府尖形高台：多禄茂王所建，地基矩方，每方一里，周围四里；台高二百五十级，每级宽二丈八尺五寸，高二尺五寸；顶上宽容五十人。造工者每日三十六万人。
    　　　　四、亚细亚洲嘉略省茅索禄王茔墓：亚尔德弥细亚王后，追念其夫王，建造茔墓。下层矩方，四面各有贵美石柱二十六株。穿廊圆拱，各宽七丈余。内有石梯至顶，顶上铜辇一乘，铜马二匹，茅索禄王像一尊。其奇异：一制度，二崇高，三精工，四质料纯细白石筑造。将毕，王后忆念其夫王，怅闷而殂。
    　　　　五、亚细亚洲厄佛俗府供月祠庙：宏丽奇巧。基址建在湖中，以免地震摧倒。高四十四丈，宽二十一丈，内有细白石柱，凡一百五十七株，各高约七丈。庙内多细石绝巧人像。庙外四面各有桥，以通四门；桥最宽阔，以细白石为之。正门前，安置美石精工神像。筑工者至二百二十年乃成。

    Explanation

    691

    Three: The Conical High Platform in Memphis, Egypt, Built by King Dorotheus
    The conical high platform in Memphis, Egypt, was constructed by King Dorotheus. The base of the platform is rectangular, with each side measuring one li (approximately 500 meters), and the surrounding perimeter is four li. The platform is 250 steps high, with each step being 28.5 chi (approximately 9.5 meters) wide and 2.5 chi (approximately 0.83 meters) high. The top of the platform can accommodate 50 people. The construction involved 360,000 workers each day.

    Four: The Tomb of King Mausolus in Halicarnassus, Asia Minor
    The tomb of King Mausolus in Halicarnassus, Asia Minor, was built by Queen Artemisia in memory of her husband, King Mausolus. The lower level of the tomb is rectangular, with 26 precious stone columns on each of the four sides. The corridors are arched, each being over 7 zhang (approximately 23.1 meters) wide. Inside, there is a stone staircase leading to the top, where there is a copper carriage, two copper horses, and a statue of King Mausolus. The uniqueness of the tomb lies in its system, height, craftsmanship, and the use of pure fine white stone. As the construction neared completion, Queen Artemisia remembered her husband and died of grief.

    Five: The Moon Temple in Ephesus, Asia Minor
    The Moon Temple in Ephesus, Asia Minor, is magnificent and ingeniously crafted. The foundation is built on a lake to prevent damage from earthquakes. The temple is 44 zhang (approximately 145.2 meters) high and 21 zhang (approximately 70 meters) wide. Inside, there are 157 fine white stone columns, each about 7 zhang (approximately 23.1 meters) high. The temple contains many finely carved stone statues of people. There are bridges on all four sides of the temple, connecting to the four gates; the bridges are very wide and made of fine white stone. At the main entrance, there are beautifully carved stone statues of deities. The construction took 220 years to complete.

    692

    六、欧逻巴洲亚嘉亚省供木星人形：斐第亚，天下名工，取山中一最坚大石，雕刻木星人形，身体弘大，工精细巧，安坐庙中。时有讥笑者语工师曰：“设此宏大之躯起立，宁不冲破庙宇乎？”工师答曰：“我已安置之，万不能起立。”
    　　　　七、法罗海岛高台：厄日多国多禄茂王建造，崇隆无际。高台基址，起自丘山，以细白石筑成。顶上多置火炬，夜照海艘，以便认识港涯丛泊。
    　　　　古时七奇之外，欧逻巴洲意大理亚国罗玛府营建公乐场一蜒，体势椭圆，周围楼房异式四层，高二十二丈余，俱以美石筑成。空场之径七十六丈，楼房下有畜养种种猛兽诸穴，于公乐之时，即出猛兽，在场相斗。观者坐团圆台级，层层相接，高出数丈，能容八万七千人座位。其间各有行走道路，不相逼碍。此场自一千六百年来，至今现存。

    Explanation

    692

    Translation

    Six: Statue of Jupiter in Achaia, Europe

    Phidias, a famous craftsman of the world, took a very hard and large stone from the mountain and carved a statue of Jupiter. The body of the statue is grand and large, the work is finely detailed and skillful, and it sits in the temple. Sometimes there were people who mocked and said to the master craftsman, "If this large body were to stand up, wouldn't it break the temple?" The craftsman replied, "I have already placed it, it will never stand up."

    Seven: High Platform on the Faroe Islands

    The high platform on the Faroe Islands was built by King Harald Bluetooth of Jutland. It towers boundlessly. The base of the high platform starts from the hill and is built with fine white stones. Many torches are placed on the top, lighting the ships at night to help recognize the harbor and where to dock.

    Beyond the Ancient Seven Wonders, a Public Entertainment Venue in Rome, Italy

    In addition to the ancient seven wonders, a public entertainment venue was constructed in Rome, Italy. The venue is oval in shape, surrounded by four-story buildings of different styles, over twenty-two zhang high, all built with beautiful stones. The diameter of the open field is seventy-six zhang. Under the buildings, there are pits for keeping various fierce animals. During the public entertainment, the fierce animals are let out to fight in the field. The spectators sit on tiered platforms, layer by layer, several zhang high, and it can accommodate 87,000 seats. There are walking paths between them, not obstructing each other. This venue has existed since 1600 years ago and still exists today.

    693

    海舶百种不止，约有三等。小者仅容数十人，用以传书信，不以载物。其腹空空，自上达下，唯留一孔，四围点水不漏。下镇一石，一遇风涛，不习水者尽入舟腹，密闭其孔，涂以沥青，使水不进；操舟者，缚其身于樯桅，任水飘荡。其腹空虚，水不沉溺，船底有镇石，亦不翻覆。俟浪平，舟人自解缚，万无一失。一日可行千里。中者容数百人，自小西洋以达广东，则用此舶。其大者，上下八层，高约八丈。最小一层，镇以沙石千余石，使舶不倾侧震盪。二、三层载货与食用之物。海水得淡水最艰，须裝千余大桶，以足千人一年之用，他物称是。上近地平板一层，中下人居之，或装细软切用等物。地平板外，则虚百步，为扬帆习武游戏之地。前后各建屋四层，为尊贵者之居。中有甬道，可通头尾。尾建水阁，可纳凉，以待贵者游息。舶两旁列大铳数十门，其铁弹有三十余斤重者。上下前后，有风帆十余道。

    Explanation

    693

    Sea vessels come in more than a hundred varieties, roughly categorized into three types. The smallest ones can only accommodate dozens of people, used for transmitting letters and messages, not for carrying goods. Their hulls are empty, from top to bottom, with only one hole remaining, and they are waterproof all around. At the bottom, a stone is placed; when encountering wind and waves, those not accustomed to water all enter the hull. The hole is sealed tightly and coated with asphalt to prevent water from entering; the boatman ties himself to the mast, letting the water carry him. Its hull is hollow, and water does not sink it; the bottom of the ship has a stabilizing stone, so it does not capsize. When the waves calm, the boatman unties himself, and there is no chance of failure. They can travel a thousand miles in a day. The medium-sized ones can accommodate hundreds of people, used from the small Western regions to Guangdong. The largest ones have eight levels, top to bottom, about eight zhang high. The smallest layer is stabilized with more than a thousand stones of sand and gravel, preventing the ship from tilting and shaking. The second and third levels carry cargo and food supplies. It is very difficult to obtain fresh water from seawater, so more than a thousand large barrels are carried to meet the needs of a thousand people for a year, and other items are similarly provided. The uppermost level near the deck is flat, with the middle and lower levels inhabited by people or carrying fine and essential items. Beyond the flat deck, there is an empty space of a hundred steps, used for hoisting sails, practicing martial arts, and playing games. Four-story buildings are constructed at the front and back, for the residence of the nobles. There is a corridor in the middle, connecting the front and back. A water pavilion is built at the rear, providing a cool place for the nobles to rest and enjoy themselves. Large cannons are placed on both sides of the ship, with some of the iron balls weighing more than thirty jin. There are more than ten sails on the top, bottom, front, and back.

    694

    桅之大者，二十丈，周一丈二尺；帆阔八丈，约需白布二千四百丈为之。铁猫重六千三百五十余斤，其缆绳周二尺五寸，重一万四千三百余斤。水手二三百人，将卒锐士三四百人，客商数百。有舶总管贵官一员，是西国国王所命，以掌一舶之事，有赏罚生杀之权。又有舶师三人，通天文二士。舶师专掌候风使帆，整理器用，吹号头，指使夫役，探试浅水礁石，以定趋避。

    Explanation

    694

    The largest masts are twenty zhang long with a circumference of one zhang two chi; the sails are eight zhang wide, requiring approximately two thousand four hundred zhang of white cloth to make. The iron anchor weighs more than six thousand three hundred fifty jin, and its cable rope has a circumference of two chi five cun, weighing more than fourteen thousand three hundred jin. There are two or three hundred sailors, three or four hundred soldiers and elite warriors, and hundreds of merchants. There is one high-ranking official in charge of the ship, appointed by the king of the Western country, to oversee all matters concerning the ship, with the power to reward, punish, and even take life. There are also three ship captains and two scholars who understand astronomy. The ship captains are responsible for observing the wind and handling the sails, organizing and maintaining equipment, blowing the conch shell, directing the laborers, exploring shallow waters and reefs, and determining whether to approach or avoid certain areas.

    695

    通天文士专掌窥测天文，昼测日，夜测星，用海图量取度数，以识险易，知里道。又有官医，主一舶疾病。有市肆贸易食物。大舶不畏风浪，独畏山礁浅沙；又畏火，舶上火禁极严，千人之命攸系。其起程但候风色，不选择日时，亦未尝有大失。若多舶同走，大者先行引路，舶后尾楼，夜点灯笼照视。灯笼周二丈四尺，高一丈二尺，皆玻璃板凑成。行海昼夜无停。有山岛可记者，指山岛行，至大洋中，万里无山岛，则用罗经以审方。审方之法，全在海图量取度数，即知舶行至某处，离某处若干里，瞭如指掌。

    Explanation

    695

    Specialized officials in astronomy were responsible for observing celestial phenomena, measuring the sun during the day and the stars at night, using sea charts to measure degrees, to identify dangers and difficulties, and to know the route. There were also official doctors on board to treat illnesses. There were marketplaces for trading food. Large ships were not afraid of wind and waves, but only feared reefs and shallow waters; they also feared fire, with strict fire regulations on board, as the lives of a thousand people depended on it. Departures were made only when the wind conditions were favorable, without choosing specific days or times, and there were never major mistakes. When multiple ships traveled together, the larger ones led the way first, with the stern tower of the ship lighting lanterns at night for guidance. The lanterns were two zhang and four chi in circumference and one zhang and two chi in height, all made of glass plates. Sailing the sea was continuous, day and night. If there were mountains or islands that could be recorded, they navigated by pointing to them; when reaching the open ocean, where there were no mountains or islands for thousands of miles, they used the compass to determine direction. The method of determining direction was entirely dependent on measuring degrees with a sea chart, thus knowing where the ship had traveled and how far it was from a certain place, as clear as the palm of one's hand.

    696

    [张山来曰：极西巧思独绝，然吾儒正以中庸为佳，无事矜奇斗巧也。]
    　　讱庵偶笔　新安汪口口
    　　　　孝感县一妇，不孝于姑，雷下击之。妇急以血袴蒙头，雷为所厌，歘然坠地，形如鹰而稍大。其家以香汤沐浴之，奉于香火座上。雷仍自褫其翅羽，其家又为作法事，一旦风雨飞腾而去。此妇自以为得计，每出入必挟血片自随，一日河边漂衣，天无纤云，忽闻雷轰，妇已毙矣。

    Explanation

    696

    Zhang Shanlai said: The ingenuity of the far West is unparalleled, yet we Confucians truly value the Doctrine of the Mean, and there is no need to boast of extraordinary cleverness or compete in ingenuity.

    In Xiaogan County, there was a woman who was disrespectful to her mother-in-law. A thunderbolt struck her down. In a panic, she quickly covered her head with a piece of bloody cloth, and the thunderbolt was repelled, suddenly falling to the ground. Its shape resembled an eagle but was slightly larger. Her family bathed it with fragrant water and worshiped it on an altar of incense. The thunderbolt then shed its wings and feathers, and the family performed a ritual ceremony. One day, amidst wind and rain, it flew away. This woman thought it was a clever plan and always carried a piece of bloody cloth with her when going out or coming in. One day, while washing clothes by the river, the sky was clear without a single cloud. Suddenly, she heard thunder, and the woman was already dead.

    697

    [张山来曰：鬼神之属，类恶污秽，污秽之取恶固宜，但往往偶一相值，即不能运其威灵，诚不可解。我若为雷神，则以柳下惠“尔焉凂我”之度量，效皋陶“执之而已”之用法，并行不悖，亦何不可？]
    　　　　康熙癸丑，上海县有人以假银买猪三十六头，又有他人以钱四百託买一头，同载入舟。俄而疾雷揖篷轰击，三十六头，一时皆毙。独一头无恙。则用钱所买者也。卖猪人以假银买卖，为人所执，讼之于县。县官诘之，供云：“实系卖猪得来，非某假造。”官问：“汝识其人否？”曰：“买猪人虽识其貌，不识其住处。而载猪之船，现在郎家桥。”于是押同舟子物色其人，果获之，县官痛责枷示焉。

    Explanation

    697

    Zhang Shanlai said: "The spirits generally dislike filth and impurity, and it is appropriate for filth and impurity to be hated. But often, when they meet by chance, they cannot exert their power and influence, which is truly inexplicable. If I were a thunder god, I would maintain the virtue of Liu Xiaohui's 'you cannot pollute me,' and apply Gao Yao's principle of 'punishment alone,' and both can coexist without contradiction, so why not?"

    In the year 1673 of the Kangxi era, a person in Shanghai County bought thirty-six pigs with counterfeit silver, and another person bought one pig with four hundred copper coins, and they loaded them onto the same boat. Suddenly, a fierce thunderstorm struck the boat, and all thirty-six pigs died instantly. Only the one bought with copper coins remained unharmed. It turned out that the pig bought with copper coins was the one that survived. The seller of the pigs was accused of using counterfeit silver in the transaction and was taken to court. The county magistrate interrogated him, and he confessed, saying, "The silver was indeed obtained from selling pigs; I did not forge it." The magistrate asked, "Do you recognize the person?" He replied, "I recognize the appearance of the person who bought the pig, but not their residence. The boat carrying the pigs is now at Langjia Bridge." The magistrate then ordered the boatman to search for the person, and they were indeed found. The county magistrate severely reprimanded and publicly punished the culprit.

    698

    [张山来曰：雷所击者，不孝与用铜为多，而光棍不与焉，则何也？吾非谓不孝与用铜不当击，只以光棍为更当击耳。雷之不及光棍，殆亦畏之耶？抑多而不胜击邪？]
    　　　　高怀中，业鳝面于扬州小东门，日杀鳝数千。一婢悯之，每夜分，窃缸中鳝，从后窗抛入河，如是积年。一日面店被焚，婢踉跄逃出，为火所伤，困卧河滨。夜深睡去，比醒而痛减，火疮尽愈。视之，有河中污泥，堆于疮处，而地有鳝行迹，始知向者所放生来救之也按医书：河底泥，能涂汤火伤。高感其异，遂为罢业。及拆锅，下有洞穴，生鳝数石盘其中，尽举而纵之河。

    Explanation

    698

    Zhang Shanlai said: Those struck by lightning are mostly filial impiety and those who use copper, while ruffians are not among them. Why is that? I do not mean that filial impiety and the use of copper should not be struck; I only mean that ruffians deserve to be struck more. Lightning does not strike ruffians, is it perhaps afraid of them? Or are there too many to strike?

    Gao Huazhong, who makes eel noodles at the small eastern gate of Yangzhou, kills thousands of eels daily. One servant girl pities him and, every night, steals the eels from the vat, throws them into the river from the back window, and does this for many years. One day, the noodle shop is burned down, and the servant girl stumbles out, is injured by the fire, and lies exhausted by the riverbank. She falls asleep at night, and when she wakes up, the pain has lessened. The burns have all healed. Looking at it, there is river mud piled on the wound, and there are traces of eels on the ground. She realizes that the eels she released before have come to save her. According to medical texts, river mud can treat burns. Gao is moved by this and decides to stop his business. When he takes down the pot, there is a hole below, with several baskets of live eels inside, and he releases them all into the river.

    699

    上海朱锦，初投潘尚书为家人。后其子游泮，入谢于公。潘曰：“汝子已系朝廷士子，可以门生礼见，勿复作主仆观也。”即检其靠身文书还之。朱不胜感激，曰：“荷洪恩，须当报效，庶慊微心耳。”潘曰：“我富贵已足，何赖于汝？”朱恳请不已。潘沉吟再四，乃曰：“现今文庙圮坏。汝能修葺，贤于报我远矣！”朱即独力营缮，颇称华焕。此事已过百余年，人亦无有忆及之者。顺治己亥科，会元朱锦亦上海人，官翰苑，至康熙壬子殁。临卒时，文庙正梁，年久朽坏，亦以是刻崩殒。视其建造之姓名，即朱锦也，始知会元乃其后身。事详《上海志》。又缙云郑赓唐，天启丁卯孝廉，亦以儒学为兵火所毁，躬自督造，晨夕不辍。其子唯飏、载飏相继登进士。今人唯知崇饰寺观，以希冥福，而于幼所诵法之圣人，反秦越视之。抑知东家氏之灵爽，固若是其彰彰也乎！

    Explanation

    699

    Zhu Jin from Shanghai initially served as a servant to Secretary of State Pan. Later, when his son entered the school and came to thank the official, Pan said, "Your son is now a scholar of the court; you may see me with the etiquette of a disciple, and no longer as a master and servant." He then returned Zhu Jin's personal documents to him. Zhu Jin was overwhelmed with gratitude and said, "I bear great favor; I must repay it, to satisfy my humble wish." Pan replied, "I am already wealthy and honored; what do I need from you?" Zhu Jin pleaded earnestly. After some thought, Pan said, "The Confucian temple is now in ruins. If you can renovate it, your service to me would be far more valuable!" Zhu Jin immediately took on the task of rebuilding the temple, which turned out splendid and magnificent. This event occurred over a hundred years ago, and no one remembers it now. In the year of the pig in 1659 during the Shunzhi era, Zhu Jin, the first-place winner of the imperial examination, was also from Shanghai. He served in the Hanlin Academy and passed away in the year of the rabbit in 1712 during the Kangxi era. At the time of his death, the main beam of the Confucian temple, which had been old and rotten, suddenly collapsed. When they looked at the names of those who had built it, it was Zhu Jin himself, revealing that the first-place winner was his reincarnation. The details are recorded in the Shanghai Gazetteer. Another example is Zheng Gengtang of Jinyun, a Xiaolian from the Dingmao year of the Tianqi era, who also rebuilt the Confucian temple after it was destroyed by war, personally overseeing the construction day and night without ceasing. His sons, Wei Yang and Zai Yang, successively passed the imperial examination. Today, people only know how to decorate temples and shrines in hopes of seeking blessings in the afterlife, while treating the sages they revered in their youth as distant and indifferent. How could they know that the spirit of the Eastern neighbor is so clearly evident?

    700

    [张山来曰：此事若论功，当以潘为首，而朱次之，岂为潘已富贵耶？至于不报前之朱锦，而报于百余年后之同名者，则又何也？]
    　　　　仪真孔姓者，于荒年购得《孔氏家谱》，遂诣县冒陈圣裔。时值变乱之余，圣胄散落，县为申请，得补奉祀生，遂于家安设圣位。然其人无行，淫人之妻；夫死，遂娶为妾；而己妻亦有淫行，乡里薄之。邻有塾师，夜梦一儒者乘车，上竖一旗，题曰“司马牛”，弟子从者甚众，皆头带包角巾罩于髻上，方项有带者，语塾师曰：“来日此处有事，汝当避之。”觉而骇甚，如言避去。至午后，火发，孔姓者从外奔归，见火势尚缓，亟入，欲攫其谱。甫进门内，火忽四合，夫妻遂焚死。

    Explanation

    700

    Zhang Shanlai said: If we consider merit, it should be ranked with Pan as the first and Zhu as the second; is it because Pan is already wealthy and noble? As for not reporting the previous Zhu Jin, but reporting one who shares the same name after more than a hundred years, what is the reason for this?

    A man named Kong from Yizhen purchased the Kong Family Genealogy during a famine and went to the county office to falsely claim himself as a descendant of the sage. At a time when chaos had just subsided, the sage's descendants were scattered. The county office applied on his behalf, and he was granted the position of a sacrificial attendant, and he set up a shrine at home. However, the man had no virtue, committing adultery with someone else's wife; after the husband died, he married her as a concubine; and his own wife also engaged in adulterous behavior, causing the villagers to look down on him. A private school teacher in the neighborhood dreamed of a scholar riding in a carriage, with a banner raised on top, inscribed with "Simazi," accompanied by many disciples, all wearing headscarves wrapped around their buns and bands around their necks. The scholar told the teacher, "There will be something happening here tomorrow; you should avoid it." Awakening with great fear, he followed the dream's warning and avoided the place. By mid-afternoon, a fire broke out. The man named Kong ran back from outside, seeing that the fire was still slow, he quickly entered to retrieve his genealogy. As soon as he stepped inside, the fire suddenly intensified, and both he and his wife were burned to death.

    701

    [张山来曰：此事予犹及见之。然亦此人不肖，故遭此报耳。]
    　　柳轩丛谈　寄园寄所寄
    　　　　婺源江君辅，幼工奕，称国手。年十七，忽一人扣户，称江北某家，延请角技。君辅袱被随之往，月余，抵中州某宦宅。其人先入内，见某宦，诈云：“吾途穷，鬻吾子为归串。”既得金，立契，复涕泗曰：“父子情，不忍面别，请从后门去，免吾子牵衣惨状也。”宦信之。君辅方久坐堂上，讶无出肃客者。忽一鬅头婢肩水桶，目江大声曰：“尔新来仆，速出汲。”江惊异，厉声争之。宦从内出，持券示曰：“尔父卖尔去，复何云？”江曰：“异哉！君数千里遣使迎我手谈，乃为此不经语乎？谁为吾父？”出所著《奕谱》呈宦证之。宦大惊曰：“汝果能胜我，言即不谬。”甫对着，君辅连胜数局，宦爽然，深相礼貌。其地有国手，从无出其右，宦忽请对局，辅又连胜。宦大喜，待为上客，盘桓数月，作书叠荐好奕钜公处，获金数百归。

    Explanation

    701

    Zhang Shanlai said: I still witnessed this event. However, it was also due to this person's unworthiness that he suffered this retribution.

    In Wuyuan, Jiang Junfu was skilled in chess from a young age and was known as a national master. At seventeen, a man suddenly knocked on his door, claiming to be from a family in northern Jiang, inviting him to compete in skills. Jiang Junfu packed his bedding and followed along. After more than a month, they arrived at the residence of a certain official in Zhongzhou. The man went inside first, met the official, and falsely claimed, "I am at the end of my journey, selling my son as a means of returning home." After receiving money, he drew up a contract and wept, saying, "The father-son relationship is unbearable to face the separation. Please leave through the back door to avoid the pitiful scene of my son holding onto my clothes." The official believed him. Jiang Junfu had been sitting in the hall for a long time, surprised that no one came out to receive guests. Suddenly, a maid with a messy hairstyle and carrying a water bucket looked at Jiang and shouted loudly, "You, the newly arrived servant, hurry out to draw water." Jiang was surprised and puzzled, shouting sternly to argue against it. The official came out from inside, holding the contract and showing it, saying, "Your father sold you away, what else can you say?" Jiang replied, "Strange! You sent messengers from thousands of miles away to invite me for a game, yet you speak such nonsensical words? Who is my father?" He produced his chess manual and presented it to the official as proof. The official was greatly surprised and said, "If you can indeed beat me, your words are not mistaken." As soon as they started playing, Jiang Junfu won several consecutive games. The official was disappointed and surprised, treating him with deep courtesy. In that place, there was a national master, and no one was better than him. The official suddenly asked to play a game, and Jiang won again. The official was greatly pleased, treating him as a guest of honor. They lingered for several months, and the official wrote letters repeatedly recommending him to high-ranking officials who were good at chess. Jiang earned hundreds of gold pieces and returned home.

    702

    [张山来曰：此当是某宦故作狡狯耳！不然，卖子为仆，岂不睹面而遽成交邪？]
    　　啸虹笔记　寄园寄所寄
    　　　　篆学图书，多出于新安，为他郡所不及。如汪梦龙，休宁西门人，名涛，字山来，多膂力，人呼之“梦龙将军”。真草隶篆，以及诸家书法，无所不精。每写一家，从不致杂入一笔。大则一字方丈，小则径寸千言。铁笔之妙，包罗百家，前无古人。少时至楚中贩米，逆旅暇日，偶至一寺，见衣冠者十余辈，在佛殿以沙聚地，成字径丈，曰“岳阳楼”。山来笑谓曰：“是可以墨书也，何艰于八法乃尔耶？”众惊愕，因白之郡守，延入署，煮墨一缸，山来以碎布蘸墨，书于扁上，顷刻成。守叹赏久之，因嘱山来落款于后，曰“海阳汪涛书”。至今楼虽屡修，而此匾不能易也。其徒王言，字纶紫，北门人。纶紫篆书出宦光之上，隶书直追中郎，至于行楷，各尽其妙。

    Explanation

    702

    Zhang Shanlai said: "This is likely a trick or prank by a certain official! Otherwise, selling one's son as a servant, wouldn't one have to meet in person before making such a deal?"

    Shixiong Notes
    Ji Yuan Ji Suo Ji
    Seal Carving and Calligraphy
    Seal carving and calligraphy works are mostly from Xin'an, surpassing those of other counties. Wang Menglong, from Ximen in Xiuning, was also known as Shanlai. He was strong and muscular, earning him the nickname "General Menglong." He was proficient in all styles of calligraphy, including regular, cursive, clerical, and seal scripts, as well as various schools of calligraphy. Whenever he wrote in one style, he never mixed in a single stroke from another style. Large characters could be as big as a square zhang, while small ones could fit a thousand words in an inch. The beauty of his brushwork encompassed all schools, unmatched by anyone before him. In his youth, he traveled to Chu to sell rice. One day, he visited a temple and saw more than ten well-dressed men gathering sand on the ground in the Buddha hall, forming a character as large as a zhang, which read "Yueyang Tower." Zhang Shanlai smiled and said, "This can be written with ink, why is it so difficult with the eight strokes?" The men were surprised and reported this to the governor, who invited him to the office. The governor heated a pot of ink and Zhang Shanlai dipped a piece of cloth in it, writing on the banner. In a short while, it was done. The governor admired it for a long time and then asked Zhang Shanlai to inscribe his name at the end, writing "Wang Tao of Haiyang." Even though the building has been repaired many times, this plaque remains unchanged. His disciple Wang Yan, courtesy name Lunzi, is from the northern gate. His seal script surpasses that of Guan Guang, and his clerical script rivals that of Zhong Rang. As for running and regular script, he excels in each.

    703

    [张山来曰：仆与汪君同字山来，彼于书法精妙乃尔，仆则十指如悬锤，深以为憾。岂灵秀之气，为彼所独得邪？犹忆为童子时，得一图章，形扁而空其中，一面刻“月色江声共一楼”七字，一面刻“雪夜书千卷，花时酒一瓢”二句，俱朱文。其傍一刻“辛酉秋日篆”五字，又“汪涛”二字，一刻“山来”二字。今此石尚存箧中。向亦不知山来为谁，由今观之，真足发一笑也。]

    Explanation

    703

    Zhang Shanlai said: I share the same name as Mr. Wang, but he is so skilled in calligraphy, while I have fingers like hanging hammers, deeply regretting my lack of talent. Is it that the spirit of refinement is uniquely his? I still remember when I was a child, I obtained a seal, flat in shape and hollow inside, one side carved with seven characters, "The moonlight and river sounds share one floor," and the other side carved with two lines, "In a snowy night, read thousands of scrolls; in the season of flowers, drink a ladle of wine," both in red seal script. Next to them, five characters "Xinyou autumn day seal" were carved, and 'Wang Tao' two characters, another 'Shanlai' two characters. Now this stone still exists in the box. Before, I did not know who Shanlai was, from today's perspective, it truly deserves a laugh.

    704

    燕觚　钮琇玉樵觚胜
    　　　　宣城高检讨遗山，言其族兄某，于崇祯中训蒙村庙。暑夕散徒，纳凉庭间，忽见庙殿青灯影影，因从窗楞窥之。内有一人，危冠方袍，南面而坐。两傍童子以次侍立，约十余人，深目巨鼻，貌极狰狞。高拍窗惊呼，殿内人从容徐步出揖曰：“吾亦师也。所训诸徒，皆三十年后公侯将相。上帝悯其目不识丁，欲使稍习文字，略知仁义。天下将乱，孑遗之民，不至被其卤莽啖噬也。吾身隐少微，适奉帝命来此，分方授业，暂假庙席，月余事毕矣。”语后入殿，息灯，寂无所见。
    　　　　[张山来曰：公侯将相中，尽有“没字碑”在，想未在村庙中读书耳。然皋、夔、稷、契，所读何书？即不识字，未为不可。但徒为舞文辈地耳！]

    Explanation

    704

    The Yan Gu (燕觚) by Niu Xiu, Yujiao Gu Sheng (玉樵觚胜)

    Gao Yishan, a censor from Xuancheng, recounted that his cousin, during the reign of Emperor Chongzhen, was teaching at a village temple. One summer evening, after dismissing his students, he sought coolness in the courtyard. Suddenly, he noticed faint shadows of green lamps in the temple hall. Curious, he peered through the window lattice and saw a figure wearing a formal headgear and robe, sitting facing south. On either side, about ten children stood in order, their deep-set eyes and large noses making them appear extremely ferocious. Gao, startled, pounded on the window and shouted. The figure in the temple calmly stepped out, bowed, and said, "I am also a teacher. The students I instruct will all become dukes, marquises, generals, and prime ministers thirty years from now. The Heavenly Deity pities that they cannot read or write, so I am here to teach them a bit about literature and the principles of benevolence and righteousness. The world is about to descend into chaos, and the remaining people will not be devoured by their savagery. I, who am hidden in the constellation of Shao Wei, have just received the imperial command to come here, divide the area, and impart knowledge. I will temporarily use this temple for a month or so, and then my task will be completed." After speaking, the figure returned to the temple, extinguished the lights, and there was nothing to be seen.

    [Commentary by Zhang Shanlai: Among the dukes, marquises, generals, and prime ministers, there are many who are like "stone tablets without inscriptions," meaning their achievements or identities are unknown. It seems they did not study in village temples. However, what did Yao, Shun, Yu, and Ji read? Even if they did not know how to read, it was not necessarily a bad thing. But it is only suitable for scholars and literati!]

    705

    京城东偏有民家，生一女，能言之岁，忽曰：“我工部郎中郑濂妇也，何以在此？我欲归我家矣。”迹郑之居，与女家相去二里许，某秘之，不以告。女甫能行，即出户觅郑居。或时趋出巷外，其家辄抱持之，防其逸。而女之求归益坚，不得已，以闻于郑，郑乃迎之，盖八龄矣。重堂邃室，皆若素游，直入踞床，南面而为妇言曰：“我之子与媳安在？不速出见？”众方匿笑旁睨，濂适自外来，起而曰：“我别夫子日久，岂遂不相识耶？”笼箧之庋，香履之存，靡不一一指点其处。郑郎中以事近怪，不逾宿而遣之。然闻者惊相传告，旋彻内庭。今上召询濂，濂不敢隐，因命续再世之婚。濂辞以“年齿甚悬，且臣之子已生孙矣，居室名言，恐有未顺。”上曰：“天命之也，待十三岁而婚，谁曰不宜？”濂奉旨届期成礼，伉俪如初。

    Explanation

    705

    In the eastern part of the capital city, there lived a commoner who had a daughter. When she was old enough to speak, she suddenly said, "I am the wife of Langzhong Zheng Lian of the Ministry of Works. Why am I here? I want to return to my own home." They traced Zheng Lian's residence, which was about two li away from her home. The family kept this a secret and did not tell anyone. When the girl could just walk, she would go out to look for Zheng Lian's home. Sometimes she would rush out of the alley, and her family would hold her back to prevent her from leaving. Her desire to return became even stronger, and they had no choice but to inform Zheng Lian, who then welcomed her. She was eight years old at the time.

    The deep and spacious rooms were all as if she had always been there. She directly entered and sat on the bed, speaking to the women facing south, "Where are my son and daughter-in-law? They must come out quickly!" The crowd was secretly laughing and looking sideways. Zheng Lian happened to come from outside and stood up, saying, "It has been a long time since we last met, how could you not recognize me?" She pointed out the exact locations of boxes, chests, and fragrant shoes, knowing everything in the house.

    Zheng Lian, considering the matter strange, sent her away within a few days. However, those who heard about it were surprised and passed the news on, eventually reaching the inner palace. The emperor summoned Zheng Lian to inquire, and Zheng Lian dared not hide the truth and thus ordered the continuation of the marriage. He explained, "The age difference is significant, and my son has already had grandchildren. The names of the rooms and the formalities might not be appropriate." The emperor said, "It is the will of heaven; wait until she is thirteen years old and get married. Who would say it is inappropriate?" Zheng Lian followed the imperial command and completed the ceremony on the appointed date, and they became husband and wife once again.

    706

    [张山来曰：不识定情之夕，亦有所痛楚否？]
    　　豫觚　钮琇玉樵觚胜
    　　　　永城有张生者，屡就童子试，不遇。读书芒砀山天齐寺。攻苦之暇，散步殿庑，见东帝座下判官像貌伟丽，戏拊其背曰：“人间安得如公者，吾与论心订交乎？”是夕，生篝灯禅堂，披简孤坐，忽闻扣门声，且曰：“君所愿交者来矣。”启扃而迎，则昼所见判官也。始颇疑惧，继稍款洽。坐谈之顷，温语庄言，纚纚动听。生且喜得佳友。由是定更辄来，夜分乃去，率以为常。

    Explanation

    706

    Zhang Shanlai said, "Do you not know if on the night of our engagement, there was also some pain?"

    In the collection Yu Gu, Niu Xiu Yuqiao Gu Sheng:

    In Yongcheng, there was a young man named Zhang who repeatedly took the examination for the rank of scholar but did not succeed. He went to study at the Tianqi Temple in Mangdang Mountain. During the breaks in his intense study, he would take walks in the halls of the temple, where he saw a judge under the Eastern Emperor with a majestic appearance. Playfully patting the judge's back, he said, "How can one find someone like you in the world? I wish to discuss my heart and make friends with you." That night, Zhang lit a lamp in the meditation hall and sat alone with his books, when suddenly he heard a knock on the door, and someone said, "The one you wish to befriend has arrived." Opening the door to greet him, it was the judge he had seen during the day. At first, Zhang was quite suspicious and fearful, but later he became somewhat friendly and comfortable. During their conversation, the judge's words were warm and serious, and they sounded pleasant and convincing. Zhang was delighted to have found a good friend. From then on, the judge would come at a fixed time and leave at midnight, and this became a regular occurrence.

    707

    生久之与习，因自陈轗轲有年，莫测荣枯所诣，乞其搜示冥册。神颦蹙曰：“君无显秩，即一芹犹难撷也。奈何？”生不觉愤恸，坚请为之回斡。神徐曰：“当为君图之。”阅数夕至，曰：“已得之矣。山东某邑，有与君同姓者，应于明年入泮。吾互易其籍，可暂得志。然事久必露，君其慎之！”嗣后神不复见，生亦归里。试果获售，悉如神言。浮沈黉宫十余载，忽梦神仓皇前诉曰：“吾因与君一日之契，潜窜衿录，已蒙帝谴，法当远戍。兹行与君永别耳。”生觉而惘然。未几，亦以试劣被黜。

    Explanation

    707

    After a long time, the protagonist became familiar with the deity and began to recount his many setbacks, unable to discern the path of his future prosperity or decline. He requested the deity to search and reveal his fate in the underworld. The deity frowned and said, "You do not hold a high-ranking position, so even a small achievement like picking a tiny herb is difficult to attain. What can I do?" The protagonist, overcome with anger and sorrow, firmly requested the deity to intervene. The deity calmly said, "I will find a way for you." After several nights, the deity returned and said, "I have found a solution. In a certain county in Shandong, there is someone with your surname who should pass the examination next year. I will secretly swap our records, allowing you to temporarily achieve success. However, this plan will eventually be exposed, so you must be cautious." After this, the deity did not appear again, and the protagonist returned home. The examination went as the deity had predicted, and the protagonist passed. He rose and fell in the educational institutions for over a decade. One day, he suddenly had a dream in which the deity appeared in a state of distress and said, "I helped you because of our brief acquaintance, but I secretly swapped your records, and I have been punished by the emperor. I will be exiled to a distant place, and this will be our permanent farewell." The protagonist woke up feeling lost. Not long after, he was demoted due to poor performance in the examination.

    708

    [张山来曰：神虽因生被谴，而爱才若此，殊足千古！]
    　　　　李通判者，山西汾州人。其前世为乡学究，年逾五旬，闲居昼卧，梦二卒持帖到门云：“吾府延君教授，请速往。”挟之上马，不移时，至一府第，如达官家。青衣者引之入，重闼焕丽，曲槛纡回，最后书室三楹。坐顷，两公子出拜，锦衣玉貌，皆执弟子礼。日夕讲课不辍。书室外院，地逼厅事，时闻传呼鞭笞之声，特不见主人为怪，且不晓是何官秩。请于二子，二子曰：“家君即出见先生矣。”未几，主人果出，冠带殊伟，晤语间，礼意款洽。学究因言：“晚辈承乏幕下，久且阅岁，不无故园之思。”主人微晒曰：“君至此，已不可归。然自后当有佳处，幸勿复多言。”学究凄然不乐，竟不知身在冥府也。一日，主人开宴，邀学究共席。

    Explanation

    708

    Zhang Shanlai said: "Despite being born, the deity was punished, yet loved talent so much, truly worthy of being remembered throughout history!"

    Li Tongpan, a magistrate from Fenyang, Shanxi, was in a previous life a village scholar. At the age of over fifty, he lived idly and slept during the day. One day, he dreamed of two officials holding a summons arriving at his door and saying, "Our court invites you to teach, please come quickly." They escorted him on horseback, and in no time, they arrived at a grand mansion, like that of a high-ranking official. A servant in blue attire led him inside, where the gates were grand and splendid, and the railings curved around. Finally, they reached a study room with three rooms. After sitting for a while, two young men came out to greet him, dressed in fine clothes and with beautiful appearances, all observing the etiquette of students. They taught continuously day and night without stopping. Outside the study room, the ground was close to the main hall, and from time to time, he heard the sounds of calls and whips, but he did not find the master strange and did not know what rank he held. He asked the two young men, who replied, "Our father will come out to see you shortly." Soon after, the master indeed came out, dressed in a magnificent cap and sash. During their conversation, they were friendly and cordial. The scholar then said, "I have been serving in the office for many years and cannot help but miss my hometown." The master smiled slightly and said, "You are here now and cannot return. However, from now on, there will be good places, so please do not speak too much." The scholar felt sad and unhappy, not realizing that he was in the underworld. One day, the master held a banquet and invited the scholar to share a table.

    709

    辞以寒素不宜与先辈抗礼，强之乃行。厅事设有四宴，扫径良久，一僧肩舆而至，极驺从之盛，曰“大和尚”。又一僧至，如前，曰“二和尚”，直据南面两宴。学究、主人，依次列坐。主人与二僧语，学究皆不解。酒果亦并非人间物。酒半，忽见一梯悬于堂檐，二僧出蹑之，冉冉而去。主人促学究从而上，攀援甚苦，倏然堕地，则已托生本州李氏矣。襁褓中能语如成人，但冥府有勿言之约，不敢道前世事。生四岁，握笔为制义，评骘其父文，可否悉当。后登崇祯乙榜。顺治初，通判扬州。天兵南下，出迎裕王。王手掖之，如旧相识，曰：“当时事犹能记忆耶？”一笑驰去。潜窥裕王状貌，即所见“二和尚”也。而“大和尚”未知出世为何如人。

    Explanation

    709

    The scholar declined on the grounds of his humble birth and said it was inappropriate to be on equal terms with the senior scholars, but was forced to go. The hall was set up with four feasts, and the path was swept for a long time. A monk arrived in a sedan chair, accompanied by a splendid retinue, and was called the "Great Master." Another monk arrived in the same manner, and was called the "Second Master," taking a position of honor facing south at two of the feasts. The scholar and the host sat in order. The host conversed with the two monks, but the scholar could not understand them. The food and drink were not of this world. Halfway through the meal, a ladder suddenly appeared suspended from the eaves of the hall. The two monks climbed it and ascended slowly, disappearing into the air. The host urged the scholar to follow them up, but he struggled greatly in his attempts to climb. Suddenly, he fell to the ground, having been reincarnated into the Li family in the same state. Even as a baby, he could speak like an adult, but there was a pact with the underworld not to speak of it, so he dared not mention his past life. At the age of four, he held the brush to write essays on the classics and evaluated his father's writings, all judgments being appropriate. Later, he passed the imperial examination in the year of the Water Ox (乙丑) during the reign of Chongzhen. At the beginning of the Shunzhi era, he served as the deputy governor of Yangzhou. When the heavenly army marched south, he met the Prince of Yu outside. The prince pulled him by the arm, as if they were old acquaintances, and asked, "Can you still remember what happened then?" They laughed and rode away. He secretly observed the appearance of the Prince of Yu and realized it was the same as the "Second Master" he had seen before. But the identity of the "Great Master" remains unknown.

    710

    窦四者，沈丘槐店窦生之佃也。康熙庚午夏日，四妇将逼娩期，梦黑丈夫颀而髯，谓之曰：“我欲暂托汝家，幸勿加害，当有以报。”次日之晡，产一龙，蜿蜒逾尺，鳞角俱备，项间有黄鬃如马鬣，拂拂而动。妇极惊怖，意欲斫除。忽飞蟠屋梁。因忆前梦，姑置豢焉。不三日，骤长数丈，夭矫游行，就乳则体仍缩小，如初生时。熟习日久，饲以鸡卵，亦能啖也。沈丘范令，亲往其家视之。

    Explanation

    710

    In the year of Gengwu in the Kangxi era, during the summer, a woman named Sui Si, who was a tenant of the Sin family in Huaidian village of Shenqiu, was approaching her delivery date. She dreamed of a tall, bearded dark-skinned man who told her, "I wish to temporarily stay in your home. Please do not harm me, and I will repay you." The next day, in the evening, she gave birth to a dragon that was more than a foot long, with scales and horns fully formed, and a yellow mane on its neck like a horse's mane, fluttering and moving. The woman was extremely frightened and intended to cut it off. Suddenly, the dragon flew and coiled around the beam. Recalling her dream, she decided to keep it and raise it. Within three days, the dragon suddenly grew several zhang in length, gracefully and freely moving. When it suckled, its body returned to its original size, as if it had just been born. Over time, it became familiar with its environment and was fed with chicken eggs, which it could also eat. The magistrate of Shenqiu, Fan, personally visited her home to see the dragon.

    711

    [张山来曰：不知此龙何以报母？]
    　　秦觚　钮琇玉樵觚胜
    　　　　崇祯末，蒲城人屈曼者，为县隶，性嗜酒。一日持檄下乡，中途醉卧，夜半乃醒。时朗月如昼，见古槐树间，有年少书生，乌巾绒袍，仰月呼吸。俄而口吐一珠，色赤于火，以手承弄。曼踉跄而前，遽向生手夺取吞咽。生怒争不已，既而曰：“假汝经年，仍当归我耳。”随失所在。曼吞珠后，觉体甚飘忽，举念即至其所。旋有黠者，雇曼入省会投文，距西安二百余里，食顷已到，并不见其跋涉之迹。试之他事皆然。众咸谓其得隐形术。适御史巡蒲，录诸讼牒，怨家重赂曼，径入堂掣牒，左右无见者。御史微觉阶前有半体人，案牒翻翻自动，心甚骇异，急以所佩印重按，忽得人手，其全体亦遂现。立命箠毙。曼埋逾夕，其地坟起，成一小穴，若有物出入状。盖书生取珠为之。

    Explanation

    711

    Zhang Shanlai said: "How can this dragon repay its mother?" (Source: Qin Gu, Niu Xiu, Yuqiao Gu Sheng)

    At the end of the Chongzhen era, there was a man named Qu Man from Pucheng, who was a county official and had a fondness for alcohol. One day, he was holding a document and went down to the village, where he fell asleep drunk on the way. He woke up at midnight. The moon was bright as if it were daytime, and he saw a young scholar under an ancient locust tree, wearing a black headband and a woolen robe, breathing in the moonlight. Suddenly, the scholar spat out a pearl, red like fire, and played with it in his hand. Qu Man stumbled forward and suddenly grabbed the pearl from the scholar's hand and swallowed it. The scholar became angry and continued to argue, but then said, "I will borrow it from you for a year, and it will still return to me." After that, the scholar disappeared. After swallowing the pearl, Qu Man felt his body was very light, and with a thought, he arrived wherever he wanted. Soon after, a clever person hired Qu Man to deliver documents to the provincial capital, which was over two hundred miles from Xi'an. In the time it took to eat a meal, he had already arrived, and no one saw any signs of his journey. He tried other things, and they all worked the same way. Everyone thought he had acquired the art of invisibility. Just then, a censor was touring Pucheng and was recording various legal documents. Qu Man's enemy heavily bribed him, and he directly entered the hall and grabbed the documents. No one saw him. The censor faintly sensed a half-bodied person on the steps, and the documents on the desk were flipping automatically. He was very startled and quickly pressed his seal heavily. Suddenly, he got a human hand, and the entire figure also appeared. He immediately ordered to be beaten to death. Qu Man was buried overnight, and the ground rose up, forming a small hole, as if something was entering and exiting. It turned out that the scholar had taken the pearl and was responsible for it.

    712

    [张山来曰：屈曼得珠，反以自毙，想亦书生启御史之衷耳。]
    　　吴觚　钮琇玉樵觚胜
    　　　　嘉兴东门外，有史痴者，娶妇甚美，遣之别嫁，佯狂行乞于市。所乞之家，货必倍售。以是遇其来，辄施以钱。或有过门不入者，虽招与之，掉头不顾也。蓬首，发如乱丝；沍寒时，身衣单衫，以破絮缠两足，日至河中濯之，曳冰而走，琤琮有声，以为乐。乞钱沽酒，饮辄醉，余钱置道旁墙隙中，云：“有缘者任得之。”间与人言祸福，多奇验。有老妪素相识，忽诣之曰：“诘朝当有少钱助汝。”是夜，即于妪门端坐而逝。人闻其死，争致赙钱，妪果大获。既举棺，轻若无人，盖尸解矣。

    Explanation

    712

    张山来说：屈曼获得了宝贵的珍珠，却因此自取灭亡，想来也是书生启发了御史的心意。

    吴觚 钮琇玉樵觚胜

    在嘉兴东门外，有一个名叫史痴的人，他娶了一位非常美丽的妻子，后来却让她另嫁他人，自己则假装疯狂，在市场上乞讨。他乞讨的人家，货物总是卖得特别好。因此，每当他来到时，人们总是给他施舍钱财。有时有人经过他的门而不进去，即使他招手邀请，对方也会掉头不顾。他头发蓬乱，如同乱丝；在寒冷的天气里，他只穿着一件单衣，用破布裹着双脚，每天到河里洗澡，拖着冰块行走，发出清脆的声音，以此为乐。他用乞讨来的钱买酒喝，喝醉后就把剩下的钱放在路边的墙缝中，说：“有缘的人可以随意拿去。”他偶尔会与人谈论祸福，大多都很灵验。有一天，一位他认识的老妇人突然来找他，说：“明天早上你会得到一些钱帮助你。”当天晚上，他就坐在老妇人的门前去世了。人们听说他死了，纷纷给他送葬钱，老妇人果然得到了很多。当他们抬着他的棺材时，棺材轻得仿佛里面没有人，原来他已经尸解了。

    713

    余所交“海内三髯”，一为慈溪姜西溟，一为郃阳康孟谋，其一则阳羡生陈其年也。其年未遇时，游于广陵，冒巢民爱其才，延致梅花别墅。有童名紫云者，儇丽善歌，令其执役书堂。生一见神移，赠以佳句，并图其像，装为卷帙，题曰“云郎小照”。适墅梅盛开，生偕紫云徘徊于暗香疏影间。巢民偶登内阁，遥望见之，忽佯怒，呼二健仆缚紫云去，将加以杖。生营救无策，意极彷徨，计唯得冒母片言，方解此厄。时已薄暮，乃趋赴母宅前，长跪门外，启门者曰：“陈某有急，求太夫人发一玉音。非蒙许诺，某不起也。”因备言紫云事。

    Explanation

    713

    The Three Bearded Men I Have Known

    The three bearded men I have known within the realm are Jiang Ximing of Cixi, Kang Mengmou of Heyang, and Chen Qini, the scholar from Yangxian. When Chen Qini was not yet successful, he wandered in Guangling. Mao Chaomin, admiring his talent, invited him to his Meihua Villa. There was a child named Zi Yun, who was graceful, beautiful, and skilled in singing. He ordered her to serve in the study. Chen Qini was immediately struck by her beauty, gave her beautiful verses, and drew her portrait, arranging it into a scroll and titling it "A Small Portrait of Cloud Boy." Just as the plum blossoms in the villa were in full bloom, Chen Qini and Zi Yun wandered amidst the faint fragrance and sparse shadows of the flowers. Mao Chaomin happened to ascend to the inner chamber and saw them from afar. Suddenly pretending to be angry, he called two strong servants to bind Zi Yun and take her away, intending to punish her with a beating. Chen Qini had no way to rescue her; he was extremely distressed, thinking only of getting a few words from Mao's mother to resolve this crisis. It was already dusk, so he hurried to the front of Mao's mother's house, knelt down outside the door, and the person who opened the door said, "Tchen has an urgent matter, requests the lady to speak a word. Unless I receive your consent, I will not rise." He then explained the matter of Zi Yun.

    714

    顷之，青衣媪出曰：“先生休矣！巢民遵奉母命，已不罪云郎。然必得先生咏梅绝句百首，成于今夕，仍送云郎侍左右也。”生大喜，摄衣而回，篝灯濡墨，苦吟达曙。百咏既就，亟书送巢民。巢民读之击节，笑遣云郎。其后紫云配妇，合卺有期矣，生惘惘如失，赋《贺新郎》赠之云：“小酌荼蘼酿。喜今朝钗光钿影，灯前滉漾。隔着屏风喧笑语，报道雀翘初上，又悄把檀奴偷相。扑朔雌雄浑不辨，但临风私取春弓量。送尔去，揭鸳帐。〇六年孤馆相依傍。最难忘，红蕤枕畔，泪花轻飏。了尔一生花烛事，宛转妇随夫唱。努力做藁砧模样。只我罗衾浑似铁，拥桃笙难得纱窗亮。休为我，再惆怅。”此词竞传人口，闻者为之绝倒。

    Explanation

    714

    After a while, an elderly woman in blue appeared and said, "You may rest now! Chao Min has obeyed his mother's command and has not punished Yun Lang. However, you must compose a hundred absolute couplets about plum blossoms tonight, and still send Yun Lang to serve you." The scholar was greatly pleased, put on his clothes and returned, lit a lamp and dipped his brush in ink, suffering through the night in poetic composition. The hundred poems were completed, and he hurriedly wrote and sent them to Chao Min. Chao Min read them and clapped in admiration, smiled and dismissed Yun Lang. Later, Ziyun was married to a woman, and the wedding date was set. The scholar felt lost and disoriented, wrote the Hè Xīn Láng to give to her, which read:

    "A small drink of tea made from the tea plant. Delighted today by the gleam of hairpins and ornaments, the light floating of tears before the lamp. Through the screen, the sound of laughter and conversation, reported that the magpie's tail had just risen, and quietly compared the betel nut slave. The male and female are indistinguishable, only when facing the wind, privately measuring the spring bow. Send you away, and lift the phoenix bed curtain. Six years of solitary reliance in a guesthouse. The most unforgettable, the red clusters by the pillow, the light floating of tears. Completed your whole life's marriage, the wife follows the husband's lead. Strive to be like a chopping block. Only my quilt is as hard as iron, holding the peach mat, it's hard to see through the silk window. Do not grieve for me again."

    This poem spread by word of mouth, and those who heard it were amazed.

    715

    [张山来曰：闻髯在水绘园，每年索俸三百余金。辟疆讶其多，髯曰：“我不须金，但以某郎伴我，一夕一金耳！”然不知为紫云、为杨枝也。]
    　　　　合肥宗伯所宠顾夫人，名媚，性爱狸奴。有字乌员者，日于花栏绣榻间徘徊抚玩，珍重之意，逾于掌珠。饲以精粲嘉鱼，过餍而毙。夫人惋悒累日，至为辍膳。宗伯特以沉香斫棺瘗之，延十二女僧，建道场三昼夜。
    　　　　[张山来曰：此猫享用太过，但不识工于捕鼠否？]

    Explanation

    715

    Zhang Shanlai said: "I heard that the bearded one is in Shuihui Garden, asking for a salary of more than three hundred gold each year. Pi Jang was surprised by the amount, and the bearded one said, 'I don't need gold; I just need a certain young man to accompany me, one gold per night!' But it's unclear whether it's Ziyun or Yangzhi."

    The Conzhu Lady, who is the favorite of the Master of Hefei, named Mei, loved cat companions. There was one named Wuyuan, who daily wandered and played with the cat among the flower trellises and embroidered beds, with affection more precious than a pearl on one's palm. Fed with finely prepared fish, it died from overeating. The lady was grieved for several days, even to the point of stopping her meals. The Master specially made a coffin of sandalwood and buried it, inviting twelve female monks to hold a ritual for three days and nights.

    Zhang Shanlai said: "This cat was too pampered, but I wonder if it was good at catching mice?"

    716

    虞初新志卷二十
    　　三侬赘人广自序　苏州汪价三侬啸旨
    　　　　余小时读书西圃，以林鸟为里舍。每展卷，自首讫尾，方理他册，不抽阅，不中辍。坐必竟夜，不停晷，不知寒饿，不栉发頮面。
    　　　　一夕，正拈枯管作时论，忽闻棂外呦呦鬼声，自思不敢为孽，伯有、彭生断不我厉，我岂畏倛头恶刹者耶？燃火迹之，声出竹畦中，见一败叶为蛛丝所罥，风入窍中鸣。余始悟曰：“向以为鬼而嗥者，即此是也。”又一夕，疑耳室有偷儿在焉，持杖逐之。见颀然而立者，人也；以杖横击，偷之衣纷然而坠，但无声息。遽以灯照，乃老苍头浣其故衣，悬之室中。因思天下事原无实相，皆是人以其意造之，嗣是无疑惧心。
    　　　　余尝为牧猪奴戏，凡讌集诩为豪举，辄得大采。又尝事狭斜游，每遇名姝，无乞介人缠头者，或反以橐金佽助膏火。二者皆有利焉，宜其溺矣。忽思轻侠亡赖，非大雅所乐闻，正当一尝恶趣，即解脱耳。一意敕断，更不复为。

    Explanation

    716

    虞初新志卷二十
    　　三侬赘人广自序　苏州汪价三侬啸旨
    　　　　我小时候在西圃读书，以林中的鸟声作为邻居。每次翻开书卷，从头读到尾，才去处理其他书籍，不中途翻阅，不中断。坐读必定通宵，不停歇，不知冷饿，不梳头洗脸。
    　　　　一天晚上，正拿着枯笔写时论，忽然听到窗外传来呦呦的鬼声，自思不敢作恶，伯有、彭生断不会伤害我，我难道还怕那些恶煞吗？点火追踪，声音来自竹林中，看见一片败叶被蛛丝缠绕，风从孔中吹过发出声响。我才明白说：“刚才以为是鬼在叫，原来是这个。”又一天晚上，怀疑耳室里有小偷，手持木棍追赶。看见一个高大的人站在那里，是人；用木棍横扫，小偷的衣服纷乱地落下，但没有声音。立刻用灯照看，原来是老仆人在洗旧衣服，挂在屋内。因此想到天下事原本没有实相，都是人按照自己的意思创造的，从此不再有疑惧之心。
    　　　　我曾做过牧猪奴的游戏，每次聚会都自夸豪举，总是得到好评。又曾涉足狭斜游，每次遇到名妓，不求人帮忙，反而用自己的钱资助她们。这两者都有利可图，难怪会沉迷。忽然想到轻佻无赖的行为，不是高雅人士所乐见的，应当尝试一下恶趣，然后解脱。坚定地决定不再做这样的事，再也不会了。

    717

    向应京兆试，数见刖于有司。友人同斥者，多惝怳悲惶，泪簌簌雨下。余则廓落宴笑，犹故吾也。甲申当国变，天地裂崩，邑令修故事，群士大夫临于县庭，口呼大行，含辛以为泪。余独号踊，几不欲生，平日泪不轻挥，谓其近于妇人也；自丧二亲以来，中心抽割，唯此一恸。
    　　　　余鲜兄弟，止仲子一人，早游芹水，会逢世乱，乃隐于市。端木货殖，亦何所讥？阃以内，妻妾二人，雍容井臼，各生二男，共保抱之，无异视。四子友爱，一如同产。二氏皆先我化去。奉倩哀殒，蒙庄鼓歌，俱失物情之正。余唯顺天委运，礼以制哀而已。诸子善承吾教，亦喜诵古人书，亦竞为歌诗，亦嗜杯酌，亦精于奕，亦涉书林画苑，亦好作四方游。余尝戏语曰：“诸如类我，不忝以生，颓老不遇，幸无克肖。”今皆得成遂，皆有妻孥，皆服章缝为圣门弟子，骎骎乎有进取之意，得者自得，失者自失，不以萦老人之怀。

    Explanation

    717

    I took the Jingzhao magistrate examination and was harshly criticized by officials on multiple occasions. Friends who were also criticized often felt confused, sad, and fearful, with tears falling like rain. I, however, remained calm and cheerful, as I always was. In the year of Jia Shen (1644), when the nation underwent a great change, the heavens and earth seemed to split apart. The county magistrate followed traditional customs, and officials gathered in the county hall, calling out the name of the deceased emperor and suppressing their tears. I alone wailed and jumped in grief, almost wanting to die. I do not easily shed tears, believing that doing so is unmanly; since the death of my parents, I have felt a deep emotional pain, but this was the only time I truly grieved.

    I have few brothers, only one son, who studied early and encountered turmoil in the world, so he went into hiding in the city. Engaging in trade is not something to be criticized. Within the home, I have two wives, who are both well-mannered and manage household affairs. Each has given birth to two sons, and I treat them all equally. The four sons are loving and friendly, just as if they were siblings. Both wives have passed away before me. I lamented their passing as if I had lost my own life, but I also accepted their departure as part of the natural order. I have simply followed the will of heaven and used rituals to control my grief. My children follow my teachings well, enjoy reading ancient texts, compete in writing poetry, enjoy drinking, are skilled in chess, are interested in books and art, and enjoy traveling. I once jokingly said, "If they are similar to me, they will not disgrace themselves by being born. Old age and misfortune are fortunate if they do not exactly resemble me." Now they have all achieved their goals, have wives and children, dress in scholarly attire, and follow the teachings of Confucius. They are eager to advance. Those who succeed do so, and those who fail do so, without troubling my heart.

    718

    至若朋友，吾性命也。愿言结契，莫非俊人；率尔相遭，便如夙昔。脱口披肝膈之言，对面领诗书之气。有若志迹乘离、判若行路者，即其人可知矣。鼎新以后，同学吾友，仕粵东者死兵合浦令陈室臣，大埔令蒋文若，化州守曹蜚孟，粤西者死疾兴安令王非台，宰峄者死墨误峄县令吴丕能，帅河北者死颠连河北左营游击沈元培，贡大廷者死于鬼、于盗侯公羊病而死祟，张政起为盗劫杀，仕兗仕苕仕汾者，皆以真朴不能突梯上官，并见黜落兗州通判项莘友，武康令吴定远，平遥令朱兼两，以进士为吏部选人，沉废数十年，不能沾一命者多有。

    Explanation

    718

    As for friends, they are my life. Wishing to form bonds, none are unworthy men; meeting casually, it feels as if from old times. Speaking openly with words that reveal one's innermost thoughts, meeting face-to-face with the aura of poetry and books. Those whose aspirations and paths diverge, as if traveling different roads, then it is known about that person. After the new era, my fellow students and friends: those who served in Guangdong and died in battle as the magistrate of Hepu, Chen Shichen; the magistrate of Dabu, Jiang Wenruo; the governor of Huazhou, Cao Feimeng; those who served in western Guangdong and died of illness as the magistrate of Xing'an, Wang Feitai; the magistrate of Yi, who died of black ink (a euphemism for suicide) as the magistrate of Yi, Wu Pinen; the commander of Hebei, who died in a series of misfortunes as the left camp commander of Hebei, Shen Yuanpei; the candidate for the imperial examination, who died by ghosts, by bandits, Hou Gongyang, who died of illness and was haunted; Zhang Zhengqi was killed by bandits; those who served in Yan, Taohu, and Fen, all of them died because of their sincerity and simplicity, unable to flatter their superiors, and were demoted. The deputy governor of Yanzhou, Xiang Xinyou; the magistrate of Wukang, Wu Dingyuan; the magistrate of Pingyao, Zhu Jianliang, as jinshi (imperial examination graduates) selected by the Ministry of Personnel, were submerged and wasted away for decades, unable to receive even a minor appointment, and many of them were in such a state.

    719

    嗟嗟！士人著进贤冠，为南面贵人，可谓荣矣！乃累累遭挫辱，终其身困踣不聊，以至死。余虽不幸，犹得优游林水，泰然以韦布老。酒国诗城，长为三侬汤沐邑，此非天纵之耇民哉？余一生遭罹，大抵平乐，间有奇厄，冥冥之中，默为提救。壬申，随先君官楚，道经彭泽。江岸忽崩，檣柁尽折，舟压其下，料无生理。食顷，有声{门赤}然，舟浮水面，是岁家中不戒于火，藏书数万卷悉成灰烬。归而典衣赁屋，复集数千卷。

    Explanation

    719

    Alas! A scholar wearing the hat of virtue, serving as a high-ranking official facing south, can truly be called honored! Yet, he suffers repeated setbacks and humiliations, living a life of constant distress and despair until death. Although I have been unfortunate, I still manage to live a leisurely and comfortable life in the natural scenery, calmly growing old in a simple and humble manner. In a place rich in wine and poetry, I always have a fief for maintenance and enjoyment. Is this not a man divinely endowed with wisdom and longevity? Throughout my life, I have mostly experienced peace and joy, occasionally facing extraordinary misfortunes, but in the unseen realm, I have been silently helped and rescued.
