In the year Ren Shen, I followed my father to serve in the state of Chu, passing through Pengze. Suddenly, the riverbank collapsed, the mast and rudder were completely broken, and the boat was crushed and submerged, with no chance of survival. After a short while, there was a sudden noise, and the boat floated back to the surface. That year, my home suffered a fire without warning, and my library of tens of thousands of volumes turned to ashes. I returned home, pawned my clothes, rented a house, and again gathered several thousand volumes.

    720

    乙酉城陷，为乱兵所掠，仅存零帙，遍从书肆配合，其粗有头讫者，又得数百卷。辛卯，被一穷戚胠窃殆尽。于三四年中，节汤糜之费，又聚得数十卷。丁酉遇祸，皂隶入吾室，枵然乌有也；见几上书，捆之以去。因忆往昔平阳书乘，珍护甚严，唯恐饱蟫鼠之腹。乃于二十余年之内，一灾于火，二灾于兵，三灾于盗，四灾于皂隶，可胜叹哉！乙酉，江左鼎沸，海上帅纵兵劫民舍，口呼缚儒冠者，破我闼而入，剿掠靡遗，余几被絷，越墙而仅免。己亥，入豫州，过老儿庄，群盗截劫。一魁曰：“彼书生者，行李可怜，不足供东道。”大笑扬鞭而去。

    Explanation

    720

    The city fell in the year of the Earth Rooster (乙酉), and my belongings were plundered by rebel soldiers, leaving only a few scattered volumes. I gathered them all from bookshops, restoring those that had some semblance of completeness, and obtained several hundred more scrolls. In the year of the Metal Rabbit (辛卯), a poor relative stole almost everything. Over the course of three or four years, I saved from the expenses of soup and porridge to collect another few dozen scrolls. In the year of the Fire Rooster (丁酉), I encountered disaster, and the servants entered my room, leaving nothing behind; they saw the books on the table and tied them up and took them away. Reflecting on the past, I remembered the days in Pingyang when I took great care to protect my books, fearing they might be devoured by bookworms and rats. Within twenty years, I suffered three disasters by fire, two by soldiers, three by thieves, and four by servants—how can one bear to lament such misfortunes! In the year of the Earth Rooster (乙酉), the region east of the Yangtze River was in chaos, and the naval commander allowed his troops to plunder the people's homes, calling out to bind those wearing scholar's caps. They broke into my door, plundering without leaving anything behind, and I nearly was captured, jumping over the wall and barely escaping. In the year of the Earth Pig (己亥), I entered Yuzhou and passed through Laorizhuang, where a group of bandits intercepted and robbed. One leader said, "That scholar has pitiable luggage, not enough to serve as a host." They laughed loudly and whipped their horses away.

    721

    余于行路，凡三遇虎。壬申，先君命余至荆州谒贺惠藩，道经玉泉山，有虎踞崖。仆夫骇走，虎跃入田，攫一鸡，掠余马尾越涧去。庚子，游密之超化砦，饮于张鉴空山斋，红蕊侑酒，不觉狂醉，扶置马上，鼾然据鞍而行。闻从人欢噪声，次日始知有虎引二子饮涧中，都无动色。甲辰，游富春山，登子陵钓处，因访桐君，见山凹绝巇，一白额虎坐瞷溪流。余与众客方侧行岩下，虎张爪竖尾，欲来扑人。众客噤战俯地，余拱手语之曰：“山君山君，闻声久矣，今日得瞻神采，幸无妨我去路。仆所携三寸弱管耳，当挥斥成长律奉献。”虎点首者三，一啸跳入丛莽。与众客越宿樵子之庐，燃灯疾书五排六十韵。天方曙，以诗焚故处，祝之曰：“一言相赠，余不爽约。君有英神，能无印可乎？”是夜，梦虎头人来谢教，持鹿酒共酌。兴正酣，为役夫催起，乃惊失之。

    Explanation

    721

    I encountered the tiger three times during my travels. In the year of Ren Shen, my late father commanded me to go to Jingzhou to pay respects to Governor He Hui, and on the way, we passed through Yüquan Mountain, where a tiger was perched on a cliff. The driver was frightened and ran away, and the tiger jumped into a field, seized a chicken, grazed my horse's tail, and jumped over a ravine. In the year of Geng Zi, I traveled to the Chao Hua Fort in Mi and drank at Zhang Jian's mountain hermitage, where Red Pearl served the wine. I became so drunk that I was carried onto the horse and slept soundly, leaning on the saddle. The next day, I realized that a tiger had led two children to drink in the ravine, but they showed no fear. In the year of Jia Chen, I traveled to Fuchun Mountain and climbed the place where Zilin fished. Because of visiting Tong Jun, I saw a deep ravine in the mountain and a white-tipped tiger sitting and watching the stream. I and the guests were walking slowly along the rock face, and the tiger spread its claws and raised its tail, wanting to pounce on people. The guests were silent and cowering on the ground, but I bowed and spoke to it: "Mountain lord, mountain lord, I have heard your voice for a long time. Today I have the opportunity to see your divine appearance. Please do not obstruct my path. I only carry a weak three-inch pipe, but I will expand it into a long poem and present it to you." The tiger nodded three times, roared, and jumped into the dense forest. With the guests, I spent the night in the hut of a woodcutter and lit a lamp to quickly write fifty stanzas of sixty rhymes. The sky was just beginning to dawn, and I burned the poem at the original place, praying to it: "I give one word as a gift, and I will not break the promise. You have divine spirit, can you not approve?" That night, I dreamed of a person with a tiger head coming to thank me for teaching, bringing deer wine and drinking together. The mood was at its height, but I was urged by the servant to get up and woke up in surprise.

    722

    余短于目，穷睫之力，不及寻丈，道途拱揖，不辨为谁。迨老而视不加眊，昏暮能审文字点画，灯下书红笺，能作细楷，以光常内敛也。相传文人目多眚，归咎读书焚膏继晷，以致损明。此言近诬，殆由天分。宋学士作《咨目瞳文》，罪其失职，冤矣！余诎于目，而耳倍聪，嘤嚶私语，虽远必闻，睡梦之中，有声即觉。四足者无羽翼，予之角者去其齿，殆是之谓乎？贱目眶大而睛露，有议其蜂目不祥、鹰目为暴者，此世俗之惑也。古有兽其形而人其心者，羲、农之牛首而蛇身是也；有人其形而兽其心者，桀、纣之长巨姣美而筋骨越劲是也。而又何法相之足去乎？
    　　　　余足不健于行，然亦曾走百里，不见苦攰。至如登山觅胜，扪萝跻险，命且不惜。不能守“齿刚舌柔”之说，好齮龁刚物，未六十而齳然落其二。时逞舌锋，以言语抵忤人，人以不堪。初时不省，后乃悔之。吾年既迈，有客相见，必减我年数，誉我以红颜，则其为衰惫，亦可知也。

    Explanation

    722

    I am short-sighted, and even with the utmost effort of my eyelids, I cannot see beyond a few feet. On the road, I bow and greet people but cannot recognize who they are. But as I grew old, my vision did not worsen; in dim light, I can still discern the strokes of characters, and under the lamp, writing on red paper, I can write fine calligraphy, because my light is usually inwardly contained. It is said that scholars often have eye problems, attributing it to reading by burning fat to continue through the night, which damages the eyes. This statement is nearly false; it is probably due to natural disposition. A Song dynasty scholar wrote a text titled "Inquiry on the Eye and Pupil," accusing the eyes of failing in their duty, which is unfair! I am short-sighted but have exceptionally sharp hearing; even distant whispers are heard, and in sleep, any sound will wake me. Four-legged creatures without wings, those with horns lose their teeth—this is what it means, I suppose? My eye sockets are large and my eyes are prominent, and some say my eyes are like bees' and are ominous, or like eagles' and are fierce, but this is a common misconception. In ancient times, there were creatures with animal forms but human hearts, such as Fuxi and Shennong, who had the heads of cattle and bodies of snakes; there were also people with human forms but animal hearts, such as Jie and Zhou, who were tall, beautiful, and strong. Why should we discard the concept of physiognomy?

    My feet are not strong in walking, yet I have walked a hundred miles without tiring. As for climbing mountains to find scenic spots, grasping vines and scaling dangerous places, I would not hesitate to risk my life. I cannot adhere to the saying "teeth are hard, tongue is soft"; I like to bite hard objects, and before sixty, two teeth fell out. Often using sharp words to contradict others, people find it unbearable. At first, I did not understand, later I regretted it. As I grow old, when guests meet me, they always reduce my age and praise my youthful appearance, then it is known that I am old and tired.

    723

    余在蓉江，受异人术，能炼臂为铁，听力士仡如虎者张拳击之，余臂无恙。至十数击，而彼拳萎苶，不能举矣。海昌查伊璜尝言有豪客者，铁臂与余无二。客本武林窭人也，伊璜宴客湖心亭，客艧破舟畔索酒，伊璜拉与同饮，酣叫尽欢。饮毕，悉以余馔赠之。后客仗剑从军，底定闽粤，以功帅于交广之间，锡有封爵。伊璜以明史事挂累，客感酒食之惠，阴为营救，冤乃白。同一臂术耳，客以窭而侯，余特用之以戏，犹是孱书生也，可哂也！
    　　　　庚子，擢得白发，为文以骂之。白发对以肊曰：“鹿，仙畜也，千年而苍，又千年而白。龟，四灵之一也，五百年而紫，又五百年而白。然则白也者，物老而圣，斯足以当之。”余由是得老而娱，得白而喜。吾愿天下学道人，共闻斯语。

    Explanation

    723

    While in Rongjiang, I received a technique from a mysterious person that allowed me to refine my arm into iron. I tested this ability against a powerful warrior as strong as a tiger, who opened his fist to strike me, but my arm remained unharmed. After several strikes, his fist grew weak and listless, and he could no longer lift it. Hai Chang's Zha Yihuang once spoke of a powerful guest with an iron arm just like mine. This guest was originally a poor person from Wulin. Zha Yihuang hosted him at the Lake Heart Pavilion, where the guest, sitting on the shore of a broken boat, asked for wine. Zha Yihuang invited him to join in the drinking, and they enjoyed themselves thoroughly. After the feast, Zha Yihuang gave him all the remaining food. Later, the guest took up arms and joined the military, pacifying Fujian and Guangdong, and was bestowed with a title and rank for his achievements. Zha Yihuang faced trouble due to involvement in historical matters, and the guest, grateful for the hospitality, secretly rescued him, clearing his name. It is the same arm technique, yet the guest, starting as a poor man, became a marquis, while I merely used it for play, remaining a weak scholar—a laughable situation!

    In the year Gengzi, I grew white hair and wrote to mock it. The white hair responded in my heart, saying, "A deer is a divine animal, turning gray after a thousand years and white after another thousand years. A turtle is one of the four divine creatures, turning purple after five hundred years and white after another five hundred years. Therefore, when things grow old, they become sacred, and this is sufficient to describe it." Thus, I found joy in growing old and happiness in turning white. I wish that all scholars who study the Dao may hear these words together.

    724

    余南土弱夫，素倚舟楫，与鞍辔不相谋。随李御史渡河，撤舆而马，御史振策逐余马而驰，余身若翥霄堮之外，目迷阴曀，耳轰怒涛，始而惊，既而爽，终而安焉。后此群骑并出，余马必先骛。崇祯末，习射于石岗之汝南书墅，弓张矢落，同学者以为笑。余愤欲胜之，味射义“志正体直，持而审固”之语，悬的者三匝月，心柔手熟，忽焉大进。以是知人不贵自然，贵勉然。性不可恃，而习有可通，大抵然矣。
    　　　　余善饮而不善啖，饭可二缶，常食不能噉大脔；客之饕者，喜并余餐。侨朔方者数年，日食蒸饼不托之属，生酱鲜葱有同嗜焉，归而馔且兼人，反觉稻粱之寡味。五岁时，私闯酒室，垂首盎面，吸取浮醴，遂至沉顿。家人遍索，乃酣卧于瓶罍之侧。长而僭称大户，常时列宴，众客支离，狂花病叶，独沛国朱抡生搴旗对垒，终夕不言散，时有“朱鸡啼”、“汪天亮”之目。主人悦，间亦取憎侍者。

    Explanation

    724

    I am a weak man from the southern lands, who has always relied on boats and oars, and never engaged with horse gear. Following Censor Li across the river, we exchanged our sedan chairs for horses. The censor urged his horse to chase after mine, and I felt as if I were soaring above the clouds, my eyes dazzled by the darkness, and my ears deafened by the roaring waves. At first, I was startled, then refreshed, and finally at ease. Afterward, when the group of riders set off, my horse always led the way. In the waning days of the Chongzhen era, I practiced archery at the Shigan Academy in Runan. The bowstring was taut, and the arrow fell, which made my classmates laugh. I became determined to improve, pondering the archery maxim "Mind upright, body straight, hold firmly and steady." For three months, I practiced with the target, my heart and hands becoming soft and skilled, and I suddenly made great progress. From this, I learned that one should not rely on nature, but on effort. Nature cannot be relied upon, but habit can be cultivated, and this is generally true.

    I am good at drinking but not at eating. I can eat two jars of rice, but I cannot chew large pieces of meat. Guests who are gluttonous enjoy sharing my meals. I lived in the northern regions for several years, eating steamed buns and other simple foods every day, and I shared a taste for fresh soybean paste and green onions. Upon returning, I ate twice as much as before, finding rice and grains to be bland. At five years old, I secretly entered the wine room, hung my head, and drank the floating wine, becoming drunk and falling asleep. My family searched everywhere, and I was found lying drunk beside the jars. As I grew older, I arrogantly claimed to be a wealthy person, often hosting banquets where guests were disheveled and drunk. Only Zhu Lunsheng from Pei Kingdom competed fiercely, and we stayed up all night without dispersing. He was nicknamed "Red Chicken Crows," and I was called "Wang Tianliang." The host was pleased, but sometimes he also took a dislike to the servants.

    725

    计余一生，曾有二醉：壬寅，与合肥龚伯通饮于怀庆之高台寺。同饮者，王蜀隐、沈云门。所饮者，五香柿酒，此朔方烧醴之最俊者。四人篝灯细酌，自酉达卯，倾二罌无剩沥，饮时但觉甜美可人，无茗艼意。从者报曰：“日高舂矣！”四人启户而视，触受风色，心目迷眩，一时俱倒。余睡至日晡而复。三公者，相对哕咯，病不起者累日。是年在邺之旅舍，候李御史行旆，痴坐无憀，闻西郊演剧，观者甚众，趁步一往。台之旁，列肆酤酒，士商聚饮，不觉流涎，因选席而坐，傲然独酌。已而兴发，拉客中之豪者并釂，拇战不已，遂曼及他席。大众轰饮，余玉山颓矣。彼此造次，未及叙姓氏，亦未识余邸舍，群起而掖余，舁之野庙神幔之前。迨晓，怪笑而回。“名教中自有乐地”，昔贤所云，时复戢之。

    Explanation

    725

    Counting my entire life, I have had two instances of drunkenness: in the year of Renyin, I drank with Gong Botong of Hefei at the Gao Tai Temple in Huaiqing. My companions were Wang Shuyin and Shen Yunmen. We drank five-spice persimmon wine, the finest among the northern distilled liquors. The four of us lit lamps and sipped slowly, from酉to卯, finishing two罌with no drop left. While drinking, it was only sweet and pleasing, without any hangover feeling. A servant reported, "It's already high noon!" The four of us opened the door to look, and were immediately overwhelmed by the wind, dizzy and disoriented, all falling down at once. I slept until late afternoon and then woke up. The other three, relative to me, were retching and sick for several days. That year, while waiting for the magistrate Li at an inn in Ye, sitting aimlessly, I heard there was a play in the west suburbs, with many spectators. I went there. Near the stage, there were stalls selling wine, and scholars and merchants were drinking together. I couldn't help but salivate, so I chose a seat and drank alone, feeling proud. Later, feeling inspired, I pulled some of the more daring guests to finish their drinks, and we kept playing drinking games. We gradually moved to other tables. Everyone drank together, and I was completely drunk. In the rush, we didn't have time to exchange names or know each other's addresses. They all helped me up and carried me to a shrine in a field. By morning, I returned with strange laughter. "There is a place of joy within the norms of society," as the ancient sages said, sometimes I would restrain myself.

    726

    余不习铛杓，而洞于茶理。友人戴惕庵，为邑之陆羽。余时过领日铸，以消七碗之兴。及至杞子国，有马布庵者，又卢埜之后劲也。一枪一旗，居然独步。尝戏语之：“若与吾乡惕庵共品泉源，正未知谁当北面。”余于甲辰偶然禁酒，有句云：“我当上奏天帝庭，酒星谪去补茶星。”此亦老侬谩言，非实尔也。性好食醋，失此则诸味不调。又好秋末蟹、夏初蚕豆，二物充庖，不想他味。人以汪生所嗜，不殊屈到之芰、姬文之昌歜。近日俗尚食烟，余每语人：“奈何以火烧五脏？请观筒中垢腻，将何以堪？”其人猛省，誓不再食。少焉忆之，便渝戒矣。病酒之夫，狂饮不待明朝；难产之妇，好合何须满月？嗜烟之酷，乃至同与酒色，何惑溺也！

    Explanation

    726

    I am not skilled in cooking, but I understand tea deeply. My friend Dai Ti'an is the local Lu Yu. I often visit him to enjoy the seven cups of tea, to satisfy my craving for tea. When I arrived in the mythical land of Qi Zi, there was Ma Bu'an, who is a strong finisher in the tradition of Lu Yu. His tea leaves, one spear and one flag, are truly unmatched. I once jokingly told him, "If you and my friend Ti'an were to compare the quality of water, I wonder who would show respect to whom." In the year Jia Chen, I accidentally abstained from alcohol and wrote, "I shall present a petition to the Heavenly Emperor's court, asking that the star of wine be demoted to the star of tea." This is just the idle talk of an old man, not meant to be taken seriously. I have a fondness for vinegar, and without it, all flavors are out of balance. I also enjoy crabs at the end of autumn and broad beans at the beginning of summer. These two delicacies fill my kitchen, and I have no desire for anything else. People say that my tastes are not unlike those of Qu Dao, who loved lotus, and Ji Wen, who favored the dish Changchu. Recently, there has been a trend of smoking, and I often tell people, "How can you burn your internal organs with fire? Look at the residue inside the pipe, and you will see how it can be endured?" Some people suddenly realize and swear never to smoke again. But soon after, they forget their vow. A person addicted to alcohol, drunk without waiting for the morning; a woman suffering from difficult childbirth, no need to wait for a full month to be together? The addiction to smoking is as severe as that to wine and women, what a state of being!

    727

    余家常乏，独衣冠必鲜整。人目之，若雄于財者。然少而惜福，茧丝不以附内体，服之矜重，不轻为尘涴，即至褛裂，亦不轻掷。《记》曰：“敝帷不弃，为埋马也。”尝记先大夫于余入泮时，制一西洋布袍，凡遇佳节良讌，则衣之，几三十年，不之澡濯。有劝余改作亵衣者，贾子曰：“冠虽敝，弗以苴履。”先人所赐，吾不忍也。先人之敝庐，不过数楹，团聚家人，三世不易其旧。余日坐卧者，止于半舫，围塞书卷，栉比鳞次，容我头足一席地耳。俯仰之余，不见其窄。出而翔步王公之第，崇构迢峣，霞垂云耸，佘处之落落然，了无与也。“公自见其朱门，贫道如游蓬户”，大智之言，岂欺我哉！

    Explanation

    727

    My family often lacks resources, but my clothing and attire are always fresh and tidy. People look at me, as if I am wealthy. However, from a young age, I have been careful with my blessings; silk does not cling to my inner body. I wear them with solemnity, not lightly allowing them to be soiled by dust. Even when they become tattered, I do not easily discard them. The Book of Rites says, "Do not discard old curtains, for they can be used to bury horses." I remember that when I entered the school, my late father made a Western-style cotton robe for me. Whenever there were good festivals and banquets, I would wear it. For nearly thirty years, I did not wash or clean it. Someone once advised me to turn it into underclothes, but the Book of Rites says, "Even if the hat is old, do not use it to wrap shoes." Given by my ancestors, I cannot bear to discard it. The old house of my ancestors, no more than a few rooms, gathers the family, and for three generations, it has not changed its old state. The place where I sit and sleep, no more than half a boat, is filled with books, arranged in rows like fish scales, leaving just enough space for my head and feet. In the midst of daily life, one does not see its narrowness. Stepping out into the grand residences of nobles, the towering structures hang like clouds and rise like mountains. I feel out of place, with nothing to connect me. "The noble sees his red gate, while the poor wander like a beggar," a wise saying, how could it deceive me?

    728

    余爱楼居及庋板之房，不耐卑庳下湿。又爱短檐净几，其窗四辟，晨起披襟，爽受风日。如入闇室幽暧，便闷欲绝。又爱舟行，放浆芦洲蓼渚之间，率其宕往，有会心处，嗒尔忘归。余向不喜浴，虽夏月，亦止以巾拭汗，老始习之，乃觉除淹消瘕，体气荣杨，即沍寒，且乐就澡室焉。

    Explanation

    728

    I love living in high-rise buildings and rooms with shelves for books, but I cannot tolerate low, damp places. I also prefer rooms with short eaves and a clean desk, where the windows are open on all sides. In the morning, I open my robe and enjoy the cool breeze and sunlight. Like entering a dark, damp room, it makes one feel suffocated and nearly faint. I also enjoy sailing a boat, letting the oars drift in the reed marshes and lotus ponds, following one's whimsical journey, where there is a place of understanding, one forgets to return. I used to dislike bathing, even in summer, I only used a towel to wipe sweat. Only began to practice it in old age, then realized it could remove dampness, dissolve tumors, and make the body healthy and vibrant, even in freezing cold, one is happy to go to the bathhouse.

    729

    余得天强固，不婴重疴，偶尔违和，亦不用药，医之以至清之酒，医之以至快之书。辛巳午月，贱体忽惫，头涔涔作楚，一日夕不思汤饵，若染时疠者。适有饷余佳酿，呼至床头开看，芬香拉鼻，急命温之。取太史公《荆轲传》连饮连读，瞬息之间，拍案而起。古书难信，切不可以身试方。吾友贾静子，睢阳才人也，体有不适，欲行“倒仓”之法。余诤之曰：“奈何于腹中演戏法？”不听，一服之后，下泄不止而毙。岂惟药石，即平时饮膳，皆可伤人。余尝于醉后饮养花宿水，不死；于相国寺僧舍误中鲜菌毒，不死；此小人倖免也。子美死于白酒牛脯，太白纵饮采石，捉月而亡。李、杜，诗人之魁也，皆以轻率自殒其生，可不慎哉！

    Explanation

    729

    I was endowed with robust health and did not suffer from serious illnesses; occasionally feeling unwell, I did not need medicine, treating myself with the purest wine and the most delightful books. In the month of May during the year Xin Si, my humble body suddenly became exhausted, feeling dizzy and uncomfortable. For a whole day and night, I did not want any food or medicine, as if infected by a seasonal epidemic. Just then, I was given a fine wine as a gift. I called it to the bedside, opened it, and the pleasant smell filled my nose. I quickly ordered it to be warmed. I took the Records of the Grand Historian's chapter on Jing Ke and drank the wine while reading it simultaneously. Within an instant, I jumped up from my bed. Ancient books are unreliable; one should not risk one's health on them. My friend Jia Jingzi, a talented person from Suizhou, felt unwell and wanted to try the 'dao cang' method. I advised him, saying, "How can you perform magic tricks inside your stomach?" He did not listen, and after taking it once, he suffered from diarrhea and died. Not only medicines, but even daily food and drink can be harmful. I once drank water used to water flowers after getting drunk, but I did not die; I once accidentally ate poisonous mushrooms in a temple in the Xiangguosi area, but I did not die; this was just a narrow escape for this lowly person. Du Fu died from drinking strong wine and eating beef, and Li Bai drowned while trying to catch the moon while drinking. Li Bai and Du Fu, the greatest poets of all time, both died due to their recklessness; how careful one must be!

    730

    壮时不免房帷之好，后乃以渐而淡。至为汗漫游，遂与色远。即燕赵歌姬，充列侑饮，从无一人沾昵者。北妓入席，见客即拜，立而执役，主人加之诃叱。余命之入坐，诸执事悉令隶人司之，北人且谓介人坏其乡俗礼貌。知命之年，便绝婉娈，友人俱诮其假，席间每引为笑资。李賸斋至谓“五十断欲，不如捐馆作泉下人”。彼长余四龄，竟以啖牛胾，淫一妖妪而殂。夫精、气、神，人之“三宝”，而丹药之壬也。

    Explanation

    730

    In my youth, I could not avoid the pleasures of the bedroom, but later these desires gradually became less intense. When I began to wander aimlessly, I distanced myself from such indulgences. Even if there were courtesans from Yan and Zhao filling the ranks to entertain with drinks, there was never anyone with whom I became intimate. Northern courtesans entering the hall, bowing to guests upon arrival and standing to serve, were often harshly scolded by their hosts. I ordered them to sit down, and all tasks were assigned to servants, leading northerners to criticize me for ruining their local customs and manners. At the age of knowing one's destiny, I completely gave up on romantic entanglements, and friends would mock me for being insincere, often using this as a source of amusement during gatherings. Li Shengzai even said, "It's better to die than to renounce desires at the age of fifty." He was four years older than me and eventually died after eating beef and having relations with a witch. Essence, energy, and spirit are the 'three treasures' of humans and are the essence of alchemy.

    731

    先祖遇一异人，授以“龙虎吐纳”之法，习练四十年，道成，夏月盖重衾卧炽日中，无纤汗；冬以大桶满贮凉水，没顶而坐，竟日不知寒。余以骨顽无仙分，不之向学，然于玄牝要诀，颇熟闻之：大要以宝神啬精为主。世之愚伦，纵情雕伐，以致阳弱不起，乃求助于禽虫之末。蛤蚧，偶虫也，采之以为媚药；山獭，淫毒之兽，取其势以壮阳；海狗以一牡管百牝，鬻之助房中之术。何其戕真败道，贵兽而贱人也！且方士挟采阴之说，谓御女可得长生，则吾未见蛤蚧成丹、山獭尸解、海狗之白日冲举也。

    Explanation

    731

    My ancestor encountered a mysterious figure who taught him the "Dragon and Tiger Breathing" technique. After practicing for forty years, he achieved mastery, able to sleep under heavy quilts in the scorching summer sun without breaking a sweat; in winter, he could sit in a large bucket filled with cold water, submerging his head, and remain unaware of the cold all day. I, being stubborn and lacking the spiritual aptitude for such practices, did not pursue them, yet I am quite familiar with the essential principles of conserving essence and spirit. In the world, those who indulge excessively in physical desires often become weak and unable to recover, and thus turn to animal-based remedies. Geckos, which are small creatures, are collected to be used as aphrodisiacs; mountain otters, known for their lewd nature, are used to enhance virility; male seals, who mate with many females, are sold to aid in sexual practices. How degrading and contrary to the path of cultivation is this, valuing animals over humans! Moreover, alchemists who promote the idea that controlling women can lead to immortality have not seen geckos turning into elixirs, mountain otters achieving spiritual liberation, or male seals flying into the heavens on a white day.

    732

    记诵之外，无时不亲操诸务，盥漱泛扫，不以烦厮役。花则手灌之，草则手薅之，鱼鸟则手饲之。或杂伍渔樵，或混同佣乞，或时与童稚相嬲，掷弄觽鲽以嬉，故年虽近髦，人以为有童心。举步轻跃，容色亦不衰，不似龙钟齿豁人。年来游兴不减，梦想时在湖滣岳麓。诸子惜余筋力，柅余车不得远行。在家闲极，有花即看，有酒即饮，有对弈者即终日。老友相值，即解杖头以醵；缁流之上者，乐共余谈；余亦乐坐旃檀之室，谓之清时小太平。适与红裙会，方袍骨董，不至以唐突取厌。赠邗水桂姬有“休将量大欺红袖，但得情痴恕白头”之句，非乞怜语，佳人会生怜耳。

    Explanation

    732

    Beyond rote memorization, I never fail to personally handle various tasks, washing, brushing, and sweeping without bothering the servants. I water the flowers by hand, weed the grass by hand, and feed the fish and birds by hand. Sometimes I mingle with fishermen and woodcutters, sometimes mix with laborers and beggars, and occasionally play with children, throwing and playing with fish for fun. Thus, though I am nearing old age, people think I still have a childlike heart. My steps are light and agile, and my appearance shows no signs of aging, unlike an old man who is toothless and hunched. My desire for travel remains undiminished, and I often dream of the scenic areas by the lakes and mountains. My sons pity my strength and stop me from traveling far. At home, I am very idle, looking at flowers when they bloom, drinking wine when it is available, and spending the whole day playing chess if there is someone to play with. When old friends meet, I take out money from my belt to share. The highest among the monks are happy to talk with me, and I also enjoy sitting in a room of sandalwood, calling it a small peace in clear times. When meeting with a woman in a red skirt, monks and scholars, I do not act rudely and cause disgust. I gave a gift to a lady by the Yangtze River with the lines, "Do not use your large capacity to bully a woman, but if you are emotionally痴痴 (deeply in love), forgive my old age." It is not a plea for pity, but a true expression of affection that a good woman would understand.

    733

    孙子数人，与长者点定文字，粗为疏解。群小则牵绕衣裙，分枣栗与之，各餍所欲而往。分之必均，偶有参差，聚而向老人计较，尤可爱也。
    　　　　余行李半天下，所至以客为家。客两河者，前后十数年，始于察荒李御史幕，怀孟薛宗伯知之，呼至其家，与仲蒨二兄读书翕园。后为贾大中丞召修省志，别去。越三年，会吊宗伯之丧，黄门卫公先生正在读《礼》，留与崥山草堂，商榷今古。又为洛阳太守朱灿煌邀阅试卷，别去。介人之久于兹土者，实以宗伯父子恩分滋深，故依刘御李，马首不能他指耳。时沈宫詹绎堂先生分巡大梁，清慈明允，为海内岳牧表。余驱车八郡，历收河岳之英，倦则以钧阳清署为归焉。其他逆旅主人，无不款暱如戚属。水行则戒榜人无妨缓棹，柯上逍遥；陆行则常与执辔者试走，舍舆马而徒，恣其流览。余之所为通，余之所为介也。

    Explanation

    733

    The young ones, together with the elder, would correct and revise the writing, providing a rough explanation. The children would tangle around their clothes, and dates and chestnuts would be distributed to them, each satisfying their desires and then going away. When distributing, it was necessary to ensure equality; if there were occasional discrepancies, they would gather and discuss with the elder, which was especially lovable.

    I have traveled half the world, treating wherever I go as home. Among my guests in the two rivers, there were more than ten years, starting from the council of Censor Li, who was inspecting famine, and Grand Master of Rites Xue from Huai and Meng knew about it, calling me to his home, where I studied with my two brothers, Zhong and Qian, in Xiyuan Garden. Later, I was summoned by Grand Censor Jia to compile provincial records and left. After three years, I happened to attend the funeral of Grand Master of Rites, when Mr. Wei, the Yellow Gate, was reading the Book of Rites and invited me to stay at Qingshan Cottage, where we discussed ancient and modern times. I was also invited by Governor of Luoyang, Mr. Zhu Canhuang, to review examination papers and left again. Among those who have stayed long in this land, it is actually because the affection between Grand Master of Rites and his father and son has deepened, so I relied on Liu and governed Li, and my horse's head could not point elsewhere. At that time, Mr. Shen, the Palace Censor, was touring Daliang, clear, kind, bright, and just, serving as a model for leaders within the country. I drove my carriage through eight prefectures, collecting the best from rivers and mountains, and when tired, I treated Junyang's clean office as my place to return. Other inn hosts were all close and friendly like family. When traveling by water, I instructed the boatman not to hinder the slow rowing, wandering freely along the banks; when traveling by land, I often rode with the driver, leaving the carriage and horses behind, and freely exploring. What I do is to be open-minded, and what I do is to be an intermediary.

    734

    余殚精音律，于古今离合之义，无不博综。吾邑陆君扬，弦索化工手也，从余考订音声，字有讹舛，悉为厘正。遂使八风二十四气，相为嘘吸。海内名公卿，以及文章之士，皆与之游，其名直达禁掖。擘阮传人，乃以介人为导师，亦可异也。余尝作一想，取尼父《猗兰操》、桓子野《挽歌》、孔明《梁父吟》、谢安《洛生泳》、嵇康《广陵散》、袁山松《行路难》、李太白《乌夜啼》，令相如鼓琴，桓伊吹玉{遂}，高渐离击筑，祢衡挝渔阳鼓，君扬出而欹冠短袖，为之提掇其间，左顾右盼，意气激昂，拨清弦，发哀弄，人声天籁，云委雪飞，一洗梨园法曲之陋，顾不乐哉！

    Explanation

    734

    I have exhausted myself in the study of music theory, and I have thoroughly understood the principles of music that have been established over time, both ancient and modern. My friend Lu Junyang from our town is a master of the strings and plucking, and he helps me examine and correct the sounds. If there are errors in the notation, they are all corrected. Thus, the eight winds and twenty-four seasonal changes breathe together in harmony. Famous officials throughout the country, as well as scholars and literati, all associate with him, and his name reached the inner court. The person who plays the guqin is taught by Jièren as a teacher, which is also remarkable. I once had an idea: I would gather the "Ode to the Orchid" by Confucius, the "Dirge" by Huan Ziye, the "Liangfu Ode" by Zhuge Liang, the "Lo-song Ode" by Xie An, the "Guangling Scatter" by Ji Kang, the "Difficult Road" by Yuan Shansong, and the "Crow Cries at Night" by Li Bai. I would let Sima Xiangru play the qin, Huan Yi blow the flute, Gao Jianli play the zhú, Mi Heng play the Yuyang drum, and Lu Junyang come out with a tilted hat and short sleeves, leading and supporting in the middle. Looking left and right, with high spirits, plucking the clear strings, producing mournful tunes, human voices and the sounds of nature, like clouds and snow, washing away the crudeness of the imperial music. Isn't it delightful?

    735

    博塞之事，盛于魏晋，近日士大夫，皆以奉十斋打叶子为名流雅尚，相煽成风，浸淫海内。余不之效，只是黑白二子，比势覆局，“木野狐”之诮，恐亦在所不免。当余少贱，颇耽戏术，射覆藏钩，与夫“顷刻花”、“逡巡酒”之类种种幻化，皆所熟谙。至于召请乩仙，尤极灵响，即非真仙，当亦才鬼。己卯应试失利，情怀愺恅，舞仙童以释闷，令其搬演杂剧，穷姿尽态，有老梨园所不到者。

    Explanation

    735

    Games of chance and strategy were particularly popular during the Wei and Jin dynasties. In recent times, scholars and officials alike have embraced playing ten rounds of card games as a refined and fashionable pastime, spreading this trend across the land. I do not follow this custom; instead, I play with just black and white pieces, comparing strategies and flipping the board. The criticism of being called a "wooden fox," however, is likely unavoidable. When I was young and of humble status, I was quite fond of various games and tricks, including guessing hidden objects and concealed hooks, as well as the rapid changes of "instant flowers" and the waiting games of "wine that waits." I was well-versed in all these forms of entertainment. Summoning a spirit medium was especially effective, and even if they were not true spirits, they were likely talented ghosts. In the year of the rabbit, I failed the imperial examination and felt dispirited. To relieve my boredom, I danced with fairy children and had them perform various plays, using every posture and expression, surpassing even the skills of experienced actors.

    736

    一时传播，男妇聚观，拥塞堂庑，终日哄笑，匝月而不散，窗几悉遭挤毁。余深悔其贱，固逃匿于外以谢之。世俗无聊，动拈骰子以卜。乙亥试，玉峰，同寓友人，竞卜休咎。余一呼而六子皆赤，果于是年入泮。先君六旬时，遘疾弥月，医药不能疗。余心焚灼，抱骰盆跽于中庭，祝曰：“大人病果无患，幸赐吉征！”一掷而五子各色，独一子旋转不定。余默恳之，一跃而成顺色，病亦旋瘳。昔寄奴喝子成卢，明皇叱子成四，慈圣之侧立不仆，光献之盘旋三日，精诚所注，符应立呈，樗蒲有神，岂虚也哉！

    Explanation

    736

    At one time, the news spread, and both men and women gathered to watch, causing the halls and corridors to be jammed, with everyone laughing all day long, and the crowd did not disperse for a month. The windows and tables were all destroyed by the crowd. I deeply regret its cheapness and hid outside to avoid it. The common people, being bored, often pick up dice to predict their fortunes. During the year of Yihai, Yufeng, a friend who lives in the same lodging, competed to predict good or bad luck. I called out and all six dice turned red, and indeed, I entered the school that year. My father, at sixty, fell ill for a month, and medicine could not cure him. My heart was burning with anxiety, and I knelt in the courtyard holding a dice cup, praying, "If my father's illness is truly not serious, please grant me a good sign!" I threw the dice, and five of them showed different colors, while one kept spinning and not settling. I silently pleaded with it, and it suddenly turned to a favorable color, and my father's illness soon improved. In the past, Emperor Wu of Liu Song drank and the dice turned to a winning combination, Emperor Ming of Tang shouted and the dice turned to four, Empress Wu stood beside her and did not fall, and Empress Dowager Gaozu spun around for three days. With sincere devotion, the signs appear immediately, and dice have a divine nature, is this not true?

    737

    余与汉阳李云田偶过汴市，见有争钱而相搏者。云田曰：“古人名钱曰刀，以其铦利能杀人也；执两『戈』以求『金』，谓之『钱』，亦以示凶害也。”余曰：“执两『戈』以求『金』，谓之『钱』，执两『戈』以求『贝』，谓之『贱』。执『十戈』以求『贝』，则谓之『贼』而已矣！”云田曰：“两『戈』一『金』，当更有精义。子试说之！”余曰：“两『戈』不敌一『金』，钱真神物也！”云田曰：“得一『金』而来两『戈』，岂不可危？”余曰：“操两『戈』以求一『金』，亦复何畏？”有一老父笑而前曰：“此贪者之必济以酷也。敬领两公高论，老夫快极！惜王介甫不得一证斯言。”

    Explanation

    737

    I happened to pass through Bian market with Li Yuntian from Hanyang, and I saw people fighting over money. Li Yuntian said, "Ancients called money 'knife,' because it was sharp and could kill; holding two '戈' to seek '金,' called '钱,' also shows its danger." I replied, "Holding two '戈' to seek '金,' called '钱,' holding two '戈' to seek '贝,' called '贱.' Holding ten '戈' to seek '贝,' called '贼' already!" Li Yuntian said, "Two '戈' and one '金,' there must be deeper meaning. Try to explain it!" I said, "Two '戈' are not enough for one '金,' money is truly a divine object!" Li Yuntian said, "Getting one '金' and two '戈' coming, isn't it dangerous?" I said, "Holding two '戈' to seek one '金,' what's there to fear?" An old man laughed and approached, saying, "This shows that greed inevitably leads to cruelty. Respectfully accepting the high opinions of the two gentlemen, I am very pleased! Unfortunately, Wang Jiefu was unable to prove this statement."

    738

    乙巳，从三衢假道至汾水、开化道中，资斧告匱，伥伥乎靡所骋。适遇一蒙馆，其馆师教读“心广体胖”，“胖”音为伴。余入语之曰：“先生误矣。胖，蒲官切，当读如盘。”馆师日“门下精于翻切乎？愿受台教。”因教以上字母，下韵脚，中间过脉，如“经坚丁颠”诸诀，一一指授，呼调数四。令其师弟同余念诵，一堂之中，齐声唱和。初如小儿喤喤学语，舌本都强，少焉渐觉柔利。至数百遍，而趁口以出，自然通协。主人闻之狂喜，出揖余曰：“等宇切法，里俗罕传，村塾蠢儿，肉橐衣楦，何幸得公提诲！请问公姓氏，今将何往？何为停车于此？”余实告以前往江右，行李空乏之故。主人曰：“是不难。”命家僮立取青钱文绮见饷。余拜受之，得以即时就道。余于字学，童而习之，音义略无讹舛，不谓浪游乃受其益，以解字而得酒食，以切韵而得钱财，是亦学圃之美谈也。

    Explanation

    738

    On the day of Yi Si, traveling from Sanqu through the route to Fen River and Kaihua Road, I found myself running low on money and provisions, feeling lost and without direction. Just then, I came across a small private school, and the teacher was instructing the children in the phrase "broad-minded and contented," pronouncing "fat" as "ban." I approached him and said, "Sir, you are mistaken. 'Fat' should be pronounced as 'pan,' following the phonetic annotation 'Pu Guan Qie.'" The teacher replied, "Are you skilled in phonetic transcription? I would be honored to learn from you." I then taught him about the initial consonants, rhymes, and connecting vowels, using mnemonics like "Jing Jian Ding Dian" to explain the rules step by step. We practiced calling out the sounds several times. I instructed the teacher and his students to recite together, and in the classroom, they all sang in harmony. At first, it sounded like young children struggling to speak, with stiff tongues, but gradually, they became more fluent and natural. After hundreds of repetitions, they spoke fluently and naturally. The owner heard this and was overjoyed, coming out to bow to me and say, "The method of phonetic transcription is rarely passed down in our local customs. These village school children, like sacks of meat or shoe lasts, are fortunate to receive your guidance! May I ask your name and surname? Where are you going? Why have you stopped here?" I honestly told him that I was heading to Jiangxi, explaining that I had run out of money and provisions. The owner said, "That is not difficult." He ordered his servant to fetch some silk fabric as a gift. I bowed and accepted it, allowing me to continue my journey immediately. I had learned the art of phonetics since childhood, and my pronunciation and meaning were never incorrect. I never expected that my aimless wandering would bring such benefits, gaining food and drink through solving puzzles and money through phonetic transcription. This is also a beautiful story in the garden of learning.

    739

    二氏皆视世人蠢俗，故一以冲举歆之，一以轮回惧之。余明于死生之故，不溺其说。然其标旨清微，振辞高妙，有足豁懵人之阂塞者，故夫道家之六甲秘文、万毕神木，释氏之三车要义、四谛真言，罔不洞究。我若静地修玄，不在采芝咽液；高座说法，不在竖拂拈槌，将使上清羽客，鳖守丹炉；大善知识，都向篱门外瞌睡也。

    Explanation

    739

    Both Daoism and Buddhism view the common people as foolish and vulgar, hence one entices them with the promise of transcendence, while the other instills fear through the cycle of rebirth. I understand the nature of life and death and am not swayed by their doctrines. Yet, their teachings are clear and subtle, their words powerful and elegant, and they have the potential to enlighten the confused and remove their blockages. Therefore, I have thoroughly investigated the esoteric texts of Daoism, such as the Six Jia secret writings and the Wan Bi sacred trees, as well as the core teachings of Buddhism, including the Three Vehicles and the Four Noble Truths. If I were to practice Daoism, I would not engage in the superficial practices of alchemy and herb cultivation. If I were to teach Buddhism, I would not perform the ritualistic gestures of a Zen master, leading to a situation where Daoist immortals would guard the alchemical furnace idly and Buddhist teachers would be inactive outside the main activity area.

    740

    余不信星相家言。李虚中、唐举，世无其人。二家推余限度，按余部位，皆云至贵之格，公卿将相，早于年三、四十内得之。人多以此佞余，余初亦喜闻其佞。逮其后来，往往不验。今阅七十甲子矣，黄粱熟矣，痴梦不复作矣，虽欲信之，又乌得而信之？
    　　　　又不信师巫之术。吾疁多有女巫，召人先灵与人叙语。余幼随家人往，果于隔户隐隐有声，家人白日见鬼，哭而问讯。余恶之，从后闼密侦，见一人垂首瓮中作语，遂发其奸。余在河南，与李御史同谒嵩岳，见有所谓“马子”者，托神附体，俨坐堂檐；执绳棍者，森列左右。愚民朝山者，有不谒神座，竟拜“马子”酬愿而去。忽而恫喝逻索，众皆惊窜，财如阜积。余恶之，令御史皆缚之至，众“神”叩头，哀乞免死。

    Explanation

    740

    I do not believe in the words of astrologers. Li Xu Zhong and Tang Ju, though they are said to exist, are not to be found in the world. When these two experts calculated my fate and examined my features, they all claimed that I would have a most noble destiny, becoming a minister, general, or prime minister within thirty to forty years. Many people flattered me with this, and I was initially pleased to hear such compliments. But later, it often did not prove true. Now I have lived through seventy cycles of the sixty-year cycle, the millet porridge is cooked, and I no longer make foolish dreams. Even if I want to believe, how can I believe?

    I also do not believe in the practices of shamans and witches. In my hometown, there are many female shamans who summon the spirits of the deceased to converse with people. When I was young, I went with my family, and indeed, there were faint sounds coming from behind the door. Family members saw ghosts in broad daylight and cried, asking questions. I detested this and spied from behind the door, seeing one person leaning their head in a jar speaking, and thus exposed their deceit. When I was in Henan, with Li Yushi, I visited Mount Song and saw people called 'Ma Zi' who claimed to be possessed by spirits, sitting formally on the eaves of the hall. People holding ropes and sticks stood densely on both sides. Simple people who come to the mountain, some do not worship the deity but instead worship 'Ma Zi' and fulfill their wishes. Suddenly, they threatened and searched, and everyone was shocked and fled, wealth accumulating like a hill. I detested this and ordered the Yushi to bind them all and bring them. The 'spirits' knelt and begged for mercy.

    741

    声色移人，余性亦有殊焉者。喜泉声，喜丝竹声，喜小儿烺烺诵书声，喜夜半舟人欵乃声；恶群鸦声，恶驺人喝道声，恶贾客筹算声，恶妇人詈声，恶男人咿嚘声，恶盲妇弹词声，恶刮锅底声。喜残月色，喜晓天雪色，喜正午花色，喜女人淡妆真色，喜三白酒色；恶花柳败残色，恶热熟媚人色，恶贵人假面乔妆色。至余平日，有喜色，无愁苦色；有笑声，无嗟叹声。窃谓屈原之《九叹》、梁鸿之《九噫》、卢照邻之“四愁六恨”、贾谊之“长太息”、杨雄之《畔牢愁》、殷深源之“咄咄怪事”，皆其方寸偪仄，动与世怼。惜不与介人同时，为作旷荡无涯之语以广之。

    Explanation

    741

    Sound and color can change a person, and my nature is also particularly affected by certain things. I enjoy the sound of flowing water, the sound of musical instruments, the bright and clear sound of children reciting books, and the sound of a boatman rowing at midnight. I dislike the sound of crows in a flock, the loud commands of servants clearing a path, the sound of merchants counting and calculating, the abusive language of women, the weak and whining sound of men, the sound of a blind woman reciting stories, and the sound of scraping the bottom of a pot. I enjoy the color of a crescent moon, the color of snow at dawn, the color of flowers at noon, the natural and understated makeup of a woman, and the color of rice wine. I dislike the color of withered flowers and trees, the color of something that is overly warm and alluring, and the artificial and pretentious appearance of a wealthy person. In my daily life, I have a joyful demeanor and no appearance of sorrow or distress; I have a laugh and no sighs or laments. I believe that the works of Qu Yuan's "Nine Laments," Liang Hong's "Nine Sighs," Lu Zhaolín's "Four Sorrows and Six Grievances," Jia Yi's "Long Sighs," Yang Xiong's "Beyond the Prison of Sorrow," and Yin Shenshun's "Dudu Strange Matters" all reflect their constrained hearts and constant conflict with the world. It is a pity that I was not born in the same era as a broad-minded and free-spirited person who could write boundless and expansive words to broaden their perspective.

    742

    余不识金钱之数，不知方物之值，不闻营殖之方，不设会计之籍。傥然而来者，傥然而去。室中忽盈忽虚，若与阿家翁无与焉。年七岁时，族伯亡，应余承祧。有宗人出而争嗣。郡司马某当谳，得宗人赇，袒之。余起告曰：“争为人后者，利其产耳。儿不愿如俗情奉人宗祀。”遽辞以出。司马谓先君曰：“有是佳儿，宜不赖此！”其为志大财疏，自童龀已然矣。倾余行箧，从无十金之积。白镪青蚨，亦数来数往，但不恋清寒吾辈人。余曾坐皋比，收诸生修脯；亦曾心织笔耕，卖文字作生活；亦曾以文应采风之使，得受前茅上赏。不以事生产，不以食孱孱八口，床头阿堵，不知何故咄嗟而散？

    Explanation

    742

    I do not know the value of money, nor do I understand the worth of goods, nor have I heard of methods of cultivation or business, nor do I maintain accounting records. Money comes and goes unpredictably; the room suddenly becomes full and then empty, as if my father is not involved in these matters. When I was seven years old, my uncle died, and I was expected to inherit his position. A clan member contested the succession. The governor of the prefecture, upon hearing the case, accepted a bribe from the clan member and favored him. I stood up and said, "The dispute over inheritance is for the sake of the property. I do not wish to follow common custom in honoring the ancestors." I quickly declined and left. The governor told my father, "Having such a good son, one should not rely on this!" It is evident that I have great aspirations but little wealth, even from a young age. My travel bag is always empty, and I never have a savings of ten taels of silver. Silver and copper coins come and go, but I do not cling to the poverty of our kind. I have once sat on a seat and collected tuition from students; I have also written and sold my work for a living; I have also written articles in response to a government official collecting folk songs and received a high reward. I do not engage in production, nor do I provide for my eight dependents, yet the money in my room disappears in a moment.

    743

    余最僻古器，幸而购得，宝玩不已，倘或失去，经时怏怏，如忆故人。向在东都，所得当道之赆，悉置三代尊彝，真赝各半。橐负抵舍，家人意其貲重，启视之，确确然皆邙土中物也。余夸而家人笑，不久即星失。假使余囊金以归，要亦垂手尽，不能作临沮守钱翁。人言介人痴，不痴也。

    Explanation

    743

    I am most fond of ancient artifacts, fortunate to have obtained them, I treasure and enjoy them endlessly. If they were to be lost, I would be unhappy for a long time, as if missing an old friend. When I was in Eastern Capital, I received gifts from officials, and kept all the ritual vessels from the Three Dynasties, half genuine, half fake. I carried them back to my home, and my family thought they were very valuable. When I opened and looked at them, they were clearly all things from the Loess Plateau. I boasted about them, and my family laughed, and soon they were lost, like stars disappearing. If I had carried gold back, it would also have been spent quickly, and I could not become a money-keeper like the man from Línjǔ. People say I am foolish, but I am not.

    744

    向有三畏：畏盗，畏猘犬，畏笑面多机智人。不幸旋触党人怒，卒吹蜮沙，兴文字狱，执余而囚之。余日事著述，若不知有狴犴者。客谯余曰：“子才之不戢以至于斯，今犹是放宕其辞以自骋乎？”余曰：“马迁腐刑，居蚕室而著《史记》；陆平原临刑曰：『古人立言以垂不朽，吾所恨者，予书未成耳！』蔡中郎被收，请黥首刖足，继成汉史。此三贤者，介人之师也。子乌足以知之？”或又引善恶报应之说曰：“子有何恶而遘此刑狱？”余曰：“盗跖为暴，肝人之肉而食之，卒得上寿。柳下惠操行修洁，以黜辱没其年。崇侯虎进炮烙以痡百姓，国灭不与其难。西伯修德行仁，囚于羑里。司马魋欲杀圣人，终柄宋国。仲尼贤过尧舜，拘于匡、围于蒲、微服于宋。信如报应之语，则是盗跖、崇侯、司马之善报为不爽，而柳下、西伯、仲尼之恶报为断如也！有是理乎？”

    Explanation

    744

    Previously, I had three fears: fear of thieves, fear of fierce dogs, and fear of people with smiling faces who are highly intelligent or cunning. Unfortunately, I soon encountered the anger of political opponents, and eventually, the poisonous sand was blown, leading to the establishment of a literary inquisition. They arrested me and imprisoned me. Despite this, I continued to write diligently, as if unaware of the prison. A guest criticized me, saying, "Your unchecked talent has led to this, and now you still indulge in your writing to satisfy yourself?" I replied, "Sima Qian was castrated and wrote the Records of the Grand Historian while in prison; Lu Yun said before his execution, 'Ancient people established their words to last forever, and my regret is that my writings are not yet completed!' Cai Yong was arrested and requested to be tattooed on the head and have his feet amputated so he could continue writing the history of the Han dynasty. These three virtuous figures are models for others. How could you possibly understand this?" Someone else then brought up the concept of retribution, asking, "What evil have you committed to suffer such punishment?" I said, "Diao Zhi lived a violent life, eating human flesh, and eventually lived to an old age. Liu Xiaohui, though virtuous, was disgraced and died prematurely. Chong Hou proposed cruel punishments and escaped punishment himself. King Wen of Zhou, though imprisoned in Youli, later became a legendary ruler. Sima Tui attempted to kill Confucius but eventually took control of the state of Song. Confucius, though more virtuous than Yao and Shun, was imprisoned in Kuang, surrounded in Pu, and disguised in Song. If we believe in the words of retribution, then the good fortune of Diao Zhi, Chong Hou, and Sima Tui was not mistaken, but the misfortune of Liu Xiaohui, King Wen, and Confucius was clear! Is there such a thing as this?"

    745

    知己之恩，侔于生我。古人云“士为知己者用”，又云“士屈于不知己，而伸于知己”，又云“感恩则有之，知己则未也”，又云“天下有一人知己，可以不恨”。甚矣知己之难也！而余之生也，凡得知己者十。发未燥，应童子试，甬东谢象三先生目之曰：“渥洼之神驹也，困以盐车，恐未得千里腾逸。”此一知己。楚黄曹石霞先生令疁，月两课士，余辄冠一军。迨解官，放浪西子湖与白门诸山水间，连手吟唱，狂叫绝倒。此一知己。光州唐雪灵先生，选邑士廿人，时校艺于衙斋，文必面阅，必戒诸少隽者奉余为经师；辛卯之役，谓余必抡元。及报罢，仰天嚄唶，至于流涕。此一知己。湘潭沈旭轮先生李吴，三简首诸士，曰：“时文中古文，盲、腐二史，其鼻祖也。

    Explanation

    745

    The grace of a true friend is equal to the gift of life itself. The ancients said, "A man of talent serves those who understand him," and also said, "A man of talent is suppressed by those who do not understand him, but finds fulfillment through those who do." They also said, "Gratitude may be felt, but true understanding is rare," and "If one has even one true friend in the world, one can have no regrets." How difficult it is to find a true friend! In my life, I have known ten such friends. Before my hair had dried, I took the examination for young scholars, and Mr. Xie Xiangsan of Yongdong looked at me and said, "You are the divine steed of the Wòwā spring, but if you are harnessed to a salt cart, you may not achieve the freedom to run a thousand miles." This was my first true friend. Mr. Cao Shixia of Chu Huang, the magistrate of Liú, tested scholars twice a month, and I always ranked first. When he resigned from his post, we roamed freely through the West Lake and the White Gate mountains, singing and laughing together, unrestrained and joyful. This was my second true friend. Mr. Tang Xuering of Guangzhou selected twenty scholars from the county and reviewed their works in his study. He insisted that the younger and more talented scholars regard me as their master of the classics. In the year of Xinyou, he predicted that I would surely win first place in the examination. When I failed, I groaned to the heavens and wept bitterly. This was my third true friend. Mr. Shen Xulun of Xiangtan, known as Li Wu, selected the top scholars and said, "In the modern writings, the ancient prose, the blind and the corrupt histories, they are the progenitors of later styles."

    746

    终恐不利时官之目！”此一知已。之莱李琳枝先生，以省方试士，拔余罪隶之中，弁冕都人士，序予文曰：“介人之文，能令人悲，亦能令人怒；能令人喜，能令人下酒，能令人已疾。是介人以文生天下，而群伧乃欲报之以杀，忍乎哉？”此一知己。河阳薛行屋先生，人伦渊薮，坐余澹友轩，相与订千秋业。余断梗，又折角如意也，而先生折官位辈行以交，诧为“珠采玉英，希世之宝”，此一知己。七闽黄石斋先生，讲学湖上，弟子数千人，蚁升庑下。《易正》一书，荃蹄爻象，妙契图先，独以授余，曰：“沧桑而变，唯此子不刊其书。谯周之得文立藩卫门墙，吾何恨矣？”此一知己。吾乡之文，久没云雾中，潜壶许子，与余力刷之，并草松陵，分题汉上，他无可与语者。尝曰：“有志三代，同心二人。”此一知己。

    Explanation

    746

    The final translation of the source text is as follows:

    "Ultimately, I fear it may be harmful to the officials' eyes!" This is one person who understands me. Mr. Cai Li Linzhi, while inspecting scholars, selected me from among the ranks of criminals, and as a dignitary, he wrote about my writings: "Jie Ren's writings can make people sad, they can also make people angry; they can make people happy, they can make people drink wine, they can even cure illnesses. Jie Ren creates writings that bring joy to the world, yet the common people wish to kill him for them, how can one bear this?" This is one person who understands me. Mr. Xue Xingwu from Heyang, a deep wellspring of human relationships, sat with me in my tranquil friend's pavilion, and we discussed our legacies for a thousand years together. My broken reed, and my broken如意 (ruiyi) that was once a cherished possession, and Mr. Xue, despite our differences in rank and status, considers me a "pearl of great price, a treasure of the world," this is one person who understands me. Mr. Huang Shizhai from Qimen, lecturing by the lakeside, had thousands of disciples climbing the steps below. The book The Correcting of the Yi, with its profound and accurate interpretation of the hexagrams, was uniquely given to me, saying: "In times of change, only this person will not destroy their writings. Like Zao Zhou, who established a school to protect and promote learning, what more could I regret?" This is one person who understands me. The writings of my hometown have long been obscured by clouds and mist, but with the help of Xu Zi from Qianhu, we have worked together to revive them, writing together in Songling and dividing topics in Hanshang. There is no one else with whom I can converse. He once said, "We share the same aspiration for the Three Dynasties, and a mutual commitment between two people." This is one person who understands me.

    747

    上洋妓王翩仙，姿才无辈，颇不近贵人。得余文，必焚檀拜读，读已又拜；相对清谈，无一语堕人间粉泽者。此一知己。有授伪秩官人，偕邑中雕面少年，密谋倾余。事且露，主者曰：“斯人制作，胚胎大家，必将羽仪天下，必务杀之。”再击不中，叹曰：“才士固不可杀！”爱我之口，无可准的。若辈方欲割我以刃，而肯称为“大家”、呼为“才士”，此亦一知己。李献吉，前朝之文人也，葬于崆峒山，冢已崩阤，几出狸首。颖人无过而问焉者，余语禹州史太守：“张良洞旁黄石冢，聂政墓侧姊嫈坟，大抵荒唐，为土人耳食语。独明诗人李献吉墓，埋骨不过百年，没于丰草，碑识无存焉。为太守者，所当急为表治，以培风雅。”守即鸠工往茸，余亲为舆土而封，出故碑而重泐之曰：“明诗人李梦阳之墓”。

    Explanation

    747

    A Courtesan of Shanghai named Wang Pianxian, unparalleled in talent and beauty, did not favor the wealthy and powerful. Upon receiving my writings, she would burn incense and bow before reading them, and after reading, she would bow again. Our conversations were always refined and free from worldly trivialities. She was my first true friend. There were those who had been granted false ranks, accompanied by youths with tattooed faces, who secretly plotted to undermine me. The matter was about to be revealed, and the leader said, "This person's creations embody the essence of great scholars and will serve as a model for the world; we must kill him." They made two attempts, but both failed. They lamented, "A talented person cannot be killed!" Those who admired me were unsure of what to do. While they intended to harm me with a blade, they still called me a 'great scholar' and a 'talented person.' This was another form of friendship. Li Xianji, a famous poet from the previous dynasty, was buried in Mount Kongtong. His tomb had collapsed, almost exposing his remains. No one from Ying passed by and asked about it. I spoke to the governor of Yu Prefecture, saying, "The tomb of Yellow Stone near Zhang Liang's cave and the tomb of Nie Zheng's sister beside Nie Zheng's grave are mostly absurd, mere tall tales told by the locals. Only the tomb of the Ming dynasty poet Li Xianji, whose bones have been buried for no more than a hundred years, is now overgrown with grass, and the stele inscription is no longer there. As the governor, you should urgently restore it to preserve the literary tradition." The governor immediately gathered workers to repair it, and I personally carried earth and sealed it. I retrieved the old stele and inscribed it again, saying, "The tomb of the Ming dynasty poet Li Mengyang."

    748

    云间彭燕又，当代之文人也。以五十年老孝廉，授汝宁司李，才华震荡，不屑以肺石绳人。或议其有文才，无吏干。一日来谒李御史于汴署，余从屏后觇之，见其内衷红褶，心为窃骇。御史甚加礼遇，肃之坐，谈论甚洽，茶凡三点，燕又渐忘分位，以足加膝，哆口横议，旁若无人。御史微哂，无憎意，入而呼余曰：“子见夫狂司李乎？”余曰：“见之，才不检制，幸夫子怜而恕之。”御史曰：“我无责乎尔。天下岂皆爱才者？恐终以是祸。”未几，巡方使者会稿至，御史谓余曰：“彭司李挂弹章矣！款迹累累，罪且不测。”余切恳御史转旋，为文人留一生地。御史难之，曰：“直指驻节彰德，汴之去邺也远；疏发，追无及矣！”余为跽请，乃删其重大者数条，遣一干役，策飞骑诣直指所，追还原疏，更为改缮。燕又得从薄谴以归。余初不令燕又知也。

    Explanation

    748

    A Scholar of Cloudy Sky: The Tale of Peng Yanyou

    Peng Yanyou, a scholar of literature from Cloudy Sky, was a contemporary figure. At the age of fifty, he was appointed as the magistrate of Rulin, a position he held with great talent and dynamism, though he showed little regard for strict governance. Some criticized him for having literary talent but lacking administrative skills. One day, he came to pay respects to Inspector Li at the Bianzhou office. I observed him from behind a screen and was startled to see him wearing a red skirt underneath his official robes. Inspector Li treated him with great respect, inviting him to sit and engaging in pleasant conversation. They drank three cups of tea, and Peng Yanyou gradually forgot his position, placing his foot on the knee of the inspector, speaking loudly and arrogantly, as if no one else was present. Inspector Li smiled slightly, showing no resentment. He then called me into the room and asked, "Did you see the mad magistrate?" I replied, "Yes, he is talented but uncontrolled. Please show mercy and forgive him." Inspector Li said, "I have no responsibility in this matter. Not everyone in the world loves talent. I fear this will ultimately bring him trouble." Soon after, the provincial inspector arrived with his report. Inspector Li told me, "Peng Yanyou has filed a complaint! He has committed numerous offenses, and his crime is unpredictable." I urgently pleaded with Inspector Li to intervene and save the scholar's life. Inspector Li hesitated, saying, "The Inspector General is stationed in Zhangde, and Bianzhou is far from Ye. If the report is submitted, it will be too late to recall it!" I prostrated myself in request, and he then deleted several serious points from the report. He sent a messenger on a fast horse to the Inspector General's office, recalling the original report and rewriting it. Peng Yanyou was punished lightly and returned home. Initially, I did not let Peng Yanyou know about this.

    749

    余方童丱，尝梦一人，纤细娟好，自称“金銮否人”，以绿沉笔一矢授余曰：“乾德初，蒙公见借，今以奉还。”由是文思大进，放骋词涂，不可捉搦。患难后，于资善僧寮，曾昼梦作文，有朱衣人裂而掷之地。余启之曰：“岂以文受祸，不当更费隃糜耶？今后但为蹢涔杯水之文，不复为惊涛怒壑之文；但为软面滑口之文，不复为聱牙棘齿之文；但为依篱傍闼之文，不复为开疆凿嶂之文；但为女子镜奁娇昵之文，不复为丈夫棨戟森峨之文。如是可乎？”朱衣人色霁而去。及余提笔，匠心独诣，其为砰奇如故也。又梦朱衣人怒诃曰：“违吾意旨，由汝虎视文林，但无望龙门烧尾！”余乃绝意金闺，日与麵生者为友，上追风人，下逮三唐吟老，遥相鼓吹。

    Explanation

    749

    When I was just a child, I once dreamt of a man who was slender and beautiful, introducing himself as "a person from the Golden Phoenix No Place." He gave me a green-sunken pen and said, "At the beginning of the Qian De period, I borrowed this from you; now I return it." Since then, my literary thoughts have greatly advanced, and I have freely expressed myself in writing, beyond control. After suffering hardships, I had a daytime dream in the Temple of the Wise and Good where a man in red clothing tore and threw my writing to the ground. I asked him, "Is it because of writing that I suffer misfortune? Should I not waste more ink? From now on, I will write like stagnant water in a cup, not like raging waves and angry ravines; I will write like smooth and easy-to-say words, not like rough teeth and thorny jaws; I will write like writings that lean on walls and doors, not like writings that open new territories and carve through mountains; I will write like a woman's cherished mirror and makeup box, not like a man's imposing and majestic spear and halberd. Is this acceptable?" The man in red clothing calmed down and left. When I took up my pen, my creativity remained as original as ever. Then I dreamt again of the man in red clothing scolding me, "You disobey my intention; you may look down on the literary world like a tiger, but do not expect to burn your tail at the Dragon Gate!" I then abandoned my ambitions, daily associating with strangers, following the footsteps of the ancient poets, including the poets of the three Tang dynasties, echoing each other from afar.

    750

    余壮盛时，力为时文，若科目可旦暮掇焉者。甲午，同考官某，与余有神契，欲收之夹袋，密相招，授以关节。余惊复之曰：“科名为何物，可以闇汶获之？且余命多蹇剥，恐非桂籍中人，文之售不售，无所逃命。若使一日诡遇，是与命拗也，人祸天谴，均有之矣！”当事怪恨，便与余绝。老而力为古文。岁戊午，薛黄门卫公先生谋之要津，欲以“博学宏词”荐，余上札启谢曰：“价夙遭屯难，沉痼书城，雕虫琐事，不足名家，实乏史材，无容忝窃。宏博之称，非所据也。

    Explanation

    750

    In my youthful vigor, I strove to write popular literary works, believing that success in the imperial examinations could be easily attained. In the year of Jia Wu, a co-examiner with whom I shared a deep spiritual affinity wished to include me in his list of candidates and secretly invited me, offering me inside information. I was startled and replied, "What is the examination system, that it can be obtained so easily? Moreover, my fate has often been difficult and harsh, and I doubt I am destined to succeed in the imperial examinations. Whether my writings are successful or not, it cannot escape my fate. If I were to achieve unexpected success one day, it would go against my fate, and both human calamity and divine retribution would befall me!" The official was displeased and thus severed ties with me. In my old age, I turned to writing classical prose. In the year of Wu Wu, Mr. Xue Huangmen Wei Gong, a high-ranking official, sought to recommend me for the prestigious title of "Extensive Learning and Eloquent Speech." I submitted a formal letter of thanks, stating, "I have long suffered from misfortunes, deeply immersed in books, preoccupied with trivial matters, unworthy of being a renowned scholar, lacking the material for history, and unable to accept such honor. The title of extensive learning is not something I deserve."

    751

    且也山麋野性，不乐冠裳；岂其濛汜余年，顿忘丘首？孝然窜河渚，仲蔚没蓬蒿，匹夫有志，不可回也。”固辞而后已。刑部伴阮刘公，结三十年中州缟紵，近为侍从亲臣，出督芜关税，迎余栾江之署，饮酒赋诗。公于署前方池之上构一新亭，镌御赐“松风水月”字为之额，朝夕瞻对，题曰“敬亭”，志不忘君也。余为之颂，系之以诗。复命日，拟以余才缓颊左右，余恳止之曰：“草泽寒蜩，久甘噤伏，岂可以不祥名字，上干帝座？”公为默然，退语幕客曰：“此公老钝，命与才违。”余之古今文，洵非逢年之物，天下钜公，谬以富贵相貽，此世间诩为奇遇，蠖屈鼠拱感涕以受者，而余顾麾而去之，若将凂焉！然则介人七尺，其为不翥之末翎、早飘之败叶也，审矣！

    Explanation

    751

    Moreover, a mountain deer possesses a wild nature, disliking the trappings of office; how could one suddenly forget their homeland in the remaining years of life? Xiao Ran fled to the riverbank, Zhong Wei became lost in the wilderness, a common man has his aspirations, which cannot be swayed. Firmly declining, he persisted until it was over. The Ministry of Punishment official, Liu, accompanied by Yuan, had been associated with the literati of the central plains for thirty years, recently serving as a close attendant to the emperor and sent out to oversee the customs of Wuhu. He welcomed me to the office in Lujiang, where we drank wine and composed poetry. He constructed a new pavilion on the front pond of the office, inscribed with the emperor’s bestowal of the words “Pine Wind and Moonlight” as its name, gazing upon it morning and evening, naming it “Jingting” as a testament to his loyalty to the emperor. I composed a eulogy for it, attaching a poem. On the day of his return, he proposed that I use my talents to smooth matters for him, but I earnestly declined, saying, “A humble scholar, long content to remain silent, how could I dare to use an ill-omened name to approach the imperial seat?” He fell silent, then spoke to his attendants, “This man is old and dull, his fate and talent do not align.” My writings, ancient and modern, are indeed not suited for the times; great officials have mistakenly bestowed wealth and status upon me, which the world considers a rare opportunity, and those who humble themselves like a worm or a mouse, moved to tears by such favors, yet I turned away from it, as if it were something to be avoided! Thus, a man of seven feet, is he not like a feather that cannot fly, or a leaf that falls too early, truly so!

    752

    向集自少至老所为诗古文辞，删九而存一。客见之，问曰：“其中所称最快意之作，可得闻乎？”余曰：“流落散人，实多笔墨之乐，试为足下略言一二。李御史察荒两河时驻节归德，余入谒，御史手授《丙申诗刻》一册，凡百有余首。余回寓，命从者焠灯酾酒，依韵和之，漏五下而卒业。黎明投入宪府。御史立邀进署，大呼曰：『君以一夕敌我一年，才之相去，奚但百倍而巳！』遂留幕，内。可为大快者，此其一。
    　　　　“河阳妓小红儿，性豩，善饮，常倚其量以压人。一日，余取大觥容数升者奉之，红儿不辞，曰：『我善酒，尔善诗，尔成一诗，我尽一爵。今日试以诗酒一决楚汉。』余吟红饮，酣对数巡，红儿微有醺态。余乃一连叠詠，红不能支，跽而乞降。余纵之睡，自吟自饮，坐客各举杯称贺。可为大快者，此其二。

    Explanation

    752

    Xiang Ji, from his youth to old age, wrote poems and classical prose, deleting nine out of ten and keeping only one. A guest saw this and asked, "Among the works you have kept, which is the one you consider the most satisfying?" I replied, "A wandering and scattered person, indeed, finds much joy in writing. Let me briefly recount a few instances.

    When the Inspector Li was inspecting the famine in the two rivers and stayed in Guide, I went to pay my respects. The Inspector personally gave me a copy of the 'Bing Shen Shi Ke' (丙申诗刻), which contained over a hundred poems. Upon returning to my lodging, I ordered my attendant to light a lamp and pour wine, and I followed the rhyme to compose in response. By the fifth gong strike, the task was completed. At dawn, I presented the poems to the Inspector. The Inspector immediately invited me into his office and shouted loudly, 'You completed the task in one night as if it were a year, the difference in talent is not just a hundredfold.' He then invited me to join his staff. This is one of the great joys.

    In Heyang, there was a courtesan named Xiao Hong'er, who was generous and skilled at drinking, often relying on her capacity to outdo others. One day, I presented her with a large cup that could hold several liters. Xiao Hong'er did not refuse, saying, 'I am good at drinking, and you are good at writing. You write a poem, and I finish a cup. Today, let's settle this like a battle between Chu and Han.' We engaged in several rounds of drinking and composing. After a few rounds, Xiao Hong'er showed signs of intoxication. I then composed several poems in quick succession. Xiao Hong'er could no longer hold her ground and knelt to surrender. I let her sleep, and I continued to sing and drink alone. The guests raised their cups to congratulate. This is another of the great joys.

    753

    “缪侍读念斋先生过疁，有青楼何嫒以诗晋谒，备陈堕落苦状。侍讲心恻，呼其嬤尽偿所值，听其择人而字，无他染也。余作《种德记》以赠之。一夕，余病不能饮，而为酒纠，为之约法曰：『苟有犯，不能饮者，罚以酒；能饮者，罚以诗。』即以缪侍讲损金与何媛落籍为题。众闻以诗赠缪，皆应曰：『诺！』一客曰：『奈何能饮而不罚之酒？』余曰：『若以酒罚能饮者，则是赏也，非罚也。』余乃随罚随吟，令小童录之，计所为诗，竟得免酒三十二瓯。侍讲笑曰：『昔人讌集，诗不成者，罚依金谷酒数。未闻有不与饮而罚之诗者，有之，自介人始矣。』余私喜曰：『不意于风雅林中，而得逃酒法。』余素负酒人之名，每罚即俯首受之，无可解免，此番乃得以诗硬抵，公然强项不饮，众不敢哗。可为大快者，此其三。

    Explanation

    753

    During his visit to Liu, Scholar Niuzhai encountered a courtesan named He Aiyun from a brothel who presented him with a poem, detailing her unfortunate circumstances. Moved by her plight, Scholar Niuzhai called for her caretaker and ordered her to be fully compensated for her services, allowing her to choose a suitable husband without any further complications. I wrote a "Record of Virtuous Deeds" to present to her as a gift. One evening, I was unable to drink due to illness but was responsible for overseeing a drinking party. I established the following rules: "If anyone violates the rules, those who cannot drink will be punished by drinking; those who can drink will be punished by composing poetry." The topic for the poetry contest was the financial support provided by Scholar Niuzhai to help He Aiyun leave the brothel. When everyone heard that I would be punished by composing poetry instead of drinking, they all agreed, saying, "Yes!" One guest asked, "Why not punish those who can drink by making them drink?" I replied, "If those who can drink are punished by drinking, it would be a reward, not a punishment." I then composed poetry as I was being punished, and a young boy recorded it. In total, I composed thirty-two poems, thereby avoiding drinking thirty-two cups of wine. Scholar Niuzhai smiled and said, "In ancient gatherings, those who failed to compose poetry were punished by drinking according to the 'Jin Gu Valley Wine Count.' I have never heard of anyone being punished by composing poetry without drinking. If there is such a case, it must have started with Jie." I privately rejoiced, thinking, "I never expected to find a way to avoid drinking among the refined and cultured." I have always been known as someone who enjoys drinking, and whenever I was punished, I would bow my head and accept it, with no way to avoid it. This time, however, I was able to resist drinking by composing poetry, openly and stubbornly refusing to drink, and no one dared to complain. This is indeed a great joy, and it is the third such occasion.

    754

    “戊子入乡闱，号舍中啾然有声，其鸣甚哀。余信为场屋文鬼，大声诵余向日《秋啸》诗曰：『三年龌龊逢逻卒，七义光芒吓主翁』，其声遂灭。有顾香王者，邑之才士，以不得青其衿而死。余为立传，人阅之，喜其描情绘意，有若写生，无不颐解。己酉，客上箬僧伽舍，邻寓有二生，披而读之，忽相抱而哭，至于失声。余惊问之，彼亦负奇诧傺，而不得一遇者，其为此态也，盖重有所伤也。我之诗，可以妥鬼精灵；我之文，可以役人情性。可为大快者，此其四。

    Explanation

    754

    In the year Wu Zi (戊子, 1878), I entered the provincial examination. In the examination hall, I heard a faint, sad sound, which was very mournful. I believed it to be the ghost of a failed scholar in the examination hall. I loudly recited my poem "Autumn Roar" from the past, saying: "Three years of filth, encountering the patrol officer; seven virtues of brilliance, frightening the master." The sound then disappeared. There was a man named Gu Xiang Wang, a talented scholar from the county, who died before passing the exams. I wrote a biography for him. People read it and were pleased with how vividly it depicted emotions and intentions, as if it were a living painting. In the year Ji You (己酉, 1889), I was a guest at the Sangha Hall in Shangruo. Two scholars nearby opened and read the biography, and they suddenly embraced and cried, to the point of losing their voice. I was surprised and asked them why, and they were talented but frustrated and unappreciated, having never had a chance to succeed. Their emotional reaction was deeply moving. My poetry can soothe the spirits of ghosts and spirits; my prose can influence people's emotions and nature. This is the fourth great satisfaction I have experienced.

    755

    “周少司农栎园先生，被蜚语中以闽事，穷极栲讯，终无赇证。时臬司李官以谳决失轻，此次逮问，与司农同系刑部，死者数人，滞于狱者八载。世祖忽念无辜，有贷死意，廷议改流宁古，将为散戍征人。升遐之日，特谕放令还乡。辛丑，偕王过客司李束藁南归，道经雪苑，留宿宋公牧仲家。余适邂逅。宋出上赐先相国古画同观。司农一一赏鉴毕，列坐开宴。余曰：『姑缓之，请再观今画。』取余所著《火山客谯》阅之。诸公叫读不已，都忘杯箸，鼓掌而笑，巾帻尽欹。主人劝且饮。诸公曰：『得此奇文，愈读愈快，正如身入龙藏，争看宝贝，唯恐其尽，谁肯撤而去之？』竟阅达旦，不备宾礼。可为大快者，此其五。

    Explanation

    755

    Mr. Liuyuan, the younger official of agriculture of Zhou, was slandered with accusations related to Fujian affairs, subjected to exhaustive and harsh interrogation, and ultimately found without evidence of bribery. At the time, Inspector Li, who was involved in a lenient verdict, was arrested and interrogated along with the official of agriculture, and they were both imprisoned in the Ministry of Justice. Several people died, and the official of agriculture remained imprisoned for eight years. The Emperor suddenly remembered their innocence and had the intention to commute the death sentence. The court debated changing the sentence to exile in Ninggu, and they were to be sent as scattered conscripts. On the day of the emperor's death, a special decree was issued to release and allow them to return home. In the year Xin Chou, accompanied by Wang, the guest of Li Shugao, they returned south, passing through Xue Yuan, and stayed overnight at Song Gong Mu Zhong's house. I happened to meet them. Song took out an ancient painting granted by the emperor to his late chancellor for us to view together. The official of agriculture appraised each item one by one, and we sat down and started a banquet. I said, "Let's take it slowly, please read the modern painting again." He took my work 'Volcanic Guest' to read. The gentlemen kept shouting and reading aloud, completely forgot about the cups and chopsticks, clapped hands and laughed, and their headgear was all tilted. The host urged them to drink. The gentlemen said, "Found this wonderful text, the more we read, the faster we became, just like entering the dragon's treasure trove, competing to look at the treasures, afraid that it would end, who would want to leave?" They read until dawn, without observing proper etiquette. What is greatly satisfying, this is the fifth one.

    756

    “覃怀沈云门，嶔崎异人，与余订金石交。艰得子嗣，颇制于内，不容置妾媵。秘一人于外宅，产一男聪颖明俊，且八龄矣。托为里人儿，携至家。夫人见而惊异曰：『阿渠家生此九苞凤？』云门进启曰：『此即夫人子。』讯得其实，夫人大喜逾望，涓日为育麟之宴，亲朋制锦称庆，文皆属余捉刀。一为中书段玉美，一为给谏薛卫公，一为河北大将军鲍济宇，一为大总戎鲁璧山，一为怀庆太守彭悟山；一为张乾雅诸同学兄弟。一日之内，横笔挥霍，悉副其请，无一雷同门面语。可为大快者，此其六。

    Explanation

    756

    Tang Huai Shen Yunmen, an eccentric and extraordinary person, established a friendship with me that was as firm as metal and stone. It was difficult for him to obtain a descendant, being quite controlled by internal factors, and he could not allow secondary wives. He kept one person secret in an outer residence, who gave birth to a son who was intelligent, bright, and handsome, and was already eight years old. He entrusted the boy to be a child of the village and brought him home. The lady saw and was astonished, saying, "Ah Qiu's family gave birth to this nine-feathered phoenix?" Yunmen presented and said, "This is the lady's son." After inquiring and obtaining the truth, the lady was greatly pleased beyond expectation. She chose a day to hold a feast to nurture the precious child, and relatives and friends made brocade to celebrate. All the writing was written by me. One was the Cabinet Minister Duan Yumei, one was the Censor Xue Weigong, one was the Generalissimo of Hebei Bao Jiuyu, one was the Grand Commander Lu Bishan, one was the Governor of Huaiqing Peng Wushan; one was Zhang Qianya and his classmates and brothers. Within one day, writing freely and quickly, all fulfilled their requests, with no one repeating the same formal expressions. What can be greatly pleasing, this is the sixth.

    757

    “庚子修豫志，午日，贾大中丞邀饮开府，谈次论及诸葛孔明、王景略二人优劣，互有异同。适襄城余令献襄酒三百器，陈列阶前，诸同事并启分贶。中丞笑曰：『请诸公各草《葛王优劣论》一篇，佳者悉持去，不须分也。』诸同事闻言贾勇，各就席构思。余伸纸摇笔，不加点窜，俄顷而稿毕。中丞令余口诵，余音辞郎鬯铿戛，中丞为之击节叹赏，诸同事皆撤笔长嘘，自坏己作。余进揖谢赐。督军校四人，担酒于前，余拥之徐步而出。可为大快者，此其七。尝见馆孩村腐妄为诗文，多有口自吟诵，忭手点头、自鸣其得意者，若稍知痛痒，则不然矣。韩愈曰：『小称意则人小怪，大称意则人大怪。』刘蜕曰：『十为文不得十如意。』则求余所为最快意之作，当又绝少也。”

    Explanation

    757

    During the year Gengzi, when the history of Henan was being compiled, on the day of Wu, Chancellor Jia invited us to a banquet at his residence. During the conversation, we discussed the merits and demerits of Zhuge Liang and Wang Jing, finding both similarities and differences. Coincidentally, the magistrate of Xiangcheng presented three hundred jars of Xiangcheng wine, which were arranged in front of the steps. All the colleagues together offered to distribute the wine as gifts. The chancellor smiled and said, "Please, gentlemen, each write an essay titled 'The Comparison of the Merits and Demerits of Zhuge and Wang.' Those who write well can take all the wine without dividing it." Upon hearing this, the colleagues became enthusiastic and each took their seat to think and compose. I extended the paper and began writing, without any revisions, completing the draft in a short while. The chancellor ordered me to recite it, and my voice and style were smooth and resonant, causing the chancellor to clap and praise enthusiastically. The colleagues all put down their pens and sighed, destroying their own works. I bowed forward and thanked for the gift. Four military officers carried the wine ahead, and I escorted them out slowly. This is one of the great joys, the seventh. I have often seen vulgar literati writing poetry and prose carelessly, many of whom would recite their own works, nodding and clapping their hands, and boasting of their own satisfaction. If they had any sense of right and wrong, they would not do so. Han Yu said, "If one is slightly pleased, people will find it slightly strange; if one is greatly pleased, people will find it greatly strange." Liu Tuo said, "Ten times writing, not all ten times are satisfactory." Therefore, seeking my own most satisfactory works, there are probably very few.

    758

    有议余文多游戏者。余曰：“方朔之《客难》，假难以征辞；崔实之《答讥》，因讥以寓兴；崔骃之《达旨》，寄旨以纬思；韩愈之《释言》，凭言以摅志；扬雄之《解嘲》，托嘲以放意；班固之《宾戏》，随戏以逞怀也。”客曰：“子云拟经之徒，孟坚述史之士，奈何鼓其舌颖，以笔墨为游戏乎？”余曰：“昔孔子目冉父为犁牛，斥宰予为朽木，睹仲由之好勇，取暴虎以示规；闻言偃之弦歌，举割鸡以志喜。

    Explanation

    758

    There are those who criticize my writings for being too playful. I respond: "Sima Xiangru's Guest's Difficulty uses the difficulty to prove his point; Cui Shi's Reply to Criticism conveys encouragement through criticism; Cui Yin's Conveying Intent weaves thoughts to express his meaning; Han Yu's Explain Words uses words to express his aspirations; Yang Xiong's Explain Mockery employs mockery to express his intentions; and Ban Gu's Guest Play follows the play to express his feelings." The critic says, "Yang Xiong, who imitated the classics, and Ban Gu, who wrote history, how can they use their eloquence and treat writing as a game?" I reply: "In ancient times, Confucius called Ran Bo a plow ox, dismissed Zai Yu as rotten wood, observed Zilu's bravery, and used a wild tiger to illustrate a lesson; he heard Yan Hui's music and singing, and used the act of cutting a chicken to express joy."

    759

    游戏之语，虽圣人有所不废，而况为圣人之徒哉？”少辨方言，作《侬雅》四卷。蒙难时，作《火山客谯》十五卷，《广禅喜》一卷。会有感喟，作《鼠吓》五卷。豫游最久，作《中州杂俎》二十四卷。同人问讯，作《千里面目》六卷。老闲半舫，作《化化书》十二卷、《人林题目》八卷、《蟹春秋》一卷。《三侬赘人诗文全集》，未定卷数。今虽衰臷，踵门而乞文者，必应之，如偿夙逋，不以为疲。后有作者，得吾书而秘之中郎之帐，听之；如李汉序韩文以行，寿之百世，听之；即不然，如张伯松不喜《法言》，叱覆酱瓿，亦听之。

    Explanation

    759

    Playful words, even sages do not discard them, how much more so for followers of the sages? Slightly distinguishing dialects, he wrote Nongya in four volumes. During times of hardship, he wrote Huoshanke Qiao in fifteen volumes and Guang Chanxi in one volume. When moved by emotion, he wrote Shu Xia in five volumes. During his longest travels, he wrote Zhongzhou Zazu in twenty-four volumes. When greeting fellow scholars, he wrote Qianli Mianmu in six volumes. In his old age, idling on a half-boat, he wrote Huahua Shu in twelve volumes, Renlin Titing in eight volumes, and Xie Chunqiu in one volume. The complete collection of poems and writings of San Nong Zui Ren, with an undetermined number of volumes. Now, although he is old and weak, anyone who comes to ask for writing, he will surely respond, as if repaying an old debt, without feeling tired. Future writers, if they find my books and keep them in the accounts of Zhonglang, are welcome to do so. If they follow the example of Li Han, who wrote the preface for Han Yu's writings, and ensure they last for a hundred generations, that is also acceptable. If not, like Zhang Bosong, who disliked Fayan and ordered it to be thrown into a jar of sauce, they are also welcome to do so.

    760

    [张山来曰：文近万言，读之不厌其长，唯恐其尽，允称妙构。
    　　　　予素不识三侬，而令嗣柱东，曾通缟紵，因索种种奇书，尚未惠读，不知何日方慰予怀也！]
    　　板桥杂记　余怀澹心
    　　　　金陵为帝王建都之地，公侯戚畹，甲第连云，宗室王孙，翩翩裘马。以及乌衣子弟，湖海宾游，靡不挟弹吹箫，经过赵李。每开筵宴，则传呼乐籍，罗绮芬芳，行酒纠觞，留髡送客，酒阑棋罢，堕珥遗簪。真欲界之仙都，升平之乐国也。
    　　　　旧院人称“曲中”，前门对武定桥，后门在钞库街。妓家鳞次，比屋而居，屋宇精洁，花木萧疏，迥非尘境。到门则铜环半启，珠箔低垂。升阶则猧儿吠客，鹦哥唤茶；登堂则假母肃迎，分宾抗礼；进轩则丫鬟毕妆，捧艳而出；坐久则水陆备至，丝肉竞陈；定情则目挑心招，绸缪宛转。纨绔少年，绣肠才子，无不魂迷色阵，气尽雌风矣！

    Explanation

    760

    Zhang Shanlai said: The text is nearly ten thousand words long, and one reads it without tiring of its length, only fearing it will end. It truly deserves the title of a masterpiece.

    I have never known the Three Nongs, but your honorable son, Zudong, once exchanged white silk and brocade, and therefore sought various strange books. I have not yet had the privilege of reading them. I do not know when it will finally console my heart!

    Banqiao Miscellaneous Notes by Yu Hua Danxin

    Jinling is the land where emperors established their capital. Princes, marquises, and close relatives of the imperial family, whose mansions stretch up to the clouds. Royal descendants and young nobles, riding in fine furs and horses. As well as the young nobles of the Wu Yi clan and travelers from the lakes and seas, none are without carrying bows and playing flutes, passing through the homes of Zhao and Li. Every time a banquet is held, announcing the musicians, with silks and fragrances, serving wine and managing the cups, keeping the guests entertained until they leave. When the wine is finished and the games are done, earrings and hairpins are dropped. Truly the capital of desire, the land of peace and joy.

    The old courtyard is known as "the heart of the music district." The front gate faces Wuding Bridge, and the back gate is on Chaokou Street. The houses of courtesans are lined up, one after another, with sparse flowers and trees, far from the mundane world. When you arrive at the door, the copper ring is half open, and the pearl curtains are lowered. When you ascend the steps, the little dogs bark at the guests, and the parrots call for tea. When you enter the hall, the surrogate mother greets you solemnly, treating guests with equal courtesy. When you enter the pavilion, the maids are fully dressed, presenting their beauty. After sitting for a while, all kinds of delicacies are served, with music and food presented. When feelings are established, the eyes flirt and the heart is drawn, with tender and intricate interactions. Young nobles and talented scholars with embroidered hearts, none can resist the charm of the courtesans, losing themselves in the feminine allure.

    761

    妓家仆婢称之曰“娘”，外人呼之曰“小娘”，假母称之曰“娘儿”。有客称客曰“姐夫”，客称假母曰“外婆”。
    　　　　乐户统于教坊司，司有一官以主之，有衙署，有公座，有人役、刑杖、签牌之类。有冠有带，但见客则不敢拱揖耳。
    　　　　妓家分别门户，争妍献媚，斗胜夸奇。凌晨则卯饮淫淫，兰汤滟滟，衣香满室；停午乃兰花茉莉，沉水甲煎，馨闻数里；入夜而擫笛搊筝，梨园搬演，声彻九霄。李、卞为首，沙、顾次之，郑、顿、崔、马，又其次也。
    　　　　长板桥在院墻外数十步，旷远芊绵，水烟凝碧。迴光、鹫峰两寺夹之。中山东花园亘其前，秦淮朱雀桁绕其后，洵可娱目赏心，漱涤尘襟。每当夜凉人定，风清月朗，名士倾城，簪花约鬓，携手闲行，凭栏徙倚。忽遇彼姝，笑言宴宴，此吹洞箫，彼度妙曲，万籁皆寂，游鱼出听，洵太平盛事也。

    Explanation

    761

    In the courtesan's household, servants and maids addressed her as "娘" (mother), while outsiders called her "小娘" (little mother), and her adoptive mother referred to her as "娘儿" (little mother). When a guest arrived, the courtesan addressed him as "姐夫" (sister’s husband), and the guest called her adoptive mother "外婆" (grandmother).

    The courtesan's household was under the jurisdiction of the Jiaofangsi (Music and Entertainment Bureau), which was headed by an official, had an administrative office, a seat of authority, and subordinates, as well as tools of authority such as whips and official documents. The courtesan's adoptive mother wore formal attire, including hats and sashes, but when she greeted guests, she did not bow or perform formal gestures.

    The courtesans in the household were divided into different levels, each competing to be the most beautiful and charming, and to outdo each other in displaying their talents and uniqueness. In the early morning, they engaged in rituals involving abundant drink and fragrant bathwater, filling the room with the scent of perfume. By midday, the air was filled with the fragrance of orchids, jasmine, and incense, noticeable for miles.
