In the evening, they played flutes and zithers, and performed in the imperial music academy, their music so beautiful that it seemed to reach the heavens. The Li and Bian families were the most prominent, followed by the Sha and Gu families, and then the Zheng, Dun, Cui, and Ma families.

    The Long Board Bridge was located several steps outside the courtyard wall, surrounded by vast and lush scenery, with mist over the water appearing blue or green. The Huiguang and Jiufeng temples were situated on either side of the bridge. The Zhongshan East Garden stretched out in front, and the Qinhuai River and Zhuque Bridge encircled the area from behind. This scene was truly pleasing to the eye and heart, cleansing one of worldly worries. Whenever the night grew cool and people settled down, with clear wind and bright moon, famous scholars and literati gathered in the city, adorned with flowers and arranging their hair, strolling hand in hand and leaning on the railing. Suddenly, they would encounter a beautiful woman, laughing and chatting pleasantly, while someone played a flute and another sang a beautiful melody. The surroundings were completely silent, and even the fish emerged to listen. This was indeed a glorious event of peace and prosperity.

    762

    秦淮灯船之盛，天下所无。两岸河房，雕栏画槛，绮窗丝障，十里珠帘。客称“既醉”，主曰“未归”。游揖往来，指目曰：“某名姬在某河房”，以得魁首者为胜。薄暮须臾，灯船毕集，火龙蜿蜒，光耀天地，扬槌击鼓，蹋顿波心。自聚宝门水关至通济门水关，喧阗达旦。桃叶渡口，争渡者喧声不绝。余作《秦淮灯船曲》，中有云：“遥指钟山树色开，六朝芳草向琼台。一围灯火从天降，万片珊瑚驾海来。”又云：“梦里春红十丈长，隔帘偷袭海南香。西霞飞出铜龙馆，几队娥眉一样妆。”又云：“神弦仙管玻璃杯，火龙蜿蜒波崔嵬。云连金阙天门迥，星舞银城雪窖开。”皆实录也。嗟乎，可复见乎！
    　　　　教坊梨园，单传法部，乃威武南巡所遗也。然名妓仙娃，深以登场演剧为耻。若知音密席，推奖再三，强而后可，歌喉扇影，一座尽倾。主之者大增气色，缠头助采，遽加十倍。至顿老琵琶、妥娘词曲，则只应天上，难得人间矣！

    Explanation

    762

    The splendor of the Qinhuai River boat procession is unparalleled in the world. On both banks of the river, riverside houses feature carved railings and painted thresholds, intricate windows and silk screens, and a ten-mile stretch of pearl curtains. The guest claims to be drunk, while the host says they haven't returned home. As guests and hosts come and go, they point and say, "A famous courtesan is in a certain riverside house," and the one who wins the first prize is considered the victor. As dusk approached, all the lantern boats gathered, the fire dragon winding and shining, illuminating the heavens and earth, beating drums and paddling in the middle of the waves. From the water gate at Jubei Gate to the water gate at Tongji Gate, the noise continued until dawn. At Taoye Ferry, the noise of people scrambling to cross the river never ceased. I composed the "Qinhuai Lantern Boat Song," in which I wrote, "From afar, the colors of Zhongshan Mountain open up, the fragrant grass of the Six Dynasties leads to the jade pavilion. A circle of lights descends from the heavens, ten thousand pieces of coral ride the sea." I also wrote, "In a dream, the ten-zhang-long spring red, the fragrance of Hainan is secretly stolen through the curtain. The western sunset flies out of the copper dragon pavilion, several groups of graceful eyebrows wear the same makeup." And I wrote, "The divine strings and fairy pipes, glass cups, the fire dragon winds and the waves are towering. Clouds connect the golden palace and the distant heavenly gate, stars dance in the silver city and the snow-filled ice caves open." All of this is a true record. Alas, will we ever see it again?

    The Imperial Music Bureau and the Pear Garden, the exclusive department of music, is what was left behind by the emperor's southern inspection. However, famous courtesans and divine beauties deeply consider performing on stage as a disgrace. If they know that the audience is knowledgeable and the setting is intimate, and they are praised repeatedly, they will reluctantly agree. Their singing voices and fan shadows captivated the entire audience. The host's appearance greatly improved, and the tip was doubled tenfold. As for Dun Lao's pipa and Tu Nang's lyrics, they are only fit for heaven, not easily found in the human world.

    763

    裙屐少年，油头半臂，至日亭午，则提篮挈榼，高声唱卖逼汗草、茉莉花，娇婢掩帘，摊钱争买，捉腕捺胸，纷纭笑谑。顷之，乌云拥雪，竟体芳香矣。盖此花苞于日中，开于枕上，真媚夜之淫葩，殢人之妖草也。建兰则大雅不群，宜于纱橱文榭，与佛手木瓜同其静好。酒兵茗战之余，微闻香泽，所谓王者之香，湘君之佩，岂淫葩妖草所可比缀乎？
    　　　　南曲衣裳妆束，四方取以为式，大约以淡雅朴素为主，不以鲜华绮丽为工也。初破瓜者，谓之“梳栊”；已成人者，谓之“上头”。衣衫皆客为之措办，巧样新裁，出于假母，以其余物，自取用之。故假母虽年高，亦盛妆艳服，光彩动人。衫之短长，袖之大小，随时变易，见者谓是时世妆也。

    Explanation

    763

    Young men of the gentry, with their loose-topped and short-sleeved attire, would carry baskets and containers at noon on a sunny day, loudly shouting to sell herbs and jasmine flowers. A beautiful maid would close the curtains, and people would spend money to buy, engaging in playful and intimate interactions, laughing and joking in a lively manner. Soon, the dark clouds would surround the white snow, and the entire body would be filled with a fragrant aroma. This flower blooms in the sun and opens on the pillow, truly a seductive and captivating blossom that enchants the senses. The orchid, on the other hand, is elegant and unique, suitable for silk screens and literary pavilions, and shares the same peaceful and harmonious beauty as the Buddha's hand and papaya. After indulging in wine and tea, one might faintly smell the fragrance, which is said to be the scent of a king and the ornament of the Lord of Xiang, far beyond the reach of such seductive and captivating flowers.

    The costumes and makeup of southern opera are taken as a model by all directions, generally emphasizing simplicity and elegance, not focusing on bright and luxurious decorations. Young girls who have just started wearing their hair up are called "combing the braid," while older girls who have completed their coming-of-age rituals are called "tying the hair." Clothes are provided by guests, cleverly designed and newly made by the foster mother, and taken and used by oneself. Thus, even though the foster mother is elderly, she still wears elaborate and attractive clothing, dazzling and captivating. The length of the shirt and the size of the sleeves change with the times, and those who see it call it the fashion of the time.

    764

    曲中女郎，多亲生之女，故怜惜倍至。遇有佳客，任其留连，不计钱钞；其伧父大贾，拒绝勿与通，亦不顾也。从良落籍，属于祠部。亲母则所费不多，假母则勒高价，谚所谓“娘儿爱俏，鸨儿爱钞”者，盖为假母言之也。旧院与贡院遥对，仅隔一河，原为才子佳人而设。逢秋风桂子之年，四方应试者毕集，结驷连骑，选色征歌，转车子之喉，按阳阿之舞，院本之笙歌合奏，回舟之一水皆香。或邀旬日之欢，或订百年之约。蒲桃架下，戏掷金钱；芍药栏边，闲抛玉马。此平康之盛事，乃文战之外篇。迨夫士也色荒，女兮情倦，忽裘敝而金尽，亦遂欢寡而愁殷。虽设阱者之恒情，实冶游者所深戒也。青楼薄倖，彼何人哉！

    Explanation

    764

    Courtesans of the entertainment district are often the biological daughters of their own, hence they are treated with excessive care and affection. When a good guest comes, they allow them to stay as long as they wish without charging; but they reject and do not associate with coarse, wealthy merchants, nor do they care. When a courtesan leaves the profession and is registered as a free person, she is recorded under the Cíbù (祠部). The cost to the biological mother is not high, but the madam charges a high price. The proverb "The daughter loves beauty, the madam loves money" refers to the madam's greed. The entertainment district is across a river from the imperial examination hall, originally set up for talented scholars and beautiful women. During the year when the imperial examinations are held, all those who come to take the exams gather from all directions, with carriages and horses arriving in numbers, choosing beautiful women and hiring singers, with the courtesans' flexible and beautiful voices and graceful dances, and the ensemble performances of music and singing in the entertainment district, the water around the boats smells fragrant. Some invite a week of pleasure, others make a lifetime commitment. Under grapevines, they play with money, and near the peony fence, they idly throw "jade horses." This is a grand event in the entertainment district, the outside chapter of the literary battle. When scholars become obsessed with beauty, and women grow tired of their emotions, suddenly their clothes are worn out and their money is spent, and they also lose their pleasures and are filled with sorrow. Although it is the constant nature of those who set traps, it is something those who indulge in pleasure should deeply avoid. The courtesans of the entertainment district, who are seen as fickle or unfaithful, who are they?

    765

    曲中市肆，精洁殊常。香囊云舄、名酒佳茶、饧糖小菜、箫管琴瑟，并皆上品。外间人买者，不惜贵价；女郎赠遗，都无俗物。正李仙源《十六楼集句》诗中所云“市声春浩浩，树色晓苍苍。饮伴更相送，归轩锦绣香”者是也。
    　　　　虞山钱牧斋《金陵杂题绝句》中，有数首云：“淡粉轻烟佳丽名，开天营建记都城。而今也入烟花部，灯火樊楼似汴京。”“一夜红笺许定情，十年南部早知名。旧时小院湘帘下，犹记鹦哥唤客声。”旧院马二娘，字晁采“惜别留欢恨马蹄，勾阑月白夜乌啼。不知何与汪三事，趣我欢娱伴我归。”“别样风怀另酒肠，伴他薄倖耐他狂。天公要断烟花种，醉杀扬洲萧伯梁。”“顿老琵琶旧典型，檀槽生涩响零丁。南巡法曲谁人问？头白周郎掩泪听。

    Explanation

    765

    The markets within the district are exceptionally clean and neat, far surpassing the ordinary. Perfumed pouches, cloud shoes, famous wines, fine teas, sweets, and musical instruments like flutes and zithers are all of the highest quality. People from outside the area are willing to pay high prices for these goods, and gifts given by young women are free from anything vulgar or common. This is exactly what is described in Li Xianyuan's poem "Sixteen Towers Collection of Verses": "The market sounds are vibrant in spring, the tree colors are fresh in the morning. Drunkards bid each other farewell, and the returning carriages are fragrant with brocade." In Qian Muzhai's "Miscellaneous Verses on Jinling," there are several poems that describe the beauty and grandeur of the city: "The light and smoke of a beautiful name, the founding of the capital is recorded. Now it has entered the entertainment district, the lights and buildings resemble those of Bianjing." "One night, a red note pledged affection, ten years in the south made one famous. In the old courtyard under the Xiang curtain, one still remembers the sound of a parrot calling guests." "In the old courtyard, Ma Er's daughter, also known as Chao Cai, laments the parting and the lingering joy, hating the horse's hooves. The moon is white and the night birds cry in the theater. I do not know what happened with Wang San, but he made me enjoy myself and accompany me home." "A unique charm and a different heart for wine, accompanying a fickle lover and enduring his recklessness. Heaven intends to end the cycle of love and drinking, drowning the Yangzhou Xiao Boliang." "The old lute has lost its former glory, the strings sound dull and the sound is lonely. Who would ask about the southern tour's music? The white-haired Zhou Lang weeps while listening."

    766

    ”绍兴周禹锡，喜听顿老琵琶“旧曲新诗压教坊，缕衣垂白感湖湘。闲开闺集教孙女，身是前朝郑妥娘。”郑女英，小名妥娘，载《列朝诗选?闺集诗》中“新城王阮亭《秦淮杂诗》中有二首云：“旧院风流数顿杨，梨园往事泪沾裳。樽前白发谈天宝，零落人间脱十娘。”“旧事南朝剧可怜，至今风俗斗婵娟。秦淮丝肉中宵发，玉律抛作残笛钿。”以上皆伤今吊古、感慨流连之作，可佐南曲谈资者，录之以当哀丝急管。黄浩翁云：“解作江南断肠句，世间唯有贺方回。”倘遇旗亭歌者，不能不画壁也。以上纪雅游

    Explanation

    766

    Zhou Yusix of Shaoxing enjoyed listening to the old lute played by Dun. Old tunes and new poems overshadow the music hall; wearing fine clothes and with white hair, he felt moved by Hunan. Leisurely opening the collection of women's poetry to teach her granddaughter, he was the former Ving Tuo娘. Ving Ying, with the pet name Tuo娘, is recorded in the "Selected Poems of Women" section of the "Poems of the Successive Dynasties." In Wang Ruanting's "Miscellaneous Poems of Qinhuai" in Xincheng, there are two poems that say: "The old courtesans of the past were counted among the beautiful Duns; the memories of the Pear Garden left tears on her clothes. At the wine cup, white hair discussed the days of Tianbao; scattered among the people, Tuo Shiyang was no more." "The old stories of the Southern Dynasties are very pitiful; until today, customs compete in beauty. At midnight, the silk and meat of Qinhuai were served; the jade rules were thrown away as broken flutes and jade hairpins." All of the above are works that lament the present and mourn the past, full of deep emotional reflection and nostalgia. These can serve as material for discussions of Southern music, recorded to match the mournful strings and urgent pipes. Huang Haoweng said: "Understanding the heart-wrenching sentences of Jiangnan, in the world, only He Fanghui can do this." If encountered by a singer at the flag pavilion, they would not be able to avoid being painted on the wall. The above records elegant travels.

    767

    [八琼逸客曰：此记须用冷金笺，画乌丝栏，写《洛神赋》小楷，装以云鸾缥带，贮之蚊龙箧中，薰以沉水、迷迭，于风清日白、红豆花间开看之可也。]
    　　　　余生万历末年，其与四方宾客交游，及入范大司马莲花幕中为平安书记者，乃在崇祯庚辛以后。曲中名妓，如朱斗儿、徐翩翩、马湘兰者，皆不得而见之矣。则据余所见而编次之，或品藻其色艺，或仅记其姓名，亦足以征江左之风流，存六朝之金粉也。昔宋徽宗在五国城，犹为李师师立传，盖恐佳人之烟没不传，作此情痴狡狯耳。“风乍起，吹绉一池春水”，干卿何事？“彼美人兮，巧笑情兮，美目盼兮。”“中心藏之，何日忘之！”

    Explanation

    767

    The Elegant Guest of the Eight Precious Stones said: This record should be written on cold gold paper, with black grids drawn on it, in small, neat calligraphy of the Luo Shen Fu (Ode to the Goddess of the Luo River), bound with cloud and phoenix ribbons, stored in a box adorned with mosquito and dragon patterns, and perfumed with sandalwood and patchouli. It can be read on clear, sunny days among the red beans and flowers.

    I was born at the end of the Wanli era of the Ming dynasty. My interactions with guests from all over and my service in the military under General Fan, writing peace reports, took place after the Geng Xin years of the Chongzhen era. Famous courtesans from the entertainment quarters, such as Zhu Dou'er, Xu Pianpian, and Ma Xianglan, I never had the chance to meet. Therefore, I compiled what I had seen, either describing their beauty and talents in detail or simply recording their names. This is sufficient to attest to the elegance of Jiangnan and preserve the splendor of the Six Dynasties. In the past, Emperor Huizong of Song, while in the Five Countries City, still wrote a biography for Li Shishi, fearing that the memory of such a beautiful woman would be lost. He did so out of a mix of genuine affection and playful mischief. "The wind suddenly rises, ruffling a pool of spring water," what does this have to do with you? "Ah, that beautiful woman, her smile is charming, her eyes are bright." "I keep her in my heart, when will I ever forget her!"

    768

    尹春，字子春，姿态不甚丽，而举止风韵，绰似大家。性格温和，谈词爽雅，无抹脂鄣袖习气，专工戏剧排场，兼擅生旦。余遇之迟暮之年，延之至家，演《荆钗记》，扮王十朋，至《见娘》《祭江》二出，悲壮淋漓，声泪俱迸，一座尽倾，老梨园自叹弗及。余曰：“此许和子、永新歌也，谁为韦青将军者乎？”因赠之以诗曰：“红红记曲采春歌，我亦闻歌唤奈何。谁唱江南断肠句，青衫白发影婆娑。”春亦得诗而泣，后不知其所终。嗣有尹文者，色丰而姣，荡逸飞扬，顾盼自喜，颇超于流辈。太平张维则昵宠之，唯其所欲，甚欢。欲置为侧室，文未之许，属友人强之，文笑曰：“是不难，嫁彼三年，断送之矣。”卒归张。未几文死，张后十数年乃亡，仕至监司，负才华，任侠，轻财结客，磊落人也。

    Explanation

    768

    尹春，字子春，虽然姿色并不出众，但她的举止风韵却如同大家闺秀。她性格温和，言谈爽朗优雅，没有涂抹脂粉或遮掩袖子的习惯，专门擅长戏剧表演，同时擅长扮演生旦角色。我在晚年时遇到了她，邀请她到家中演出《荆钗记》，她扮演王十朋，在《见娘》和《祭江》两出戏中，表演得悲壮淋漓，声泪俱下，全场观众无不为之动容，即使是经验丰富的老演员也自叹不如。我对她说：“这就像许和子、永新的歌声一样，谁是能欣赏这种歌唱的韦青将军呢？”因此我赠给她一首诗：“红红记得春天的歌曲，我也听到歌声呼唤奈何。谁在唱江南的断肠诗句，青衫白发的身影在摇曳。”尹春也因诗而流泪，后来她的下落不明。随后有一位名叫尹文的人，她容貌丰满而美丽，举止洒脱飞扬，眼神中流露出自得其乐，远超她的同辈。太平的张维则非常宠爱她，满足她的一切愿望，两人十分愉快。张维则想让她成为自己的妾室，但尹文拒绝了。张维则让朋友劝说她，尹文笑着说：“这并不难，我嫁给他就三年，然后离开他。”最终她嫁给了张维则。不久之后尹文去世，张维则在她去世十多年后也去世了，他官至监司，才华横溢，行侠仗义，轻视财物，结交朋友，是一个正直的人。

    769

    李十娘，名湘真，字雪衣。在母腹中闻琴歌声，则勃勃欲动。生而娉婷娟好，肌肤玉雪，既含睇兮又宜笑，殆《闲情赋》所云“独旷世而秀群”者也。性嗜洁，能鼓琴清歌，略涉文墨，爱文人才士。所居曲房秘室，帷帐尊彝，楚楚有致。中构长轩，轩左种老梅一树，花时香雪霏拂几榻。轩右种梧桐二株，巨竹十数竿。晨夕洗桐拭竹，翠色可餐，入其室者，疑非尘境。余每有同人诗文之会，必至其家。每客用一精婢，侍砚席、磨隃麋、爇都梁、供茗果。暮则合乐酒宴，尽欢而散。然宾主秩然，不及于乱。于时流寇讧江北，名士渡江侨金陵者甚众，莫不艳羡李十娘也。十娘愈自闭匿，称善病，不妆饰，谢宾客。阿母怜惜之，顺适其意，婉语辞逊，概勿与通。惟二三知己，则欢情自接，嬉怡忘倦矣。后易名贞美，刻一印章，曰“李十贞美之印”。余戏之曰：“美则有之，贞则未也。

    Explanation

    769

    Li Shi Niang, whose given name was Xiangzhen and courtesy name Xueyi, was said to have stirred with life in her mother's womb upon hearing the sounds of the qin and singing. Born, she was graceful and beautiful, with skin as white as jade, her gaze both charming and inviting, much like the description in "Xianqing Fu" of being "unique in the world and outstanding among the crowd." She had a strong inclination towards cleanliness, could play the qin and sing softly, had a basic knowledge of literature and writing, and favored scholars and talented men. Her dwelling featured private rooms and chambers adorned with curtains and ritual vessels, arranged with elegance and taste. In the middle of her home, there was a long veranda where an old plum tree was planted on the left, its fragrant blossoms gently brushing against the tables and beds during the flowering season. On the right side, two wutong trees and several large bamboo poles were planted. Every morning and evening, the wutong and bamboo were washed and polished, their green hues so vibrant they seemed edible, making one doubt if they were in a mundane world. Whenever I held poetry and literature gatherings with fellow literati, I would always visit her home. For each guest, she would use a skilled maid to serve inkstone and brush, grind ink, light incense, and provide tea and fruits. At dusk, they would gather for music, wine, and feasts, enjoying themselves to the fullest before parting ways. However, the host and guests maintained a proper decorum, avoiding any disorder. At that time, there was much unrest in the north, and many famous scholars had crossed the river to settle in Jinling. None of them did not envy Li Shi Niang. Li Shi Niang became even more reclusive, claiming to be prone to illness, not adorning herself, and declining visitors. Her mother, out of pity and understanding, accommodated her wishes, using gentle words to decline all visitors. Only with a few close friends did she share her joy, forgetting fatigue in their delightful company. Later, she changed her name to Zhenmei and carved a seal, inscribed with "Li Shi Zhenmei's Seal." I teased her, saying, "There is beauty, but not necessarily virtue."

    770

    ”十娘泣曰：“君知儿者，何出此言？儿虽风尘贱质，然非好淫荡检者流，如夏姬、河间妇也。苛儿心之所好，虽相庄如宾，情与之洽也；非儿心之所好，恐勉同枕席，不与之合也。儿之不贞，命也如何？”言已，涕下沾襟。余敛容谢之曰：“吾失言，吾过矣！”十娘有兄女曰媚姐，十三才有余，白皙，发覆额，眉目如画。余心爱之，媚亦知余爱，娇啼婉转，作掌中舞。十娘曰：“吾当为汝媒。”岁壬午，入棘闱。媚日以金钱投琼，卜余中否。及榜发落第，余乃愤郁成疾，避栖霞山寺，经年不相闻矣。鼎革后，秦州刺史陈澹仙，寓丛桂园，拥一姬，曰姓李。余披帏见之，媚也。各黯然掩袂。问十娘，曰：“从良矣。”问其居，曰：“在秦淮水阁。”问其家，曰：“已废为菜圃。”问其“老梅与梧竹无恙乎？”曰：“已摧为薪矣。”问：“阿母尚存乎？”曰：“死矣。”因赠以诗曰：“流落江湖已十年，云鬟犹卜旧金钱。雪衣飞去仙哥老，休抱琵琶过别船。”

    Explanation

    770

    Shi Niang wept and said, "If you truly know me, why would you say such a thing? Though I am of lowly status in the world of courtesans, I am not one who enjoys promiscuity or lacks propriety, like Xia Ji and the women of Hejian. If it is someone I truly like, even if we are as formal as guests, our feelings will be harmonious; if it is not someone I truly like, I fear that even if we share a bed, our emotions will not be in harmony. My lack of fidelity is fate, what can I do?" After she finished speaking, tears streamed down her cheeks. I composed myself and apologized, saying, "I spoke wrongly, I was in error!"

    Shi Niang had a sister-in-law named Mei Jie, who was thirteen and a half years old, fair-skinned, with hair covering her forehead, and eyebrows and eyes as beautiful as paintings. I loved her, and Mei also knew that I loved her. She wept softly and gracefully, performing a dance in my palm. Shi Niang said, "I will act as your matchmaker."

    In the year Renwu, I entered the imperial examination. Mei daily cast money into the qin (a type of divination tool) to predict whether I would pass the exam. When the results were announced and I failed, I became so angry and depressed that I fell ill. I retreated to the Xiqia Mountain Temple and did not hear from him for a year.

    After the upheaval, Chen Danxian, the governor of Qin Prefecture, lived in the Conggui Garden and had a concubine named Li. When I lifted the curtain, it was Mei. We both sighed and covered our sleeves. I asked about Shi Niang, and he said, "She has become a good woman." I asked about her residence, and he said, "She lives in the Qinhuai Water Pavilion." I asked about her family, and he said, "Her family has been turned into a vegetable garden." I asked if the old plum tree and the wutong bamboo were still well, and he said, "They have been cut down for firewood." I asked if her mother was still alive, and he said, "She is dead." Therefore, I presented him with a poem saying:

    "Wandering in the rivers and lakes for ten years, her cloud-like hair still betrays her old divination.
    The snow-white dress flies away as the fairy brother grows old, do not carry the pipa to another boat."

    771

    葛嫩，字蕊芳。余与桐城孙克咸交最善，克咸名临，负文武才略，倚马千言立就，能开五石弓，善左右射，短小精悍，自号“飞将军”。欲投笔磨盾，封狼居胥，又别字曰武公。然好狭邪游，纵酒高歌，其天性也。先昵珠市妓王月，月为势家夺去，抑郁不自聊，与余闲坐李十娘家。十娘盛称葛嫩才艺无双，即往访之。阑入卧室，值嫩梳头，长发委地，双腕如藕，面色微黄，眉如远山，瞳人点漆。叫“请坐”，克咸曰：“此温柔乡也，吾老是乡矣！”是夕定情，一月不出，后竟纳之闲房。甲申之变，移家云间，间道入闽，授监中丞杨文骢军事。兵败被执，并缚嫩。主将欲犯之，嫩大骂，嚼舌碎，含血噀其面，将手刃之。克咸见嫩抗节死，乃大笑曰：“孙三今日登仙矣！”亦被杀。中丞父子三人同日殉难。

    Explanation

    771

    Ge Nen, known by the poetic name Ruifang. I had the closest friendship with Sun Kesong from Tongcheng. Sun Kesong, whose given name was Lin, was endowed with both literary and martial talents. He could write a thousand words while leaning on a horse, was skilled in drawing a powerful bow, and could shoot with both hands. He was short and robust, and he called himself the "Flying General." He aspired to abandon his scholarly pursuits for military service, to achieve the glory of erecting a monument at Mount Juxu, and he also gave himself the alias "Wu Gong." However, he was fond of indulging in licentious activities and drinking and singing wildly, which was his natural disposition. He had formerly been close to Wang Yue, a courtesan from the Pearl Market, but she was taken by a powerful family, leaving him depressed and unable to console himself. One day, we were casually sitting at Shi Niang's house. Shi Niang highly praised Ge Nen's unparalleled talent, and I immediately went to visit him. I unexpectedly entered his bedroom, where I found him combing his hair. His long hair cascaded to the ground, his wrists were as smooth as lotus roots, his complexion was slightly yellow, his eyebrows were like distant mountains, and his pupils were like lacquer. He called out, "Please sit," and Sun Kesong remarked, "This is the land of comfort, and I shall spend my old age here!" That night, we became lovers, and he did not leave for a month. Later, he finally took her into a secluded room. During the fall of the Ming dynasty in 1644, he moved his family to Yunjian (Shanghai) and secretly entered Fujian, where he was appointed by the Supervisor of the Central Censorate, Yang Wenchong, to military affairs. After a military defeat, he was captured along with Ge Nen, and they were bound together. The commander wanted to violate Ge Nen, but she cursed vehemently, bit off her tongue, spat blood at his face, and was about to kill him with her own hands. Sun Kesong saw Ge Nen die bravely and laughed loudly, saying, "Sun San has ascended to immortality today!" He was also killed. The three members of the Yang family died the same day.

    772

    李大娘，一名小大，字宛君。性豪侈，女子也而有须眉丈夫之气。所居台榭庭室，极其华丽，侍儿曳罗绮者十余人。置酒高会，则合弹琶琶筝瑟，或狎客沈元、张卯、张奎数辈，吹洞箫，唱时曲。酒半，打十番鼓。曜灵西匿，继以华灯；罗帏从风，不知喔喔鸡鸣、东方既白矣。大娘曰：“世有游闲公子、聪俊儿郎，至吾家者，未有不荡志迷魂、没溺不返者也。然吾亦自逞豪奢，岂效龊龊倚门市娼，与人较钱帛哉？”以此得“侠妓”声于莫愁、桃叶间。后归新安吴天行。天行钜富，赀产百万；体羸，素善病，后房丽姝甚众，疲于奔命。大娘郁郁不乐。曩所欢胥生者，赂仆婢通音耗。

    Explanation

    772

    Rida, also known as Xiaoda, with the courtesy name Wan Jun, was a woman of extravagant nature who possessed the qualities of a man despite being female. Her residence was adorned with grand pavilions and splendid rooms, and she had over ten maidservants wearing fine silk robes. During grand banquets, she would have music performances with various stringed instruments, and sometimes intimate guests like Shen Yuan, Zhang Mao, and Zhang Kui would play the duxiao and sing popular songs. Halfway through the drinking, they would perform the Ten-Pattern Drum. As the sun set in the west, splendid lights followed, and the silk curtains fluttered in the wind, unaware of the rooster's crow or the east turning white. Rida said, "In the world, idle young nobles and clever, handsome young men who come to my house are all captivated and entranced, drowning and never returning. But I also enjoy luxury myself; how could I emulate the petty courtesans who haggle over money?" Because of this, she gained a reputation as a "chivalrous courtesan" among famous courtesans like Mochou and Taoye. Later, she married Wu Tianxing from Xin'an. Wu Tianxing was extremely wealthy, with a fortune of a million, but he was weak and often ill. His harem was filled with many beautiful women, exhausting him with constant service. Rida was depressed and unhappy. Her former lover, Xusheng, bribed servants to communicate with her.

    773

    渐托疾，荐胥生能医，生得入见大娘。大娘以金珠银贝纳药笼中以出，与生订终身约。后天行死，卒归胥生。胥生本贫士，家徒四壁立，获吴氏资，渐殷富，与大娘饮酒食肉相娱乐，教女妓数人歌舞。生复以乐死。大娘老矣，流落阛阓，仍以教女娃歌舞为活。余犹及见之，徐娘虽老，尚有风情。话念旧游，潸焉出涕，真如华清宫女说开元、天宝遗事也！昔杜牧之于洛阳城东重睹张好好，感旧伤怀，题诗以赠，未云：“朋游今在否，落拓更能无。门馆恸哭后，水云秋景初。斜日挂衰柳，凉风出座隅。酒尽满襟泪，短歌聊一书。”正为今日而说。余即出素扇以贻之，大娘捧扇而泣，或据床以哦，哀动邻壁。

    Explanation

    773

    Gradually feigning illness, she recommended a scholar named Xū as a capable physician, who was then allowed to meet Dàniáng. Dàniáng placed gold, pearls, silver, and shells into his medicine box before presenting it to him, and they made a lifelong pact. Later, when Tān Xíng died, she ultimately returned to Xū. Xū, originally a poor scholar with nothing but four walls standing, gained wealth from the Gū family and gradually became prosperous. He lived with Dàniáng, drinking wine and eating meat, enjoying themselves, and teaching several female performers to dance and sing. Xū himself died from pleasure. As Dàniáng grew old, she wandered in the bustling marketplace, still earning a living by teaching young girls to dance and sing. I still had the chance to see her, and though she was an elderly woman, she still retained her charm. Recalling her past experiences, tears welled up in her eyes, much like the palace women of the Huaqing Palace recounting the stories of the Kaiyuan and Tianbao eras! In the past, Du Mu encountered Zhang Hǎohǎo again in the east of Luoyang, moved by nostalgia and sorrow, he wrote a poem to give her, which included the lines: "Where are my old friends now? Can they still be carefree and free-spirited? After weeping at the gates, the autumn scenery of water and clouds begins. The slanting sun hangs over withered willows, and a cool breeze blows from the corner of the room. When the wine is gone, tears fill my sleeves; let me write a short song to express my feelings." These lines perfectly describe today's situation. I then presented her with a white fan, and Dàniáng held the fan and wept, sometimes sitting on the bed and reciting poetry, her sorrow moving even those in the neighboring rooms.

    774

    顾媚，字眉生，又名眉，庄妍靓雅，风度超群，鬓发如云，桃花满面，弓弯纤小，腰肢轻亚。通文史，善画兰，追步马守真，而姿容胜之，时人推为南曲第一。家有眉楼，绮窗绣帘，牙笺玉轴，堆列几案；瑶琴锦瑟，陈设左右；香烟缭绕，檐马丁当。余常戏之曰：“此非眉楼，乃迷楼也。”人遂以迷楼称之。当是时，江南侈靡，文酒之宴，红妆与乌巾紫裘相间，座无眉娘不乐。而尤艳顾家厨食品，差拟郇公李太尉，以故设筵眉楼者无虚日。然艳之者虽多，妒之者亦不少。适浙东一伧父，与一词客争宠，合江右某孝廉互谋，使酒骂座，讼之仪司，诬以盗匿金犀酒器，意在逮辱眉娘也。余时义愤填膺，作檄讨罪，有云：“某某本非风流佳客，谬称浪子端王，以文鸳彩凤之区，排封豕长蛇之阵；用诱秦诓楚之计，作摧兰折玉之谋。种夙世之孽冤，煞一时之风景”云云。伧父之叔为南少司马，见檄，斥伧父东归，讼乃解。

    Explanation

    774

    Gu Mei, courtesy name Meisheng, and also known as Mei, was exceptionally beautiful and graceful, with a demeanor that surpassed the ordinary. Her hair was like clouds, and her face was as radiant as peach blossoms. Her figure was slender and delicate, with a waist that was light and supple. She was well-versed in literature and history, skilled in painting orchids, and followed in the footsteps of Ma Shouzhen, surpassing her in beauty. She was considered the best in the southern style of music or performance. Her home had a room named "Mielou" (Mey's Room), adorned with embroidered curtains, ivory paper, and jade scrolls, piled up on tables; jade zithers and brocade se were arranged on both sides; incense smoke lingered, and the sound of rain on the eaves could be heard. I often joked with her, saying, "This is not Mielou, but Milocou (Mey's Maze)." People began to call it Milocou (Mey's Maze). At that time, the southern region was known for its luxury and extravagance, where literary and wine gatherings were common, and the presence of Gu Mei was essential for enjoyment. Her cooking was particularly admired, compared to famous chefs. Gatherings at her home were frequent. While many admired her, there were also many who envied her. A man from Zhejiang, a poet, and a scholar from Jiangxi plotted to accuse Gu Mei of stealing gold and rhinoceros horn wine vessels, intending to disgrace her. I was filled with righteous indignation and wrote a proclamation to condemn their crimes, stating, "They are not charming and elegant guests, but rather rogue playboys, arranging a beautiful and harmonious scene, only to set up a powerful and dangerous force; using deceptive strategies to destroy the beauty and preciousness of everything. They sow long-standing grudges and curses, ruining the beauty and charm of the moment." The uncle of the man from Zhejiang, who was a Southern Vice Marshal, saw the proclamation and ordered the man to return east, and the lawsuit was resolved.

    775

    眉娘甚德余，于桐城方瞿庵堂中，愿登场演剧为余寿。从此摧幢息机，矢脱风尘矣。未几，归合肥龚尚书芝麓。尚书雄豪盖代，视金玉如泥沙粪土，得眉娘佐之，益轻财好客，怜才下士，名誉盛于往时。客有求尚书诗文及乞画兰者，缣笺动盈箧笥，画款所书“横波夫人”者也。岁丁酉，尚书挈夫人重过金陵，寓市隐园中林堂。值夫人生辰，张灯开宴，请召宾客数十百辈，命老梨园郭长春等演剧，酒客丁继之、张燕筑及二王郎，中翰王式之，水部王桓之串《王母瑶池宴》。夫人垂珠帘，召旧日同居南曲呼姊妹行者与燕，李大娘、十娘、王节娘皆在焉。时尚书门人楚严某，赴浙监司任，逗留居樽下，褰帘长跪，捧卮称“贱子上寿”，坐者皆离席伏，夫人欣然为罄三爵，尚书意甚得也。余与吴园次、邓孝威作长歌纪其事。嗣后还京师，以病死。殓时现老僧相。吊者车数百乘，备极哀荣，改姓徐氏，又称徐夫人。尚书有《白门柳传奇》行于世。

    Explanation

    775

    Meiniang showed great kindness to me, and at Fang Qu'an Temple in Tongcheng, she willingly performed a play to celebrate my birthday. From then on, she abandoned worldly pursuits and vowed to leave behind the mundane world. Not long after, she married Gong Shizhu, a high-ranking official from Hefei. Gong Shizhu was a powerful and influential figure who regarded wealth and precious things as worthless. With Meiniang's assistance, he became even more generous, hospitable, and appreciative of talent, earning greater fame than before. Many people sought his poetry and paintings, and the paintings were often signed with the title "Madame Hongbo." In the year Ding You, Gong Shizhu and his wife returned to Nanjing and stayed in a specific location within a garden. On the occasion of Meiniang's birthday, they lit lamps and held a grand banquet, inviting dozens of guests. They commanded the veteran actors Guo Changchun and others to perform a play, while guests such as Ding Jizhi, Zhang Yanzu, and the two Wangs, Wang Shizhi (a mid-level official) and Wang Huanzhi (a water department official), played roles in "The Banquet of Queen Mother's Jade Pool." Meiniang hung a pearl curtain and called upon her former companions from the southern opera troupe, including Li Dama, Shima, and Wang Jie, to join in the festivities. At the time, Gong Shizhu's student Yan, who was traveling to a position as an inspector in Zhejiang, lingered and knelt down, holding a cup and offering a toast to wish longevity. All the guests stood up and bowed, and Meiniang happily finished three cups of wine, which pleased Gong Shizhu greatly. I, along with Wu Yuanci and Deng Xiaowei, wrote a long poem to commemorate this event. Later, they returned to the capital, where Meiniang died of illness. During her funeral, she appeared to have the form of an old monk. Hundreds of carriages came to pay their respects, and she was given a grand funeral. Her surname was changed to Xu, and she was also known as Madame Xu. Gong Shizhu wrote a work titled "The Legend of the White Willow" that was published.

    776

    董白，字小宛，一字青莲。天姿巧慧，容貌娟妍，七八岁时阿母教以书翰，辄了了。少长，顾影自怜，针神曲圣，食谱茶经，莫不精晓。性爱闲静，遇幽林远涧，片石孤云，则恋恋不忍舍去。至男女杂坐，歌吹喧阗，心厌色沮，意弗屑也。慕吴门山水，徙居半塘，小筑河滨，竹篱茅舍。经其户者，则时闻咏诗声或鼓琴声，皆曰：“此中有人。”已而扁舟游西子湖，登黄山，礼白岳，仍归吴门。丧母抱病，赁居以棲。

    Explanation

    776

    董白, also known as Xiaowan and Qinglian, was naturally gifted and wise, with a beautiful appearance. At the age of seven or eight, her mother taught her writing, and she quickly grasped it. As she grew older, she often looked at her own reflection and felt a sense of self-pity, but she excelled in embroidery and calligraphy, and had a deep knowledge of culinary recipes and tea preparation. She loved tranquility and solitude, and would linger in quiet, secluded places like deep forests, distant streams, solitary stones, and lone clouds, reluctant to leave. She disliked noisy, crowded environments where men and women mingled, singing and music filled the air, which made her feel disheartened and indifferent. She admired the scenery of Wu Gate and moved to Bantang, where she built a small house by the river, surrounded by bamboo fences and thatched cottages. Passersby often heard the sounds of reciting poetry or playing the zither from her home. She traveled by boat to West Lake, climbed Mount Huang, and paid respects to White Peak, before returning to Wu Gate. After losing her mother, she was ill and rented a place to live.

    777

    随如皋冒辟疆，过惠山，历澄江、荆溪，抵京口，涉金山绝顶，观大江竞渡以归。后卒为辟疆侧室，事辟疆九年，年二十七，以劳瘵死。辟疆作《影梅庵忆语》二千四百言哭之，同人哀辞甚多，惟吴梅村宫尹十绝，可传小宛也。其四首云：“珍珠无价玉无瑕，小字贪看问妾家。寻到白堤呼出见，月明残雪映梅花。”又云：“念家山破定风波，郎按新词妾按歌。恨杀南朝阮司马，累侬夫婿病愁多。”又云：“乱梳云髻下妆楼，尽室苍黄过渡头。钿盒金钗浑抛却，高家兵马在扬州。”又云：“江城细雨碧桃村，寒食东风杜宇魂。欲吊薛涛怜梦断，墓门深更阻侯门。”

    Explanation

    777

    Following Ruguao, she accompanied Ma Piqiang, passing through Huishan, traversing Chengjiang and Jingxi, reaching Jingkou, climbing to the summit of Gold Mountain, watching the dragon boat races of the Yangtze River and returning. Later, she became Ma Piqiang's concubine, serving him for nine years. At the age of twenty-seven, she died of tuberculosis. Ma Piqiang wrote "Memories of the Shadow Plum Pavilion" with 2,400 words to mourn her, and many mournful poems were written by his friends. Only Wu Meichun's ten quatrains can be passed down for Xiao Wan. The four quatrains say: "Pearls are priceless, jade is flawless; she asked me about my family with greedy eyes. Found me at the White Causeway and called me out; the bright moon and the remaining snow reflect on the plum blossoms." Another quatrain says: "Thinking of my hometown being destroyed, I decided to settle down; the husband recited new verses, and I sang them. Hated the Southern Dynasty's Yuansima, caused my husband to fall ill with worry." Another quatrain says: "Rushed down the makeup tower with disheveled hair; the whole family hurriedly crossed the river. Threw away the jeweled box and golden hairpin; the army of the Gao family was in Yangzhou." Another quatrain says: "In the misty rain of the city, the village of green peach blossoms; on Cold Food Day, the spirit of the cuckoo in the east wind. Wanted to mourn Xue Tao but her dream was broken; the tomb gate is deep and even more obstructed by the noble gate."

    778

    卞赛，一曰赛赛，后为女道士，自称玉京道人。知书，工小楷，善画兰鼓琴，喜作风枝袅娜，一落笔，画十余纸。年十八，游吴门，居虎丘。湘帘棐几，地无纤尘。见客初不甚酬对，若遇佳宾，则谐谑间作，谈词如云，一座倾倒。寻归秦淮，遇乱，复游吴门。梅村学士作《听女道士卞玉京弹琴歌》赠之，中所云“昨夜村头吹筚篥，教坊也被传呼急。碧玉班中怕点留，乐营门外卢家泣。私更妆束出江边，恰遇丹阳下渚船。剪就黄絁贪入道，携来绿绮诉婵娟”者，正此时也。在吴作道人装，然亦间有所主。侍儿柔柔，承奉砚席如弟子，指挥如意，亦静好女子也。逾两年，渡浙江，归于东中一诸侯。不满意，进柔柔当夕，乞身下发。复归吴，依良医郑保御，筑别馆以居。长斋绣佛，持戒律甚严，刺舌血书《法华经》，以报保御。又十余年而卒，葬于惠山祇陀庵锦树林。

    Explanation

    778

    卞赛，又名赛赛，后来成为女道士，自称玉京道人。她通晓书籍，擅长小楷书法，精于绘画兰花、击鼓和弹琴，喜欢画风枝袅娜的画作，一落笔就能画出十几张。她十八岁时，游历吴门，居住在虎丘。她的居所挂着湘帘，摆着棐几，地面一尘不染。初见客人时，她不多言，但若遇到佳宾，则会开怀笑谈，言辞如云，让在座的人都为之倾倒。不久后，她回到秦淮，遇到乱世，再次游历吴门。梅村学士（高启）作《听女道士卞玉京弹琴歌》赠予她，诗中所云“昨夜村头吹筚篥，教坊也被传呼急。碧玉班中怕点留，乐营门外卢家泣。私更妆束出江边，恰遇丹阳下渚船。剪就黄絁贪入道，携来绿绮诉婵娟”正是描述此时的情景。她在吴地穿着道人服饰，但也偶尔有所主持。她的侍女柔柔，像弟子一样侍奉她的砚席，指挥如意，也是一个安静美好的女子。两年后，她渡过浙江，归于东中的一位诸侯。因不满意，她将柔柔送入道观，自己则请求出家。她再次回到吴地，依附于良医郑保御，修建别馆居住。她长期斋戒，绣佛像，持戒律非常严格，刺舌血书《法华经》，以此报答郑保御。又过了十多年，她去世，葬于惠山祇陀庵的锦树林。

    779

    玉京有妹曰敏，颀而白如玉肪，风情绰约，人见之，如立水晶屏也。亦善画兰鼓琴，对客为鼓一再行，即推琴敛手，面发赪。乞画兰，亦止写篠竹枝兰草二三朵，不似玉京之纵横枝叶、淋漓墨沈也，然一以多见长，一以少为贵，各极其妙，识者并珍之。携来吴门，一时争艳，户外履恒满。乃心厌市嚣，归申进士维久。维久宰相孙，性豪举，好宾客，诗文名海内，海内贤豪多与之游。得敏益自喜，为闺中良友。亡何，维久病且殁，家中替。后嫁一贵官颍川氏，三年病死。
    　　　　范珏，字双玉，廉静，寡所嗜好。一切衣饰歌管、艳靡纷华之物，皆屏弃之。惟阖户焚香瀹茗，相对药炉经卷而已。性喜画山水，摹仿史痴、顾宝幢，槎枒老树，远山绝磵，笔墨间有天然气韵，妇人中范华原也。

    Explanation

    779

    玉京有妹曰敏，颀而白如玉肪，风情绰约，人见之，如立水晶屏也。亦善画兰鼓琴，对客为鼓一再行，即推琴敛手，面发赪。乞画兰，亦止写篠竹枝兰草二三朵，不似玉京之纵横枝叶、淋漓墨沈也，然一以多见长，一以少为贵，各极其妙，识者并珍之。携来吴门，一时争艳，户外履恒满。乃心厌市嚣，归申进士维久。维久宰相孙，性豪举，好宾客，诗文名海内，海内贤豪多与之游。得敏益自喜，为闺中良友。亡何，维久病且殁，家中替。后嫁一贵官颍川氏，三年病死。
    　　　　范珏，字双玉，廉静，寡所嗜好。一切衣饰歌管、艳靡纷华之物，皆屏弃之。惟阖户焚香瀹茗，相对药炉经卷而已。性喜画山水，摹仿史痴、顾宝幢，槎枒老树，远山绝磵，笔墨间有天然气韵，妇人中范华原也。

    Translation:
    Yu Jing had a sister named Min, who was tall and fair, like jade fat, with a graceful and charming demeanor. People who saw her were as if they were standing before a crystal screen. She was also skilled in painting orchids and playing the zither. When she played the zither for guests, she would stop after a few measures, put down the instrument, and her face would turn red. When asked to paint orchids, she would only paint a few bamboo and orchid sprigs, unlike Yu Jing's more elaborate and wet paintings. However, one excelled in abundance, the other in scarcity, each with their own unique beauty, and those who understood appreciated both. When she came to Wu, everyone vied to see her, and her home was always filled with visitors. She grew tired of the city's noise and returned to her husband, Jinsheng Weijiu. Weijiu was the grandson of a chancellor, generous and enjoyed entertaining guests, famous for his poetry and writings throughout the land, with many talented and noble people visiting him. He was very happy to have Min as a good friend in the home. Soon after, Weijiu fell ill and died, and the household declined. She later married a high-ranking official from Yingchuan, and after three years, she died of illness.
    　　　　Fan Jue, courtesy name Shuangyu, was clean and quiet, with few interests. He avoided all luxurious and extravagant things, only closing the door, burning incense, brewing tea, and reading books and scriptures. He enjoyed painting landscapes, imitating the styles of Shi Chi and Gu Baozhuang, painting old trees with twisted branches and distant mountains with deep ravines, with a natural and elegant charm in his paintings, the female version of Fan Huayan.

    780

    顿文，字小文，琵琶顿老女孙也。性聪慧，略识字义，唐诗皆能上口。授以琵琶，布指濩索，然意弗屑，不肯竟学。学鼓琴，雅歌《三叠》，清泠泠然，神与之浃，故又字曰琴心云。琴心生于乱世，顿老赖以存活，不能早脱乐籍。赁屋青溪里，荜门圭窦，风月凄凉。屡为健儿伧父所厄，最后为李姓者挟持，牵连入狱；虽缘情得保，犹守以牛头阿旁也。客有王生者，挽余居间营救，偕往访之，风鬟雾鬓，憔悴可怜，犹援琴而鼓，弹“别凤离鸾”之曲，如猿吟鹃啼，不忍闻也。余说内乡许公，属其门生直指使者纵之，复还故居。吴郡王子其长主张燕筑家，与琴心比邻，两相慕悦。王子故轻侠，倾金钱，赈其贫悴。将携归置别室，突遘奇祸。收者至，见琴心，诧曰：“此真祸水也！”悯其非辜，驱之去，独捕王子。王子被戮，琴心逸，后终归匪人。嗟乎！佳人命薄，若琴心者，其尤哉！其尤哉！

    Explanation

    780

    Dun Wen, courtesy name Xiaowen, was the granddaughter of the old Pípá master. She was naturally intelligent, with a basic understanding of character meanings, and could recite Tang dynasty poems fluently. When taught the pipa, her fingers moved clumsily, and she felt it beneath her dignity, refusing to complete her studies. However, when she learned to play the guqin, she sang the refined songs of 'San Die' (Three Stanzas), with a clear and cool sound that deeply moved her spirit. Thus, she was also called Qinxin. Born in troubled times, she relied on her grandfather to survive and could not escape from the music register early. She rented a house in Qingxi, with a thatched door and a small window, where the wind and moonlight were bleak. She often suffered oppression from strongmen and coarse men, and was finally seized by a man surnamed Li, who implicated her in a case, leading to her imprisonment. Although she was spared due to her innocence, she was still guarded by a grim-faced demon. A guest named Wang, acting on my behalf, came to rescue her, and we went together to visit her. Her hair was disheveled like mist and clouds, looking thin and pitiful. Yet, she still played the guqin, playing the piece 'Farewell to Phoenix and Phoenix,' which sounded like the cries of apes and cuckoos, making it unbearable to listen to. I spoke to Governor Xu of Neixiang, instructing his student, the inspector, to release her, and she returned to her old home. Wang Ziqiang, the eldest son of Wu Jun, built his home next to Qinxin, and they mutually admired each other. Being a light-hearted and generous man, he spent money to help her out of poverty. He planned to take her home and set up a separate room for her, but suddenly a strange calamity occurred. When the authorities arrived and saw Qinxin, they were amazed and said, 'This is truly a source of disaster!' They pitied her innocence and drove her away, but only arrested Wang Ziqiang. Wang Ziqiang was killed, and Qinxin escaped, later ending up with a bad person. Alas! A beautiful woman's fate is often thin, like Qinxin, how particularly so!

    781

    沙才，美而艳，丰而逸，骨体皆媚，天生尤物也。善弈棋，吹箫度曲。长而修容，留仙裙，石华广袖，衣被灿然。后携其妹曰媺者，游吴郡，卜居半塘，一时名噪，人皆以“二赵”、“二乔”目之。惜也才以疮发，剜其半面；媺归吒利，郁郁死。

    Explanation

    781

    Shacha, beautiful and charming, plump and graceful, her bones and body all exude charm, a natural treasure indeed. Skilled in chess, playing the flute, and composing music. Tall and well-proportioned, wearing an immortal skirt and broad sleeves of stone flower, her clothes and robes are splendid and bright. Later, she traveled with her sister, named Mei, to Wu Prefecture, where they settled in Ban Tang, gaining fame for a time. People all called her the "Second Zhao" or "Second Qiao." Alas, Shacha's beauty was marred by a disease that left a scar on half her face; Mei returned to Chale and died of sorrow.

    782

    马娇，字婉容，姿首清丽，濯濯如春月柳，滟滟如出水芙蓉，真不愧“娇”之一字也。知音识曲，妙合宫商，老伎师推为独步。然终以误堕烟花为恨，思择人而事，不敢以身许人，卒归贵竹杨龙友。龙友名文骢，以诗画擅名，华亭董文敏亟赏之。先是闽中郭圣仆有二妾，一曰李陀那，一曰珠玉耶。圣仆殁，龙友得玉耶，并得其所蓄书画瓶砚几杖诸玩好古器，复拥婉容，终日摩挲笑语为乐。甲申之变，贵阳马士英册立福王，自为首辅，援引怀宁阮大铖构党煽权，挠乱天下。以至五月出奔，都城百姓焚烧两家居第。以龙友乡戚有连，亦被烈炬，顷刻灰烬。时龙友巡抚苏松，尽室以行，玉耶亦殉，婉容莫知所终。龙友父子殉难闽峤，母丐归金陵，依家仆以终天年。婉容有妹曰媺，亦著名。又有小马媺者，轻盈飘逸，自命风流。真州盐贾用千金购得，奉溧阳陈公子。公子昵之，未久，并奁具赠豫章陈伯玑，生一子一女，如王子敬之有桃根也。

    Explanation

    782

    Ma Jiao, courtesy name Wanrong, was renowned for her clear and beautiful appearance, her hair as fresh and vibrant as a willow branch in spring, and her face as pure and elegant as a lotus flower emerging from water. Truly, she lived up to the name "Jiao." She had a refined appreciation for music and a perfect harmony of musical tones, which the master of old arts considered unparalleled. However, she regretted having fallen into the world of courtesans and entertainers, and she sought to choose a suitable person to serve, but she did not dare to give herself to anyone. Eventually, she returned to Guiyang and married Yang Longyou, whose name was Wencong. Yang Longyou was famous for his poetry and painting, and he was highly praised by Dong Wenmin of Huating. Prior to this, Guo Shengfu of Fujian had two concubines, one named Li Tona and the other named Zhu Yuye. After Guo Shengfu's death, Yang Longyou obtained Zhu Yuye and all her collection of ancient paintings, calligraphy, vases, inkstones, desks, and other antiquities. He also took in Ma Jiao, and they spent their days together, enjoying the pleasure of caressing and chatting. In the year of Jia Shen (1644), the fall of the Ming Dynasty occurred, and Guizhou's Ma Shiying proclaimed the Prince of Fu as emperor, becoming the chief minister. He invoked Huaining's Ruan Dacheng to form factions and foment power struggles, causing chaos in the world. By May, the capital's residents burned down the homes of both families. Because Yang Longyou was related to them, his property was also destroyed by fire, reduced to ashes in an instant. At the time, Yang Longyou was the governor of Suzhou and Songjiang, and he led his entire family away. Zhu Yuye also perished, and the fate of Ma Jiao remains unknown. Yang Longyou and his son died in Fujian, and his mother begged to return to Nanjing, where she lived with a servant until the end of her life. Ma Jiao had a sister named Mei, who was also famous. There was also a young woman named Xiaoma Mei, who was graceful and ethereal, considering herself free-spirited and charming. A salt merchant from Zhenzhou bought her with a thousand gold pieces and presented her to Chen Gongzi of Lianyang. Chen Gongzi was closely attached to her, and after a short while, he gave her dowry and trinkets to Chen Boji of Yuzhang, and she bore him a son and a daughter, just as Wang Xizhi had his daughter, Tiaogeng.

    783

    顾喜，一名小喜，性情豪爽，体态丰华，趺不纤妍，人称为“顾大脚”，又谓之“肉屏风”。然其迈往不屑之韵，凌霄拔俗之姿，则非篱壁间物也。当之者似李陵提步卒三千人抵鞮汗山入陿谷，往往败北生降矣。汉武帝《悼李夫人赋》有云“佳侠含光”，余题四字颜其室。乱后不知从何人以去，或曰归一公侯子弟云。
    　　　　米小大，颇著美名，余未之见，然闻其纤妍俏洁，涉猎文艺，粉掐墨痕，纵横缥帙，是李易安之流也。归昭阳李太仆。太仆遇祸，家灭。

    Explanation

    783

    Gu Xǐ, also known as Xiao Xi, was a person of an open and generous personality, with a full and graceful physical appearance. His feet were not slender or delicate, and he was nicknamed "Gu Dajiao" (Con Greatfoot) and "Rou Pingfeng" (Meat Screen). However, his bold and unconcerned demeanor, along with his extraordinary and elevated bearing, set him apart from ordinary people. Those who faced him were often compared to Li Ling, who led 3,000 infantrymen to the Dihushan and entered the narrow valley, often defeated and forced to surrender. Emperor Wu of Han's "Lament for the Lady Ruo" includes the line "beautiful and noble, containing light," which I inscribed on his room. After the chaos, it is unknown who he went with, or perhaps he returned to a noble family.

    Mi Xiaodai was also quite famous, though I have not met him. However, I have heard that he was slender and delicate, with a graceful and refined appearance, and had a wide range of interests in literature and the arts. He was said to be a contemporary of Li Qingzhao. He returned to Zhao Yang and became the Taifu. The Taifu encountered misfortune, and his family was destroyed.

    784

    王小大，生而韶秀，为人圆滑便捷，善周旋。广筵长席，人劝一觞，皆膝席欢受。又工于酒，纠觥录事，无毫发谬误，能为酒客解纷释怨，时人谓之“和气汤”。扬州顾尔迈，字不盈，镇远侯介弟也，挟戚里之富，往来平康，悦小大，贮之河庭。时时召客大饮，效陈孟公、高季式，授女将军酒正印，左右指麾，客皆极饮沾醉。有醉而逸者，锁门脱履，卧地上，至日中乃醒。时吴桥范文贞公官南大司马，不盈为揖客，出入辕戟，有古任侠风，书画与郑超宗齐名。
    　　　　张元，清瘦轻佻，临风飘举。齿少长，在少年场中，纤腰踽步，亦自楚楚，人呼之为“张小脚”。
    　　　　刘元，齿亦不少，而佻达轻盈，目睛闪闪，注射四筵。曾有一过江名士与之同寝，元转面向里帷，不与之接。拍其肩曰：“汝不知我为名士耶？”元转面曰：“名士是何物？值几文钱耶？”相传以为笑。

    Explanation

    784

    Wang Xiaoda was born with a graceful and refined appearance, and he was smooth and agile in dealing with people, excelling at socializing. At grand banquets, when people offered him a cup, he happily accepted it while kneeling on the mat. He was also skilled in wine, managing the wine and recording the proceedings without any error, and was able to resolve disputes and grievances among wine guests. People of the time called him "Harmonious Soup." Gu Ermai from Yangzhou, whose courtesy name was Bu Ying, was the younger brother of the Duke of Zhenyuan. He possessed the wealth of the imperial clan and frequented the entertainment quarters, pleased with Xiaoda, he kept him in his garden. He often invited guests for grand drinking sessions, imitating Chen Mengong and Gao Jishi, giving the female general the wine official seal, directing left and right, and the guests all drank to excess and became drunk. Some who got drunk and escaped, locked the door, removed their shoes, and lay on the ground, waking up only at noon. At the time, Fan Wen Zhen Gong from Wuqiao served as a high-ranking military official. Bu Ying served as a host, entering and exiting the official's courtyard, displaying a style of ancient chivalry, and his calligraphy and painting were as famous as Zheng Chaozong's.

    Zhang Yuan was slender and flirtatious, fluttering in the wind. Young in age, among the young, with a slender waist and slow steps, he also looked elegant, and people called him "Zhang Small Foot."

    Liu Yuan was also not old, and he was flirtatious and light, with bright eyes focused on all the tables. Once a famous scholar from across the river shared a bed with him. Yuan turned his face to the inner curtain and did not interact with him. The scholar patted his shoulder and said, "Don't you know I am a famous scholar?" Yuan turned his face and said, "What is a famous scholar? How much is he worth?" It was passed on and became a source of laughter.

    785

    崔科，后起之秀，目未见前辈典型，然有一种天然韶令之致。科亦顾影自怜，矜其容色，高其声价，不屑一切。卒为一词林所窘辱。
    　　　　董年，秦淮绝色，与小宛姊妹行，艳冶之名，亦相颉颃。钟山张紫淀作悼小宛诗，中一首云：“美人在南国，余见两双成。春与年同艳，花推月主盟。蛾眉无后辈，蝶梦是前生。寂寂皆黄土，香风付管城。”
    　　　　李香，身躯短小，肤理玉色，慧俊宛转，调笑无双，人名之为“香扇坠”。余有诗赠之云：“生小倾城是李香，怀中婀娜袖中藏。何缘十二巫峰女，梦里偏来见楚王。”武塘魏子一为书于粉壁，贵阳杨龙友写崇兰诡石于左偏，时人称为三绝。由是香之名盛于南曲，四方才士，争一识面以为荣。

    Explanation

    785

    Cui Ke, a promising young talent, had not seen the typical examples of his predecessors, yet he possessed a natural grace and charm. Cui Ke also admired himself, took pride in his appearance, raised his reputation, and disdained everything else. Eventually, he was humiliated by the literary establishment.

    Dong Nian, a courtesan of exceptional beauty from the Qinhuai River, competed with Xiao Wan in terms of reputation for beauty and allure. Zhang Zidi from Zhongshan wrote a poem in memory of Xiao Wan, one of which said: "A beauty in the southern land, I have seen two become one. Spring and year share the same splendor, flowers and the moon vie for dominance. There are no successors among the graceful brows, the butterfly dream is of a past life. In silence, they all turn to dust, the fragrant wind is entrusted to the writing brush."

    Li Xiang, with a short and delicate body, fair skin, intelligence, and charm, was unmatched in her wit and humor. People called her "Xiang Shanzhui." I wrote a poem for her: "From a young age, she was the beauty of the city, Li Xiang. Her grace is hidden in her bosom and sleeves. How could she be the girl of the twelve peaks of Wu, appearing in dreams to meet the King of Chu?" Wei Ziyi from Wutang wrote it on the whitewashed wall, and Yang Longyou from Guiyang painted a strange stone and towering orchids on the left side. At the time, people called these three masterpieces. Thus, Li Xiang's reputation became widespread in the southern style of Chinese opera, and talented scholars from all over the country sought to meet her.

    786

    珠市在内桥旁，曲巷逶迤，屋宇湫隘。然其中有丽人，惜限于地，不敢与旧院颉颃。以余所见，王月诸姬，并著迷香、神鸡之胜，又何羡红红、举举之名乎？恐遂湮没无闻，使媚骨芳魂，与草木同腐，故附书于卷尾，以备金陵轶史云。
    　　　　王月，字微波，母胞生三女：长即月，次节，次满，并有殊色。月尤慧妍，善自修饰，颀身玉立，皓齿明眸，异常妖冶，名动公卿。桐城孙武公昵之，拥致栖霞山下雪洞中，经月不出。于牛女渡河之夕，大集诸姬于方密之侨居水阁，四方贤豪，车骑盈闾巷。梨园子弟，三班骈演，水阁外环列舟航如堵墙。品藻花案，设立层台以坐状元。二十余人中，考微波第一，登台奏乐，进金屈卮。南曲诸姬皆色沮，渐逸去。天明始罢酒。

    Explanation

    786

    The Pearl Market is situated beside the Inner Bridge, with winding alleys and humble, narrow houses. Yet within these modest surroundings, there are beautiful women, who, regrettably limited by their circumstances, dare not compete with the old courtesans. As I have seen, Princess Moon and her attendants are renowned for their fragrance and charm, so why envy the fame of Red and Lifted? Fearing they would be forgotten, letting their charm and beauty decay like grass and wood, I have appended this account to the end of the book, to serve as a record of the extraordinary history of Jinling.

    Princess Moon, styled as Ripple, was born to a mother who gave birth to three daughters: the eldest is Moon, followed by Jade and Full, all of whom are exceptionally beautiful. Moon is especially intelligent and beautiful, skilled at dressing herself, tall and graceful, with white teeth and bright eyes, unusually alluring, and her reputation moved the high officials. Master Sun of Tongcheng was fond of her, taking her to a snow cave under Mount Qixia and keeping her there for a month without leaving. On the night when the Cowherd and Weaver Girl cross the river, he gathered all the concubines at Fang Mi's floating pavilion. Scholars and heroes from all directions filled the streets with carriages, and actors from the Pear Garden performed in three rows. Boats surrounded the waterside pavilion like a wall. They set up a platform to seat the top-ranked beauty, and among more than twenty, Ripple was ranked first. She ascended the platform, played music, and was presented with a golden cup. The southern concubines were disheartened and gradually left. They finally stopped drinking at dawn.

    787

    次日，各赋诗纪其事。余诗所云“月中仙子花中王，第一嫦娥第一香”者是也。微波绣之于帨巾，不去手。武公益眷娈，欲置为侧室。会有贵阳蔡香君名如蘅，强有力，以三千金啖其父，夺以归。武公悒悒，遂娶葛嫩也。香君后为安庐兵备道，携月赴任，宠专房。崇祯十五年五月，大盗张献忠破庐州府，知府郑履祥死节，香君被擒。搜其家得月，留营中，宠压一寨。偶以事忤献忠，断其头，函置于盘，以享群贼。嗟乎！等死也，月不及嫩矣。悲夫！

    Explanation

    787

    The next day, each composed a poem to commemorate the event. My poem spoke of "a celestial beauty in the moon, the king of flowers, the first Chang'e, the first fragrance." Wei Bo embroidered it on her handkerchief and did not leave it from her hand. Wu Gong grew increasingly fond of her and wanted to set her as a concubine. There was a woman from Guiyang named Cai Xiangjun, whose courtesy name was Ruhe, who was powerful and influential. She offered three thousand gold pieces to bribe her father and took her away. Wu Gong was deeply troubled and so married Ge Nen. Later, Xiangjun became the military commissioner of Anlu, took Yue to her new post, and was favored exclusively. In the 15th year of the Chongzhen era, in May, the great bandit Zhang Xianzhong captured the prefecture of Luzhou, the prefect Zheng Lüxiang died a loyal death, and Xiangjun was captured. Her home was searched, and Yue was found. She was kept in camp and was favored above all others. Accidentally offending Zhang Xianzhong, she had her head cut off, placed in a box, and presented to the bandits. Alas! In death, it is all the same, but Yue did not match Ge Nen. How sad!

    788

    王节，有姿色。先归顾不盈，后归王恒之。甘淡泊，怡然自得，虽为姬侍，有荆钗裙布风。妹满，幼小好戏弄，窈窕轻盈，作娇娃之态。保国公买置后房，与寇白门不合，复归秦淮。
    　　　　寇湄，字白门。钱牧斋诗云：“寇家姊妹总芳菲，十八年来花信违。今日秦淮恐相值，防他红泪一沾衣。”则寇家多佳丽，白门其一也。白门娟娟静美，跌宕风流，能度曲，善画兰，粗知拈韵，能吟诗，然滑易不能竟学。十八九时，为保国公购之，贮以金屋，如李掌武之谢秋娘也。甲申三月，京师陷，保国公生降，家口没入官。白门以千金予保国赎身，匹马短衣，从一婢南归。

    Explanation

    788

    Wang Jie was a woman of beauty. She was first married to Gu Bu Ying and later to Wang Hengzhi. She found contentment in a simple life, being self-satisfied and at ease, even though she was a concubine, embodying the grace and humility of a woman with a simple life. Her younger sister, named Man, was young and playful, graceful and slender, displaying the charming demeanor of a young girl. The Duke of Guo purchased her and kept her in his harem, but she did not get along with Kou Baomen, and she returned to the Qinhuai River.

    Kou Baomen, also known as Bai Men, was a woman of grace and beauty, lively and charming, skilled in singing and performing music, adept at painting orchids, and had a basic understanding of composing poetry and reciting it. However, she was slick and easy to start but hard to finish in her learning. At the age of eighteen or nineteen, she was purchased by the Duke of Guo and kept in a luxurious environment, similar to how Li Zhanwu treated Xie Qiuniang. In March of the year 1644, Beijing fell to the Manchu forces, and the Duke of Guo surrendered, leading to his family being taken into official custody. Kou Baomen paid a thousand gold pieces to the Duke to redeem herself, returning to the south in a simple and humble appearance, accompanied by a single maid.

    789

    归为女侠，筑园亭，结宾客，日与文人骚客相往还，酒酣耳热，或歌或哭，亦自叹美人之迟暮，嗟红豆之飘零也。既从扬州某孝廉，不得志，复还金陵。老矣，犹日与诸少年伍。卧病时，召所欢韩生来，绸缪悲泣，欲留之同寝，韩生以他故辞，执手不忍别。至夜，闻韩生在婢房笑语，奋身起唤婢，自箠数十，咄咄骂韩生负心禽兽，行欲啮其肉。病甚剧，医药罔效，遂死。蒙叟《金陵杂题》有云：“丛残红粉念君恩，女侠谁知寇白门？黄土盖棺心未死，香丸一缕是芳魂。”以上纪丽品

    Explanation

    789

    She became a female hero, built garden pavilions, and gathered guests, daily interacting with literati and poets. When drunk and flushed, she sometimes sang and sometimes cried, lamenting the late blooming of a beautiful person and the scattering of red beans. Having followed a certain scholar from Yangzhou, she was unsuccessful and returned to Jinling. In her old age, she still mingled daily with young people. When ill, she called her beloved Han to come, and they wept close together, wanting to keep him in the same bed. Han declined due to other reasons, and they held hands, unwilling to part. At night, she heard Han laughing and talking in the maid's room, sprang up to call the maid, beat herself many times, repeatedly cursed Han as a heartless beast, and wanted to bite his flesh. The illness became severe, medical treatment was ineffective, and she died. Meng叟 in Jinling Miscellaneous Notes said:

    "The remnants of red powder remember your kindness, who knows the female hero like Kou Baomen. Though covered with earth, the heart is not yet dead, a thread of fragrant medicine is the soul's fragrance."

    790

    金陵都会之地，南曲靡丽之乡。纨茵浪子，潇洒词人，往来游戏，马如游龙，车相接也。其间风月楼台，尊罍丝管，以及娈童狎客，杂伎名优，献媚争妍，络绎奔赴。垂杨影外，片玉壶中，秋笛频吹，春莺乍啭；虽宋广平铁石为肠，不能不为梅花作赋也。一声《河满》，人何以堪？归见梨涡，谁能遣此！然而流连忘返，醉饱无时，卿卿虽爱卿卿，一误岂容再误。遂尔丧失平生之守，见斥礼法之士，岂非黑风之飘堕、碧海之迷津乎？余之缀葺斯编，虽曰传芳，实为垂戒。王右军云：“后之览者，亦将有感于斯文也。”

    Explanation

    790

    Nanjing, the capital city of the region, is a place renowned for its southern music and luxurious culture. Young nobles and carefree poets, coming and going in a lively manner, with horses like dragons and cars touching each other, create a bustling scene. Among the elegant buildings and music-filled gatherings, as well as young boys, courtesans, and talented performers, competing for attention and coming in a continuous stream, the atmosphere is vibrant and dynamic. Beneath the willow shadows, in a small jade-like space, autumn flutes are played frequently, and spring birds chirp; even if one has a heart of stone like Song Guangping, they cannot help but write a poem about the plum blossoms. A single note of "He Man" stirs deep emotions, and seeing the dimples on her cheeks, who can forget this! However, becoming too immersed in pleasure, losing oneself in endless indulgence, even though one loves oneself, a mistake cannot be repeated. Thus, one loses their lifelong principles and is criticized by those who uphold propriety, is like being blown off course by black winds or lost in the blue sea. I have compiled this collection, though it may be seen as spreading beauty, it is actually meant as a warning. As Wang Xizhi once said, "Those who read this in the future will also be moved by these words."

    791

    瓜洲萧伯梁，豪华任侠，倾财结客，好游狭斜。久住曲中，投辖轰饮，俾昼作夜，多拥名姬，簪花击鼓为乐。钱宗伯诗所云“天公要断烟花种，醉杀瓜洲萧伯梁”者是也。
    　　　　嘉兴姚壮若，用十二楼船于秦淮，招集四方应试知名之土百有余人，每船邀名妓四人侑酒，梨园一部，灯火笙歌，为一时之盛事。先是嘉兴沈雨若，费千金定花案，江南艳称之。

    Explanation

    791

    Xiao Bolang from Guazhou was known for his extravagant and adventurous lifestyle. He lavishly entertained guests and associates, frequently visiting brothels or places of vice. He lived in brothels for a long time, hosting lavish drinking parties until dawn, living a life of debauchery, possessing many famous concubines, and engaging in hedonistic pleasures such as wearing flowers and beating drums for entertainment. As Qian Zongbo's poem says, "Heaven is trying to eliminate the seed of debauchery, drunk to death, the Guazhou Xiao Bolang," referring to him.

    Yao Zhuangruo from Jiaxing used twelve large boats on the Qinhuai River to gather over a hundred well-known scholars from all directions who were taking exams. He invited four famous courtesans to each boat to accompany the drinking, along with a group of entertainers, creating a scene of lively entertainment, which was a grand event of the time. Previously, Shen Yuruo from Jiaxing spent a thousand gold pieces to arrange a lavish event, which was highly praised in the Jiangnan region.

    792

    曲中狎客，则有张卯官笛，张魁官箫，管五官管子，吴章甫弦索，钱仲文打十番鼓，丁继之、张燕筑、沈元甫、王公远、宋维章串戏，柳敬亭说书。或集于二李家，或集于眉楼，每集必费百金，此亦销金之窟也。张卯尤滑稽婉腻，善伺美人喜怒。一日偶触李大娘，大娘手碎其头上骔帽，掷之于地。卯徐徐拾起，笑而戴之以去。
    　　　　张魁，字修我，吴郡人，少美姿首，与徐公子有断袖之好。公子官南都府佐，魁来访之。阍者拒，口出亵语，且诟厉，公子闻而扑之，然卒留之署中，欢好无似。移家桃叶渡口，与旧院为邻。诸名妓家往来相熟，笼中鹦鹉见之，叫曰：“张魁官来！阿弥陀佛！”魁善吹箫度曲、打马投壶，往往胜其曹耦。每晨朝，即到楼馆，插瓶花，爇炉香，洗岕片，拂拭琴几，位置衣桁，不令主人知也。以此仆婢皆感之，猫狗亦不厌焉。后魁面生白点风，眉楼客戏榜于门曰：“革出花面蔑片一名张魁，不许复入。

    Explanation

    792

    In the brothel, there were male companions who played the flute, such as Zhang Mao, who played the flute; Zhang Kui, who played the flute; Guan Wuguan, who played the pipe; Wu Zhangfu, who played the stringed instruments; Qian Zhongwen, who played the ten-drum ensemble; Ding Jizhi, Zhang Yanzhu, Shen Yuanfu, Wang Gongyuan, and Song Weizhang, who performed in plays; and Liu Jingting, who told stories. They would gather either at the homes of the two Li families or at the Mei Pavilion, and each gathering would cost a hundred gold pieces. This place was also a den of extravagance. Zhang Mao was particularly witty and charming, skilled at sensing the moods of beautiful women. One day, he accidentally angered a courtesan named Li Dalang, who threw his hat on the ground, smashing it. Zhang Mao calmly picked it up, smiled, and put it back on before leaving.

    Zhang Kui, also known as Xiuwo, was from Wu County. He was handsome in his youth and had a close relationship with Xu Gongzi, a man who held a government position in the southern capital. When Zhang Kui visited Xu Gongzi, the gatekeeper refused him entry and spoke rudely, even insulting him. Xu Gongzi heard this and struck the gatekeeper, but eventually kept Zhang Kui in his office, and they became close friends. Later, Zhang Kui moved to Taoye Ferry, living near an old brothel. He became familiar with the courtesans, and even the parrot in the cage would call out, "Zhang Kui is coming! Amitabha!" Zhang Kui was skilled in playing the flute, composing music, and playing games like horse racing and archery, often outperforming his peers. Every morning, he would arrive at the pavilion, arrange flowers in vases, light incense, clean tea leaves, and tidy up the musical instruments and clothes, all without letting the owner know. Because of this, the servants and maids were all grateful to him, and even the cats and dogs did not dislike him. Later, Zhang Kui developed a skin condition called "white dot wind," and the guests at the Mei Pavilion jokingly posted a notice on the door: "Expel the flower-faced scoundrel named Zhang Kui; do not allow him to return."

    793

    ”魁惭恨，遍求奇方洒削，得芙蓉露，治之良已，整衣帽，复至眉楼，曰：“花面定何如？”乱后还吴，吴中新进少年，搔头弄姿，持箫擫管，以柔曼悦人者，见魁则揶揄之，肆为诋诃。以此重穷困。龚宗伯奉使粤东，怜而赈之，厚予之金，使往山中贩岕茶，得息颇厚，家稍稍丰矣。然魁性僻，尝自言曰：“我大贱相，茶非惠泉水不可沾唇，饭非四糙冬舂米不可入口，夜非孙春阳家通宵椽烛不可开眼。”钱财到手辄尽，坐此不名一钱，时人共非笑之，弗顾也。年过六十，以贩茶、卖芙蓉露为业。庚寅、辛丑之际，余游吴，寓周氏水阁，魁犹清晨来插瓶花、爇炉香、洗岕片、拂拭琴几、位置衣桁如曩时。酒酣烛跋，说青溪旧事，不觉流涕。丁酉再过金陵，歌台舞榭，化为瓦砾之场，犹于破板桥边，一吹洞箫。矮屋中一老姬启户出曰：“此张魁官箫声也！”为呜咽久之。又数年，卒以穷死。

    Explanation

    793

    Zhou Qiu felt ashamed and resentful, searching everywhere for unique methods to treat his condition, and eventually obtained the lotus dew, which effectively healed him. He then fixed his clothes and hat and returned to the Mei Building, saying, "How would a flower-like face be?" After the chaos, he returned to Wu, where the young men who had risen to prominence were self-conscious and showy, holding flutes and pipes, and using gentle and graceful manners to please others. When they saw Zhou Qiu, they mocked him and openly criticized and insulted him, thus making him even more destitute. Gong Zongbo, who was sent as an envoy to Guangdong, pitied him and provided relief, giving him a large sum of money. He was sent to the mountains to trade tea, earning a good profit, and his family gradually became more prosperous. However, Zhou Qiu had a peculiar personality and once said, "I have a very lowly appearance; tea cannot touch my lips unless it is from Hui Spring, rice cannot enter my mouth unless it is coarse winter rice, and I cannot open my eyes at night unless it is with the all-night candles from Sun Chunyang's house." Money disappeared as soon as it came into his hands, and as a result, he had no money at all. People commonly mocked him, but he did not care. By the age of sixty, he made a living by trading tea and selling lotus dew. During the years of Gengyin and Xinchou, I traveled to Wu and stayed at Zhou's Water Pavilion. Zhou Qiu still came in the morning to arrange flowers in vases, light incense, wash tea leaves, dust the zither table, and organize his clothes as before. When drunk and the candles were burning low, he talked about old stories from Qingxi, and tears involuntarily welled up. During the year Dingyou, I passed through Jinling again, where the music pavilions and dance platforms were turned into fields of rubble. Still, he blew a flute by the broken board bridge. A short woman opened the door and said, "This is the sound of Zhang Qiu's flute!" She wept for a long time. After several more years, he died in poverty.

    794

    岁丙子，金沙张公亮、吕霖生、盐官陈则梁、漳浦刘渔仲、雉皋冒辟疆盟于眉楼。则梁作盟文甚奇，末云：“姓盟不如臂盟，臂盟不如心盟。”
    　　　　中山公子徐青君，魏国介弟也。家赀钜万，性豪侈，自奉甚丰，广蓄姬妾。造园大功坊侧，树石亭台，拟于平泉、金谷。每当夏月，置宴河房，日选名妓四五人，邀宾侑酒。木瓜佛手，堆积如山；茉莉珠兰，芳香似雪。夜以继日，把酒酣歌，纶巾鹤氅，真神仙中人也。福王时加中府都督，前驱班剑，呵导入朝，愈荣显矣。乙酉鼎革，籍没田产，遂无立足；群姬雨散，一身孑然，与佣丐为伍，乃至为人代杖。其居第易为兵道衙门。一日，与当刑人约定杖数，计偿若干。

    Explanation

    794

    In the year of Ding Si (1646), Zhang Gongliang of Jinsha, Lü Linsheng, Chen Zeliang of Yanguan, Liu Yuzhong of Zhangpu, and Mao Bijiang of Zhigao swore an alliance at Mei Tower. Zeliang composed an oath text that was very peculiar, concluding with the words: "An oath by name is not as good as an oath by arm, and an oath by arm is not as good as an oath by heart."

    Zi Xuqingjun, the younger brother of the Prince of Zhongshan, was a man of great wealth, living a lavish and extravagant life. He treated himself very generously and maintained a large number of concubines. He built a garden beside the Daguofang, planting trees, stones, pavilions, and terraces, comparable to the famous gardens of Pingquan and Jingu. Every summer, he would hold banquets in his riverside pavilion, selecting four or five famous courtesans each day to entertain his guests with wine. Papayas and Buddha's hands were piled up like mountains, while jasmine and pearl orchids filled the air with fragrance as sweet as snow. Night after night, he drank and sang joyfully, wearing a silk cap and a crane robe, truly a person among the immortals. During the reign of King Fu, he was appointed as the Middle Commandery Governor, leading the way with guards carrying halberds, shouting as they entered the court, becoming even more honored and prominent. In the year of Ding You (1645), when the dynasty changed, his land and property were confiscated, leaving him with nowhere to stand. His concubines scattered like rain, and he found himself alone and destitute, living among beggars, even to the point of being beaten on behalf of others. His residence was converted into a military office. One day, he agreed with the executioner on the number of strokes, calculating the compensation.

    795

    受刑时，其数过倍，青君大呼曰：“我徐青君也。”兵宪林公骇问左右，有哀王孙者，跪而对曰：“此魏国公之公子徐青君也，穷苦为人代杖。此堂乃其家厅，不觉伤心呼号耳。”林公怜而释之，慰藉甚至，且曰：“君尚有非钦产可清还者，本道当为查给，以终余生。”青君跪谢曰：“花园是某自造，非钦产也。”林公唯唯，厚赠遗之，查还其园，卖花石、货柱础以自活。吾观《南史》所记，东昏宫妃卖蜡烛为业；杜少陵诗云：“问之不肯道姓名，但道困苦乞为奴。”呜呼！岂虚也哉！

    Explanation

    795

    When Qīng Jūn was being punished, the severity of the punishment was disproportionately severe. Qīng Jūn cried out loudly, saying, "I am Qīng Jūn of the Prince of Wei!" The military governor Lín Gōng was startled and asked those around him. Someone who felt sorrow for the prince's descendant knelt and replied, "This is the son of the Prince of Wei, Qīng Jūn, who is poor and has taken another's punishment. This hall is his ancestral hall, and he was moved to tears and cried out in sorrow." Lín Gōng, moved by compassion, released him and offered him comfort. He said, "If you still have any non-royal property that can be returned, I will investigate and give it back to you, so that you may live out your remaining years comfortably." Qīng Jūn knelt and thanked him, saying, "The garden is my own creation, not royal property." Lín Gōng nodded and gave him generous gifts, investigating the return of the garden, and allowing him to sell garden stones and column bases to support himself. I have read in the Nánshǐ (History of the Southern Dynasties) that during the Eastern Wu period, a palace concubine sold candles as her profession; Du Fu's poetry says, "When asked, she would not reveal her name, but only say that she was in poverty and begged to be a slave." How true this is!

    796

    同人社集松风阁，雪衣、眉生皆在，饮罢，联骑入城，红妆翠袖，跃马扬鞭，观者塞途。太平景象，恍然心目。
    　　　　丁继之扮张驴儿娘，张燕筑扮宾头卢，朱维章扮武大郎，皆妙绝一世。丁、张二老，并寿九十余。钱虞山《题三老图诗》末句云：“秦淮烟月经游处，华表归来白鹤知。”不胜黄公酒垆之叹。
    　　　　无锡邹公履游平康，头戴红纱巾，身着纸衣，齿高跟屐，佯狂沉缅，挥斥黄金不顾。初场毕，击大司马门鼓，送试卷。大合乐于妓家，高声自诵其文，妓皆称快。或时阑入梨园，氍毹上为“参军鹘”也。

    Explanation

    796

    A group of friends gathered at the Pine Wind Pavilion, with Snowy Clothing and Eyebrow both present. After drinking, they rode together into the city, dressed in red and green, galloping on horseback. Spectators filled the streets. A scene of peace and prosperity, vividly present in the mind.

    Ding Jizhi dressed up as Zhang Luer's mother, Zhang Yanzhu dressed up as Bin Tolu, and Zhu Weizhang dressed up as Wu Dalang. All were exceptionally talented. Ding and Zhang, both elderly, lived to be over ninety years old. Qian Yushan, in the final line of his poem "Ode to the Three Elders," said: "The smoke and moonlight of Qinhuai River, where we once strolled, only the white crane at the monument knows." It evokes the sorrow of the wine shop of Huang Gong.

    In Wuxi, Zou Gongli toured the entertainment district, wearing a red silk scarf and paper clothing, with high-heeled wooden clogs. He pretended to be mad and indulged himself, spending gold freely without hesitation. After the first examination, he struck the drum at the Grand Marshal's gate to submit his exam papers. He held a grand musical performance at a brothel and loudly recited his own writings. The courtesans all praised him. Sometimes he would enter the theater uninvited and perform as a "Military Governor's Hawk" on the stage.

    797

    柳敬亭，泰州人，本姓曹，避仇流落江湖，休于树下，乃姓柳。善说书，游于金陵，吴桥范司马、桐城何相国引为上客。常往来南曲，与张燕筑、沈公宪俱。张、沈以歌曲，敬亭以弹词，酒酣以往，击节悲吟，倾靡四座，盖优孟、东方曼倩之流也。后入左宁南幕府，出入兵间。宁南亡败，又游松江马提督军中，郁郁不得志。年已八十余矣，间遇余侨寓宜睡轩中，犹说《秦叔宝见姑娘》也。
    　　　　莱阳姜如须，游于李十娘家，渔于色，匿不出户。方密之、孙克咸并能屏风上行，漏下三刻，星河皎然，连袂闲行，经过赵、李，垂帘闭户，夜人定矣。两君一跃登屋，直至卧房，排闼哄张，势如贼盗。如须下床，跪称：“大王乞命！毋伤十娘！”两君掷刀大笑，曰：“三郎郎当！三郎郎当！”复呼酒极饮，尽醉而散。盖如须行三。如须高才旷代，偶效樊川，略同谢傅，秋风团扇，寄兴扫眉，非沉溺烟花之比。聊记一条，以存流风余韵云尔。

    Explanation

    797

    Liu Jingting, originally from Taizhou, was born with the surname Cao but changed it to Liu after fleeing his enemies and wandering the rivers and lakes, resting under a tree. Skilled in storytelling, he traveled in Jinling, where he was welcomed as a guest of honor by Fang Sima of Wuqiao and He Xiangguo of Tongcheng. He often associated with Southern opera and was accompanied by Zhang Yanzhu and Shen Gongxian. Zhang and Shen performed songs, while Liu Jingting recited dan ci, the narrative music. When drunk, they clapped in rhythm and sang mournfully, captivating all who listened. He was compared to Ousheng and Dongfang Manqian, historical figures known for their wit and storytelling. Later, he joined the staff of Left Nanning and served among the troops. After Left Nanning's defeat, he wandered in the army of General Ma in Songjiang, feeling dejected and unfulfilled. By the time he was over eighty, he occasionally met me in the Yishui Pavilion where I was staying, still reciting the story of Qin Shubao meeting a young lady.

    Jiang Ruxu of Laizhou, meanwhile, was wandering in the home of Li Shiniang, fishing for favors and hiding himself from the outside world. Fang Mizi and Sun Kexian were both skilled in walking stealthily on screens, and at three hours past midnight, the Milky Way shone brightly as they wandered arm in arm, passing by Zhao and Li, pulling down the curtains and closing the doors. It was late at night. The two of them suddenly leaped onto the roof, breaking into Li Shiniang's bedroom, causing a commotion like thieves and robbers. Jiang Ruxu got up from bed, kneeling and pleading, "Great King, spare my life! Do not harm Shiniang!" The two of them threw away their knives and laughed, saying, "Third Brother is clumsy! Third Brother is clumsy!" They then called for more wine and drank until they were thoroughly drunk, dispersing afterward. Jiang Ruxu was the third son. He was a man of exceptional talent, surpassing the ages, occasionally imitating Fan Chuan and somewhat like Xie Fu, expressing sentiment through painting eyebrows in the autumn wind and hand fan, not comparable to being deeply involved in the world of courtesans. I will briefly record this to preserve the remaining charm and grace.

    798

    陈则梁，人奇文奇，举体皆奇。尝致书眉楼，劝其早脱风尘，速寻道伴，言词切至。眉楼遂择主而事，诚以惊弓之鸟，遽为透网之鳞也。扫眉才子，慧业文人，时节因缘，不得不为延津之合矣。
    　　　　十七、八女郎，歌“杨柳岸晓风残月”，若在曲中，则处处有之，时时有之。予作《忆江南》词云：“江南好景本无多，只在晓风残月下。”思之只益伤神，见之不堪回首矣！沈公宪以串戏擅长，同时推为第一。王式之中翰、王恒之水部，异曲同工，游戏三昧，江总持、柳耆卿依稀再见，非如吕敬迁、李仙鹤也。

    Explanation

    798

    Chen Zeliang, a person and his writings are both extraordinary, and everything about him is extraordinary. He once wrote a letter to Mei Lou, urging him to quickly escape from the world of fame and fortune and to swiftly find a spiritual companion, with words that were direct and to the point. Mei Lou then chose a master to serve, truly like a startled bird that suddenly became a fish that had escaped the net. Talented young women, enlightened literati, the right time and circumstances, could not help but form a rare and auspicious union.

    Young women in their late teens or early twenties, singing the line "Willow bank, morning breeze, waning moon," if it were in a song, it would exist everywhere and at all times. I composed a Memories of Jiangnan poem that says: "The good scenery of Jiangnan is inherently rare, only existing in the early morning breeze and the waning moon." Thinking about it only increases sorrow, seeing it is unbearable to look back! Shen Gongxian is skilled in performing in plays, and at the same time, he is considered the best. Wang Shi's Zhonghuan and Wang Heng's Shuibü, though different in method, achieve the same result, fully absorbed in their craft, like Jiang Zong and Liu Yong, almost seen again, unlike Lu Jingqian and Li Xianhe.

    799

    乐户有妻有妾，防闲最严，谨守贞节，不与人客交语。人客欲强见之，一揖之外，翻身入帘也。乱后，有旧院大街顾三之妻李三娘者，流落江湖，遂为名妓。忽为匪类所持，暴系吴郡狱中。余与刘海门梦锡兄弟及姚翼侯、张鞠存极力拯之，致书司李李蠖庵，仅而得免。然亦如严幼芳、刘婆惜，备受箠楚决杖矣。三娘长身玉色，倭堕如云，量洪善饮，饮至百觥不醉。时辛丑中秋之际，庭兰盛开，置酒高会，黄兰丛及玉峰女士冯静容偕来。居停主人金叔侃，尽倾家酿，分曹角胜，轰饮如雷，如项羽章邯钜鹿之战，诸侯皆作壁上观。饮至天明，诸君皆大吐，静容亦吐，髻鬟委地，或横卧地上，衣履狼藉。惟三娘醒，然犹不眠，倚桂树也。兰丛贾其余勇，尚与翼侯豁拳，各尽三四大斗而别。嗟乎！俯仰岁月之间，诸君皆埋骨青山，美人亦栖身黄土。河山邈矣，能不悲哉！

    799

    In households with a wife and concubines, the most stringent measures were taken to prevent inappropriate behavior, and they strictly adhered to chastity, not engaging in conversation with visitors. Visitors who wished to see them were greeted with a bow and then immediately sent back into the inner chamber. After a period of chaos, there was a woman named Li San娘, the wife of Gu San from Old Courtyard Street, who wandered and drifted, eventually becoming a famous courtesan. Suddenly, she was seized by bandits and was violently imprisoned in Wu Prefecture. I and my brothers Liu Hai and Meng Xi, along with Yao Yi Hou and Zhang Ju Cun, made great efforts to rescue her, writing a letter to the magistrate Li Huo An, and she barely managed to avoid the situation. However, like Yan You Fang and Liu Po Xi, she also suffered severe beatings and punishments. San娘 was tall and fair-skinned, with long, flowing hair, and she had a large capacity for drinking, able to drink a hundred cups without getting drunk. It was during the Mid-Autumn Festival of the year Xin Chou, when the courtyard orchids were in full bloom, and a grand banquet was held. Huang Lan Cong and Lady Yu Feng, Feng Jing Rong, came together. The host, Jin Shu Kan, poured out all his family's wine, and they divided into teams to compete, drinking loudly like thunder, like the Battle of Julu between Xiang Yu and Zhang Han, where the other lords watched from the sidelines. They drank until dawn, and all the guests vomited, including Jing Rong, whose hair fell to the ground. Some lay on the ground, their clothes and shoes in disarray. Only San娘 was awake, but she did not sleep, leaning against an osmanthus tree. Lan Cong, with the remaining courage, still played a game of dice with Yi Hou, each drinking three or four dou and then parting. Alas! In the blink of an eye, all the guests are buried in green hills, and the beautiful woman also rests in yellow earth. The rivers and mountains are far away, how can one not feel sad!

    800

    李贞丽者，李香之假母，有豪侠气，尝一夜博输千金立尽。与阳羡陈定生善。香年十三，亦侠而慧，从吴人周如松受歌，玉茗堂《四梦》皆能妙其音节，尤工《琵琶》。与雪苑侯朝宗善，阉党阮大铖欲纳交于朝宗，香力谏止，不与通。朝宗去后，有故开府田仰以重金邀致香。香辞曰：“妾不敢负侯公子也。”卒不往。盖前此大铖恨朝宗，罗致欲杀之，朝宗逃而免。并欲杀定生也，定生大为锦衣冯可宗所辱。
    　　　　云间才子夏灵胥作《青相篇》，寄武塘钱漱广，末段云：“二十年来事已非，不开画阁锁芳菲。那堪两院无人到，独对三春有燕飞。风弦不动新歌扇，露井全飘旧舞衣。花草朱门空后阁，琵琶青冢恨明妃。独有青楼旧相识，蛾眉零落头新白。梦断何年行雨踪，情深一调留云迹。院本伤心正德词，乐府销魂教坊籍。为唱当时《乌夜啼》，青衫泪满江南客。”观此可以尽曲中之变矣，悲夫！

    Explanation

    800

    Rizhenli was the adoptive mother of Li Xiang, a woman of bold and chivalrous spirit. She once gambled away a thousand gold pieces in a single night. She was on good terms with Chen Dingsheng of Yangxian. At the age of thirteen, Li Xiang was also chivalrous and intelligent. She learned singing from Wu people Zhou Rusong, and was adept at the musical tones of Yumingtang's "Four Dreams," especially skilled in playing the pipa. She was close to Xueyuan Hou Chaoyong, but the eunuch party's Ruan Dacheng wanted to befriend Chaoyong, and Xiang strongly advised against it, refusing to associate with him. After Chaoyong left, an old official Tian Yang invited Xiang with a large sum of money. Xiang refused, saying, "I dare not betray Master Hou." She ultimately did not go. Previously, Dacheng had harbored resentment towards Chaoyong, concocting charges to kill him, but Chaoyong escaped and was spared. Dacheng also intended to kill Dingsheng, who was greatly humiliated by Jinwei Feng Kezong.

    The talented scholar Xia Lingxu of Yunnan wrote "Qingxiang Pian" and sent it to Wu Tang Qianshuang. The concluding section reads: "In the past twenty years, things have changed, and the painted hall no longer locks the fragrant flowers. How can one bear that both halls are deserted, with only swallows flying in the spring. The wind strings do not move the new song fans, and the well is full of the old dance clothes. The flowers and grass of the vermilion gate are empty in the back hall, and the pipa at the green tomb laments Mingfei. There is only an old acquaintance from the green building, with the eyebrows faded and the hair newly white. When will the dream end, and where are the traces of the rain? Deep feelings are left in one tune, lingering in the clouds. The court drama is heartbroken with Zhengde's lyrics, and the music hall is soul-stirring with the teaching records. Singing the 'Wuyeti' of that time, the green robe is wet with tears of the Jiangnan guest." By observing this, one can see the changes in the world of music, truly a pity.

    801

    附录：盒子会
    　　　　沈周作《盒子会辞》，其序云：“南京旧院，有色艺俱优者，或二十、三十姓，结为手帕姊妹。每上灯节，以春檠、巧具、肴核相赛，名『盒子会』。凡得奇品为胜，输者罚酒酌胜者。中有所私，亦来挟金助会，厌厌夜饮，弥月而止。席间设灯张乐，各出其技能，赋此以识京城乐事也。”辞云：“平乐灯宵闹如沸，灯火烘春笑声内。盒奁来往斗芳邻，手帕绸缪通姊妹。东家西家百络盛，装肴饤核春满檠。豹胎间挟鲤冰脆，乌榄分欃椰玉生。不论多同较奇有，品里输无倒赔酒。呈丝逞竹会心欢，裒钞裨金走情友。哄堂一月自春风，酒香人语百花中。一般桃李三千户，亦有愁人隔墙住。”

    Explanation

    801

    Appendix: The Box Competition

    Shen Zhou wrote the Box Competition Discourse, and in its preface he said: "In the old quarters of Nanjing, there were courtesans who were both beautiful and talented. Some formed handkerchief sisterhoods, consisting of twenty or thirty families. Every Lantern Festival, they would compete with each other by displaying their most exquisite spring lanterns, ingenious devices, and a variety of foodstuffs, calling it the 'Box Competition.' The winner was determined by the quality of the displayed items, and the loser had to pay for the winner's drinks. Friends and acquaintances also contributed money to support the event, and the festivities lasted for a full month. In the meantime, the hall was lit with lanterns, music was played, and each participant showcased their skills. This was written to record the joyful events of the capital."

    The discourse goes: "On the Lantern Festival, the streets of the capital are as lively as boiling water, with lights illuminating the spring and laughter filling the air. The box competition is held, where handkerchief sisters from neighboring houses compete by displaying their finest delicacies. The box is filled with spring lanterns, ingenious devices, and a variety of foodstuffs. The dishes range from rare delicacies like leopard fetus and ice-cold carp to black olives, nuts, and coconut and jade-like treats. The competition is judged by the quality of the items, and the loser must pay for the winner's drinks. The participants perform music and dance, and friends contribute money to support the event. The festivities last for a full month, with the hall filled with the aroma of food and the sound of people talking. Among the thousands of households, there are those who enjoy the festivities, and others who are separated by walls."
